technology.zh: 军队以复合弓骑兵、重装骑兵、攻城器械、石弩、城堡工事和地中海—中东传统兵器为主，拥有成熟的筑城与攻城经验，但尚未使用后世意义上的火药武器
technology.en: The army relied on composite-bow cavalry, armored cavalry, siege engines, traction and counterweight artillery traditions, fortification systems, and eastern Mediterranean weapons, possessing mature expertise in fortification and siegecraft but not yet employing later true gunpowder weapons

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自十字军诸国，特别是耶路撒冷沿海据点与安条克、的黎波里等拉丁势力；北面需警惕鲁姆苏丹国、亚美尼亚奇里乞亚与格鲁吉亚力量变化，内部则要防止阿尤布家族支系竞争削弱统一指挥
threats.en: The main threats came from the Crusader states, especially the Latin coastal strongholds of the Kingdom of Jerusalem along with Antioch and Tripoli; to the north the polity had to watch shifts in the power of the Sultanate of Rum, Cilician Armenia, and Georgia, while internally it needed to prevent rivalry among Ayyubid family branches from weakening unified command

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

populationDescription.zh: 人口主体集中于埃及尼罗河谷与三角洲，叙利亚城镇和灌溉平原人口密度次之。埃及提供了压倒性的农业与税收基础，叙利亚则贡献军事、商业和战略城市网络
populationDescription.en: The majority of the population was concentrated in the Egyptian Nile Valley and Delta, with secondary concentrations in Syrian towns and irrigated plains. Egypt supplied the overwhelming agricultural and fiscal base, while Syria contributed military, commercial, and strategic urban networks

description.zh: 法蒂玛/马穆鲁克首都，伊斯兰世界中心
description.en: Fatimid-Mamluk capital, center of the Islamic world

description.zh: 古代学术中心与地中海大港
description.en: Ancient center of learning and great Mediterranean port

description.zh: 倭马亚王朝首都，世界最古老连续有人居住的城市
description.en: Umayyad capital, world's oldest continuously inhabited city

description.zh: 三大宗教圣城，十字军争夺焦点
description.en: Holy city of three faiths, focal point of the Crusades

socialClasses.zh: 上层为苏丹家族、埃米尔、军官、法学家和大商人；中层包括城市手工业者、小商人、书记员、宗教学者与地方地主；下层为农民、雇工、搬运工及奴隶。穆斯林居主导地位，但基督徒与犹太人构成重要城市与财政社群
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the sultan’s family, emirs, officers, jurists, and major merchants; the middle strata included artisans, small traders, clerks, religious scholars, and local landholders; the lower strata comprised peasants, laborers, porters, and slaves. Muslims were dominant, but Christians and Jews formed important urban and fiscal communities

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的长期对等盟友；更常见的是与部分穆斯林邻邦、阿拉伯部族及阿尤布家族支系形成临时合作关系，并视情势与拜占庭或意大利海商进行务实接触
allies.en: There were no permanent long-term allies in the full sense; more common were temporary cooperative arrangements with neighboring Muslim powers, Arab tribal groups, and Ayyubid family branches, alongside pragmatic contacts with Byzantium or Italian maritime merchants when circumstances required

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为十字军拉丁诸国，尤其是耶路撒冷王国残余沿海据点、安条克公国与的黎波里伯国；局部时期也与奇里乞亚亚美尼亚、格鲁吉亚或某些穆斯林竞争者发生冲突
enemies.en: The principal enemies were the Latin Crusader states, especially the remaining coastal strongholds of the Kingdom of Jerusalem, the Principality of Antioch, and the County of Tripoli; at times the polity also clashed with Cilician Armenia, Georgia, or particular Muslim rivals

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持埃及为核心、控制叙利亚、遏制十字军海岸据点、调解家族分封冲突并保持与商人共和国贸易渠道为重点；阿迪勒一世相较萨拉丁更重视稳健平衡、谈判休战与逐步整合资源
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving Egypt as the core, controlling Syria, containing Crusader coastal enclaves, mediating conflicts among family appanages, and maintaining trade channels with the maritime republics; compared with Saladin, Al-Adil I placed greater emphasis on cautious balance, negotiated truces, and gradual consolidation of resources

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪伊斯兰世界
era.en: The medieval Islamic world of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的尼罗河灌溉管理、制糖工艺、医院与医学教育体系、城市供水与市场监管、石制堡垒建筑、攻城机械、纸张使用和发达的文书行政传统
keyInnovations.en: Mature Nile irrigation management, sugar-processing techniques, hospitals and medical training, urban water and market regulation, stone fortress architecture, siege machinery, widespread paper use, and an advanced documentary-bureaucratic tradition

strengths.zh: 拥有埃及这一东地中海最富庶农业税源，控制开罗与亚历山大等商业中心，军事上兼具骑兵机动与坚固要塞体系，且具备较强的财政动员与宗教合法性
strengths.en: It possessed Egypt, the richest agricultural tax base in the eastern Mediterranean, controlled major commercial centers such as Cairo and Alexandria, combined cavalry mobility with strong fortress systems, and enjoyed solid fiscal mobilization capacity and religious legitimacy

weaknesses.zh: 统治结构高度依赖家族分封与个人权威，叙利亚诸地整合成本高；军队中封授关系复杂，内部竞争可能削弱统一战略；海军力量相对有限，难以彻底拔除十字军沿海据点
weaknesses.en: The ruling structure depended heavily on family appanage and personal authority, making the integration of Syrian territories costly; military obligations tied to land assignments were complex, and internal rivalry could weaken unified strategy; naval power was relatively limited, hindering the complete elimination of Crusader coastal enclaves

outlook.zh: 1200年前后总体前景稳定且有利。若阿迪勒一世能继续维持家族平衡、保障埃及财政与叙利亚防线，则政权可在未来数十年保持地区强权地位；但若继承纠纷加剧或十字军与海上势力联合施压，统一性将受到考验
outlook.en: Around 1200 the overall outlook was stable and favorable. If Al-Adil I could continue balancing the family, protecting Egypt’s finances, and securing the Syrian front, the polity could remain a regional great power for decades; however, intensified succession disputes or coordinated pressure from Crusaders and maritime powers would test its unity

description.zh: 萨拉丁之后的继承政权，统治埃及并深度介入叙利亚与十字军战争。
description.en: The successor state after Saladin, ruling Egypt and deeply involved in Syria and the Crusades.

ideology.zh: 泛伊斯兰防御十字军、家族分封与务实外交、维护萨拉丁遗产
ideology.en: Pan-Islamic defense against Crusaders, family appanage system and pragmatic diplomacy, preserving Saladin's legacy

relationship.zh: 阿迪勒一世（萨拉丁之弟）在萨拉丁1193年去世后的继承纷争中凭借高超的政治手腕和军事实力脱颖而出，于1200年前后统一了阿尤布家族的核心领地。他以务实外交著称，在维持反十字军圣战姿态的同时与法兰克人进行有限度的贸易和外交往来。阿迪勒通过将各地分封给子侄来维持家族团结，但这也埋下了日后内斗的种子。
relationship.en: Al-Adil I (Saladin's brother) emerged from the succession struggles following Saladin's death in 1193 through superior political skill and military strength, reunifying the Ayyubid family's core domains by around 1200. Known for pragmatic diplomacy, he maintained the posture of anti-Crusader jihad while conducting limited trade and diplomatic exchanges with the Franks. Al-Adil preserved family unity by distributing territories among sons and nephews, though this also sowed seeds of future internal conflict.

description.zh: 阿尤布王朝由萨拉丁于1171年推翻法蒂玛王朝后建立，以库尔德裔军事家族统治埃及和叙利亚。萨拉丁在1187年哈丁战役中击溃十字军、收复耶路撒冷，成为伊斯兰世界最伟大的英雄之一。然而他在1193年去世后，帝国按照家族分封传统被分配给诸子和兄弟，引发了长达数年的内部争夺。阿迪勒一世（1145-1218年）是萨拉丁最能干的兄弟，他先后击败或收买了其他家族竞争者，最终控制了埃及、大马士革和北叙利亚。阿迪勒的统治风格与萨拉丁截然不同——他更重视经济利益和商业外交，与威尼斯等意大利商业城邦保持贸易关系，在对待十字军问题上采取防御而非进攻姿态。他将开罗建设为地中海世界最繁荣的城市之一，大力发展尼罗河灌溉农业和红海-印度洋贸易。然而阿尤布王朝的根本弱点在于缺乏明确的继承制度——帝国更像是一个由同族诸王协调管理的松散联邦，而非中央集权国家。
description.en: The Ayyubid dynasty was established by Saladin after he overthrew the Fatimid dynasty in 1171, ruling Egypt and Syria as a Kurdish military family. Saladin's crushing defeat of the Crusaders at the Battle of Hattin in 1187 and recovery of Jerusalem made him one of Islam's greatest heroes. Yet after his death in 1193, the empire was distributed among sons and brothers according to family appanage tradition, triggering years of internal struggle. Al-Adil I (1145–1218) was Saladin's most capable brother, who defeated or co-opted other family rivals to eventually control Egypt, Damascus, and northern Syria. Al-Adil's governing style differed markedly from Saladin's — he prioritized economic interests and commercial diplomacy, maintained trade relations with Venice and other Italian mercantile cities, and took a defensive rather than offensive stance toward the Crusaders. He developed Cairo into one of the Mediterranean world's most prosperous cities, investing heavily in Nile irrigation agriculture and Red Sea–Indian Ocean trade. Yet the Ayyubid dynasty's fundamental weakness was the absence of clear succession rules — the empire resembled a loose federation coordinated by kinsman-kings rather than a centralized state.

role.zh: 萨拉丁之弟，以务实外交统合阿尤布帝国
role.en: Saladin's brother, unified the Ayyubid empire through pragmatic diplomacy

role.zh: 阿迪勒之子，未来的苏丹，将与腓特烈二世谈判
role.en: Son of Al-Adil, future sultan who would negotiate with Frederick II

leader.zh: 突厥裔马穆鲁克将领团

ideology.zh: 军事精英统治、奴隶兵制度下的精英骑兵传统、通过军功获取权力与地位
ideology.en: Military elite rule, elite cavalry tradition within the slave-soldier system, acquiring power and status through martial merit

relationship.zh: 马穆鲁克（"被拥有者"）是阿尤布王朝的军事支柱。这些主要来自钦察草原和高加索地区的突厥奴隶士兵，在年幼时被购买并在军事学校中接受严格训练，成为精锐骑兵。阿尤布苏丹依赖马穆鲁克军团的战斗力维持统治，但马穆鲁克将领的军事权力也在持续增长，成为朝廷中不可忽视的政治力量。
relationship.en: The Mamluks ('the owned ones') were the Ayyubid dynasty's military backbone. These Turkic slave-soldiers, purchased mainly from the Kipchak steppe and Caucasus region as youths, received rigorous training in military schools to become elite cavalry. Ayyubid sultans depended on the Mamluk corps' fighting prowess to maintain their rule, but the military power of Mamluk commanders was steadily growing, making them an increasingly formidable political force at court.

description.zh: 马穆鲁克制度是伊斯兰世界独特的军事奴隶精英体系。阿尤布王朝时期，苏丹们从中亚钦察草原、高加索和俄罗斯南部大量购入突厥和切尔克斯少年奴隶，在开罗城堡等军事学校中对其进行骑术、射箭、剑术和伊斯兰教义的全面训练。这些马穆鲁克在完成训练后被释放为自由人，成为苏丹的精锐私军。他们享有丰厚的军饷、伊克塔（军事采邑）和社会地位，但被期望对主人保持绝对忠诚。到1200年前后，马穆鲁克埃米尔已在军事指挥体系中占据核心位置——他们控制着最精锐的骑兵部队，在战场上往往是决定胜负的关键力量。马穆鲁克制度的内在矛盾在于：苏丹需要这些精锐战士维持军事优势，但他们的军事实力也构成了对王权的潜在威胁。这一矛盾将在1250年达到临界点——马穆鲁克将领在抵抗第七次十字军的战争中发动政变，推翻了阿尤布王朝，建立了马穆鲁克苏丹国，开创了一个持续近三百年的军事奴隶王朝。
description.en: The Mamluk system was a unique military slave-elite institution of the Islamic world. During the Ayyubid period, sultans purchased large numbers of Turkic and Circassian youth from the Central Asian Kipchak steppe, Caucasus, and southern Russia, training them comprehensively in horsemanship, archery, swordsmanship, and Islamic doctrine at military schools such as the Cairo Citadel. After completing training, these Mamluks were manumitted as free men, becoming the sultan's elite personal army. They enjoyed generous stipends, iqta (military fiefs), and social status, but were expected to maintain absolute loyalty to their master. By around 1200, Mamluk emirs occupied core positions in the military command structure — controlling the most elite cavalry units and often serving as the decisive factor on battlefields. The inherent contradiction of the Mamluk system was that the sultan needed these elite warriors for military supremacy, but their military power also constituted a potential threat to royal authority. This contradiction would reach a breaking point in 1250 — Mamluk commanders staged a coup during the war against the Seventh Crusade, overthrew the Ayyubid dynasty, and established the Mamluk Sultanate, founding a military slave dynasty that would endure for nearly three centuries.

role.zh: 阿尤布时期马穆鲁克军事精英的代表人物
role.en: Representative of the Mamluk military elite during the Ayyubid period

name.zh: 伊斯玛仪派刺客（阿萨辛）

leader.zh: 拉希德丁·锡南的继承者

ideology.zh: 伊斯玛仪派什叶教义、政治暗杀作为战略工具、山堡独立自治
ideology.en: Ismaili Shia doctrine, political assassination as a strategic tool, mountain fortress autonomy

relationship.zh: 叙利亚伊斯玛仪派（尼扎里派）以"阿萨辛"之名闻名于世，其山堡网络在阿尤布王朝的叙利亚领地内构成一股半独立的军事力量。拉希德丁·锡南（"山中老人"）在1193年去世后，叙利亚尼扎里派虽然实力有所下降，但仍保持着令十字军和穆斯林政权都心存忌惮的暗杀网络和山堡体系。阿尤布苏丹对其采取既遏制又利用的策略。
relationship.en: The Syrian Ismailis (Nizari sect), known worldwide as the 'Assassins,' maintained a network of mountain fortresses within Ayyubid Syrian territories as a semi-independent military force. After Rashid ad-Din Sinan ('Old Man of the Mountain') died in 1193, the Syrian Nizaris' power somewhat declined, but they preserved an assassination network and fortress system that kept both Crusader and Muslim regimes wary. Ayyubid sultans adopted a strategy of both containment and exploitation toward them.

description.zh: 叙利亚尼扎里伊斯玛仪派——即十字军口中的"阿萨辛"——是中世纪最神秘也最令人畏惧的政治力量之一。这一什叶派极端分支起源于11世纪波斯的哈桑·萨巴赫运动，以位于险峻山区的堡垒链为基地，以精心策划的政治暗杀作为影响力工具。叙利亚分支的巅峰时期在拉希德丁·锡南（约1132-1193年）的领导下达到——他将马斯亚夫及叙利亚海岸山脉中的一系列城堡发展为半独立的微型国家。锡南以"山中老人"之名在十字军和穆斯林世界同样令人闻风丧胆，据传他多次派遣"费达伊"（敢死队员）执行刺杀任务，目标包括十字军领主、穆斯林将领甚至萨拉丁本人。锡南去世后（约1193年），叙利亚尼扎里派失去了独立行动的最强领袖，日益受到阿尤布王朝的政治影响和军事压力。然而他们的山堡体系仍然坚固，暗杀网络依旧令人畏惧。尼扎里派最终将在1270年代被马穆鲁克苏丹拜巴尔斯征服，而他们的波斯总部阿拉穆特则在1256年被蒙古人摧毁。
description.en: The Syrian Nizari Ismailis — the 'Assassins' of Crusader lore — were one of the most mysterious and feared political forces of the medieval period. This extreme Shia offshoot originated in Hasan-i Sabbah's 11th-century Persian movement, using chains of mountain fortresses as bases and meticulously planned political assassinations as tools of influence. The Syrian branch reached its peak under Rashid ad-Din Sinan (c.1132–1193), who developed Masyaf and a chain of castles in Syria's coastal mountains into a semi-independent micro-state. Known as the 'Old Man of the Mountain,' Sinan was equally feared across the Crusader and Muslim worlds, reportedly dispatching 'fidai' (devotees) on assassination missions targeting Crusader lords, Muslim generals, and even Saladin himself. After Sinan's death (c.1193), the Syrian Nizaris lost their strongest independent leader and came increasingly under Ayyubid political influence and military pressure. Yet their fortress system remained formidable and their assassination network still inspired fear. The Nizaris would ultimately be conquered by Mamluk Sultan Baybars in the 1270s, while their Persian headquarters at Alamut was destroyed by the Mongols in 1256.

role.zh: 叙利亚尼扎里派最伟大的领袖（1193年去世），遗产仍深刻影响该组织
role.en: Greatest leader of the Syrian Nizaris (died 1193), whose legacy still profoundly shaped the organization

name.zh: 阿拉伯伊斯兰哈里发政权

governmentForm.zh: 以逊尼派哈里发为中心的神权君主制，兼具宗教象征权威与有限的直接领地统治
governmentForm.en: A Sunni caliphal theocratic monarchy combining supreme religious symbolism with direct rule over a limited territorial domain

socialStructure.zh: 社会由哈里发家族、宫廷官僚、乌理玛与法学家、军事奴隶与职业军人、城市商人和手工业者、乡村农民、受保护的非穆斯林社群以及奴隶构成。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the caliphal household, court bureaucrats, ulama and jurists, military slaves and professional soldiers, urban merchants and artisans, rural cultivators, protected non-Muslim communities, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 阿拔斯皇室、宫廷文官集团、高级乌理玛、与哈里发结盟的军政精英
rulingClass.en: The Abbasid royal house, court bureaucratic elite, senior ulama, and military-political notables aligned with the caliph

succession.zh: 原则上为王朝世袭，由在位哈里发指定继承人并争取宗教与政治精英承认，但在实践中常受宫廷权力平衡影响。
succession.en: In principle dynastic hereditary succession, with the reigning caliph designating an heir and seeking recognition from religious and political elites, though in practice shaped by court power balances.

structure.zh: 1200年的阿拔斯政权以哈里发宫廷为核心，下设御前书记机构、财政与赋税机关、司法宗教体系、巡察与邮传网络，以及管理巴格达及其周边伊拉克地区的地方行政机构。哈里发的实际统治范围有限，但其诏令、册封与宗教名义在更广泛的逊尼派世界仍具高度合法性。
structure.en: In 1200 the Abbasid polity was centered on the caliphal court, supported by chancery offices, fiscal and tax departments, judicial-religious institutions, inspection and postal networks, and local administration for Baghdad and surrounding Iraq. The caliph's effective territorial rule was limited, but his decrees, investiture, and religious prestige retained strong legitimacy across much of the Sunni world.

function.zh: 起草诏令、外交文书、任命状与册封文件，维系哈里发在伊斯兰世界的礼仪与法统权威。
function.en: Drafted decrees, diplomatic correspondence, appointments, and investiture documents, sustaining the caliph's ceremonial and legal authority across the Islamic world.

function.zh: 管理地税、市场税、地产收入、国库拨款与官员俸给。
function.en: Managed land tax, market dues, estate revenues, treasury disbursements, and official salaries.

function.zh: 协调卡迪任命、宗教捐赠、学校与清真寺事务，并维护逊尼派法学秩序。
function.en: Coordinated qadi appointments, religious endowments, schools and mosques, and upheld Sunni legal order.

function.zh: 维持信使路线、收集地方情报、监督官员并传递军政文书。
function.en: Maintained courier routes, gathered provincial intelligence, monitored officials, and transmitted military and administrative documents.

function.zh: 负责宫廷供给、仪仗、宫门守卫以及近卫军编制管理。
function.en: Oversaw palace provisioning, ceremonial functions, gate security, and administration of the guard forces.

name.zh: 巴格达与伊拉克地方行政署

function.zh: 管理城市治安、运河与灌溉、粮食征调、乡村赋役和地方司法协作。
function.en: Handled urban order, canals and irrigation, grain requisitioning, rural taxation and labor obligations, and local judicial coordination.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政集中于巴格达、瓦西特及伊拉克腹地若干城镇与农业区。城市由总督、副总督、税吏、市场监官和卡迪共同治理，乡村则依靠税务承包者、地主、部族首领与灌溉管理者协助统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was concentrated in Baghdad, Wasit, and several towns and agricultural districts of Iraq. Cities were governed by governors, deputies, tax officials, market inspectors, and qadis, while the countryside relied on tax contractors, landholders, tribal shaykhs, and irrigation managers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以伊斯兰教法为核心，主要由逊尼派法学家与卡迪实施；宫廷另以行政敕令和御前裁断处理财政、治安和政治案件。哈里发通过任命法官、支持学校和宗教捐赠来维护其正统性。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on Islamic law, chiefly administered by Sunni jurists and qadis; the court also used administrative decrees and sovereign adjudication for fiscal, security, and political cases. The caliph upheld legitimacy through judicial appointments, schools, and religious endowments.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收来源包括土地税、穆斯林什一税、城市商税、关津税、市场征费、国有庄园收入以及非常规贡纳。由于领土有限，财政更依赖伊拉克核心区农业、城市商业和宫廷直属地产。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew on land tax, Muslim tithe, urban commercial dues, transit tolls, market levies, revenues from crown estates, and occasional tribute. Because the realm was territorially limited, finances relied heavily on agriculture in core Iraq, urban commerce, and estates directly tied to the court.

religion.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰教为官方与主导信仰，同时巴格达也存在什叶派社群、基督徒、犹太人及其他受保护宗教群体。
religion.en: Sunni Islam was the official and dominant faith, while Baghdad also contained Shi'i communities, Christians, Jews, and other protected religious groups.

philosophy.zh: 主流思想以伊斯兰法学、神学与政治伦理为中心，结合阿拉伯-伊斯兰学术传统。阿拔斯晚期仍延续理性思辨、历史书写、语文学与礼仪政治思想，但整体更强调逊尼复兴与正统秩序。
philosophy.en: Mainstream thought centered on Islamic jurisprudence, theology, and political ethics within the broader Arabo-Islamic scholarly tradition. Late Abbasid culture still preserved rational inquiry, historiography, philology, and courtly political thought, though it increasingly emphasized Sunni revival and orthodox order.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母书写体系，用于行政、宗教、商业和学术文献；波斯语也在更广泛伊斯兰世界的宫廷与文学中广泛使用。
writingSystem.en: The Arabic script was used for administration, religion, commerce, and scholarship; Persian was also widely used in courtly and literary contexts across the wider Islamic world.

culturalAchievements.zh: 巴格达仍是伊斯兰学术、法学教育、书籍贸易、抄写装帧和诗文传统的重要中心。安-纳赛尔时期大力扶植逊尼派学校、修建宗教建筑、强化哈里发礼仪，并通过宗教网络恢复阿拔斯威望。
culturalAchievements.en: Baghdad remained a major center of Islamic scholarship, legal education, book trade, manuscript production, and literary culture. Under al-Nasir, Sunni colleges, religious architecture, and caliphal ceremony were actively promoted, helping revive Abbasid prestige through religious networks.

languageFamily.zh: 主要行政与宗教语言属闪米特语系的阿拉伯语；宫廷和外交接触中也常见印欧语系的波斯语与突厥语影响。
languageFamily.en: The primary administrative and religious language was Arabic of the Semitic family; Persian and Turkic influences, from Indo-European and Turkic linguistic traditions, were also common in courtly and diplomatic settings.

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的阿拔斯哈里发国经济已不再代表整个伊斯兰世界的总量，而主要反映巴格达及伊拉克核心区的农业、手工业、市场交换和宗教—政治消费能力。其经济体量在地区层面仍然可观，但明显逊于宋朝等超大经济体，也不及控制广阔领土的埃及叙利亚或花剌子模政权总量。
gdpDescription.en: By around 1200, the Abbasid caliphate no longer represented the total economy of the Islamic world, but mainly that of Baghdad and core Iraq, including agriculture, handicraft production, market exchange, and religious-political consumption. Its economy remained substantial regionally, but was far below giant economies such as Song China and smaller than the aggregate resources of broader territorial states like Ayyubid Egypt-Syria or Khwarazm.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、椰枣与谷物生产、纺织、皮革、金属加工、造纸、书籍贸易、建筑、香料与奢侈品转运
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, date and grain production, textiles, leatherwork, metalworking, papermaking, book trade, construction, and the transit of spices and luxury goods

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、椰枣、织物、纸张、书籍、皮革、金属器、香料、药材、染料、玻璃器与宗教用品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dates, textiles, paper, books, leather, metalware, spices, medicinal goods, dyes, glassware, and religious goods

householdWealth.zh: 城市上层家庭可拥有地产、商铺、货仓、奴仆和书籍收藏；普通城市家庭资产多为住房、工具、少量现钱与存粮；乡村家庭财富取决于土地使用权、牲畜和灌溉条件。财富分布差异显著，巴格达富商与宗教捐赠机构积累尤多。
householdWealth.en: Upper urban households might own real estate, shops, warehouses, servants, and book collections; ordinary city families typically held housing, tools, small cash reserves, and grain stores; rural wealth depended on land access, livestock, and irrigation. Wealth distribution was highly unequal, with Baghdad merchants and endowed religious institutions particularly well resourced.

unit.en: dinars per year (average per person estimate)

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和货币税收为基础的前近代城市—农业混合经济，国家通过税收、庄园、宗教捐赠监管和市场监察影响资源分配，但私人商业、行会式手工业网络和长途贸易仍发挥核心作用。
economicSystem.en: A premodern urban-agrarian mixed economy based on market exchange and monetized taxation, in which the state influenced allocation through taxes, estates, endowment oversight, and market inspection, while private commerce, guild-like craft networks, and long-distance trade remained central.

source.zh: 贡纳、馈赠与册封相关收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模足以支持宫廷运转、宗教赞助和短期军事动员，但远不足以像早期阿拔斯时代那样主导整个伊斯兰世界财政。安-纳赛尔治下财政较为稳健，重在维持巴格达威望、购买忠诚并恢复核心区秩序。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was sufficient to sustain court operations, religious patronage, and short-term mobilization, but far from the scale needed to dominate the finances of the whole Islamic world as in the early Abbasid age. Under al-Nasir, finances were relatively sound, aimed at preserving Baghdad's prestige, buying loyalty, and restoring order in the core lands.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向谨慎保守：巩固伊拉克税基、整顿宫廷地产、加强市场监管、运用宗教赞助换取合法性，并避免因过度扩张而导致财政崩坏。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively cautious and conservative: consolidating the Iraqi tax base, restoring crown lands, strengthening market oversight, using religious patronage to secure legitimacy, and avoiding overextension that could destabilize finances.

description.zh: 负责巴格达宫廷、城门与关键设施防卫，是哈里发最直接控制的核心部队。
description.en: Guarded the caliphal court, city gates, and key installations in Baghdad, forming the force most directly controlled by the caliph.

description.zh: 由突厥及其他军人集团构成的职业骑兵，是机动野战和威慑地方势力的主要力量。
description.en: Professional cavalry, including Turkic and other military elements, serving as the main force for mobile operations and deterrence against local rivals.

description.zh: 由巴格达及周边地区征召的步兵和守备力量，用于城防、巡逻、护送与应急征发。
description.en: Infantry and garrison forces recruited from Baghdad and nearby districts, used for city defense, patrols, escort duty, and emergency mobilization.

description.zh: 依赖部族首领或地方盟友在战时提供，忠诚度和持续作战能力不如常备军。
description.en: Supplied in wartime by tribal leaders or local allies, with less reliable loyalty and endurance than the standing forces.

commanderInChief.zh: 安-纳赛尔哈里发（名义统帅），由宫廷军政长官与将领实际指挥
commanderInChief.en: Caliph al-Nasir as nominal commander-in-chief, with practical command exercised by senior court military officials and generals

technology.zh: 装备以复合弓骑兵、长矛步兵、链甲或札甲、防盾、攻城器械和城防工事为主，火药尚未成为实战主力。军制上结合宫廷禁军、马穆鲁克式职业军人与地方征召传统。
technology.en: Military technology centered on composite-bow cavalry, spear infantry, mail or lamellar armor, shields, siege machinery, and urban fortifications; gunpowder was not yet a practical battlefield force. Organization combined palace guards, mamluk-style professionals, and local levy traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自伊拉克与伊朗边缘的地方军阀、花剌子模势力的扩张、阿拉伯与两河流域部族动荡，以及十字军时代整体区域格局的不稳定。1200年蒙古尚未直接威胁巴格达，但中亚草原局势已潜藏长期风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from local warlords on the Iraqi and Iranian fringes, the expansion of Khwarazmian power, unrest among Arab and Mesopotamian tribes, and the broader instability of the Crusading age. The Mongols did not yet directly threaten Baghdad in 1200, but steppe developments in Central Asia already posed a long-term risk.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于巴格达及伊拉克中下游灌溉农业带。与阿拔斯全盛时期相比，政治控制范围缩小，但巴格达仍是伊斯兰世界最大城市之一，吸引学者、商人、工匠、朝圣者与求职军人。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in Baghdad and the irrigated agricultural zone of central and lower Iraq. Compared with the Abbasid high period, political control had contracted, but Baghdad remained one of the largest cities in the Islamic world, attracting scholars, merchants, artisans, pilgrims, and soldiers seeking employment.

description.zh: 阿拔斯王朝首都，伊斯兰黄金时代中心
description.en: Abbasid capital, center of the Islamic Golden Age

socialClasses.zh: 上层为哈里发家族、宫廷显贵、乌理玛与大商人；中层包括书记、法官、教师、工匠首领和中等商人；下层为小商贩、手工业者、士兵、雇工与农民；另有奴隶、家仆和受保护的宗教少数群体。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the caliphal family, court grandees, ulama, and major merchants; the middle strata included scribes, judges, teachers, master artisans, and middling traders; the lower strata comprised petty vendors, craftsmen, soldiers, laborers, and peasants; slaves, servants, and protected religious minorities formed additional distinct groups.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的强力军事同盟，但哈里发通过册封、宗教承认与礼仪往来同部分逊尼派统治者保持合作关系，并在局部与阿尤布王朝等势力维持可用的合法性互惠。
allies.en: There were no permanent major military allies in the strict sense, but the caliph maintained cooperative ties with some Sunni rulers through investiture, religious recognition, and ceremonial exchange, and could sustain mutually useful legitimacy relations with powers such as the Ayyubids.

enemies.zh: 直接敌人并不固定，更多是周边觊觎伊拉克的地方势力、花剌子模扩张者、难以控制的部族集团，以及任何试图削弱阿拔斯宗教权威的竞争政权。
enemies.en: Direct enemies were not always fixed, but included neighboring powers coveting Iraq, expansionist Khwarazmian forces, hard-to-control tribal groups, and any rival regime seeking to undermine Abbasid religious authority.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以维持哈里发宗教宗主地位、通过册封与承认扩大影响、平衡周边强权、避免正面大规模战争为核心。安-纳赛尔尤其重视利用苏非网络、使节往来和礼仪政治重建阿拔斯名望。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve the caliph's religious overlordship, extend influence through investiture and recognition, balance neighboring powers, and avoid major direct wars. Al-Nasir in particular emphasized Sufi networks, embassies, and ceremonial politics to rebuild Abbasid prestige.

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪伊斯兰文明
era.en: Medieval Islamic civilization of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 并无1200年前后由阿拔斯巴格达独创的革命性新技术，但其社会仍高度受益于成熟的造纸术、灌溉水利、医院制度、药学、天文学计算、书籍复制、城市市场监察和法学教育网络。
keyInnovations.en: There were no uniquely revolutionary inventions originating in Abbasid Baghdad around 1200, but society still benefited greatly from mature papermaking, irrigation engineering, hospitals, pharmacology, astronomical calculation, manuscript production, urban market inspection, and networks of legal education.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于巴格达的宗教声望、文化中心地位、成熟的城市经济、仍具吸引力的学术机构，以及哈里发在逊尼派世界中的象征合法性。安-纳赛尔个人统治较有作为，能够利用礼仪、宗教与政治网络增强中央威望。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were Baghdad's religious prestige, central cultural role, mature urban economy, attractive scholarly institutions, and the caliph's symbolic legitimacy across the Sunni world. Al-Nasir's energetic rule allowed him to use ceremony, religion, and political networks to strengthen central prestige.

weaknesses.zh: 根本弱点是领土狭小、军事力量有限、财政基础远逊于早期阿拔斯盛世，并且严重依赖地方精英、军人集团与复杂的宫廷平衡。伊拉克灌溉系统长期衰退也削弱了农业税基。
weaknesses.en: Its fundamental weaknesses were limited territory, constrained military power, a fiscal base far smaller than that of the early Abbasid zenith, and heavy dependence on local elites, military groups, and delicate court balances. Long-term deterioration in Iraq's irrigation system also weakened the agrarian tax base.

outlook.zh: 在1200年，阿拔斯巴格达政权处于相对稳定且威望有所回升的阶段，短期内可继续作为伊斯兰世界的象征性核心存在；但若周边帝国进一步扩张，或出现来自东方草原的超区域征服力量，其有限的军政资源将难以长期自保。
outlook.en: In 1200, the Abbasid polity in Baghdad was relatively stable and enjoying some recovery in prestige, and could continue in the short term as a symbolic center of the Islamic world; yet if neighboring empires expanded further, or if a transregional conquering power emerged from the eastern steppe, its limited military and administrative resources would make long-term survival difficult.

description.zh: 虽然领土有限，但巴格达的阿拔斯哈里发仍具重要宗教与象征权威。
description.en: Though territorially limited, the Abbasid caliph in Baghdad retained major religious and symbolic authority.

name.zh: 奇里乞亚亚美尼亚王国

governmentForm.zh: 以鲁本王朝亲王为核心的封建亚美尼亚君主制，结合本地贵族、堡主、教会与拉丁十字军外交网络。
governmentForm.en: A feudal Armenian monarchy centered on the Rubenid prince, combining native nobility, castle lords, ecclesiastical elites, and diplomatic ties with the Latin Crusader states.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、纳哈拉尔贵族、骑士与堡主、教士、城市商人和工匠、自由农民及佃农构成；沿海城市中也有叙利亚人、希腊人、法兰克人和其他商旅群体。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, nakharar nobles, knights and castle lords, clergy, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants and tenants; coastal towns also included Syrians, Greeks, Franks, and other trading communities.

rulingClass.zh: 鲁本王朝王室、亚美尼亚纳哈拉尔贵族、主要堡垒领主以及与亚美尼亚使徒教会高层结盟的宫廷集团。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the Rubenid royal house, Armenian nakharar nobility, major fortress lords, and court factions allied with the upper hierarchy of the Armenian Apostolic Church.

succession.zh: 以王朝世袭为原则，通常由家族内指定继承人承袭，但贵族承认、教会支持与对要塞的控制对继位成败极为关键。
succession.en: Succession was dynastic and hereditary in principle, usually through a designated family heir, but noble recognition, church backing, and control of fortresses were crucial to a successful accession.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕亲王宫廷、王室财政、军事统领和教会顾问运作；实际统治依赖山地与平原要塞网络以及地方贵族的效忠关系。
structure.en: The central government operated through the princely court, royal finance, military commanders, and ecclesiastical advisers; effective rule depended on a network of mountain and plain fortresses and the loyalty of local nobles.

function.zh: 起草敕令、处理外交文书、封地文契、与拉丁、拜占庭及东方邻邦往来。
function.en: Drafted decrees, handled diplomatic correspondence, land grants, and communications with Latin, Byzantine, and eastern neighbors.

function.zh: 征收关税、地租、商税、王室领地收入，并管理铸币与库藏。
function.en: Collected customs, land dues, market taxes, royal domain income, and supervised coinage and stored revenues.

function.zh: 统筹王室卫队、封臣兵役、边境防务、要塞补给和驻军轮换。
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, feudal levies, frontier defense, fortress supply, and garrison rotation.

function.zh: 处理贵族争端、地产继承、契约纠纷、刑事案件及习惯法裁判。
function.en: Handled noble disputes, inheritance of estates, contract conflicts, criminal cases, and judgments under customary law.

function.zh: 监督港口贸易、对外商人社群、仓储、度量衡和海关征收。
function.en: Supervised port trade, foreign merchant communities, warehousing, weights and measures, and customs collection.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由贵族领主、堡主、王室任命的管理官以及教会领地共同承担。山区堡垒和沿海港口是行政核心，乡村则多依赖封建领主管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by noble lords, castle commanders, royally appointed officials, and ecclesiastical estates. Mountain fortresses and coastal ports served as administrative centers, while rural areas were largely managed through feudal lordship.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以亚美尼亚习惯法、王室敕令、封建契约和教会法并行为特征；在与法兰克人和希腊人交往密切的地区，也吸收部分拉丁与拜占庭法律实践。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Armenian customary law, princely edicts, feudal obligations, and canon law; in areas with close Frankish and Greek interaction, some Latin and Byzantine legal practices were also adopted.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地收益、农业实物征收、港口关税、市场税、通行费和王室领地收入为主；战时可加派封臣军役与临时捐输。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on land revenues, agricultural payments in kind, port customs, market taxes, tolls, and income from royal domains; in wartime, additional feudal service and extraordinary levies could be imposed.

religion.zh: 以亚美尼亚使徒教会为主体，基督教信仰在政治合法性中居核心地位；同时与拉丁教会、希腊正教会及叙利亚基督徒保持复杂互动。
religion.en: The Armenian Apostolic Church was dominant, and Christianity stood at the center of political legitimacy; the realm also maintained complex relations with the Latin Church, Greek Orthodoxy, and Syriac Christians.

philosophy.zh: 思想以亚美尼亚基督教神学、修道传统和王权护教观为主，并在十字军时代吸收部分拜占庭与拉丁经院式论辩影响。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Armenian Christian theology, monastic traditions, and ideas of sacral rulership, while also absorbing some Byzantine and Latin scholastic influences during the Crusading era.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用亚美尼亚字母书写亚美尼亚语；外交与商业场合也可见希腊语、叙利亚语、拉丁语和阿拉伯语文书。
writingSystem.en: The Armenian alphabet was used chiefly for the Armenian language; Greek, Syriac, Latin, and Arabic documents also appeared in diplomatic and commercial settings.

culturalAchievements.zh: 奇里乞亚时期以堡垒建筑、修道院文化、福音书抄写与细密插图、贵族宫廷文学以及亚美尼亚与拉丁世界之间的文化中介角色著称。
culturalAchievements.en: The Cilician period was noted for fortress architecture, monastic culture, Gospel manuscript production and illumination, aristocratic courtly literature, and its role as a cultural intermediary between the Armenian and Latin worlds.

languageFamily.zh: 亚美尼亚语属印欧语系亚美尼亚语族；境内亦通行希腊语、叙利亚语、阿拉伯语和部分法语方言。
languageFamily.en: Armenian belongs to the Indo-European Armenian branch; Greek, Syriac, Arabic, and some French dialects also circulated within the realm.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模对区域小国而言相当可观，依靠农业、畜牧、山地防御据点周边生产，以及地中海东岸与内陆之间的转口贸易。由于控制奇里乞亚平原与通往叙利亚、安纳托利亚的通道，王国具备超出人口规模的商业价值。
gdpDescription.en: For a small regional state, the economy was fairly substantial, resting on agriculture, pastoralism, production around defensive strongholds, and transit trade between the eastern Mediterranean and the interior. Control of the Cilician plain and routes to Syria and Anatolia gave the kingdom commercial importance beyond its population size.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与果园、畜牧、木材、金属与铁器加工、小规模纺织、港口贸易、驿道运输和要塞后勤。
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, vineyards and orchards, pastoralism, timber, metal and ironworking, small-scale textiles, port commerce, caravan transport, and fortress logistics.

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、牲畜、皮革、木材、蜡、盐、金属制品，以及经由港口转运的香料、丝绸、糖和奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, livestock, leather, timber, wax, salt, metal goods, and transshipped spices, silk, sugar, and luxury wares through its ports.

name.zh: 混合铸币与金属衡量体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富有限，主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、粮仓和家庭工具；城市商人和港口承包者则积累了更高流动财富。贵族财富集中于要塞、耕地、关税权和附庸劳役。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary peasant households possessed modest wealth mainly in land-use rights, livestock, stored grain, and tools; urban merchants and port contractors held more liquid wealth. Noble fortunes were concentrated in fortresses, arable land, customs rights, and dependent labor.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为封建地租、王室领地经营与港口商业并存的混合体。农业提供基本收入，沿海和通道贸易提供现金税源，并与十字军诸国、拜占庭和伊斯兰邻国形成跨文化交换网络。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed system of feudal rents, royal domain management, and port commerce. Agriculture supplied the base, while coastal and transit trade generated cash revenues, linking the kingdom to Crusader states, Byzantium, and neighboring Islamic polities.

source.zh: 市场税、罚金与杂项收入
source.en: Market taxes, fines, and miscellaneous income

category.zh: 教会支持与公共宗教建筑
category.en: Church support and public religious building

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模中等，足以维持常备驻军、宫廷支出与短期边境战事，但若遭遇长期围攻、商路中断或贵族叛乱，财政压力会迅速上升。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderate in size, sufficient to sustain standing garrisons, court expenditure, and short frontier campaigns, but prolonged sieges, trade disruption, or noble revolt would quickly strain finances.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体谨慎，优先确保要塞与边防供给，并通过鼓励贸易、维持港口秩序和稳固贵族忠诚来扩大现金税源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious, prioritizing fortress and frontier supply while expanding cash income through promotion of trade, order in the ports, and maintenance of noble loyalty.

description.zh: 由王室侍从、封臣骑士和重装贵族骑兵组成，是野战中的核心突击力量，也承担护卫与快速机动任务。
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, feudal knights, and heavily armed noble cavalry, this was the core striking arm in open battle and also served for escort and rapid movement.

description.zh: 包括矛兵、盾兵、弓箭手和各地堡垒守军，负责山口、城堡和通道防御，是王国军事体系的主体。
description.en: Including spearmen, shield bearers, archers, and fortress garrisons, they defended passes, castles, and routes and formed the main body of the kingdom’s military system.

description.zh: 由沿海守军、雇佣兵、民兵与后勤人员构成，承担港口警备、补给运输、侦察和战时增援任务。
description.en: Made up of coastal guards, mercenaries, militia, and support personnel, they handled port security, supply transport, reconnaissance, and wartime reinforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 亲王列翁二世
commanderInChief.en: Prince Leo II

technology.zh: 军事技术属十字军时代东地中海水平，重视城堡工事、石砲与投射器械、链甲与鳞甲、防御塔、山地要塞体系，以及吸收拜占庭、亚美尼亚、法兰克和伊斯兰作战经验。
technology.en: Military technology matched the eastern Mediterranean standards of the Crusading age, emphasizing castle works, stone-throwers and siege engines, mail and scale armor, defensive towers, mountain fortress systems, and a blend of Byzantine, Armenian, Frankish, and Islamic military practices.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自阿尤布王朝叙利亚诸政权、鲁姆塞尔柱土耳其人、边境突袭以及十字军与拜占庭政治变动带来的联盟不稳。内部贵族离心也可能削弱防务。
threats.en: Major threats came from Ayyubid Syrian powers, the Seljuk Turks of Rum, frontier raiding, and instability in alliances caused by shifting Crusader and Byzantine politics. Internal noble centrifugal tendencies could also weaken defense.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于奇里乞亚平原、沿海贸易据点和山地堡垒周边。主体为亚美尼亚人，同时包含希腊人、叙利亚基督徒、法兰克人、犹太人和穆斯林商旅等多元群体。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Cilician plain, coastal trading sites, and around mountain fortresses. Armenians formed the majority, alongside Greeks, Syriac Christians, Franks, Jews, and Muslim merchants and travelers.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室与纳哈拉尔贵族；其下为骑士、教士和城镇显贵；中间层包括商人、手工业者和自由农民；底层为佃农、劳役人口及战时动员民兵。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and nakharar nobles; beneath them were knights, clergy, and urban notables; the middle strata included merchants, artisans, and free peasants; below them were tenant cultivators, laboring dependents, and militia levied in wartime.

allies.zh: 与安条克公国及其他十字军国家关系密切，并寻求与教廷、部分拉丁势力及拜占庭保持灵活合作；与格鲁吉亚亦有共同基督教利益。
allies.en: It maintained close relations with the Principality of Antioch and other Crusader states, while seeking flexible cooperation with the Papacy, various Latin powers, and Byzantium; Georgia also shared common Christian interests.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为阿尤布王朝叙利亚势力、鲁姆苏丹国以及任何试图控制奇里乞亚通道与港口的周边强权。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were Ayyubid Syrian forces, the Sultanate of Rum, and any neighboring power seeking to control the Cilician routes and ports.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以生存和扩张空间为目标，通过婚姻、宗教协商、对十字军的支援与对拜占庭和伊斯兰邻邦的务实谈判，在强邻之间维持平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at survival and room for expansion, using marriage alliances, religious negotiation, support for Crusader causes, and pragmatic dealings with Byzantium and Islamic neighbors to maintain a balance among stronger powers.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非突破性发明，而是对多种传统的有效整合：山地与平原复合要塞体系、改良城墙与塔楼、攻守城器械、跨文化文书行政、港口贸易管理以及精致的抄写与插图工艺。
keyInnovations.en: Its key advances were less revolutionary inventions than effective integration of multiple traditions: combined mountain-and-plain fortress systems, improved walls and towers, siege and counter-siege machinery, cross-cultural documentary administration, port-trade management, and refined manuscript and illumination techniques.

strengths.zh: 地理位置关键，控制安纳托利亚与叙利亚之间通道；堡垒网络坚固；与十字军世界和地中海商路联系紧密；王朝在1200年前后总体稳定，具备较强外交机动性。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in a strategic position controlling routes between Anatolia and Syria, a strong fortress network, close ties to the Crusader world and Mediterranean trade, and a relatively stable dynasty around 1200 with considerable diplomatic flexibility.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与资源基础有限，长期战争承受力不足；高度依赖贵族忠诚和外部联盟；夹处拜占庭、土耳其与叙利亚强权之间，容易受地区局势剧变冲击。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a limited population and resource base, insufficient endurance for prolonged warfare, heavy reliance on noble loyalty and external alliances, and vulnerability to sudden regional shifts between Byzantine, Turkish, and Syrian great powers.

outlook.zh: 1200年时前景总体良好，若列翁二世继续巩固贵族、教会与拉丁盟友关系，国家有望进一步提升地位，并在不久后实现王国化；但其长期命运仍取决于周边穆斯林强权、安纳托利亚土耳其势力和十字军体系的兴衰。
outlook.en: In 1200 the outlook was generally favorable. If Leo II continued to consolidate ties with nobles, the church, and Latin allies, the polity was well placed to elevate its status further and soon achieve kingship; yet its long-term fate would still depend on the fortunes of surrounding Muslim powers, Anatolian Turkish states, and the wider Crusader system.

description.zh: 位于安纳托利亚东南海岸的亚美尼亚国家，与十字军和拜占庭关系密切。
description.en: An Armenian state on the southeastern Anatolian coast closely tied to the Crusader states and Byzantium.

name.zh: 安纳托利亚突厥政权（鲁姆塞尔柱苏丹国）

ruler.zh: 鲁克恩丁·苏莱曼沙阿二世

governmentForm.zh: 突厥-波斯化伊斯兰君主制，由苏丹统治，结合宫廷官僚、军事采邑与地方埃米尔体制
governmentForm.en: A Turkic-Persianate Islamic monarchy ruled by a sultan, combining court bureaucracy, military iqta assignments, and provincial amiral governance

socialStructure.zh: 社会由突厥军事贵族、波斯文官、乌里玛与宗教阶层、城市商人和手工业者、定居农民，以及在边疆活动的半游牧土库曼部落构成；希腊人、亚美尼亚人和叙利亚基督徒在城镇与乡村中继续存在
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of a Turkic military aristocracy, Persian bureaucrats, ulama and religious elites, urban merchants and artisans, settled peasants, and semi-nomadic Turkmen tribes active on the frontier; Greeks, Armenians, and Syrian Christians also remained in towns and countryside

rulingClass.zh: 以塞尔柱王族、突厥军政埃米尔、宫廷古拉姆军官及波斯化高级文官为核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Seljuk dynasty, Turkic military amirs, court ghulam officers, and high Persianate administrators

succession.zh: 王朝世袭，但实际继承常受诸王子竞争、宫廷联盟、军政贵族支持及宗教合法性影响
succession.en: Dynastic hereditary succession, though actual succession was often shaped by rivalry among princes, court alliances, support from military elites, and religious legitimacy

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹宫廷为核心，下设波斯式迪万官署处理财政、军务、文书与司法；地方由各省埃米尔、城镇总督和采邑持有人管理，边境地区依赖土库曼军首领与要塞体系
structure.en: The central government revolved around the sultan's court, with Persian-style diwans handling finance, military affairs, chancery work, and justice; the provinces were managed by amirs, urban governors, and iqta holders, while frontier regions relied on Turkmen war leaders and fortress networks

function.zh: 统筹全国政务、协调各部、起草政策并监督地方执行
function.en: Coordinated state administration, supervised departments, drafted policy, and oversaw provincial implementation

function.zh: 征收土地税、商业税、关税，核算采邑收益并管理国库
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial levies, and customs, assessed iqta revenues, and managed the treasury

function.zh: 登记军役、调动部队、核查军饷与采邑服役义务，管理要塞供应
function.en: Recorded military service, mobilized troops, audited pay and iqta obligations, and managed fortress provisioning

function.zh: 起草诏令、外交文书、册封文件并保存官方档案
function.en: Drafted decrees, diplomatic correspondence, investiture documents, and maintained official archives

function.zh: 协助卡迪体系、管理宗教基金、学校与部分公共慈善事务
function.en: Assisted the qadi system and supervised religious endowments, schools, and certain charitable functions

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分为省、城与堡寨层级。主要城市由总督或沙赫纳驻守，乡村税收多通过采邑持有人、村社长老和宗教法官协同征集。新近突厥化区域常保留部分拜占庭和亚美尼亚地方习惯。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through provinces, towns, and fortress districts. Major cities were held by governors or shihnas, while rural taxation was often collected through iqta holders, village elders, and religious judges. Recently Turkified areas frequently preserved elements of Byzantine and Armenian local practice.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派伊斯兰法为正式基础，尤其是哈乃斐法学传统，同时辅以苏丹敕令、军政惯例和地方习惯法；穆斯林与非穆斯林社群在婚姻、继承和教会事务上存在不同司法实践
legalSystem.en: The legal system was formally grounded in Sunni Islamic law, especially the Hanafi tradition, supplemented by sultanic decrees, military-administrative custom, and local usages; Muslim and non-Muslim communities often followed different judicial practices in marriage, inheritance, and ecclesiastical affairs

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以农业地税、灌溉与牧场征收、市场税、道路与桥梁过路费、关税、矿产与盐税为主；穆斯林缴纳部分宗教性课赋，非穆斯林缴纳人头税，许多税收通过采邑分配给军政持有人
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on agricultural land tax, levies on irrigation and pasture, market dues, road and bridge tolls, customs, and taxes on mines and salt; Muslims paid certain religious imposts, non-Muslims paid poll taxes, and many revenues were assigned through the iqta system to military and administrative holders

religion.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰教为国教性主导，苏菲团体影响日增；同时境内仍有希腊正教、亚美尼亚使徒教会、叙利亚基督徒与犹太社群
religion.en: Sunni Islam was the dominant state-supported religion, while Sufi circles were gaining influence; Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, Syriac Christian, and Jewish communities also remained within the realm

philosophy.zh: 政治与思想文化深受波斯化宫廷文学、伊斯兰法学、逊尼正统论和苏菲灵修影响，并吸收部分希腊-拜占庭学术遗产
philosophy.en: Political and intellectual culture was shaped by Persianate court literature, Islamic jurisprudence, Sunni orthodoxy, and Sufi spirituality, while also absorbing parts of the Greek-Byzantine scholarly legacy

writingSystem.zh: 官方与学术书写主要使用阿拉伯字母；行政和文学语言以波斯语、阿拉伯语为主，突厥语在军旅与民间口语中广泛使用
writingSystem.en: The Arabic script was used for formal writing; Persian and Arabic dominated administration and high literature, while Turkic was widely spoken in military and popular settings

culturalAchievements.zh: 鲁姆塞尔柱时期在石造建筑、商队驿站、清真寺、经学院、陵墓与城防工程上成就显著。科尼亚、锡瓦斯、开塞利等地形成融合伊朗、叙利亚、亚美尼亚和安纳托利亚本地传统的艺术风格，地毯织造、木作、石刻与金属工艺颇为发达。
culturalAchievements.en: The Seljuk Rum period achieved major advances in stone architecture, caravanserais, mosques, madrasas, mausolea, and fortification works. Konya, Sivas, and Kayseri developed artistic styles blending Iranian, Syrian, Armenian, and Anatolian local traditions, while carpet weaving, woodwork, stone carving, and metalwork flourished.

languageFamily.zh: 统治者族群语言属突厥语系；高层行政与文学深受伊朗语支波斯语传统影响，宗教学术则广泛使用闪含语系阿拉伯语
languageFamily.en: The ruling population spoke a Turkic language; high administration and literature were strongly shaped by the Iranian language Persian, while religious scholarship widely employed the Semitic language Arabic

gdpDescription.zh: 经济在1200年前后处于繁荣阶段，得益于安纳托利亚高原农业恢复、畜牧扩张、东西陆路贸易中转以及城市手工业发展。作为连接伊朗、叙利亚、黑海、地中海与拜占庭边境的内陆枢纽，其财富高于许多同时期区域王国。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was prosperous around 1200, supported by recovering Anatolian plateau agriculture, expanding pastoralism, overland transit trade, and growing urban crafts. As an inland hub connecting Iran, Syria, the Black Sea, the Mediterranean, and the Byzantine frontier, its wealth surpassed that of many contemporary regional kingdoms.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧羊与马匹饲养、地毯织造、纺织、皮革、金属加工、盐业、铜银矿开采、建筑业、驿站与运输服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, sheep raising and horse breeding, carpet weaving, textiles, leatherwork, metalworking, salt production, copper and silver mining, construction, and caravan and transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、羊毛、地毯、皮革、马匹、奴隶、金属器、染料、盐，以及过境的丝绸、香料、糖、玻璃器和奢侈纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wool, carpets, leather, horses, slaves, metal goods, dyes, salt, and transit commodities such as silk, spices, sugar, glassware, and luxury textiles

householdWealth.zh: 城乡家庭财富差异明显。城市商人、采邑军官和大工匠拥有较多货币与不动产；普通农户财富主要表现为耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备与家庭器具；边疆土库曼部落财富多以羊群、马匹和武器衡量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply between town and countryside. Urban merchants, iqta officers, and master artisans held more coin and real property; ordinary peasant wealth was mainly expressed through land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and household tools; frontier Turkmen wealth was often measured in flocks, horses, and arms.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导下的混合型农业-商贸经济。土地税和采邑制度支撑军政体系，城市市场受监管，长途贸易依赖商旅保护、驿站网络和对主要交通线的控制。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed mixed agrarian-commercial economy. Land taxation and the iqta system sustained the military-administrative order, urban markets were regulated, and long-distance trade depended on caravan protection, roadside infrastructure, and control of major routes.

source.en: Agricultural land tax and rural levies
Customs, market taxes, and transit tolls

source.zh: 采邑回收、王室领地与矿盐收入
source.en: Recovered iqta income, crown lands, and mine-salt revenues

source.zh: 人头税、罚金及杂项收入
source.en: Poll tax, fines, and miscellaneous revenues

category.zh: 道路、商队驿站、城市工程与宗教建筑
category.en: Roads, caravanserais, urban works, and religious construction

category.zh: 赈济、赏赐、外交与宗教基金补助
category.en: Relief, patronage, diplomacy, and support for religious endowments

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况稳健，既有现银、金币与贵重器物，也包括粮储和王室可迅速动员的采邑收益。由于西部边境战争与宫廷继承问题，财政需保持较高流动储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was sound, consisting of coined silver, gold, precious objects, grain reserves, and iqta revenues that the crown could quickly mobilize. Because of warfare on the western frontier and recurring succession tensions, the state needed substantial liquid reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持贸易畅通、保护税源和边防稳定；在繁荣时期鼓励修筑商队驿站、桥梁和清真寺，同时尽量避免过重盘剥城市商业。国家依靠采邑分摊军费，但也警惕地方军阀坐大。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to keep trade flowing, protect revenue sources, and stabilize frontier defense; in prosperous times it encouraged construction of caravanserais, bridges, and mosques while trying to avoid overburdening urban commerce. The state used iqta grants to spread military costs, but remained wary of overmighty provincial commanders.

name.zh: 苏丹亲军与古拉姆骑兵
name.en: Sultan's household troops and ghulam cavalry

description.zh: 由苏丹直接控制的核心职业部队，包括奴隶出身或侍从出身的精锐骑兵和宫廷卫队，是中央权力的重要支柱
description.en: The core professional troops directly controlled by the sultan, including elite cavalry of slave or household origin and palace guards, forming a major pillar of central authority

description.zh: 由持有采邑的军官和地方贵族按服役义务提供，多为装具较好的骑兵，是野战主力
description.en: Supplied by iqta-holding officers and local notables under service obligation, generally better-equipped cavalry and the main field force

description.zh: 边疆最机动的力量，擅长袭扰、侦察与快速突击，但纪律性较弱，常需通过赏赐和迁徙安置加以控制
description.en: The most mobile frontier force, skilled in raiding, reconnaissance, and rapid strikes, though less disciplined and often controlled through patronage and settlement policies

description.zh: 包括城市守军、弩手、工兵和从属基督徒或地方社群提供的辅助兵力，主要用于攻守城与交通线防护
description.en: Included urban garrisons, archers, engineers, and auxiliaries drawn from subject Christian or local communities, mainly used for siege work, defense, and route protection

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹鲁克恩丁·苏莱曼沙阿二世
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Rukn al-Din Suleiman Shah II

technology.zh: 军备以复合弓骑兵、锁子甲、长枪骑兵、攻城器械、堡垒网络和良好道路后勤为特色。尚未使用火药武器，但在机动战、边境袭扰和围城技术方面经验丰富。
technology.en: Military technology emphasized composite-bow cavalry, mail armor, lance-armed horsemen, siege engines, fortress networks, and effective road logistics. Gunpowder weapons were absent, but the polity was experienced in mobile warfare, frontier raiding, and siegecraft.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方拜占庭残余反攻、十字军诸国与奇里乞亚亚美尼亚边境冲突，以及东部高加索与伊朗方向局势变化。虽然1200年蒙古尚未入侵安纳托利亚，但欧亚草原力量变动已构成长远隐忧。
threats.en: The main threats came from possible Byzantine counterattacks in the west, conflicts with the Crusader states and Cilician Armenia, and changes in the eastern Caucasian and Iranian balance. Although the Mongols had not yet invaded Anatolia in 1200, shifts on the Eurasian steppe posed a long-term concern.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 中世纪最强攻城武器，可抛射150公斤石弹
description.en: Most powerful medieval siege weapon, hurling 150kg stone projectiles

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，分布于安纳托利亚高原农耕区、山地牧场、旧拜占庭城镇和新兴塞尔柱商业中心之间。民族与宗教构成复杂，突厥化和伊斯兰化持续推进，但希腊、亚美尼亚及其他本地居民仍占重要比例。
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million, spread across Anatolian plateau farming zones, upland pastures, old Byzantine towns, and emerging Seljuk commercial centers. Ethnic and religious composition was complex; Turkification and Islamization were advancing, but Greeks, Armenians, and other local populations still formed a major share.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、埃米尔、采邑军官、大乌里玛和富商；中层包括城镇工匠、行会成员、法官、书记员和中小地主；下层主要是农民、牧民、佃户、雇工与奴隶。非穆斯林社群常在税务和法律地位上与穆斯林有所区别。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the dynasty, amirs, iqta officers, major ulama, and wealthy merchants; the middle layers included artisans, guild members, judges, scribes, and middling landholders; the lower strata were mainly peasants, herders, tenants, laborers, and slaves. Non-Muslim communities often differed from Muslims in tax and legal status.

allies.zh: 无稳定而持久的正式盟友体系，但在不同阶段可与部分穆斯林邻国、地方亚美尼亚或边疆势力达成临时协定；与阿拔斯哈里发保持名义上的伊斯兰合法性联系
allies.en: There was no fixed and enduring alliance system, but temporary arrangements could be made at different times with neighboring Muslim powers, local Armenian authorities, or frontier forces; the sultanate also maintained nominal Islamic legitimacy through ties to the Abbasid caliph

enemies.zh: 主要对手为拜占庭诸势力、十字军国家、奇里乞亚亚美尼亚王国及部分不服从的边疆土库曼或地方军阀
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were the Byzantine forces, the Crusader states, the Cilician Armenian kingdom, and at times unruly frontier Turkmen or provincial warlords

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策务实灵活，重点在于确保安纳托利亚内陆贸易路线、分化西方基督教邻国、稳固东部边境，并通过婚姻、封授、互市和停战协议维持力量平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and flexible, focused on securing inland Anatolian trade routes, dividing western Christian neighbors, stabilizing the eastern frontier, and maintaining balance through marriage, investiture, trade arrangements, and truces.

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪伊斯兰-安纳托利亚技术阶段
era.en: Medieval Islamic-Anatolian technological stage of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 并无革命性新发明，但在商队驿站网络建设、石砌建筑、拱顶与门廊装饰、矿冶、灌溉利用、攻城器械、道路维护及骑射战术整合方面表现突出
keyInnovations.en: There were no radically new inventions, but the polity excelled in caravanserai networks, stone architecture, vaulted construction and portal decoration, mining and metallurgy, irrigation use, siege machinery, road maintenance, and the integration of mounted archery tactics

strengths.zh: 国家最强之处在于地处安纳托利亚腹地、控制关键商路、拥有高机动骑兵、能够吸收波斯化官僚传统并整合多元人口。其城市建设和贸易保护政策显著增强了财政与统治合法性。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were its position in the Anatolian interior, control of key trade arteries, highly mobile cavalry, and the ability to absorb Persianate bureaucratic traditions while integrating a diverse population. Urban development and trade-protection policies significantly strengthened both revenue and legitimacy.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点在于王朝继承反复不稳、地方采邑与边疆部落难以完全控制、中央与地方关系易紧张，以及西部与南部边境常年面临多线压力。国家凝聚力仍部分依赖强势君主个人能力。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were recurrent instability in dynastic succession, the difficulty of fully controlling provincial iqta holders and frontier tribes, tension between center and periphery, and persistent multi-front pressure on the western and southern borders. State cohesion still depended in part on the capabilities of a strong ruler.

outlook.zh: 1200年时前景总体积极，国力处于上升或高位阶段，若能维持王朝团结并继续保护贸易，仍可作为安纳托利亚首强延续数十年。然而来自草原东部的新兴力量与内部继承危机，预示其长期稳定并非无忧。
outlook.en: In 1200 the overall outlook was favorable, with the polity at or near a peak of power; if dynastic unity could be maintained and trade continued to be protected, it could remain the leading power in Anatolia for decades. Yet new forces rising on the eastern steppe and internal succession crises warned that long-term stability was not guaranteed.

description.zh: 安纳托利亚最强的突厥政权，控制内陆商道并与拜占庭和十字军接壤。
description.en: The strongest Turkic state in Anatolia, controlling inland trade routes and bordering Byzantium and the Crusaders.

governmentForm.zh: 以巴格拉季昂王室为核心的封建君主制，在强势王权统合下结合宫廷官僚、贵族附庸与教会权威
governmentForm.en: A feudal monarchy centered on the Bagrationi royal house, combining strong royal authority with court bureaucracy, noble vassalage, and ecclesiastical legitimacy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、世袭大贵族、教会等级、自由军役贵族、城镇商人与工匠、农民及山地部族共同构成；在塔玛尔时期，王权成功压制若干大贵族离心倾向并加强中央整合
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, hereditary magnates, church hierarchy, lesser military nobility, urban merchants and artisans, peasants, and mountain tribal communities; under Tamar, royal power successfully checked centrifugal tendencies among major nobles and strengthened central integration

rulingClass.zh: 巴格拉季昂王室、达迪亚尼等大贵族家族、高级教士与宫廷军政官员
rulingClass.en: The Bagrationi royal family, major noble houses such as the Dadiani, senior clergy, and court military-administrative officials

succession.zh: 王位原则上实行王朝世袭，并由贵族与教会承认；在塔玛尔时代，女性君主已获正式承认，但仍需通过加冕、共治安排与贵族支持巩固合法性
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the dynasty and recognized by nobles and the church; in Tamar's era, female sovereignty had been formally accepted, though legitimacy was still reinforced through coronation, co-rule arrangements, and noble backing

structure.zh: 王国政府由君主、王室宫廷、国务重臣、地方总督体系与教会机构组成。中央围绕宫廷和王室大臣运作，地方则通过萨埃里斯塔沃等贵族领地及直属王室领地实施统治。
structure.en: The kingdom's government consisted of the monarch, the royal court, chief ministers of state, provincial governorships, and church institutions. The center operated through the court and royal great officers, while the localities were governed through noble principalities such as the saeristavo and directly administered crown lands.

function.zh: 管理王室起居、宫廷仪式、诏令传达、接待使节与王室财产
function.en: Managed the royal household, court ceremony, transmission of decrees, reception of envoys, and royal property

function.zh: 征收地税、关税、贡赋与王室收入，监管仓储和货币征收
function.en: Collected land taxes, customs, tribute, and crown revenues, and supervised granaries and monetary intake

function.zh: 组织王家军、封臣军役、边境要塞驻防、战时动员与战利品分配
function.en: Organized the royal army, feudal levies, frontier garrisons, wartime mobilization, and the distribution of spoils

function.zh: 处理王室诏书、土地契据、贵族诉讼、刑罚裁决与教俗司法协调
function.en: Handled royal charters, land deeds, noble litigation, penal rulings, and coordination between secular and ecclesiastical justice

function.zh: 监督地方总督、城堡守将、道路驿站、山口与附庸地区的行政联络
function.en: Supervised provincial governors, fortress commanders, roads, relay stations, mountain passes, and administrative liaison with tributary regions

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托传统贵族领地、王室直属地、边防堡垒区和附庸公国。王权通过任命埃里斯塔维、监军和城堡守将来约束地方，同时保留地方贵族对农村与山地社会的实际影响力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on traditional noble domains, crown lands, frontier fortress zones, and tributary principalities. Royal authority constrained the localities by appointing eristavis, military overseers, and fortress commanders, while local nobles retained substantial influence over rural and mountain society.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合王室敕令、习惯法、贵族特权与格鲁吉亚东正教教会法。重大案件可由王室裁断，土地、婚姻、继承与宗教事务中教会法具有重要作用。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decrees, customary law, noble privilege, and Georgian Orthodox canon law. Major cases could be judged by the crown, while canon law played an important role in land, marriage, inheritance, and religious matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地出产、附庸贡赋、商税、关税、城镇征收和王室庄园收入为主。军役义务在封建关系中与财政征收并存，部分边地和教会地产享有减免或特殊待遇。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based chiefly on agrarian produce, tribute from dependents, commercial taxes, customs duties, urban levies, and revenues from royal estates. Military service obligations coexisted with fiscal exactions within the feudal order, and some frontier districts and church lands enjoyed exemptions or special arrangements.

religion.zh: 格鲁吉亚东正教为国教，王权以基督教正统性自我表述，并积极资助修道院、主教区和圣地机构；同时境内也有亚美尼亚基督徒、穆斯林与犹太人社群
religion.en: Georgian Orthodoxy was the state religion, and royal power expressed itself through Christian legitimacy while actively patronizing monasteries, bishoprics, and institutions in the Holy Land; the realm also contained Armenian Christians, Muslims, and Jewish communities

philosophy.zh: 思想文化以东正教神学、王权神授观、修道院学术传统和拜占庭—高加索基督教知识世界为核心，同时吸收部分伊朗与阿拉伯行政文化影响
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Orthodox theology, sacral kingship, monastic scholarship, and the Byzantine-Caucasian Christian learned world, while also absorbing some Iranian and Arabic administrative influences

writingSystem.zh: 使用格鲁吉亚字母书写格鲁吉亚语，教会、王室诏书、碑铭和文学作品广泛采用；在外交与商业环境中也接触亚美尼亚文、希腊文、阿拉伯文和波斯文
writingSystem.en: The Georgian script was used to write Georgian in ecclesiastical texts, royal charters, inscriptions, and literature; Armenian, Greek, Arabic, and Persian were also encountered in diplomatic and commercial contexts

culturalAchievements.zh: 塔玛尔时代是格鲁吉亚黄金时代，史诗文学、宗教建筑、壁画艺术、金属工艺与修道院文化高度繁荣。《虎皮武士》所代表的宫廷文学传统、格拉特修道院等学术宗教中心以及王国对耶路撒冷修道事业的资助均极具代表性。
culturalAchievements.en: Tamar's reign marked a Georgian golden age in which epic literature, religious architecture, mural painting, metalwork, and monastic culture flourished. The courtly literary tradition represented by The Knight in the Panther's Skin, scholarly and religious centers such as Gelati Monastery, and royal patronage of Georgian institutions in Jerusalem were especially notable.

languageFamily.zh: 卡特维尔语系
languageFamily.en: Kartvelian language family

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的格鲁吉亚经济在高加索地区属强盛水平，以农业、牧业、山区资源、贯通黑海—里海—伊朗道路的转运贸易和王室贡赋为基础。其总体规模远不及宋朝等超大型经济体，但足以支撑地区霸权、宫廷文化与持续军事行动。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200, the Georgian economy was strong by Caucasian standards, based on agriculture, pastoralism, mountain resources, transit trade linking Black Sea, Caspian, and Iranian routes, and royal tribute. Its overall scale was far smaller than that of mega-economies such as Song China, but sufficient to sustain regional hegemony, court culture, and continued military campaigning.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒酿造、畜牧、养蜂、金属采掘、铁器锻造、武器制造、石工建筑、纺织、陶器与过境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine production, pastoralism, beekeeping, metal extraction, ironworking, arms manufacture, stone construction, textiles, pottery, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 葡萄酒、蜂蜜、蜡、皮革、牲畜、金属制品、木材、奴隶、丝织品转运品、香料转运品和高加索山地特产
tradeGoods.en: Wine, honey, wax, leather, livestock, metal goods, timber, slaves, transshipped silk, transshipped spices, and Caucasian mountain products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。贵族和高级教会拥有大片土地、城堡、役使人口与战马；城镇商人和手工业者拥有中等流动财富；普通农户以土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和家庭工具为主要资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Nobles and senior churchmen controlled large estates, castles, dependent labor, and warhorses; townsmen and artisans held modest movable wealth; ordinary peasant households relied chiefly on land-use rights, livestock, grain reserves, and household tools.

economicSystem.zh: 以封建土地关系为基础、由王室再分配和边境贸易推动的混合经济。农业与贡赋构成基础，贵族领地和教会地产占重要地位，城市市场和国际商路在塔玛尔时期明显活跃。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy grounded in feudal land relations and energized by royal redistribution and frontier trade. Agriculture and tribute formed the base, noble and ecclesiastical estates were central, and urban markets and international routes became notably more active under Tamar.

source.zh: 土地税与王室庄园收入
source.en: Land tax and crown estate income
Tribute from dependents and war gains

category.en: Diplomatic gifts and maintenance of dependents

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库在塔玛尔盛世相对充盈，来源包括多年积蓄、战利品、贡赋和关税收入。其储备足以应对一次大型远征、边境危机或大规模宗教建筑资助。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was relatively full during Tamar's prosperous reign, built from accumulated savings, war gains, tribute, and customs income. Its reserves were enough to support a major campaign, a frontier emergency, or substantial patronage of religious construction.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体审慎而偏扩张，在保持王室储备的同时积极投入军备、堡垒、宗教赞助和对附庸网络的维系。得益于经济繁荣和对外胜利，王权能够在不过度压榨核心农业区的情况下维持较高支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally prudent but expansionary, preserving royal reserves while investing actively in armament, fortresses, religious patronage, and the maintenance of dependent networks. Thanks to prosperity and external victories, the crown could sustain high spending without excessively overburdening the core agrarian districts.

name.zh: 王家重骑兵与贵族骑兵
name.en: Royal Heavy Cavalry and Noble Horse

description.zh: 由王室亲军与封臣骑士构成，是格鲁吉亚军队的核心突击力量，擅长山地与平原会战中的冲击作战
description.en: Composed of the royal guard and feudal horsemen, this was the striking core of the Georgian army, effective in shock action in both mountain and open-field battles

description.zh: 包括王室步兵、地方征召兵、弓箭手和堡垒驻军，负责守城、山口防御和支援野战军
description.en: Included royal infantry, local levies, archers, and fortress garrisons, responsible for city defense, pass control, and support of field armies

description.zh: 来自附庸亚美尼亚、希尔万周边和边境山地社群的部队，兵种复杂，常用于边境袭扰和辅助作战
description.en: Raised from dependent Armenian territories, areas around Shirvan, and frontier mountain communities; varied in composition and often used for border warfare and auxiliary roles

description.zh: 负责拱卫君主、宫廷与精锐作战任务，装备和待遇优于一般军队
description.en: Tasked with protecting the monarch and court and serving in elite combat roles, better equipped and rewarded than ordinary troops

commanderInChief.zh: 塔玛尔女王与阿米尔斯帕萨拉尔
commanderInChief.en: Queen Tamar and the Amirspasalar

technology.zh: 军备以锁子甲、长枪、剑、复合弓、攻城器械和高加索山地堡垒体系为主。格鲁吉亚军队兼具拜占庭、亚美尼亚、草原和伊朗军事影响，擅长联合骑兵冲击、山地防御与快速远征。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on mail armor, lances, swords, composite bows, siege engines, and a Caucasian mountain fortress system. Georgian forces combined Byzantine, Armenian, steppe, and Iranian influences, excelling in coordinated cavalry charges, mountain defense, and rapid campaigning.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方与东南方的穆斯林诸政权、安纳托利亚突厥势力、边境部族动荡以及未来可能自欧亚草原东部西进的新兴游牧力量。尽管1200年时格鲁吉亚处于上升阶段，但其扩张前线漫长而脆弱。
threats.en: Principal threats came from Muslim polities to the south and southeast, Turkic powers in Anatolia, unrest among frontier tribes, and in the future potentially new nomadic forces advancing westward from the eastern Eurasian steppe. Although Georgia was ascending in 1200, its expansionary frontiers were long and vulnerable.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 王国人口约150万，核心集中于东格鲁吉亚和西格鲁吉亚农业区、河谷城镇与交通节点，另有不少人口分布于山地社区及附庸地区。以高加索标准看，这是一个人口相当可观且动员能力较强的基督教国家。
populationDescription.en: The kingdom's population was about 1.5 million, concentrated in the agrarian districts of eastern and western Georgia, river-valley towns, and transport corridors, with substantial numbers also living in mountain communities and tributary territories. By Caucasian standards, this was a sizable Christian state with strong mobilization capacity.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、大贵族与高级教士；中层包括小贵族、军役阶层、城市商人和工匠；下层为农民、依附人口、牧民和部分奴仆。亚美尼亚人、阿布哈兹人、山地族群及穆斯林城市居民在王国社会中也占有重要位置。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal family, magnates, and high clergy; the middle strata included lesser nobles, military service groups, urban merchants, and artisans; the lower strata comprised peasants, dependent laborers, pastoralists, and some slaves or servants. Armenians, Abkhazians, mountain peoples, and Muslim urban residents also formed important parts of the kingdom's social fabric.

allies.zh: 与奇里乞亚亚美尼亚王国保持友好和宗教—政治合作关系，并与部分十字军国家、拜占庭世界中的友好派系及高加索附庸政权维持互动
allies.en: Maintained friendly and religious-political cooperation with the Armenian Kingdom of Cilicia, as well as interactions with some Crusader states, favorable Byzantine factions, and dependent Caucasian polities

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括鲁姆苏丹国周边突厥势力、阿塞拜疆与伊朗西北诸穆斯林政权、阿勒颇与美索不达米亚方向的潜在敌对势力，以及任何挑战其高加索霸权的地方君主
enemies.en: Main rivals included Turkic forces associated with the Sultanate of Rum, Muslim polities in Azerbaijan and northwestern Iran, potentially hostile powers from the Aleppo and Mesopotamian directions, and any local rulers challenging its Caucasian supremacy

foreignPolicy.zh: 塔玛尔时期外交奉行积极扩张与宗教正统并行的政策，通过联姻、附庸、军事远征、保护基督徒社群和控制商路来建立高加索霸权，并在黑海南岸与亚美尼亚高地扩大影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Under Tamar, foreign policy combined active expansion with religious legitimacy, using marriage alliances, tributary arrangements, military expeditions, protection of Christian communities, and control of trade routes to build Caucasian hegemony and extend influence along the southern Black Sea and Armenian highlands.

era.zh: 十字军时代盛期的中世纪高加索军事—修道院文明
era.en: High medieval Caucasian military-monastic civilization of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 并无全球意义上的突破性发明，但在山地堡垒工程、石造教堂建筑、壁画与金工、复合军事组织、长距离山地后勤和修道院学术网络方面达到区域高水平
keyInnovations.en: It produced no globally transformative inventions, but reached a high regional level in mountain fortification engineering, stone church construction, fresco and metal arts, composite military organization, long-distance mountain logistics, and monastic scholarly networks

strengths.zh: 王权强势、宗教合法性稳固、军队精锐、战略地理优越、贸易通道收益可观、文化繁荣且能整合多民族边疆，是1200年前后高加索最具实力的基督教国家之一。
strengths.en: It possessed strong monarchy, firm religious legitimacy, an effective army, advantageous strategic geography, profitable trade corridors, and a flourishing culture capable of integrating a multiethnic frontier, making it one of the strongest Christian states in the Caucasus around 1200.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政基础仍有限，难与更大帝国长期消耗；政权依赖君主个人威望和贵族合作；边境过长、附庸结构松散，多民族地区在强敌压力下可能迅速动摇。
weaknesses.en: Its demographic and fiscal base remained limited, making prolonged attrition against larger empires difficult; the regime depended heavily on the ruler's personal authority and noble cooperation; and its long frontiers and loose tributary structure could unravel quickly under pressure from powerful enemies.

outlook.zh: 在1200年，格鲁吉亚王国正处于黄金时代顶峰，短期前景极为乐观，仍有进一步扩张和巩固区域霸权的空间。然而从更长时段看，来自草原与伊朗世界的新一轮大规模军事震荡将对这一繁荣秩序构成严峻挑战。
outlook.en: In 1200, the Georgian kingdom stood near the peak of its golden age, with an excellent short-term outlook and room for further expansion and consolidation of regional hegemony. Over the longer term, however, new waves of large-scale military upheaval from the steppe and Iranian world would pose a severe challenge to this prosperous order.

description.zh: 高加索基督教强国，在塔玛尔统治下达到黄金时代。
description.en: A major Christian power in the Caucasus, reaching a golden age under Queen Tamar.

ruler.zh: 阿莱克修斯三世·安杰洛斯

rulerTitle.zh: 罗马人的皇帝与独裁者
rulerTitle.en: Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans

governmentForm.zh: 以皇帝为核心的罗马官僚君主制，带有宫廷寡头化和地方军政权力分散特征
governmentForm.en: An emperor-centered Roman bureaucratic monarchy, increasingly oligarchic at court and marked by dispersed provincial military-administrative power

socialStructure.zh: 社会由皇帝与宫廷、军政贵族、教会高层、城市行会与商人、自由农民、佃农及边境军民构成；12世纪末贵族大地产扩张，自由农民基础削弱
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the emperor and court, military and civil aristocracy, high clergy, urban guildsmen and merchants, free peasants, dependent tenants, and frontier soldier-settlers; by the late 12th century aristocratic estates were expanding and the base of free peasantry was weakening

rulingClass.zh: 宫廷官僚与军政贵族家族，尤其是安杰洛斯、科穆宁、杜卡斯及其联姻网络
rulingClass.en: Court bureaucrats and military-civil aristocratic families, especially the Angeloi, Komnenoi, Doukai, and their marital networks

succession.zh: 原则上为皇位世袭与共治安排相结合，但实际上受宫廷政变、贵族拥立和军队支持强烈影响
succession.en: In principle succession combined dynastic inheritance with co-emperorship, but in practice it was heavily shaped by palace coups, aristocratic backing, and military support

structure.zh: 中央政府以皇帝和宫廷为核心，下辖财政、军务、司法、外交与皇室事务诸部门；省级治理依靠军政总督、税务官和地方贵族合作，中央控制力较科穆宁盛期减弱
structure.en: The central government was centered on the emperor and palace, with departments for finance, military affairs, justice, diplomacy, and imperial household matters; provincial governance relied on governors, fiscal officials, and local aristocratic cooperation, with weaker central control than at the Komnenian peak

function.zh: 负责地税、关税、国库收支、皇室地产与赋役征收
function.en: Handled land tax, customs duties, treasury receipts and disbursements, imperial estates, and fiscal collection

function.zh: 管理禁军名册、军饷、军械、要塞补给及省军协调
function.en: Managed guard rosters, military pay, armaments, fortress supply, and coordination with provincial forces

function.zh: 主持法庭、诉讼文书、敕令起草与官方档案
function.en: Oversaw courts, legal documentation, drafting of decrees, and official archives

function.zh: 处理使节接待、对外条约、宫廷礼仪与情报往来
function.en: Handled reception of envoys, foreign treaties, court ceremony, and intelligence exchanges

function.zh: 管理皇宫、御用工坊、宫廷供给、侍从与仪仗
function.en: Managed the palace, imperial workshops, court provisioning, attendants, and ceremonial regalia

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政承袭主题制残余并叠加12世纪的军政总督化趋势，巴尔干与小亚细亚各省由总督、税务员、主教和地方豪族共同维持秩序；边疆地区常以临时军政任命应对危机
localAdmin.en: Local administration preserved remnants of the theme system while increasingly relying on 12th-century governors with military authority; in the Balkans and Asia Minor order was maintained jointly by governors, fiscal officers, bishops, and local magnates, while frontier districts often depended on ad hoc military appointments

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系建立在罗马法传统、皇帝敕令和教会法并行基础上；《巴西利卡法典》及其后续解释仍具权威，但实际执行常受地方权贵与宫廷干预
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Roman law tradition, imperial decrees, and canon law in parallel; the Basilica and its later interpretations remained authoritative, though enforcement was often affected by local magnates and court interference

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税源为土地税、农业附加税、关税、港口税、市场税及皇室地产收入；政府亦通过特许、罚没和非常征收补充收入，但税务包征与地方截留问题严重
taxationSystem.en: The main taxes were land tax, agricultural levies, customs duties, port taxes, market dues, and revenues from imperial estates; the government also drew income from privileges, confiscations, and extraordinary levies, but tax farming and local diversion of revenues were serious problems

religion.zh: 东正教基督教为国教，宗教生活围绕牧首、修道院、圣像崇拜与礼仪历法展开，同时境内亦有亚美尼亚人、犹太人、拉丁基督徒及穆斯林商人社群
religion.en: Orthodox Christianity was the state religion, with religious life centered on the patriarch, monasteries, icon veneration, and the liturgical calendar, while Armenian Christians, Jews, Latin Christians, and Muslim merchant communities also lived within the empire

philosophy.zh: 思想传统结合希腊教父神学、古典修辞学与罗马政治理念，重视正统信仰、皇权神授与古典教育；宫廷与学者圈仍保存对古希腊哲学文本的研究
philosophy.en: Its intellectual tradition combined Greek patristic theology, classical rhetoric, and Roman political ideas, emphasizing orthodoxy, divinely sanctioned emperorship, and classical education; court and scholarly circles still preserved study of ancient Greek philosophical texts

writingSystem.zh: 以中世纪希腊文书写体系为主，使用希腊字母；行政、神学、史学和文学文献主要以希腊文记录
writingSystem.en: The civilization used the medieval Greek writing system in the Greek alphabet; administration, theology, historiography, and literature were recorded chiefly in Greek

culturalAchievements.zh: 君士坦丁堡仍是东地中海文化中心之一，拥有宏伟教堂、修道院抄写传统、历史写作、圣像艺术、镶嵌画、法律与礼仪文献积累；尽管财政衰退，宫廷文化与宗教艺术仍保持高度精致
culturalAchievements.en: Constantinople remained one of the cultural centers of the eastern Mediterranean, with monumental churches, monastic manuscript copying, historical writing, icon art, mosaics, and a large corpus of legal and ceremonial literature; despite fiscal decline, court culture and religious art remained highly refined

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支，行政与高文化语言为中世纪希腊语；帝国内并存亚美尼亚语、斯拉夫语、阿拉伯语等地区语言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch; the language of administration and high culture was medieval Greek, while Armenian, Slavic, Arabic, and other regional languages also existed within the empire

unit.zh: 海佩伦金币计价第纳尔
海佩伦金币计价第纳尔

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的东罗马经济仍具相当规模，依靠农业、港口贸易、手工业和君士坦丁堡市场体系支撑，但国力已不及11世纪中期与12世纪前半的高峰。意大利商人特权扩张削弱了国家对海运和关税的控制。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200 the Eastern Roman economy remained substantial, supported by agriculture, port commerce, crafts, and the market system of Constantinople, but it no longer matched the peaks of the mid-11th and early 12th centuries. Expanding privileges for Italian merchants weakened state control over shipping and customs.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、葡萄酒与橄榄生产、畜牧、丝织、染色、金属加工、造船、军械制造、建筑、港口转运与奢侈品加工
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine and olive production, animal husbandry, silk weaving, dyeing, metalworking, shipbuilding, arms manufacture, construction, port transshipment, and luxury goods processing

tradeGoods.zh: 丝绸、香料转口商品、葡萄酒、橄榄油、蜡、皮革、金银器、陶器、谷物、木材、奴隶与奢侈纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Silk, re-exported spices, wine, olive oil, wax, leather, gold and silverware, ceramics, grain, timber, slaves, and luxury textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化明显：君士坦丁堡富商、宫廷贵族与大地产家族拥有可观积蓄和土地，而普通农户多依赖自耕地、租佃地、牲畜与家庭器具维生，抗风险能力下降
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal: wealthy merchants of Constantinople, court aristocrats, and large landholding families held considerable reserves and estates, while ordinary peasant households depended on small freeholds, rented plots, livestock, and domestic goods, with declining resilience to shocks

unit.zh: 海佩伦金币计价第纳尔
海佩伦金币计价第纳尔

economicSystem.zh: 以农业税收国家、城市市场交换和受监管的货币经济相结合；国家仍干预粮食、军需、港口和某些高价值产业，但海上贸易 increasingly 受威尼斯人、热那亚人和比萨人影响
economicSystem.en: A combination of agrarian tax state, urban market exchange, and regulated monetary economy; the state still intervened in grain, military supply, ports, and certain high-value industries, but maritime trade was increasingly influenced by Venetians, Genoese, and Pisans

source.en: Customs, port duties, and market taxes

source.zh: 罚没、司法收入与特许费
source.en: Confiscations, judicial income, and concession fees

category.zh: 宗教捐助、外交赠礼与工程
category.en: Religious patronage, diplomatic gifts, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍拥有一定储备，但较曼努埃尔一世时代明显缩水；阿莱克修斯三世时期挥霍、赏赐与挪用严重，财政缓冲能力有限
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still retained some reserves, but it was markedly reduced compared with the age of Manuel I; under Alexios III extravagance, gifts, and diversion of funds were severe, leaving only limited fiscal resilience

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向短期筹款而非长期整顿，通过加重征收、出售官职、授予商业特权和调用储备弥补赤字，进一步侵蚀国家财政基础
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored short-term fund raising over long-term reform, using heavier exactions, sale of offices, commercial privileges, and reserve withdrawals to cover deficits, thereby further eroding the state's fiscal base

description.zh: 包括首都驻军、宫廷近卫及可机动的精锐部队，是皇帝直接控制的核心兵力，但人数与战斗力已逊于前代
description.en: Included the capital garrison, palace guards, and mobile elite troops, forming the emperor's core forces, though both numbers and effectiveness had declined from earlier centuries

description.zh: 分布于巴尔干、小亚细亚和海峡要地，承担守城、巡防与地方镇压任务，但装备和士气参差不齐
description.en: Stationed across the Balkans, Asia Minor, and strategic straits, these forces guarded fortresses, patrolled regions, and suppressed local unrest, but varied greatly in equipment and morale

name.zh: 雇佣军骑兵与外籍部队

description.zh: 大量依赖拉丁人、库曼人、突厥人及其他外籍士兵补充机动力与冲击力，忠诚度取决于薪饷与政治形势
