description.en: The principal Christian kingdom of medieval Nubia, noticeably declining by around 1200.

name.zh: 埃塞俄比亚高地基督教王国

ruler.zh: 札格维王朝过渡时期诸王（具体在位者存疑）
ruler.en: Transitional rulers of the Zagwe dynasty (specific reigning king uncertain)

governmentForm.zh: 神圣君权制的山地基督教王国，王权与教会权威紧密结合
governmentForm.en: A sacral highland Christian monarchy in which royal power was closely intertwined with ecclesiastical authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、地方贵族、教士与修道团体、自由农民、工匠和少量依附人口构成；高地农业村社与教会地产是社会组织核心
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, regional nobles, clergy and monastic communities, free peasants, artisans, and a smaller dependent population; highland agrarian village communities and church estates formed the social core

rulingClass.zh: 札格维王室、高地军事贵族、地方领主以及埃塞俄比亚正教高级神职人员
rulingClass.en: The Zagwe royal house, highland military nobility, regional lords, and senior clergy of the Ethiopian Orthodox Church

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族内部继承与贵族、教会承认相结合，虽以世袭为原则，但过渡时期可能出现竞争与共治式安排
succession.en: Succession combined dynastic inheritance within the royal family with recognition by nobles and the church; while hereditary in principle, transitional periods could involve rivalry and quasi-collegial arrangements

structure.zh: 中央王廷规模不大，以君主、宫廷官员、地方总督、军事贵族和主教网络治理高地核心区域；边远地区依赖朝贡、盟约与教会联系维系统治
structure.en: The central court was relatively compact, governing the highland core through the monarch, palace officials, provincial governors, military nobles, and a network of bishops; more distant zones were held through tribute, alliance, and church ties

function.zh: 管理王室、宫廷供给、仪式与王廷文书传达
function.en: Managed the royal family, court provisioning, ceremony, and the transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 协调地方领主、征收实物贡赋、监督王室领地
function.en: Coordinated regional lords, collected tribute in kind, and supervised royal domains

function.zh: 联络主教、修道院与教堂，管理宗教捐献、圣职支持和大型圣建工程
function.en: Liaised with bishops, monasteries, and churches, overseeing religious donations, clerical support, and major sacred building works

function.zh: 调集王家军队、贵族随从和地方守备，维护山地要道与边境安全
function.en: Mobilized royal troops, noble retinues, and local garrisons, maintaining mountain routes and frontier security

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托地区贵族、王室任命的总督、村社首领与教会地产管理者。统治较少依赖成文官僚体系，更多依赖人身效忠、宗教权威和地缘控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on regional nobles, royal governors, village headmen, and managers of church estates. Rule depended less on a written bureaucracy and more on personal allegiance, religious authority, and control of terrain.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断和基督教教规共同构成，婚姻、继承、教产与宗教秩序多由教会规范，刑事与地方争端由王权和地方领主裁决
legalSystem.en: Law consisted of customary practice, royal judgment, and Christian canon norms; marriage, inheritance, church property, and religious order were often governed by ecclesiastical rules, while criminal and local disputes were decided by royal and noble authority

taxationSystem.zh: 财政征收以土地出产、畜产、劳役和贡赋为主，税收多为实物，如谷物、蜂蜜、牲畜、布匹与手工业品；王室与教会均持有地产并享有征收权
taxationSystem.en: Revenue extraction was based chiefly on agricultural produce, livestock, labor service, and tribute, with taxes paid largely in kind such as grain, honey, animals, cloth, and craft goods; both crown and church held estates with rights of collection

religion.zh: 埃塞俄比亚正统基督教，为基督一性论传统，与亚历山大科普特教会保持宗教联系；圣徒崇拜、斋戒、礼仪历法与君权神圣观念极为重要
religion.en: Ethiopian Orthodox Christianity, within the miaphysite tradition and linked ecclesiastically to the Coptic Church of Alexandria; saint veneration, fasting, the liturgical calendar, and sacral kingship were of great importance

philosophy.zh: 思想以圣经诠释、教父传统、修道禁欲、王权神授与圣地再现观念为核心，强调耶路撒冷象征、救赎历史和虔敬生活
philosophy.en: Thought centered on biblical interpretation, patristic tradition, monastic asceticism, divine kingship, and the symbolic recreation of sacred geography, emphasizing Jerusalem imagery, salvation history, and pious living

writingSystem.zh: 采用吉兹文书写系统，主要用于礼仪、圣经、铭文和王室宗教文本；口语多样，但吉兹文是高地教会文化语言
writingSystem.en: The Ge'ez script was used, primarily for liturgy, scripture, inscriptions, and royal-religious texts; spoken languages were diverse, but Ge'ez served as the highland church's literary language

culturalAchievements.zh: 最突出成就是拉利贝拉等地的岩凿教堂建筑群、石刻与十字纹装饰、宗教手抄本传统、圣乐与礼仪文化，以及将高地景观塑造为“新耶路撒冷”的王权建筑计划
culturalAchievements.en: The greatest achievements were the rock-hewn churches of Lalibela and related sites, carved stone and cross motifs, the manuscript tradition of religious texts, sacred music and liturgical culture, and royal architectural programs that reshaped the highland landscape as a 'New Jerusalem'

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属亚非语系闪米特语族；礼仪与书面语为吉兹文，民间通行早期埃塞俄比亚闪米特诸语及周边库施特语
languageFamily.en: The main languages belonged to the Afroasiatic Semitic branch; Ge'ez was the liturgical and written language, while early Ethiosemitic vernaculars and neighboring Cushitic languages were widely spoken

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地自给农业为主，剩余有限但稳定。财富更多表现为土地、牲畜、粮食和劳役控制，而非大量货币流通。与红海和尼罗河流域的远程贸易存在，但规模不及伊斯兰核心商路。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily based on self-sufficient highland agriculture, with limited but stable surplus. Wealth was expressed more through control of land, livestock, grain, and labor than through extensive monetization. Long-distance trade with the Red Sea and Nile regions existed, but on a smaller scale than in the major Islamic commercial circuits.

mainIndustries.zh: 山地农业、畜牧业、蜂蜜与蜂蜡生产、建筑石工、木工、纺织、皮革加工和教会建筑相关工艺
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, animal husbandry, honey and beeswax production, stone construction, carpentry, weaving, leatherworking, and crafts tied to church building

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、牲畜、皮革、盐、铁器、布匹、香料转运品、宗教器物及少量奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, honey, beeswax, livestock, hides, salt, iron goods, textiles, transit spices, religious objects, and a limited range of luxury goods

name.zh: 实物贡赋与金属计值并行
name.en: In-kind tribute with limited bullion reckoning

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牛羊、农具、储粮与宗教网络保护。贵族和教会则通过土地、佃附劳动力及牲畜群积累更大财富。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in cultivation rights, cattle and sheep, tools, grain stores, and protection within religious networks. Nobles and the church accumulated greater wealth through land, dependent labor, and herds.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业庄园、村社生产、教会地产和地方贡赋为基础的低货币化经济；王室通过象征性中心、土地控制和朝贡网络整合资源
economicSystem.en: A low-monetization economy based on agrarian estates, village production, church lands, and regional tribute; the crown integrated resources through symbolic centrality, land control, and tribute networks

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 牲畜、蜂蜜、蜂蜡等实物征收
source.en: Levy on livestock, honey, beeswax, and similar goods

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 地方领主朝贡与附属地区贡纳
source.en: Tribute from regional lords and subordinate areas

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 贸易关卡、市场费与杂项收入
source.en: Trade tolls, market dues, and miscellaneous income

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

category.zh: 教会、修道院与圣建工程
category.en: Church, monasteries, and sacred building works

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库多由王室储粮、牲畜、贵重礼物、金属器物和有限贵金属积累构成，并非高度货币化现金库。储备能够支撑礼仪、建筑与短期军事征调。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted largely of royal grain stores, livestock, valuable gifts, metal objects, and limited precious metal holdings rather than a highly monetized cash reserve. It was sufficient to support ritual, building works, and short-term military levies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重稳定征收、维持王室施惠、资助宗教建筑与保障山地核心区忠诚，避免过度压榨农民以免破坏脆弱高地生产基础
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized stable extraction, maintenance of royal patronage, funding of religious construction, and preservation of loyalty in the highland core, while avoiding excessive burdens that might damage the fragile agrarian base

description.zh: 直属君主的核心武装，负责宫廷防卫、护送和战时中军
description.en: The monarch's core armed force, responsible for palace defense, escort duties, and the central field contingent in war

description.zh: 由地方领主和军事贵族召集的主要野战力量，装备和战斗力因地区与领主实力而异
description.en: The main field force raised by regional lords and military nobles, with equipment and effectiveness varying by region and lordly wealth

description.zh: 由村社和地方聚落提供，适合山地防御、道路警戒和短期征调
description.en: Provided by villages and local settlements, suited to mountain defense, road security, and short-term mobilization

description.zh: 数量较少但具机动性，主要由贵族和精锐组成，用于追击、联络和威慑
description.en: Limited in number but mobile, composed mainly of nobles and elite troops for pursuit, communications, and intimidation

commanderInChief.zh: 国王
commanderInChief.en: The King

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、剑、盾、弓和部分锁甲或皮甲为主，攻城技术有限，善于山地机动、防御和依托险要地形作战
technology.en: Armament centered on spears, swords, shields, bows, and some mail or leather armor; siege capacity was limited, but forces were effective in mountain mobility, defense, and fighting from strong terrain

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南部与东部边缘地带的非整合群体、与穆斯林低地和红海贸易走廊相关的摩擦，以及内部贵族离心倾向；大规模外敌入侵压力在1200年前后相对有限
threats.en: The main threats came from less integrated southern and eastern frontier groups, friction with Muslim lowland and Red Sea trade corridors, and centrifugal tendencies among nobles; pressure from massive external invasions was relatively limited around 1200

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于埃塞俄比亚北部和中部高地，聚落多为分散村社与教会中心，受地形、降雨和耕地条件强烈影响
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the northern and central Ethiopian highlands, with settlement in dispersed villages and church centers, strongly shaped by terrain, rainfall, and arable land

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、军事贵族和高级神职；中间层为地方自由民、骑士性武士、教会附属人员和工匠；底层为普通农民、牧民、劳役人口与依附者。修道院网络在阶层流动和社会庇护中具有重要作用。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal house, military nobility, and senior clergy; the middle included free commoners, warrior retainers, church dependents, and artisans; the lower strata comprised ordinary peasants, herders, laboring populations, and dependents. Monastic networks played an important role in social mobility and protection.

allies.zh: 与努比亚基督教王国存在天然宗教亲近与间接联系；同科普特埃及教会保持关键宗教纽带，但政治上并非稳定军事同盟
allies.en: There was natural religious affinity and indirect connection with the Nubian Christian kingdom; crucial ecclesiastical ties were maintained with the Coptic Church in Egypt, though this did not amount to a stable political-military alliance

enemies.zh: 并无持续性的单一宿敌，但与周边穆斯林低地政权、贸易据点和边疆群体存在周期性冲突与竞争
enemies.en: There was no single permanent great enemy, but periodic conflict and rivalry existed with neighboring Muslim lowland polities, trade nodes, and frontier groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持宗教合法性、保障与埃及教会联系、控制高地通道、稳固边疆臣属关系为主；对外活动较区域化，重视朝圣象征和圣地观念，而非远距离扩张
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving religious legitimacy, safeguarding ties with the Egyptian church, controlling highland routes, and stabilizing subordinate frontier relations; external action was regionally oriented, emphasizing pilgrimage symbolism and sacred geography rather than long-range expansion

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪高地王国
era.en: Medieval Highland Kingdom in the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 岩凿建筑工程技术、复杂排水与石工规划、高地梯田与混合作物农业、手抄本制作、教会礼仪音乐传统和山地交通适应技术
keyInnovations.en: Rock-hewn architectural engineering, sophisticated drainage and stone planning, highland terracing and mixed-crop agriculture, manuscript production, church liturgical music traditions, and technologies adapted to mountain movement

strengths.zh: 其优势在于强烈的宗教认同、适应高地环境的农业与防御体系、王权与教会结合所带来的合法性，以及拉利贝拉建筑工程凝聚的政治象征力
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in strong religious identity, agricultural and defensive systems adapted to the highlands, the legitimacy produced by the union of kingship and church, and the political symbolism embodied in the Lalibela building program

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括行政文官体系有限、远距离贸易与货币化不足、地方贵族离心风险、对教会网络的高度依赖，以及在低地和边疆地区的统治整合能力有限
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included a limited civil bureaucracy, modest long-distance trade and monetization, centrifugal risks from regional nobles, heavy dependence on church networks, and restricted capacity to integrate lowland and frontier zones

outlook.zh: 到1200年前后，该王国总体稳定，凭借宗教建筑、山地屏障和地方忠诚维持统治，但未来仍可能面临王朝正统性争议、地方权力重组及与周边穆斯林势力的竞争。短期内前景稳健，中长期则取决于继承安排和高地核心区整合能力。
outlook.en: Around 1200, the kingdom was broadly stable, maintaining rule through religious construction, mountain defenses, and local loyalties; however, it still faced possible disputes over dynastic legitimacy, regional power realignment, and competition with neighboring Muslim forces. Its short-term outlook was solid, while the medium-term future depended on succession arrangements and continued integration of the highland core.

description.zh: 埃塞俄比亚高地的基督教王国，以岩石教堂与王权宗教结合著称。
description.en: A Christian kingdom of the Ethiopian highlands, noted for rock-hewn churches and sacral kingship.

governmentForm.zh: 由若干曼德王邦、宗族领地与附属村社构成的分散性部族君主制联盟
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-monarchical confederation composed of several Manden chiefdoms, clan territories, and dependent village communities

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、自由民、专门职业群体与奴隶人口为基础，地方社会围绕家族长者、战士与宗教权威组织
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around clans, free commoners, hereditary specialist groups, and enslaved populations, with local communities organized around elders, warriors, and religious authorities

rulingClass.zh: 各地王族、战士贵族、宗族首领与有威望的长者
rulingClass.en: Local royal lineages, warrior aristocrats, clan chiefs, and respected elders

succession.zh: 多为王族内部的世袭继承，并受母系与父系亲属、长者会议及武力支持共同影响
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within ruling houses, shaped by paternal and maternal kinship ties, councils of elders, and military backing

structure.zh: 不存在高度集中的单一国家机构；曼登地区由若干法玛统治的邦国组成，彼此通过婚姻、盟约、贡赋与共同贸易利益维系，尼亚尼周边诸邦在政治上较具影响力
structure.en: There was no fully centralized single state apparatus; the Manden region consisted of several polities ruled by famas, linked by marriage, alliance, tribute, and shared commercial interests, with the polities around Niani holding greater political weight

function.zh: 管理法玛家族、宫廷侍从、礼仪、使者接待与王室财物
function.en: Managed the fama's family, court attendants, ceremonial duties, reception of envoys, and royal property

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金砂与地方贡品，维持公共仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, gold dust, and local tribute while maintaining public storage and redistribution

function.zh: 管理渡口、道路、市集秩序、商旅保护与部分交易征费
function.en: Oversaw ferries, roads, market order, merchant protection, and some transaction levies

function.zh: 统筹王室亲兵、地方战士动员、边界防御与惩罚性远征
function.en: Coordinated royal retainers, mobilization of local warriors, frontier defense, and punitive expeditions

function.zh: 处理宗族争端、婚姻财产、债务、奴隶归属与习惯法裁决
function.en: Handled clan disputes, marriage property, debts, slave status, and judgments under customary law

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社、宗族领袖、地区首领与附庸法玛为核心。权力通过面对面关系、口头承诺与贡赋网络运行，而非成文官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on village communities, clan leaders, district headmen, and subordinate famas. Authority operated through face-to-face ties, oral obligations, and tribute networks rather than a fully written bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于曼德习惯法、长者调解、誓言、赔偿与宗族仲裁；在与穆斯林商人接触较多的贸易节点，也开始出现有限的伊斯兰法影响。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on Mande customary norms, elder mediation, oaths, compensation, and clan arbitration; at trade nodes with stronger Muslim merchant presence, limited Islamic legal influence was also emerging.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自贡赋、市场税、渡口和道路征费、战利品分配、黄金产区控制以及对附属村社和边缘群体的劳役征调。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from tribute, market taxes, ferry and road tolls, distribution of war booty, control over gold-bearing zones, and labor obligations imposed on dependent villages and peripheral groups.

religion.zh: 以曼德传统宗教为主，崇敬祖先、地方精灵与神圣力量；伊斯兰教已通过跨撒哈拉贸易进入上层与商人圈，但尚未全面主导政治社会
religion.en: Mande traditional religion predominated, emphasizing ancestors, local spirits, and sacred forces; Islam had entered elite and merchant circles through trans-Saharan trade but had not yet come to dominate political and social life

philosophy.zh: 强调宗族伦理、口传历史、荣誉、互助与长者智慧；社会价值观重视勇武、慷慨、信誉与维系共同体秩序
philosophy.en: Thought emphasized clan ethics, oral history, honor, reciprocity, and the wisdom of elders; social values prized bravery, generosity, reputation, and maintenance of communal order

writingSystem.zh: 主要依赖口述传统与专业记忆者传承历史和法度；阿拉伯文在穆斯林商贸与宗教环境中开始有限使用
writingSystem.en: The society relied primarily on oral tradition and professional memory keepers to preserve history and norms; Arabic began to see limited use in Muslim commercial and religious contexts

culturalAchievements.zh: 口头史诗、谱系记忆、音乐吟唱、铁器工艺、木雕与织造传统高度发达，为后来马里帝国的政治文化整合奠定基础
culturalAchievements.en: Oral epic traditions, genealogical memory, musical recitation, ironworking, woodcarving, and weaving were highly developed, laying the groundwork for the political-cultural synthesis of the later Mali Empire

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系曼德语族
languageFamily.en: Mande branch of the Niger-Congo language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以粮食农业、畜牧、铁器制造、地方交换和黄金贸易控制为基础。人均产出不高，但对内陆西非贸易路线和金砂资源的掌控，使其区域经济地位持续上升。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on grain agriculture, livestock keeping, iron production, local exchange, and control of gold trade. Per-capita output was modest, but command over inland West African routes and gold dust resources gave the region a rising regional economic importance.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟、稻与其他粮食种植，畜牧业，铁器冶炼与锻造，纺织，狩猎采集，黄金采洗与转运，长途商贸服务
mainIndustries.en: Cultivation of millet, rice, and other staples, livestock herding, iron smelting and smithing, textiles, hunting and gathering, gold washing and transport, and long-distance trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 金砂、可乐果、奴隶、皮革、牲畜、铁器、布匹、盐与农产品
tradeGoods.en: Gold dust, kola nuts, slaves, leather, livestock, iron goods, textiles, salt, and agricultural produce

name.en: Gold dust, salt blocks, and barter media

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、粮仓储备、牲畜、婚姻联盟、奴隶劳力、铁器工具与宗族支持网络，而非大量铸币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to farmland, grain stores, livestock, marriage alliances, enslaved labor, iron tools, and clan support networks rather than in large accumulations of coined money.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、宗族再分配和区域贸易并存的混合经济；政治权力与对土地、人口和贸易节点的控制紧密结合。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining village production, clan redistribution, and regional trade; political power was closely tied to control of land, people, and commercial nodes.

source.zh: 黄金与采金区控制收益
市场税、渡口税与道路征费
source.en: Market taxes, ferry dues, and road tolls

source.zh: 战利品、罚金与附庸贡纳

category.zh: 王室、酋长与再分配赏赐
category.en: Royal household, chiefs, and redistributive gifts

category.zh: 贸易路线维护与宗教礼仪

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散保存在王室仓库、金砂储藏、牲畜群和粮仓之中，并非集中于单一金库。财富的流动性与再分配能力往往比静态储备更重要。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were dispersed among royal storehouses, gold-dust holdings, livestock herds, and granaries rather than concentrated in a single vault. Mobility of wealth and the capacity for redistribution often mattered more than static reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重稳健保守，通过维持贡赋网络、控制黄金通道与储备粮食来增强政治整合，避免对核心村社过度榨取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing maintenance of tribute networks, control of gold corridors, and grain reserves to strengthen political integration while avoiding excessive extraction from core village communities.

description.zh: 由各法玛维持的核心武装，负责护卫、突袭与战时指挥中枢，装备长矛、刀剑、弓与皮盾。
description.en: The core armed forces maintained by the famas, responsible for protection, raids, and wartime command, equipped with spears, swords, bows, and leather shields.

description.zh: 由村社自由民和附庸群体战时征集而成，承担守卫、伏击、围攻村寨与后勤运输任务。
description.en: Raised in wartime from village free commoners and dependent groups, they carried out guarding, ambushes, assaults on fortified settlements, and logistical transport.

description.zh: 规模仍有限，但在较开阔地带和贸易路线保护中价值日增，多由贵族与富裕战士组成。
description.en: Still limited in size, but increasingly valuable in more open terrain and in protecting trade routes, often composed of nobles and wealthier warriors.

commanderInChief.zh: 各法玛及其首席战首
commanderInChief.en: The various famas and their principal war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制长矛、刀剑、弓箭、皮盾和简易防护为主，依赖熟悉地形、亲族动员与小规模机动。尚未形成如后世萨赫勒帝国那样强大的制度化骑兵军制。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, swords, bows, leather shields, and simple protection, relying on local terrain knowledge, kin-based mobilization, and small-scale maneuver. It had not yet developed the more institutionalized cavalry systems of later Sahelian empires.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边西苏丹竞争邦国、内部宗族争斗、对黄金产区和贸易通道的争夺，以及萨赫勒更强大政权可能施加的压力。
threats.en: Principal threats came from rival Western Sudanic polities, internal clan conflicts, competition over gold zones and trade arteries, and possible pressure from stronger Sahelian states.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在上尼日尔流域与曼登腹地的村社、河谷农区、林缘地带和区域市集周边，整体呈农村化且分散聚居特征。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in village communities, riverine farming zones, forest-edge areas, and regional market surroundings in the Upper Niger basin and Manden heartland, with a predominantly rural and dispersed settlement pattern.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与贵族战士、自由民农牧人口、工匠与吟游史官等世袭职业群体、商人、附庸人口与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included royal and noble warriors, free farming and pastoral populations, hereditary specialist groups such as artisans and oral historians, merchants, dependent populations, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分邻近曼德与西苏丹地方政权保持通婚、互市和临时军事合作关系；同撒哈拉商路上的穆斯林商人群体维持务实联系。
allies.en: The polities maintained intermarriage, mutual trade, and temporary military cooperation with nearby Mande and Western Sudanic states, while also sustaining pragmatic ties with Muslim merchant communities on trans-Saharan routes.

enemies.zh: 并无单一绝对宿敌，但与争夺金源、奴隶、农地和商路控制权的周边诸邦常有冲突，亦受加纳残余势力和其他萨赫勒强权间接影响。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent archenemy, but the polities often clashed with neighboring states over gold sources, slaves, farmland, and trade-route control, and were indirectly affected by residual Ghanaian power and other Sahelian strong forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以地方均势、婚姻联盟、贸易安全与逐步整合曼登核心地区为主，避免在尚未完全统一前进行过度扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on local balance, marriage alliances, trade security, and gradual consolidation of the Manden core, avoiding excessive expansion before full unification had been achieved.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器冶炼与锻造技术成熟，农业适应河谷与稀树草原环境，长途商路组织能力增强，口传行政与谱系记忆系统在政治整合中发挥关键作用。
keyInnovations.en: Iron smelting and forging were well developed, agriculture was adapted to riverine and savanna conditions, long-distance trade organization was strengthening, and oral administrative and genealogical memory systems played a key role in political integration.

strengths.zh: 掌握上尼日尔内陆资源与黄金通道，宗族网络强韧，农业与铁器基础稳固，贸易潜力巨大，政治整合趋势明显。
strengths.en: The polities controlled inland Upper Niger resources and gold corridors, possessed resilient clan networks, had a solid agricultural and ironworking base, enjoyed major trade potential, and showed a clear trend toward political consolidation.

weaknesses.zh: 政权分散，统一性不足，对个人威望和亲族关系依赖很强，书面行政薄弱，骑兵与远程投送能力有限，内斗可能削弱共同防务。
weaknesses.en: Political authority was fragmented, unity remained incomplete, governance depended heavily on personal prestige and kinship ties, written administration was weak, cavalry and long-range projection were limited, and infighting could undermine common defense.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后，曼登诸邦处于稳定而上升的整合阶段。若能由强有力的领袖统一主要王族、控制黄金贸易并压制内部分裂，极有可能在数十年内形成更具帝国性质的西非强国。
outlook.en: Around 1200, the Manden polities were in a stable yet ascending phase of consolidation. If a strong leader could unite the principal ruling houses, control the gold trade, and suppress internal fragmentation, they were highly likely to develop within decades into a more imperial West African power.

description.zh: 马里帝国兴起之前的曼丁诸邦，逐步整合西非内陆资源与商路。
description.en: The Manden states before the rise of Mali, gradually consolidating inland West African resources and trade routes.

dynasty.zh: 索宁克及相关王族世系

capital.zh: 前昆比萨利赫地区及其附近中心
capital.en: Former Kumbi Saleh Zone and Nearby Centers

governmentForm.zh: 分散的君主制与部族联盟体，围绕跨撒哈拉贸易节点组织
governmentForm.en: Decentralized monarchies and tribal confederative polities organized around trans-Saharan trade nodes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族、穆斯林商人与书吏、自由民农牧民、工匠与职业世袭群体，以及依附人口和奴隶构成；城镇与绿洲中的伊斯兰化精英与乡村传统权威并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal lineages, Muslim merchants and clerks, free farmers and pastoralists, hereditary artisan and specialist groups, and dependent populations including slaves; Islamized urban elites coexisted with rural traditional authorities.

rulingClass.zh: 索宁克王族、地方贵族、萨赫勒军事首领，以及控制税关和商路的穆斯林商人精英
rulingClass.en: Soninke royal lineages, local nobles, Sahelian military chiefs, and Muslim mercantile elites controlling customs points and caravan routes

succession.zh: 以王族血统继承为主，但各地规则不一；常见兄终弟及、支系竞争和贵族议立，地方实力对继承结果影响很大。
succession.en: Succession was based mainly on royal lineage but varied by polity; collateral succession, rivalry among branches, and noble selection were common, with local power balances heavily shaping outcomes.

structure.zh: 无统一中央国家。各王国以宫廷、税关、商埠和绿洲据点为核心运作，王室依靠亲族、地方长老、穆斯林卡迪与书吏、以及商队税吏治理领地与贸易路线。
structure.en: There was no single centralized state. Each kingdom operated through a court, customs posts, market towns, and oasis stations, with rulers relying on kin networks, local elders, Muslim qadis and scribes, and caravan tax officials to govern territory and trade routes.

function.zh: 管理王室供给、使节接待、赏赐与朝会秩序，并协调贵族与附庸首领关系。
function.en: Managed royal provisioning, reception of envoys, patronage, and court ceremony, while coordinating relations with nobles and subordinate chiefs.

function.zh: 在市场、渡口和商道征收货税、通行税与保护费，监督商队安全与称量标准。
function.en: Collected duties, transit tolls, and protection fees at markets, river crossings, and caravan routes, and supervised caravan security and standards of weights.

function.zh: 联络属邦、村社和牧民首领，征收贡赋，处理服属关系与边缘地区忠诚问题。
function.en: Liaised with subordinate polities, villages, and pastoral leaders, collected tribute, and managed vassal ties and loyalty in frontier areas.

function.zh: 在穆斯林城镇执行伊斯兰法裁判商事、婚姻与继承案件，同时与地方习惯法权威并行。
function.en: Applied Islamic law in Muslim towns for commercial, marital, and inheritance disputes while coexisting with authorities of local customary law.

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、征召骑兵与步兵，守卫水源、绿洲、市场和对外商路。
function.en: Organized royal guards, mobilized cavalry and infantry, and protected water sources, oases, markets, and external trade routes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由村长、宗族长老、市场首领和附庸酋长负责；王室在重要贸易中心设置税吏、书记和军头，但对远方草原与沙漠边缘的控制较松散。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted mainly by village heads, lineage elders, market leaders, and subordinate chiefs; royal authority placed tax officials, scribes, and military heads in major trade centers, but control over distant savanna and desert fringes remained loose.

legalSystem.zh: 法律呈双重结构：商镇和伊斯兰社群中广泛采用马立克学派伊斯兰法，乡村和部族社会则依循祖传习惯法、赔偿制度与王命裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was dual in character: Maliki Islamic law was widely used in market towns and Muslim communities, while rural and tribal society followed inherited customary law, compensation practices, and royal judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以商路关税、市场税、黄金与盐贸易抽成、属邦贡赋、畜群征收和战利分配为主；农业田税存在但不如贸易税重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied primarily on caravan customs, market dues, shares of gold and salt trade, tribute from subordinate groups, livestock levies, and portions of war booty; agricultural taxation existed but was less important than trade duties.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教与本地宗教并存。王廷、商人和书写文化多受伊斯兰影响，但许多乡村与王权仪式仍保留祖灵、圣地与地方神力崇拜。
religion.en: Islam coexisted with indigenous religion. Courts, merchants, and literate culture were heavily influenced by Islam, yet many villages and royal rituals still preserved ancestor veneration, sacred places, and local spiritual cults.

philosophy.zh: 政治伦理强调王权威望、血统合法性、长老协商、馈赠义务与商旅保护；穆斯林学者则引入古兰经法理、道德教化与书写行政观念。
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized royal prestige, legitimacy of lineage, elder consultation, gift obligations, and protection of trade, while Muslim scholars introduced Quranic legal reasoning, moral instruction, and concepts of written administration.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母用于宗教、商贸和书信；地方语言多以口述传统、记忆谱系和仪式吟诵传承，书写普及度有限。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script was used for religion, commerce, and correspondence; local languages were transmitted largely through oral tradition, remembered genealogies, and ritual recitation, with limited literacy overall.

culturalAchievements.zh: 跨撒哈拉贸易城市文化、伊斯兰学术与法学传播、金盐交换网络的制度化、口传史诗与谱系传统、以及萨赫勒王廷礼仪与骑兵贵族文化均十分突出。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable cultural achievements included trans-Saharan urban-commercial culture, the spread of Islamic learning and law, institutionalized gold-salt exchange networks, oral epic and genealogical traditions, and the courtly and cavalry aristocratic culture of the Sahel.

languageFamily.zh: 以曼德语支和索宁克语为主，并有柏柏尔语、阿拉伯语及周边尼日尔-刚果语言在贸易和边境区域使用
languageFamily.en: Primarily Mande languages and Soninke, with Berber, Arabic, and neighboring Niger-Congo languages also used in trade and frontier regions

gdpDescription.zh: 总体经济仍具区域重要性，但已较古代加纳鼎盛时期明显衰退。财富集中于金盐转运、畜牧、地方农业和奴隶贸易，政治分裂使税收与市场整合能力下降。
gdpDescription.en: The economy remained regionally important but was clearly diminished from the peak of ancient Ghana. Wealth centered on gold-salt transit, pastoralism, local agriculture, and slave trading, while political fragmentation reduced fiscal extraction and market integration.

mainIndustries.zh: 跨撒哈拉转口贸易、黄金采集与中介、盐运、畜牧、粟米与高粱农业、皮革、纺织、金属加工和奴隶贸易
mainIndustries.en: Trans-Saharan entrepot trade, gold collection and brokerage, salt transport, pastoralism, millet and sorghum agriculture, leatherworking, textiles, metalworking, and slave trading

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、盐、奴隶、马匹、铜、布匹、皮革、象牙、可乐果、谷物和枣类
tradeGoods.en: Gold, salt, slaves, horses, copper, textiles, leather, ivory, kola nuts, grain, and dates

name.zh: 金粉、金块与外来伊斯兰金币并用
name.en: Gold Dust, Gold Nuggets, and Imported Islamic Coin

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为粮仓、牲畜、工具、亲族网络和少量金粉；商人家庭与王族则掌握远高于平均水平的金属财富、奴隶与长途贸易债权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held chiefly in granaries, livestock, tools, kin networks, and small amounts of gold dust; merchant households and royal families possessed far greater stocks of bullion, slaves, and long-distance trade claims.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室税关控制、商人网络、部族护送、地方贡赋和农村自给生产相结合的混合经济；国家影响力主要集中在贸易通道，而非全面土地官僚控制。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining royal customs control, merchant networks, tribal escort arrangements, local tribute, and rural subsistence production; state influence focused more on trade corridors than on comprehensive bureaucratic control of land.

source.en: Shares from Gold, Salt, and Mineral Trade
Livestock Levies, Fines, and Miscellaneous Income

treasuryDescription.zh: 各王国金库分散保存于王宫、商埠仓库和可信商人网络之中，不存在单一统一国库。财富常以金粉、金块、盐块、牲畜和贸易债务形式储存。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasuries were dispersed among palaces, market storehouses, and trusted merchant networks rather than concentrated in one unified state treasury. Wealth was often stored as gold dust, nuggets, salt bars, livestock, and commercial obligations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持商路畅通、保障税源与稳定附庸忠诚；由于政权分裂，财政更重短期征收和礼物政治，而非长期制度化预算。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on keeping trade routes open, securing taxable exchange, and maintaining subordinate loyalty; because of political fragmentation, finance emphasized short-term extraction and gift politics more than long-term institutional budgeting.

description.zh: 由王族、贵族随从与装备较好的战士组成，是萨赫勒地区最具机动力的主力，用于突袭、护送商队和压制附庸。
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, noble followers, and better-equipped warriors, this was the most mobile arm of the Sahel and was used for raiding, caravan escort, and coercing subordinates.

description.zh: 主要来自村社征召与附庸提供，装备长矛、标枪、弓箭和皮盾，适于守卫聚落、市场与水源点。
description.en: Drawn mainly from village levies and subordinate contingents, they carried spears, javelins, bows, and leather shields and were suited to guarding settlements, markets, and water points.

name.zh: 商路守备与沙漠向导武装
name.en: Trade Route Guards and Desert Guide Forces

description.zh: 负责绿洲、关卡与商队保护，常与柏柏尔或地方沙漠部族合作，熟悉水点、补给和长程行军。
description.en: Tasked with protecting oases, checkpoints, and caravans, often cooperating with Berber or local desert groups familiar with water sources, supply, and long-range movement.

commanderInChief.zh: 各王国国王及其首席战酋
commanderInChief.en: The Kings of Each Polity and Their Senior War Chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以骑兵作战、皮甲与锁子甲少量使用、铁矛、剑、弓箭和沙漠后勤经验为核心；缺乏重型攻城技术和高度标准化装备。
technology.en: Military technology centered on cavalry warfare, limited use of leather armor and mail, iron spears, swords, bows, and strong desert logistics experience; heavy siege capability and standardized equipment were limited.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括北方撒哈拉与马格里布势力对商路的争夺、东南方向曼德诸势力的崛起、附庸叛离、干旱与商道转移造成的资源枯竭。
threats.en: Main threats included competition over trade routes from Saharan and Maghrebi powers to the north, the rise of Mande polities to the southeast, defection of subordinates, and resource strain caused by drought and shifting commercial routes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在萨赫勒农业带、旧加纳核心区、河谷聚落、绿洲节点和商道沿线。总体人口密度不高，但在市场中心和水源附近形成较稳定聚居。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Sahelian farming belt, the old Ghana heartland, riverine settlements, oasis nodes, and caravan corridors. Overall density was modest, but more stable clustering appeared around market centers and water sources.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族、商人和穆斯林学者；中层为自由农民、牧民、地方武士和手工业者；下层包括依附民、战俘和奴隶。职业世袭与血统身份对社会流动影响显著。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of royals, nobles, merchants, and Muslim scholars; the middle consisted of free farmers, pastoralists, local warriors, and artisans; lower groups included dependents, captives, and slaves. Hereditary occupation and lineage status strongly shaped social mobility.

allies.zh: 与部分柏柏尔商旅部族、撒哈拉绿洲集团及若干地方曼德或萨赫勒酋邦维持实用性联盟，关系多随商路利益而变动。
allies.en: They maintained pragmatic alliances with some Berber merchant tribes, Saharan oasis groups, and certain local Mande or Sahelian chieftaincies, with ties shifting according to trade interests.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺商路税源的竞争性萨赫勒与曼德政权、北方沙漠武装集团，以及任何试图绕开旧加纳贸易节点的新兴势力。
enemies.en: Their principal rivals were competing Sahelian and Mande powers seeking control of trade revenues, armed desert groups from the north, and any rising forces that attempted to bypass the old Ghanaian trade nodes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持金盐贸易通道、确保撒哈拉商队安全、通过婚盟与馈赠巩固附庸、并在伊斯兰商人世界中保持信誉为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving the gold-salt corridors, ensuring security for trans-Saharan caravans, strengthening subordinate ties through marriage and gifts, and maintaining credibility within the Islamic commercial world.

era.zh: 中世纪萨赫勒贸易与早期伊斯兰化时代
era.en: Medieval Sahelian Trade and Early Islamization Era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于骆驼商队组织、沙漠补给知识、金粉衡量与长途交换制度、泥土与夯筑建筑传统、以及阿拉伯文书和伊斯兰法在商业中的应用。
keyInnovations.en: Key capabilities included camel-caravan organization, desert provisioning knowledge, systems for measuring gold dust and long-distance exchange, mud and banco building traditions, and the use of Arabic documentation and Islamic law in commerce.

strengths.zh: 仍控制西部苏丹重要贸易遗产与商路经验，拥有黄金来源腹地联系、灵活的地方联盟、成熟的商旅中介网络，以及连接撒哈拉与热带非洲的枢纽地位。
strengths.en: They still possessed the major commercial legacy and route knowledge of the western Sudan, links to gold-producing hinterlands, flexible local alliances, mature merchant-broker networks, and a strategic position linking the Sahara with tropical Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治分裂、王权碎片化、军力有限、对商路转向高度敏感，以及旧核心区在北方冲击与区域竞争后难以恢复统一主导地位。
weaknesses.en: Their greatest weaknesses were political fragmentation, splintered kingship, limited military concentration, high sensitivity to shifts in trade routes, and the difficulty of restoring unified supremacy in the old core after northern shocks and regional competition.

outlook.zh: 1200年前后，这些王国整体处于衰落与重组阶段。若无法重新整合黄金产区与萨赫勒商道，主导权将继续向新兴曼德势力和其他区域中心转移。
outlook.en: Around 1200, these kingdoms were in a phase of decline and restructuring. Unless they could re-integrate the gold zones and Sahelian trade arteries, leadership would continue to shift toward rising Mande powers and other regional centers.

description.zh: 古加纳帝国瓦解后，西苏丹地区由多支贸易型王国与部族势力分据。
description.en: After the fall of ancient Ghana, western Sudan was divided among multiple trade-based kingdoms and tribal powers.

governmentForm.zh: 封建君主制，王权正在强化
governmentForm.en: Feudal monarchy with a strengthening royal authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会以封建等级为核心，由国王、大诸侯、骑士、教士、城市市民与广大农民构成；教会与世俗贵族并列为上层支柱，农奴制在部分地区仍然存在，但北法与城市地区自由民和自治社团有所增长。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on a feudal hierarchy composed of the king, great vassals, knights, clergy, urban burghers, and the broad peasantry; the Church and secular nobility stood as parallel elite pillars, while serfdom remained in some regions, though free peasants and self-governing urban communities were growing in northern France and towns.

rulingClass.zh: 卡佩王室、王室直属官员、高级教士以及效忠王权的封建贵族
rulingClass.en: The Capetian royal house, royal officers, high clergy, and feudal nobles loyal to the crown

succession.zh: 卡佩王朝实行世袭君主制，原则上由男性嫡系继承；为巩固连续性，常在国王在世时确保王储地位获得承认。
succession.en: The Capetians practiced hereditary monarchy, in principle through the male dynastic line; to secure continuity, the heir's position was often firmly recognized during the reigning king's lifetime.

structure.zh: 1200年的法兰西仍是封建王国，但王室行政已明显扩展。国王通过宫廷、王室领地官署、巡回审判与财政管理加强对直属领地的控制，并逐步把影响力伸向诺曼底边境、奥尔良、法兰西岛及其他关键地区。许多大诸侯在法律上是国王的封臣，但实际上仍拥有相当自治权。
structure.en: In 1200, France remained a feudal kingdom, but royal administration had expanded markedly. The king strengthened control over the royal domain through the court, domain officials, itinerant justice, and fiscal management, while gradually extending influence toward Normandy's frontier, Orléanais, the Île-de-France, and other key regions. Many great princes were legally the king's vassals, yet in practice still retained considerable autonomy.

function.zh: 管理王室日常事务、宫廷礼仪、文书与对直属领地的总体协调，由总管、大臣、司膳官等高级官员构成。
function.en: Managed daily royal affairs, court ceremony, documentation, and overall coordination of the royal domain, through great officers such as the seneschal and other senior household officials.

function.zh: 征收王室领地收入、通行税、市场税、封建贡赋与司法罚金，保管国库并监督地方税收。
function.en: Collected revenues from the royal domain, tolls, market dues, feudal payments, and judicial fines, safeguarded the treasury, and supervised local revenue gathering.

function.zh: 负责王室法庭、上诉审理、特许状确认及派遣巡回法官，以扩大王权司法权。
function.en: Oversaw the royal court, appeals, confirmation of charters, and the dispatch of itinerant judges to expand royal jurisdiction.

function.zh: 由王室任命的巴伊与普雷沃管理直属领地，执行征税、司法、治安与军事召集。
function.en: Royal-appointed baillis and prévôts administered the royal domain, carrying out taxation, justice, policing, and military summons.

function.zh: 起草敕令、封赐文书、条约、司法文书与教会往来文件，并以御玺认证。
function.en: Drafted ordinances, grants, treaties, judicial documents, and correspondence with the Church, authenticating them with the royal seal.

localAdmin.zh: 地方统治呈双重结构：一方面，大量地区仍由公爵、伯爵、主教与修道院领主管理；另一方面，在王室领地内，普雷沃和巴伊逐渐形成较稳定的官僚监督网络。城市通过公社特许状获得一定自治，但其权力仍受王室或地方领主制约。
localAdmin.en: Local rule was dual in structure: many regions were still governed by dukes, counts, bishops, and abbey lords, while in the royal domain prévôts and baillis were gradually forming a more stable bureaucratic network of supervision. Towns gained limited self-government through communal charters, though their powers remained constrained by the crown or local lords.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以封建习惯法、教会法和王室司法并存。北法以习惯法为主，教会婚姻与道德案件由教会法庭裁决；王室法庭正逐步吸纳上诉案件，以“国王的和平”与最高司法权扩大权威。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined feudal customary law, canon law, and royal justice. Northern France relied chiefly on customary law, while ecclesiastical courts judged marriage and moral cases; the royal court was increasingly absorbing appeals, enlarging its authority through the king's peace and supreme jurisdiction.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自王室领地租赋、磨坊与炉灶等领主权收益、道路与桥梁通行税、市场税、司法罚金、封臣援助金及特殊时期的教会或战争筹措。全国统一直接税尚未形成，财政基础主要依托王室直属地产和封建义务。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from rents and dues on the royal domain, banal rights such as mills and ovens, road and bridge tolls, market taxes, judicial fines, feudal aids, and occasional extraordinary levies for church or war needs. A kingdom-wide regular direct tax did not yet exist; the fiscal base depended mainly on royal lands and feudal obligations.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为绝对主导信仰。教会深度影响政治合法性、教育、婚姻制度、节庆与社会伦理；修道院、主教座堂和朝圣活动广泛分布。对异端与非正统信仰的警惕正在上升。
religion.en: Roman Catholic Christianity was overwhelmingly dominant. The Church deeply shaped political legitimacy, education, marriage, festivals, and social morality; monasteries, cathedrals, and pilgrimage activity were widespread. Concern toward heresy and unorthodox belief was rising.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以拉丁基督教神学为核心，受教父传统、修道院学术与新兴经院哲学影响。巴黎正在成长为重要学术中心，辩证法、神学与法学研究日益活跃。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was centered on Latin Christian theology, shaped by patristic tradition, monastic scholarship, and emerging scholastic philosophy. Paris was growing into a major center of learning, with increasingly active study in dialectic, theology, and law.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写语言为拉丁文，文字采用拉丁字母；行政、教会、学术和正式文书多使用拉丁文，法语方言在口头生活与部分文学中广泛存在。
writingSystem.en: The principal written language was Latin in the Latin alphabet; administration, church, scholarship, and formal documents mostly used Latin, while Old French dialects were widespread in everyday speech and parts of literature.

culturalAchievements.zh: 哥特式建筑正在北法兴起，巴黎圣母院等大教堂工程体现新型拱顶与采光技术；宫廷文学、骑士文化、史诗传统与游吟诗风尚逐渐繁荣。大学雏形、抄写文化和修道院藏书推动知识传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Gothic architecture was rising in northern France, with cathedral works such as Notre-Dame of Paris displaying new vaulting and lighting techniques; courtly literature, chivalric culture, epic tradition, and lyric poetry were flourishing. Proto-university life, manuscript culture, and monastic libraries helped spread learning.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系罗曼语族；上层书面文化以拉丁语为主，民间与贵族日常使用古法语诸方言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Romance branch; elite written culture used Latin, while commoners and nobles in daily life spoke varieties of Old French

gdpDescription.zh: 法兰西王国在1200年前后是西欧人口最多、农业最具潜力的王国之一。经济以农业为根基，北方小麦、葡萄种植、畜牧、地方集市和河运贸易支撑总体繁荣。巴黎、鲁昂、奥尔良、兰斯等城市商业活动活跃，但整体仍属前工业农业经济。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200, the Kingdom of France was one of the most populous and agriculturally promising kingdoms in western Europe. Its economy rested on agriculture, with northern grain production, viticulture, livestock, local markets, and river trade supporting broad prosperity. Cities such as Paris, Rouen, Orléans, and Reims were commercially active, though the economy remained fundamentally pre-industrial and agrarian.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、葡萄酒生产、畜牧、羊毛与亚麻纺织、盐业、铁器锻造、建筑业、河运与地方市场贸易
mainIndustries.en: Cereal agriculture, wine production, livestock raising, wool and linen textiles, salt production, ironworking, construction, river transport, and local market trade

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、葡萄酒、盐、羊毛、皮革、木材、蜂蜡、金属制品、布匹与奢侈性进口香料、丝绸
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, salt, wool, leather, timber, beeswax, metal goods, cloth, and luxury imports such as spices and silk

name.zh: 德涅与里弗尔图尔努瓦体系

householdWealth.zh: 绝大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、牲畜、农具、谷物储备和家庭器具上；贵族与教会掌握大部分地产和剩余产品。城市商人和手工业者的流动财富正在增长，但总体规模仍不及地产精英。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in land-use rights, livestock, tools, grain stores, and domestic goods; nobles and the Church held much of the land and surplus. The movable wealth of urban merchants and artisans was growing, but remained smaller overall than that of landed elites.

unit.en: silver denier equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园经济与封建地租为基础，辅以城市市场、行会萌芽、区域性集市和河海贸易的混合经济。王室对全国经济的直接调控有限，但通过特许状、通行税和市场保护逐步扩大影响。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on manorial production and feudal rents, supplemented by urban markets, early guild structures, regional fairs, and river-sea commerce. Direct royal regulation of the whole economy was limited, but the crown was gradually expanding influence through charters, tolls, and market protection.

source.zh: 司法罚金与王室权利收入
封建援助与封臣义务折算
source.en: Feudal aids and commuted vassalic obligations

source.zh: 教会或非常征收与杂项
source.en: Ecclesiastical or extraordinary levies and miscellaneous

category.zh: 建筑工程与城市、道路维护
category.en: Construction and maintenance of towns and roads

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室国库规模不如后世集权国家稳定和庞大，但在腓力二世时期，随着王室领地扩张、文书管理改善和财政征收更有秩序，储备能力正在增强。资金多以银币、贵金属器物、储粮、债权及可动用封建资源形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was neither as stable nor as large as that of later centralized states, but under Philip II its reserve capacity was improving through the expansion of the royal domain, better record-keeping, and more orderly collection. Wealth existed in silver coin, precious objects, stored grain, claims, and mobilizable feudal resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以扩充王室领地收入、维护货币信誉、把司法和行政权转化为稳定财政来源为主。国王倾向谨慎积蓄与针对性开支，优先投入军事、要塞、道路和政治整合。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on enlarging revenues from the royal domain, preserving monetary credibility, and converting judicial and administrative authority into steadier income. The king favored cautious accumulation and targeted spending, prioritizing military needs, fortifications, roads, and political consolidation.

description.zh: 由王室直属骑士、伯爵和公爵封臣所提供的重骑兵核心，装备锁子甲、长枪、剑与盾，是战场突击主力。
description.en: The core heavy cavalry supplied by royal knights and the vassals of counts and dukes, equipped with mail, lance, sword, and shield, serving as the main shock arm in battle.

description.zh: 由领主征发的农民步兵、城镇民兵与随军役夫组成，武器质量不一，主要承担守城、行军支援与数量补充。
description.en: Composed of peasant infantry, town militias, and campaign servants raised by lords, with uneven armament, chiefly used for garrison duty, marching support, and numerical reinforcement.

description.zh: 分布于王室城堡、要塞和重点城市的驻军，包括受薪军士、弩手和专业守备人员，是王权日益依赖的较可靠军事力量。
description.en: Garrisons stationed in royal castles, fortresses, and key towns, including paid sergeants, crossbowmen, and professional defenders, forming a comparatively reliable force increasingly relied upon by the crown.

description.zh: 由获得特许的城市组织，在紧急时承担城防、护送和局部作战任务，忠诚度常与城市利益和王室关系有关。
description.en: Organized by chartered towns, these militias served in emergencies for urban defense, escort, and limited operations; their loyalty often depended on town interests and relations with the crown.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王腓力二世·奥古斯都
commanderInChief.en: King Philip II Augustus

technology.zh: 军事技术以高质量锁子甲、防御工事、攻城器械、弩和重骑兵战术为代表。石质城堡建设成熟，围城战在实际战争中比大会战更常见。后勤依赖封建动员、粮秣征发与河道运输。
technology.en: Military technology featured good-quality mail armor, fortifications, siege machinery, crossbows, and heavy cavalry tactics. Stone castle construction was mature, and sieges were more common in practice than great field battles. Logistics depended on feudal mobilization, requisitioning of provisions, and river transport.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自英格兰安茹帝国在法国本土的广大领地，尤其是诺曼底、安茹、阿基坦相关争端；此外，强大的法国内部诸侯若与外敌联合，也会威胁王权。弗兰德尔、神圣罗马帝国边境及教宗政治也构成次级压力。
threats.en: The principal threat came from the Angevin Empire of England and its vast continental holdings, especially disputes involving Normandy, Anjou, and Aquitaine; in addition, powerful internal French princes could endanger royal authority if aligned with foreign rivals. Flanders, the frontier with the Holy Roman Empire, and papal politics created secondary pressures.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 法兰西王国是当时拉丁西欧人口最庞大的政治体之一。人口主要分布于乡村农业地区，北法和塞纳—卢瓦尔流域尤为密集。人口增长推动垦殖、村庄扩张和市镇兴起。
populationDescription.en: The Kingdom of France was one of the most populous polities in Latin western Europe. Its people were overwhelmingly rural, with especially dense settlement in northern France and the Seine-Loire basins. Population growth encouraged land clearance, village expansion, and the rise of towns.

description.zh: 法国首都，欧洲启蒙运动中心
description.en: French capital, center of the European Enlightenment

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体可分为：王室与大贵族、高级教士、骑士与小贵族、修道院与教区神职人员、城市商人和手工业者、自由农、佃农、农奴及边缘贫民。身份、土地占有和与领主或教会的法律关系决定社会地位。
socialClasses.en: The social order may be broadly divided into the royal house and great nobles, high clergy, knights and lesser nobles, monastic and parish clergy, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, serfs, and marginal poor. Status was determined by rank, landholding, and legal relationship to lordship or church.

allies.zh: 法兰西与教廷总体保持合作关系，并在不同阶段争取佛兰德、香槟及部分法国内诸侯的支持；其海外十字军联系也使其与若干拉丁东方政权保持间接纽带。
allies.en: France maintained generally cooperative relations with the papacy and at times sought support from Flanders, Champagne, and certain internal princes; its crusading connections also gave it indirect ties with several Latin polities in the East.

enemies.zh: 最主要对手是安茹—诺曼英格兰王国及其在法国的封地网络；潜在敌手还包括不服从的强大封臣，以及在边境和低地地区与法王利益冲突的势力。
enemies.en: Its chief rival was the Angevin-Norman kingdom of England and its network of continental fiefs; potential enemies also included disobedient great vassals and powers on the frontiers and in the Low Countries whose interests clashed with the French crown.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策核心是削弱安茹帝国、扩大王室直属领地、利用封建法理挑战敌对诸侯，并通过婚姻、条约、封赐和教会支持来增强王权合法性。对外并无统一的全球性视野，而是以西欧封建均势、十字军声望和王朝利益为导向。
foreignPolicy.en: The core of foreign policy was to weaken the Angevin Empire, expand the royal domain, use feudal law against hostile princes, and strengthen royal legitimacy through marriage, treaties, grants, and church support. France had no unified global outlook; policy was guided instead by the feudal balance of western Europe, crusading prestige, and dynastic interests.

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪盛期
era.en: High Middle Ages of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 尖拱与肋拱顶推动哥特式建筑发展；重犁、三圃制和马具改良提高农业生产；石造城堡、弩与攻城技术趋于成熟；文书行政、封玺制度和城市特许状促进国家能力提升。
keyInnovations.en: Pointed arches and rib vaulting advanced Gothic architecture; the heavy plow, three-field rotation, and improved horse harnessing increased agricultural output; stone castles, crossbows, and siege techniques were mature; documentary administration, seal usage, and urban charters improved state capacity.

strengths.zh: 人口众多、农业资源丰富、巴黎及北方城市经济活跃、王室领地逐步扩张、腓力二世政治与军事能力强，且能够以法律和行政手段压制部分大诸侯。
strengths.en: It possessed a large population, rich agricultural resources, active Parisian and northern urban economies, an expanding royal domain, and a highly capable ruler in Philip II, who could restrain some great princes through law as well as war.

weaknesses.zh: 王国仍高度封建化，地方分权明显，南北差异较大，常备官僚和常备军规模有限；财政对王室领地依赖深，若遭遇长期战争或贵族联合反抗，国家动员能力仍受约束。
weaknesses.en: The kingdom remained highly feudal, with strong regional decentralization and marked north-south differences; its permanent bureaucracy and standing forces were limited. Finances depended heavily on the royal domain, so prolonged war or a coalition of rebellious nobles could still strain state mobilization.

outlook.zh: 1200年前后的法兰西前景总体乐观。若腓力二世继续击败安茹势力并把胜利转化为稳定行政控制，卡佩王朝将从名义上的封建宗主进一步成长为真正主导全国的王权核心。未来数十年，法兰西很可能成为西欧最强大的王国之一。
outlook.en: The outlook for France around 1200 was broadly favorable. If Philip II continued to defeat Angevin power and convert victory into stable administrative control, the Capetian dynasty would advance from a largely feudal overlordship toward a genuinely dominant royal center. In the coming decades, France was likely to become one of the most powerful kingdoms in western Europe.

description.zh: 卡佩王权持续增强的西欧强国，正逐步压制大封臣势力。
description.en: A rising western European kingdom under strengthening Capetian monarchy, gradually curbing great vassals.

name.zh: 德意志—意大利帝国共同体

governmentForm.zh: 选举君主制下的封建帝国联合体，由诸侯、公国、主教领地、帝国城市与意大利王国辖区构成
governmentForm.en: A feudal imperial composite under an elective monarchy, made up of princes, duchies, bishoprics, imperial cities, and the territories of the Kingdom of Italy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由高级贵族、教会神职人员、骑士与部长家臣、城市市民、自由农民、佃农与依附农组成；地区差异极大，莱茵与伦巴第城市化较高，而德意志内陆更偏向庄园封建结构
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around high nobility, church clergy, knights and ministeriales, urban burghers, free peasants, tenants, and dependent serfs; regional variation was strong, with the Rhineland and Lombardy more urbanized while the German interior remained more manorial and feudal

rulingClass.zh: 世俗诸侯、采邑伯爵、帝国主教与修道院长、王室家臣贵族，以及控制城市政务的地方精英
rulingClass.en: Secular princes, landed counts, imperial bishops and abbots, royal service nobility, and local urban elites who controlled civic government

succession.zh: 原则上由德意志诸侯选举罗马人的国王，再由教宗加冕为皇帝；1200年因霍亨斯陶芬的菲利普与韦尔夫的奥托四世并立而陷入王位继承内战
succession.en: In principle the King of the Romans was elected by German princes and later crowned emperor by the pope; in 1200 the succession was contested in a civil war between the Hohenstaufen Philip and the Welf claimant Otto IV

structure.zh: 帝国缺乏统一常设中央官僚，王权依赖巡行朝廷、宫廷官职、王室领地收入、诸侯支持与教会网络治理；意大利与德意志地区在制度上联系松散
structure.en: The empire lacked a unified permanent central bureaucracy; royal rule depended on an itinerant court, court offices, revenues from royal lands, princely support, and ecclesiastical networks, while Germany and Italy were only loosely integrated institutionally

function.zh: 起草敕令、确认特权、保存王室文书，并通过德意志、意大利与勃艮第相关书吏系统处理政务
function.en: Drafted diplomas, confirmed privileges, preserved royal records, and handled administration through German, Italian, and Burgundian clerical offices

name.zh: 宫廷财政与王室领地署
name.en: Court Treasury and Royal Demesne Office

function.zh: 管理王室地产、通行税、铸币收益、直属城市与临时征敛，协调王室收支
function.en: Managed royal lands, tolls, mint revenues, direct towns, and extraordinary levies, coordinating royal income and expenditure

function.zh: 处理贵族争端、封臣义务、王权诉讼与特权确认，作为帝国最高层级的巡回裁决机构之一
function.en: Handled noble disputes, feudal obligations, royal pleas, and privilege confirmations as one of the empire’s highest itinerant judicial bodies

function.zh: 发布召集令，协调诸侯、主教与城市的军役义务，组织护卫与围城行动
function.en: Issued summonses, coordinated military obligations of princes, bishops, and cities, and organized escorts and siege operations

function.zh: 处理主教选任争议、教会财产、修道院特许与王权和教宗关系中的教会事务
function.en: Handled episcopal election disputes, church property, monastic privileges, and ecclesiastical matters tied to royal-papal relations

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要由公爵、侯伯、伯爵、帝国主教、修道院长与自由城市自治机构承担；王权在各地通过采邑、城堡长官、代官与忠于王室的主教影响地方，但直接控制范围有限
localAdmin.en: Local administration was chiefly exercised by dukes, margraves, counts, imperial bishops, abbots, and self-governing free cities; royal authority influenced localities through fiefs, castellans, advocates, and loyal bishops, but direct control was limited

legalSystem.zh: 法律高度多元，由习惯法、封建法、教会法、城市法与王室裁决并存；1200年前后罗马法在意大利学术中心复兴，开始影响帝国法理，但尚未形成统一法典
legalSystem.en: The legal system was highly plural, combining customary law, feudal law, canon law, urban law, and royal judgment; revived Roman law at Italian scholarly centers was beginning to influence imperial jurisprudence, but no unified code yet existed

taxationSystem.zh: 帝国并无现代统一税制，财政依赖王室领地收益、通行税、铸币税、犹太社群特别贡赋、加冕或战争时期的临时征敛，以及诸侯和主教的军役与财政支援
taxationSystem.en: The empire had no modern unified tax regime; finances relied on royal demesne income, tolls, mint dues, special levies on Jewish communities, extraordinary exactions in wartime or for coronation, and military or fiscal support from princes and bishops

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教为主，服从罗马教会；修道院、主教座堂和圣徒崇拜在社会生活中核心地位显著，但王权与教宗因加冕权和主教任命传统余波持续紧张
religion.en: Latin Christianity predominated under obedience to the Roman Church; monasteries, cathedrals, and saint cults held central social importance, though royal-papal tension continued over coronation and the legacy of episcopal appointment struggles

philosophy.zh: 思想以拉丁基督教神学、教会法学、初期经院哲学和王权—教权论争为主；意大利法学中心推动罗马法研究，德意志修道院与主教学校则维系神学与编年史传统
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Latin Christian theology, canon law, early scholasticism, and debates on royal versus papal authority; Italian legal schools advanced Roman law studies while German monasteries and cathedral schools sustained theology and chronicle writing

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；正式文书和学术语言为拉丁语，地方社会广泛使用中古高地德语、中古低地德语、伦巴第与其他意大利地方罗曼语方言
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used; Latin dominated formal documents and scholarship, while Middle High German, Middle Low German, Lombard, and other Italian Romance vernaculars were widely spoken locally

culturalAchievements.zh: 修道院编年史、皇室与主教文书传统、罗曼式教堂建筑、城堡营建、骑士文学萌芽、法学教育与城市公社文化皆颇具特色；莱茵与意大利北部尤其活跃
culturalAchievements.en: Monastic chronicles, royal and episcopal diplomatic culture, Romanesque church architecture, castle building, the growth of courtly literature, legal learning, and urban communal culture were all notable, especially in the Rhineland and northern Italy

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系，以西日耳曼语支与意大利罗曼语支并存，拉丁语为跨区域书面与礼仪语言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, combining West Germanic and Italo-Romance speech communities, with Latin as the transregional written and liturgical language

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量在拉丁欧洲名列前茅，依托人口规模、莱茵流域与北意大利城市商业、阿尔卑斯交通线及农业腹地；但王位战争削弱了中央汲取能力，许多财富掌握在诸侯、教会与城市手中而非王室
gdpDescription.en: The overall economy ranked among the largest in Latin Europe, supported by population scale, the commerce of the Rhineland and northern Italian cities, Alpine transit routes, and broad agrarian hinterlands; however, the royal civil war weakened central extraction, and much wealth lay with princes, churches, and towns rather than the crown

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、葡萄酒、畜牧、林业、采盐、金属采掘与冶炼、手工业、纺织、铸币、跨阿尔卑斯贸易、河运与城市市场交换
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, viticulture, livestock, forestry, salt extraction, metal mining and smelting, crafts, textiles, minting, transalpine trade, river transport, and urban market exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 出口金属、盐、葡萄酒、木材、皮革、毛织品、奴役性劳动力残余贸易与中欧手工业品；进口香料、丝绸、染料、奢侈纺织品、地中海商品与东方珍贵货物
tradeGoods.en: Exports included metals, salt, wine, timber, leather, woolens, residual unfree human traffic in some zones, and Central European manufactures; imports included spices, silk, dyes, luxury textiles, Mediterranean goods, and precious wares from the East

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富极为有限，主要表现为土地使用权、牲畜、农具和储粮；城市商人与部分犹太放贷者、主教座堂团体和大修道院则拥有远高于平均水平的资产
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was modest and consisted mainly of land-use rights, livestock, tools, and stored grain; urban merchants, some Jewish moneylenders, cathedral chapters, and major monasteries held assets far above the average

economicSystem.zh: 以封建地租、庄园生产与地方市场为基础，同时在莱茵、法兰克尼亚、士瓦本和北意大利形成增长中的货币交换和城市商业网络，是封建与商业化并存的复合经济
economicSystem.en: The economy rested on feudal rents, manorial production, and local markets, while increasingly monetized and commercial urban networks grew in the Rhineland, Franconia, Swabia, and northern Italy, producing a mixed feudal-commercial system

source.zh: 通行税、关津与市场收入
教会与诸侯的特别资助
source.en: Extraordinary support from churchmen and princes

source.zh: 犹太社群贡赋与其他临时征敛
source.en: Levies on Jewish communities and other extraordinary exactions

category.zh: 赏赐、封臣维系与外交馈赠
category.en: Rewards, vassal maintenance, and diplomatic gifts

category.zh: 宗教捐献与教会关系经营
category.en: Religious patronage and management of church relations

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库规模不大且流动性紧张，更多表现为可迅速变现的银币、金银器、抵押品与王室可支配的教会或城市信用，而非稳定集中储备
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was modest and strained in liquidity, consisting less of a stable central reserve than of coin, precious objects, pledges, and church or urban credit that could be mobilized when needed

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向机会主义与人身统治，依靠巡行征收、特许换取资助、抵押王室权利、向城市和教会寻求贷款与支持；在内战背景下，长期财政整合能力有限
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was opportunistic and personal, relying on itinerant collection, privileges exchanged for support, pledging royal rights, and seeking loans and backing from towns and churches; long-term fiscal integration was weak under civil-war conditions

description.zh: 由诸侯、伯爵、主教和骑士封臣提供的重骑兵核心，是帝国野战军最具冲击力的部分，但服役期限有限，政治忠诚受派系影响极大
description.en: The heavy cavalry core supplied by princes, counts, bishops, and knightly vassals formed the empire’s most powerful field arm, though service terms were limited and political loyalty was heavily factional

description.zh: 由各领主召集的矛兵、盾兵、弩手和杂役步卒构成，用于围城、守城和地方作战，装备质量与训练水平差异明显
description.en: Raised by territorial lords, these spearmen, shield-bearers, crossbowmen, and supporting foot soldiers were used for sieges, garrisons, and local warfare, with very uneven equipment and training

description.zh: 来自自由城市与意大利公社的武装市民，善于守城、操持弩与巷战，北意大利城市军队尤其具有组织性
description.en: Armed townsmen from free cities and Italian communes, effective in city defense, crossbow use, and street fighting; the northern Italian urban forces were especially well organized

description.zh: 由王室家臣骑士、城堡守军和临时雇佣兵组成，是国王最可靠的直接兵力，但规模受财力限制
description.en: Composed of household knights, castle garrisons, and temporary mercenaries, these were the king’s most reliable direct forces, though their size was constrained by finance

commanderInChief.zh: 罗马人的国王士瓦本的菲利普
commanderInChief.en: Philip of Swabia, King of the Romans

technology.zh: 军备处于12世纪末拉丁欧洲典型水平，以锁子甲骑士、长枪冲击、弩、攻城塔、投石机与石砲为主；城堡网络密集，围城战比大会战更常见
technology.en: Military technology stood at a typical late twelfth-century Latin European level, centered on mail-armored knights, lance shock tactics, crossbows, siege towers, mangonels, and petraries; dense castle networks made sieges more common than set-piece battles

threats.zh: 最主要威胁是内战：支持奥托四世的韦尔夫派、倒向不同候选人的诸侯、以及教宗干预；外部则面临法国卡佩王权扩张、伦巴第城市自主、诺曼西西里与教宗政治压力，以及东部边境斯拉夫与匈牙利方向的局部冲突
threats.en: The greatest threat was internal civil war: the Welf faction backing Otto IV, princes aligning behind rival claimants, and papal intervention; externally there were pressures from Capetian France, Lombard urban autonomy, Norman Sicily and papal politics, plus localized conflict on eastern frontiers facing Slavic lands and Hungary

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 拜占庭海战秘密武器，水上不灭之火
description.en: Byzantine naval secret weapon, inextinguishable fire on water

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛，主体位于德意志诸公国、莱茵河流域、巴伐利亚、士瓦本、萨克森与北意大利平原；人口密度在意大利北部和莱茵地区较高，山区与边境地带较低
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across the German duchies, the Rhineland, Bavaria, Swabia, Saxony, and the north Italian plain; density was highest in northern Italy and the Rhineland and lower in mountain and frontier zones

socialClasses.zh: 上层为选侯级诸侯、大公、伯爵和高级教士；中层包括骑士、部长家臣、富裕市民、行会前身团体与低级神职人员；下层则为自由农、佃农、农奴、城市贫民与无地劳作者。犹太社群在若干城市中具有重要商业与金融角色，但地位脆弱
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of prince-elector level magnates, dukes, counts, and senior clergy; the middle included knights, ministeriales, wealthy burghers, proto-guild groups, and lesser clergy; the lower included free peasants, tenants, serfs, urban poor, and landless laborers. Jewish communities played important commercial and financial roles in some towns but remained vulnerable

allies.zh: 菲利普的支持者主要是霍亨斯陶芬家族盟友、部分莱茵与南德诸侯、若干帝国主教，以及在形势允许时倾向于与其合作的法国王室；但联盟结构极不稳定
allies.en: Philip’s supporters included Hohenstaufen allies, parts of the Rhineland and southern German princely elite, several imperial bishops, and at times a French monarchy inclined to cooperate with him; however, these alignments were highly unstable

enemies.zh: 直接敌手为韦尔夫派的奥托四世及其支持诸侯，政治对手还包括支持奥托的教宗英诺森三世、对王权控制保持警惕的伦巴第城市，以及在边境和阿尔卑斯通道上争利的邻近势力
enemies.en: The immediate enemy was the Welf claimant Otto IV and the princes supporting him; political adversaries also included Pope Innocent III, who favored Otto, Lombard cities wary of royal control, and neighboring powers competing over frontiers and Alpine routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以争取王位合法性、稳固德意志支持基础、争取意大利王权与寻求皇帝加冕前景为中心；在实际操作上，外交服从内战需要，优先分化诸侯并争取教会和外部君主承认
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing dynastic legitimacy, consolidating support in Germany, asserting claims in Italy, and preserving the prospect of imperial coronation; in practice diplomacy was subordinated to civil-war needs, with priority given to splitting princely coalitions and winning recognition from churchmen and outside monarchs

era.zh: 十字军时代盛期

keyInnovations.zh: 重型犁、三圃制扩展、水磨与风磨普及、石造城堡工程、罗曼式建筑技术、改良冶铁、弩与攻城器使用增多，以及经由意大利法学中心传播的罗马法学术复兴
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included wider use of the heavy plow, expansion of three-field rotation, diffusion of watermills and windmills, stone castle engineering, Romanesque construction techniques, improved ironworking, greater use of crossbows and siege engines, and the revival of Roman law scholarship through Italian legal centers

strengths.zh: 人口与资源基础雄厚，莱茵和北意大利商业发达，主教与修道院网络提供文化和行政支撑，诸侯与城市在需要时可动员庞大兵力，帝国名号仍具极高象征权威
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in a large population and resource base, the commerce of the Rhineland and northern Italy, ecclesiastical networks that supported culture and administration, the capacity of princes and towns to mobilize substantial manpower, and the still-potent symbolic prestige of the imperial title

weaknesses.zh: 中央权力碎片化、财政分散、无固定首都与常备官僚体系、法律和地方制度差异巨大，且1200年的王位争夺严重削弱了统治连续性与对外行动能力
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were fragmented central authority, dispersed finances, the lack of a fixed capital and standing bureaucracy, major legal and regional diversity, and the severe loss of governing continuity and external effectiveness caused by the royal succession struggle of 1200

outlook.zh: 短期前景取决于菲利普能否压倒奥托四世并与教宗达成某种妥协；若内战延续，诸侯和城市自治将进一步强化，帝国将更像一个松散共同体而非可集中统治的国家
outlook.en: The short-term outlook depended on whether Philip could overcome Otto IV and reach some accommodation with the papacy; if civil war continued, princely and urban autonomy would deepen further, and the empire would function ever more as a loose commonwealth rather than a centrally governable state

description.zh: 由诸侯、公国、主教领与自由城市组成的帝国结构，1200年正陷于王位争夺。
description.en: An imperial structure of princes, duchies, bishoprics, and free cities, in the midst of a royal succession struggle in 1200.

leader.zh: 弗里德里希二世（幼年）/ 士瓦本的菲利普

leaderTitle.zh: 西西里国王 / 罗马人的国王
leaderTitle.en: King of Sicily / King of the Romans

ideology.zh: 普世君主制、帝权至上、西西里与德意志联合统治、与教廷对抗
ideology.en: Universal monarchy claims, imperial supremacy, joint rule of Sicily and Germany, conflict with the papacy

relationship.zh: 霍亨斯陶芬王朝自弗里德里希一世（红胡子巴巴罗萨）以来致力于建立从北海到西西里的强大帝国。1200年时，亨利六世之子弗里德里希尚在幼年，被教宗英诺森三世监护于西西里；而亨利六世之弟菲利普在德意志与韦尔夫家族的奥托四世争夺王位。霍亨斯陶芬家族的长远愿景是统一德意志与意大利，这一野心将在弗里德里希二世手中达到顶峰。
relationship.en: The Hohenstaufen dynasty had striven since Frederick I Barbarossa to build a powerful empire from the North Sea to Sicily. In 1200, Henry VI's son Frederick was still a child under Pope Innocent III's guardianship in Sicily, while Henry's brother Philip contested the German throne against the Welf candidate Otto IV. The Hohenstaufen long-term vision was unifying Germany and Italy — an ambition that would reach its peak under Frederick II.

description.zh: 霍亨斯陶芬王朝是12-13世纪欧洲最具雄心的统治家族。自红胡子巴巴罗萨开始，该家族致力于建立横跨阿尔卑斯山南北的帝国。亨利六世通过与西西里诺曼王朝联姻，一度将这一梦想变为现实，但其1197年英年早逝立即引发继承危机。德意志诸侯分裂为支持菲利普和支持奥托的两个阵营。菲利普虽获得多数诸侯支持，但教宗英诺森三世的干预使局势更加复杂。年幼的弗里德里希——日后被称为"世界奇迹"（Stupor Mundi）——被监护于西西里，接受了独特的多元文化教育。霍亨斯陶芬家族同时控制德意志帝国与西西里王国的前景令教廷深感恐惧，因为这意味着教宗国将被南北夹击。1208年菲利普被暗杀后，奥托四世短暂统一帝国，但弗里德里希二世很快在教宗支持下复出，重新夺回霍亨斯陶芬的皇位，开启了中世纪最精彩的政治角力。
description.en: The Hohenstaufen dynasty was the most ambitious ruling family in 12th–13th century Europe. Beginning with Frederick Barbarossa, they strove to build an empire spanning both sides of the Alps. Henry VI briefly realized this dream through marriage to the Norman dynasty of Sicily, but his premature death in 1197 triggered an immediate succession crisis. German princes split between supporters of Philip and Otto. Though Philip had broader princely support, Pope Innocent III's intervention complicated matters. The young Frederick — later called 'Stupor Mundi' (Wonder of the World) — was raised in Sicily under guardianship, receiving a uniquely multicultural education. The prospect of the Hohenstaufen controlling both the German Empire and the Kingdom of Sicily terrified the papacy, as it meant the Papal States would be strategically encircled. After Philip's assassination in 1208, Otto IV briefly unified the empire, but Frederick II soon returned with papal support to reclaim the Hohenstaufen throne, launching one of the most spectacular political contests of the Middle Ages.

role.zh: 霍亨斯陶芬候选人，在内战中争夺帝位
role.en: Hohenstaufen candidate, contending for the throne in civil war

role.zh: 亨利六世之子，未来的弗里德里希二世"世界奇迹"
role.en: Son of Henry VI, the future Frederick II 'Stupor Mundi'

ideology.zh: 韦尔夫家族权益、教宗联盟、反霍亨斯陶芬、德意志内战
ideology.en: Welf family interests, papal alliance, opposition to Hohenstaufen, German civil war

relationship.zh: 韦尔夫家族的奥托四世在1198年被部分诸侯选为对立国王，与霍亨斯陶芬的菲利普争夺帝国控制权。奥托获得教宗英诺森三世的支持，在莱茵地区和北德意志拥有一批追随者。然而奥托的支持基础不如菲利普广泛，韦尔夫-霍亨斯陶芬之争实质上是德意志诸侯借机扩大自治权的政治博弈。
relationship.en: The Welf candidate Otto IV was elected anti-king by some princes in 1198, contesting imperial control with the Hohenstaufen Philip. Otto gained Pope Innocent III's backing and held followers in the Rhineland and northern Germany. However, Otto's support base was narrower than Philip's, and the Welf-Hohenstaufen struggle was essentially a political game in which German princes expanded their autonomy.

description.zh: 韦尔夫家族（又称归尔甫派）是霍亨斯陶芬王朝的世仇对手，这场家族恩怨可以追溯到12世纪中叶"狮子"亨利与红胡子巴巴罗萨的冲突。奥托四世是"狮子"亨利的幼子，母亲是英格兰金雀花王朝的玛蒂尔达公主，因此与英格兰王室有密切的血缘和政治联系。在1198年的双重选举中，奥托在科隆大主教的支持下于亚琛加冕，而菲利普则在美因茨另行加冕，形成了帝国历史上典型的双王对峙。教宗英诺森三世在权衡利弊后选择支持奥托，因为韦尔夫家族不像霍亨斯陶芬那样同时控制西西里，不会对教宗国构成战略威胁。然而奥托一旦真正获得帝位后就违背了对教宗的所有承诺，入侵意大利并威胁教宗领地——这反而促使英诺森三世转而支持年轻的弗里德里希二世。韦尔夫派的历史命运将在1214年的布汶战役中终结。
description.en: The Welf family (also known as the Guelphs) were the hereditary rivals of the Hohenstaufen, a feud traceable to the mid-12th century conflict between Henry the Lion and Frederick Barbarossa. Otto IV was the youngest son of Henry the Lion, with his mother being Matilda, an English Plantagenet princess, giving him close blood and political ties to the English crown. In the double election of 1198, Otto was crowned at Aachen with the Archbishop of Cologne's support while Philip was crowned separately at Mainz, creating a classic dual-kingship standoff. Pope Innocent III chose to support Otto after weighing the options, since the Welfs unlike the Hohenstaufen did not simultaneously control Sicily and would not strategically threaten the Papal States. However, once Otto actually gained the throne he broke all his promises to the pope, invading Italy and threatening papal territories — which in turn prompted Innocent to switch his support to the young Frederick II. The Welf cause would meet its historical end at the Battle of Bouvines in 1214.

role.zh: 韦尔夫家族候选人，教宗支持的皇帝人选
role.en: Welf candidate, the pope's chosen imperial claimant

role.zh: 为奥托加冕的关键教会诸侯
role.en: Key ecclesiastical prince who crowned Otto

ideology.zh: 教宗至上论、授职权争议遗产、教廷对帝国继承的裁定权、第四次十字军发起者
ideology.en: Papal supremacy, Investiture legacy, papal right to adjudicate imperial succession, Fourth Crusade sponsorship

relationship.zh: 教宗英诺森三世是中世纪最强势的教宗，他坚持教宗有权裁定帝国王位归属，通过《德意志的审议》一文详细论证了教会干预世俗权力的正当性。英诺森最初支持奥托四世对抗霍亨斯陶芬，同时发起第四次十字军并在欧洲范围内推行教会改革。教宗权力在他治下达到历史巅峰。
relationship.en: Pope Innocent III was the most powerful medieval pope, insisting on the papal right to adjudicate imperial succession and providing detailed canonical justification for ecclesiastical intervention in secular power through his Deliberatio treatise. Innocent initially supported Otto IV against the Hohenstaufen while simultaneously launching the Fourth Crusade and promoting church reform across Europe. Papal power reached its historical zenith under his pontificate.

description.zh: 英诺森三世（1198-1216年在位）将中世纪教宗权力推向历史巅峰。他系统阐述了教宗至上论——主张教宗作为基督在世间的代理人，有权审查和裁定一切涉及灵魂的事务，而王权的合法性归根到底也属于灵性范畴。在德意志王位争夺中，英诺森三世的立场关乎整个欧洲权力格局。他通过《德意志的审议》为自己的干预权做了详尽论证，最初支持奥托四世以防止霍亨斯陶芬同时控制德意志和西西里形成对教宗国的战略包围。同时，英诺森策划了第四次十字军（虽然其方向偏离至君士坦丁堡），召开了第四次拉特兰公会议推行大规模教会改革，批准了方济各会和多明我会的建立。教宗党的力量不限于罗马——在德意志北部和莱茵地区，亲教宗诸侯构成实质性军事力量；在意大利北部，伦巴第城市传统上支持教宗以对抗帝国直接统治。英诺森三世的遗产深刻影响了此后数百年的教俗关系。
description.en: Innocent III (r. 1198–1216) brought medieval papal power to its historical zenith. He systematically articulated papal supremacy — claiming that the Pope as Christ's vicar had authority over all matters touching the soul, and that royal legitimacy ultimately belonged to the spiritual sphere. In the German succession dispute, Innocent's stance affected the entire European power balance. Through his Deliberatio he provided detailed justification for papal intervention, initially supporting Otto IV to prevent the Hohenstaufen from controlling both Germany and Sicily, which would strategically encircle the Papal States. Simultaneously, Innocent launched the Fourth Crusade (though it veered toward Constantinople), convened the Fourth Lateran Council for sweeping church reform, and approved the founding of the Franciscan and Dominican orders. The papal party's strength extended beyond Rome — in northern Germany and the Rhineland, pro-papal princes constituted substantial military forces; in northern Italy, Lombard cities traditionally supported the papacy against direct imperial rule. Innocent III's legacy profoundly shaped church-state relations for centuries to come.

role.zh: 中世纪最强势的教宗，主张教权高于皇权
role.en: Most powerful medieval pope, asserting papal authority over imperial power

governmentForm.zh: 封建君主制，带有凯尔特部族传统、盎格鲁-诺曼行政实践与王室直辖领地相结合的混合体制
governmentForm.en: A feudal monarchy combining Gaelic kin-based traditions, Anglo-Norman administrative practices, and direct royal lordship

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、伯爵与高级教士组成的统治上层、骑士与地方领主、中小自由佃户、城镇市民、农民与依附劳役者构成；高地与低地在法律、语言与社会习俗上仍有明显差异
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the king, earls, and senior clergy at the top, followed by knights and local lords, lesser free tenants, burghers, peasants, and dependent laborers; marked differences remained between Highland and Lowland law, language, and custom

rulingClass.zh: 王室、盖尔伯爵家族、诺曼化贵族、主教与大修道院院长共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal house, Gaelic earldoms, increasingly Normanized nobility, bishops, and heads of major monasteries

succession.zh: 原则上实行父系世袭君主制，但仍受贵族承认、王室血统合法性与成年男性继承能力影响
succession.en: Succession was principally hereditary in the male line, though still shaped by noble acceptance, dynastic legitimacy, and the capacity of an adult male heir to rule

structure.zh: 1200年前后的苏格兰王权正趋向制度化。国王通过王室廷臣、巡回司法、城镇特许状、郡长与教会网络治理核心低地地区，而在高地和边疆则更多依赖伯爵、地方领主与部族首领的效忠与调停
structure.en: Around 1200 the Scottish kingship was becoming more institutionalized. The king governed the core Lowlands through royal household officers, itinerant justice, burgh charters, sheriffs, and church networks, while relying more on earls, local lords, and kindred leaders in the Highlands and frontier zones

function.zh: 负责国王日常起居、廷臣管理、文书发布、王室收入征收与宫廷随行行政
function.en: Handled the king's daily household, court personnel, issuance of writs, collection of royal income, and mobile court administration

function.zh: 通过郡长、法官与巡回法庭在王室控制地区执法、审案、执行罚金并维护王权
function.en: Used sheriffs, justiciars, and itinerant courts to enforce law, hear cases, collect amercements, and uphold royal authority in crown-controlled areas

function.zh: 管理王室庄园、市场税、王室林地、铸币收益、封臣应缴款项与临时征收
function.en: Managed royal estates, market dues, forest rights, mint income, feudal incidents, and occasional extraordinary levies

function.zh: 由受过教育的教士主导，负责拉丁文文书、土地确认、与主教区及修道院协调，并处理对外通信
function.en: Dominated by educated clerics, this office handled Latin charters, land confirmations, coordination with bishoprics and monasteries, and foreign correspondence

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以郡长辖区、伯爵领、教会辖区和王室特许城镇并存。低地与东部沿海地区的行政整合程度较高；高地、西部群岛与加洛韦等地则仍保留较强的地方自治和亲族政治
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined sheriffdoms, earldoms, ecclesiastical jurisdictions, and chartered burghs. Integration was stronger in the Lowlands and eastern seaboard; the Highlands, western seaways, and places such as Galloway retained substantial local autonomy and kin-based politics

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为混合结构：王室法、习惯法、盖尔布雷汉式传统、封建土地法与教会法并行。不同地区、身份和土地性质常决定案件适用何种法域
legalSystem.en: The legal system was mixed: royal law, customary law, Gaelic brehon-style traditions, feudal land law, and canon law coexisted. Region, social status, and the nature of the land often determined which legal sphere applied

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自王室领地收入、市场与渡口税、城镇关税、司法罚金、铸币利润、封建贡赋及偶发性的军事与王室需要征收。常规全国性直接税规模有限
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from royal demesne income, market and ferry dues, burgh customs, judicial fines, mint profits, feudal payments, and occasional levies for military or royal needs. Routine kingdom-wide direct taxation remained limited

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国家主导宗教。12世纪教会改革已深刻影响苏格兰，主教区体系、修道院网络与与罗马教廷的联系持续加强，同时地方圣徒崇拜和部分更古老的习俗仍然存在
religion.en: Roman Catholic Christianity was the dominant religion. Twelfth-century church reform had deeply reshaped Scotland, strengthening diocesan structures, monastic networks, and ties to Rome, while local saint cults and some older practices persisted

philosophy.zh: 思想基础主要是拉丁基督教神学、教会法观念、王权神授观与修道传统；在高地与群岛仍可见盖尔口传伦理、家族荣誉观和诗人传统的影响
philosophy.en: Intellectual life rested mainly on Latin Christian theology, canon-law concepts, sacral kingship, and monastic traditions; in the Highlands and Isles, Gaelic oral ethics, kin honor, and bardic culture remained influential

writingSystem.zh: 官方与教会文书主要使用拉丁字母，语言以拉丁文为主；法庭、贵族与地方社会同时使用盖尔语、早期中古苏格兰英语前身及诺曼法语影响的书写和口语环境
writingSystem.en: Official and ecclesiastical writing used the Latin alphabet, with Latin dominant in charters and records; courts, nobles, and local society also operated in Gaelic and early forms of Middle English, under some Norman French influence

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的重要文化成就在于城镇制度扩展、修道院建筑发展、圣安德鲁斯等教会中心的强化、特许状文书文化成熟，以及盖尔诗歌传统与新兴低地宫廷文化的并存
