culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements included the expansion of burgh institutions, the growth of monastic architecture, the strengthening of church centers such as St Andrews, the maturation of charter culture, and the coexistence of Gaelic poetic traditions with an emerging Lowland court culture

languageFamily.zh: 以凯尔特语族中的盖尔语为传统王国语言，同时在东南低地广泛使用日耳曼语族的早期苏格兰英语前身；拉丁文为教会与行政语言
languageFamily.en: The traditional language of the kingdom was Gaelic, a Celtic language, while early forms ancestral to Scots within the Germanic family were widespread in the southeastern Lowlands; Latin served as the language of church and administration

gdpDescription.zh: 苏格兰经济在1200年属中等规模的西北欧王国经济，人口不大但结构正在改善。农业、畜牧、渔业和地方贸易构成基础，东海岸港口与王室城镇推动了有限但增长中的商业化
gdpDescription.en: In 1200 Scotland had a middling northwestern European kingdom economy: not large in population, but gradually improving in structure. Agriculture, pastoralism, fishing, and local exchange formed the base, while east-coast ports and royal burghs encouraged limited but growing commercialization

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧养羊养牛、渔业、林产、盐业、皮革、羊毛初加工、木材与地方手工业是主要产业
mainIndustries.en: The main industries were grain cultivation, cattle and sheep raising, fishing, woodland products, salt production, leatherworking, early wool processing, timber, and local crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以羊毛、兽皮、牛、咸鱼、盐、皮革和少量木材为主；进口包括葡萄酒、精制布匹、金属器、奢侈品、教会用品与部分骑士装备
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on wool, hides, cattle, salted fish, salt, leather, and some timber; imports included wine, finished cloth, metal goods, luxuries, ecclesiastical items, and some knightly equipment

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富低且以牲畜、谷物储备、农具和土地使用权体现。低地自由佃户与城镇商人财富高于高地普通农户；贵族和教会拥有集中度很高的不动产与实物财富
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was modest and expressed in livestock, grain reserves, tools, and access to land. Lowland free tenants and burgh merchants were wealthier than ordinary Highland cultivators, while nobles and the church held highly concentrated landed and material wealth

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度以庄园和封建义务为框架，但王室特许城镇、货币交换、跨海贸易与教会地产经营正在推动更强的市场整合
economicSystem.en: The economic system remained framed by manorial and feudal obligations, but royal burghs, monetary exchange, overseas trade, and ecclesiastical estate management were promoting stronger market integration

source.zh: 铸币、渡口、林地及杂项收入
source.en: Mint, ferries, forests, and miscellaneous revenues

treasuryDescription.zh: 苏格兰王室并无近代意义上的集中现金国库，储备由现银、铸币、贵重器物、王室物资和可迅速征调的封建资源共同构成。稳定时期可维持有限盈余，但对战争和继承危机的抗压能力不算强
treasuryDescription.en: The Scottish crown did not possess a modern centralized cash treasury; reserves consisted of silver coin, valuables, royal stores, and feudal resources that could be mobilized quickly. In stable times the crown could maintain a modest surplus, but its resilience against major war or succession crisis was limited

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重在维持王室巡幸、奖赏封臣、扩大城镇税基并避免激起地方贵族反弹。通过发展王室城镇与稳定司法收益来缓慢增强财政能力
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, aiming to sustain the itinerant monarchy, reward vassals, broaden the burgh tax base, and avoid provoking noble resistance. The crown gradually strengthened its finances through royal burgh development and more regular judicial revenues

name.en: Royal Household Troops and Standing Knights

description.zh: 包括国王亲军、随从骑士、城堡守军和部分雇佣兵，是王权最可靠的军事核心，但规模有限
description.en: Included the king's household men, retinue knights, castle garrisons, and some hired troops; this was the most dependable military core of royal power, but small in size

description.zh: 由伯爵、男爵和诺曼化贵族按封建义务提供，装备优于普通步兵，在边境作战和快速突袭中尤为重要
description.en: Supplied by earls, barons, and Normanized nobles under feudal obligation, these troops were better equipped than common infantry and especially useful in border warfare and rapid raids

description.zh: 主要由低地农民、城镇民兵和部分自由佃户组成，使用长矛、盾牌、短弓和斧枪，适合守备与集结作战
description.en: Drawn mainly from Lowland peasants, burgh militias, and some free tenants, they used spears, shields, short bows, and polearms and were suited to defense and mass mustering

description.zh: 来自高地亲族势力和西部海区，机动性较强，擅长崎岖地形中的袭扰、侦察和局部冲突
description.en: Raised from Highland kindreds and western seaboard regions, these forces were more mobile and effective in raiding, reconnaissance, and rough-terrain fighting

commanderInChief.zh: 国王威廉一世，实际指挥由王室大臣、伯爵与地方领主分担
commanderInChief.en: King William I, with practical command shared among royal officers, earls, and local lords

technology.zh: 军事技术处于12世纪末西北欧水平，使用封建重骑兵、木石城堡、锁子甲、长矛步兵、弓箭和攻城器械，但尚未形成高度专业化常备军
technology.en: Military technology matched late twelfth-century northwestern European standards, using feudal heavy cavalry, timber-and-stone castles, mail armor, spear infantry, bows, and siege engines, though without a highly professional permanent army

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与英格兰王权及安茹体系相关的南部边境压力、边境领主冲突、加洛韦和高地地方势力的离心倾向，以及西部海域的挪威影响
threats.en: The main threats came from southern border pressure linked to the English crown and the Angevin realm, conflicts involving frontier lordships, centrifugal tendencies in Galloway and the Highlands, and Norwegian influence across the western seas

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 1200年的苏格兰人口约五十万，集中于福斯河以北东部低地、泰河流域、洛锡安、法夫、安格斯、马恩斯和若干沿海农业区。高地、群岛与边远西部人口稀疏但具有强烈地方认同
populationDescription.en: Scotland's population around 1200 was about 500,000, concentrated in the eastern Lowlands north of the Forth, the Tay basin, Lothian, Fife, Angus, the Mearns, and certain coastal farming districts. The Highlands, Isles, and remote west were thinner in population but strongly locally rooted

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、伯爵、男爵和高级教士；中层包括骑士、小地主、教区神职与城镇商人；底层为自由佃户、农民、牧民、劳役者及在部分地区受依附约束的农村人口。高地亲族纽带与低地封建地权体系并存
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, earls, barons, and senior clergy; the middle layers included knights, lesser landholders, parish clergy, and burgh merchants; the lower strata were free tenants, peasants, herdsmen, laborers, and in some areas more dependent rural populations. Highland kinship structures coexisted with Lowland feudal landholding

allies.zh: 苏格兰没有固定意义上的稳定大战略盟友，但与法国王国保持潜在友好，与教廷和英格兰北方若干贵族维持务实关系；亦通过婚姻和教会网络与挪威、爱尔兰海区域势力接触
allies.en: Scotland lacked a permanent grand-strategic ally, but maintained potentially friendly ties with the French kingdom and pragmatic relations with the papacy and certain northern English nobles; it also engaged through marriage and church networks with Norway and Irish Sea powers

enemies.zh: 最主要的外部对手是英格兰王国及安茹王朝主导的政治压力，尤其体现在边境、宗主权争议和北英格兰旧有权利问题上
enemies.en: The principal external rival was the Kingdom of England and the political pressure of the Angevin dynasty, especially in border affairs, overlordship disputes, and claims connected to northern England

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护王国独立、避免被英格兰完全控制、通过婚姻与封建谈判稳住边界、并借教廷与欧洲外交体系提升王权合法性为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving the kingdom's independence, avoiding full subordination to England, stabilizing borders through marriage and feudal negotiation, and enhancing royal legitimacy through the papacy and wider European diplomacy

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键发展包括石质城堡与防御工事扩展、王室城镇制度、较成熟的铸币、拉丁文特许状行政、改良犁耕和风水磨利用，以及修道院带来的农业和建筑技术传播
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included the spread of stone castles and fortifications, the royal burgh system, more regular coinage, Latin charter administration, improved plough agriculture and use of mills, and the diffusion of agricultural and building techniques through monasteries

strengths.zh: 苏格兰的优势在于王权在12世纪获得明显强化，城镇和郡长体系正逐步成形，教会改革提升了制度整合度，东部农业核心区和海运联系支撑了稳定财政与王国认同
strengths.en: Scotland's strengths lay in the marked strengthening of kingship during the twelfth century, the gradual formation of burgh and sheriff systems, improved institutional cohesion through church reform, and the support provided by the eastern agrarian core and maritime connections to royal finance and kingdom identity

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口和财政基础有限，地区差异显著，高地、加洛韦与西部海区整合不完全，军队依赖临时征召，且对英格兰强邻的战略回旋空间较小
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a limited population and fiscal base, strong regional disparities, incomplete integration of the Highlands, Galloway, and western seaways, dependence on temporary levies, and restricted strategic room for maneuver against the far stronger English neighbor

outlook.zh: 若王室继续巩固城镇、司法和王室领地管理，苏格兰在13世纪初仍有望保持稳定并增强国家结构；但一旦出现王位继承危机或英格兰加大干预，王国脆弱性将迅速暴露
outlook.en: If the crown continued to strengthen burghs, justice, and demesne management, Scotland could remain stable and further consolidate its state structures in the early thirteenth century; however, any succession crisis or intensified English intervention would quickly expose the kingdom's fragility

description.zh: 逐步整合高地与低地的北不列颠王国，与英格兰保持复杂关系。
description.en: A northern British kingdom increasingly integrating Highlands and Lowlands while managing complex relations with England.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，结合王室廷臣、教会主教与地方贵族的协商统治
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy combining royal household rule with consultation among bishops, magnates, and local nobles

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与大贵族、主教及教士、地方武士与自由农民、城镇商人手工业者以及少量奴婢和依附民组成；自由农民在北欧社会中的比重仍较高，但贵族与教会土地权力正在扩张。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and great nobles, bishops and clergy, local warriors and free peasants, urban merchants and artisans, and a smaller stratum of thralls and dependents; free peasants still formed an important part of Nordic society, though noble and ecclesiastical landholding was expanding.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王室亲信、大地主贵族、主教团与高级教会人士
rulingClass.en: The king, royal retainers, major landed nobles, bishops, and senior churchmen

succession.zh: 名义上为王朝世袭，实际上仍需通过贵族与地方议会的承认与拥戴；1200年前后克努特六世正试图强化父子继承原则。
succession.en: Nominally dynastic hereditary succession, but in practice requiring recognition and acclamation by magnates and regional assemblies; around 1200 Canute VI was working to strengthen father-to-son succession.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕国王、王室宫廷、巡回廷臣和主教体系运作，尚未形成高度文书化官僚国家；地方治理依托各地区议会、王室郡长、贵族领主与教会机构。
structure.en: Central government operated through the king, royal household, itinerant court, and episcopal network, not yet as a highly bureaucratized documentary state; local governance relied on regional assemblies, royal sheriffs, noble lords, and church institutions.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、王室收支、赦令发布和国王巡行后勤
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, royal income and outlays, issuance of charters, and logistics of the king's progresses

function.zh: 通过地方议会、王室法官和廷臣维持法律秩序，裁定土地、继承、伤害与王权案件
function.en: Maintained legal order through regional assemblies, royal judges, and court officers, deciding land, inheritance, injury, and royal pleas

function.zh: 协调主教区、教会法事务、什一税权益及修道院和主教团与王权的关系
function.en: Coordinated dioceses, canon-law matters, tithe rights, and relations between the crown and monasteries and bishops

function.zh: 组织舰队征召、海岸防务、对波罗的海远征的补给与兵役义务登记
function.en: Organized fleet levies, coastal defense, logistics for Baltic expeditions, and registration of military obligations

function.zh: 监督郡长、征收王室地租、罚金、市场税、港口税及特别征发
function.en: Supervised sheriffs, collecting rents from crown lands, fines, market dues, harbor tolls, and extraordinary levies

localAdmin.zh: 地方上以各地区的议会传统、郡与百户区组织为基础，由王室任命或认可的郡长、地方贵族和主教共同发挥作用。斯科讷、西兰、日德兰等地保持较强地域性，王权依靠巡行和誓约维系控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on regional assembly traditions and district organization, with royal sheriffs, local nobles, and bishops acting together. Scania, Zealand, and Jutland retained strong regional identities, and royal authority depended on progresses and sworn loyalty.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以地方习惯法为核心，辅以王室裁决和教会法影响。重要案件在地区议会审理，土地、婚姻和继承事务中教会法院权威日增。成文法汇编尚在发展阶段，但法的地方性很强。
legalSystem.en: Law was based primarily on regional customary law, supplemented by royal judgment and growing canon-law influence. Major cases were heard in regional assemblies, while church courts increasingly shaped marriage, inheritance, and clerical matters. Written law collections were still developing, and law remained strongly regional.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自王室地产、港口与市场税、罚金、通行费、贡赋以及战争时期的特别征发；教会什一税主要归教会，但王权常与主教共享部分资源与政治收益。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from crown estates, harbor and market dues, fines, tolls, tribute, and extraordinary wartime levies; ecclesiastical tithes belonged chiefly to the church, though the crown often shared in associated resources and political advantage.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教，属罗马天主教体系；基督教化已基本完成，但部分乡村习俗仍保留前基督教北欧传统痕迹。
religion.en: Latin Christianity within the Roman Catholic system; Christianization was largely complete, though some rural customs still preserved traces of pre-Christian Nordic tradition.

philosophy.zh: 思想以基督教神学、教会伦理、王权神授观与贵族荣誉文化为主，兼具北欧习惯法传统和骑士精神的早期影响。
philosophy.en: Thought was shaped chiefly by Christian theology, ecclesiastical morality, sacral kingship, and aristocratic honor culture, together with Nordic customary-law traditions and early chivalric influences.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系为拉丁字母，行政、教会和外交文书多用拉丁文；古诺斯语和早期丹麦语在口语和部分地方传统中使用，符文字母已退居边缘。
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Latin alphabet, with Latin used in ecclesiastical, diplomatic, and many administrative documents; Old Norse and early Danish were used in speech and some local traditions, while runes had become marginal.

culturalAchievements.zh: 12世纪末至13世纪初丹麦教会建筑、石砌教堂、王室赞助的修道院网络和历史编纂显著发展。与萨克森并列的重要文化成果包括拉丁文史学、圣徒崇拜、教堂壁画、金属工艺与波罗的海征服意识形态的塑造。
culturalAchievements.en: In the late twelfth and early thirteenth centuries Denmark saw notable growth in church architecture, stone-built churches, royally supported monastic networks, and historical writing. Important cultural achievements included Latin historiography, saints' cults, church mural traditions, metalwork, and the ideological framing of Baltic expansion.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支
languageFamily.en: North Germanic

gdpDescription.zh: 丹麦在1200年前后属于欧洲北部较为兴盛的中等规模经济体，农业基础稳固，海运与渔业发达，并通过控制海峡与波罗的海航路获取额外收益。其经济虽远不及宋朝等大型文明，但在北欧与南波罗的海地区具有明显优势。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200 Denmark was a prosperous medium-sized northern European economy with a solid agricultural base, strong seafaring and fisheries, and added gains from controlling straits and Baltic routes. Though far smaller than major civilizations such as Song China, it enjoyed a clear advantage within Scandinavia and the southern Baltic.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与畜牧业、渔业、航运、造船、木材与焦油生产、盐渍鱼贸易、手工业、港口商业、战争掠夺与贡赋收入
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming and livestock raising, fishing, shipping, shipbuilding, timber and tar production, salted fish trade, crafts, port commerce, and income from war booty and tribute

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牛马、皮革、黄油、奶酪、鱼类、木材、焦油、蜂蜡、皮毛、琥珀、铁制品，以及来自南方的葡萄酒、织物、金属器和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cattle and horses, hides, butter, cheese, fish, timber, tar, beeswax, furs, amber, iron goods, and from the south wine, textiles, metalwares, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、农具、船只份额和储粮。自由农拥有一定独立性，但社会差异扩大，贵族和教会掌握更大庄园、磨坊、森林和征收权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was chiefly embodied in land-use rights, livestock, tools, shares in boats, and stored grain. Free peasants retained some independence, but inequality was widening as nobles and the church accumulated larger estates, mills, forests, and rights of exaction.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为主的封建化经济，辅以海洋贸易、市场交换和王室控制的重要港口税收；货币流通正在扩大，但实物地租和劳役仍占重要地位。
economicSystem.en: A predominantly agrarian, increasingly feudal economy supplemented by maritime trade, market exchange, and crown-controlled port revenues; monetization was expanding, but rents in kind and labor dues remained important.

source.zh: 港口税、市场税与海峡通行收益
source.en: Harbor dues, market tolls, and strait transit income

source.zh: 贡赋、属地与波罗的海征服收益
source.en: Tribute, dependencies, and Baltic conquest revenues

source.zh: 特别征发与封臣义务折现
source.en: Extraordinary levies and commuted feudal obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银币、银锭、教会寄存财富、战利品和贵重器物构成，并分散保存在王室据点、主教座堂与可信贵族网络中。其规模足以支持数次远征，但仍受农业歉收和政治动员能力限制。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of coin, bullion, church-deposited wealth, booty, and precious objects, held in dispersed royal centers, cathedrals, and trusted noble networks. It was sufficient to support several expeditions, though still constrained by harvest failure and the limits of political mobilization.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持王室地产收益、扩大港口和海峡征收、在战争时进行临时征发，并通过与教会合作提升征收合法性。王权重视储备，以支撑波罗的海军事行动和王朝稳定。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining demesne revenues, expanding port and strait dues, imposing temporary wartime levies, and enhancing legitimacy through cooperation with the church. The crown valued reserves to support Baltic campaigns and dynastic stability.

name.en: Royal Household Troops and Heavy Cavalry

description.zh: 由国王侍从、贵族骑士和精锐武士组成，是王权的核心打击力量，适于陆上快速作战与指挥盟军。
description.en: Composed of the king's retainers, noble knights, and elite warriors, this was the core striking force of royal power for rapid land campaigning and command over allied contingents.

description.zh: 来自自由农民与地方社群，装备以矛、盾、斧和弓为主，是舰队登岸作战和本土防御的主体。
description.en: Drawn from free peasants and local communities, armed mainly with spears, shields, axes, and bows, they formed the main body for fleet-borne landings and homeland defense.

description.zh: 依托传统北欧船队征召制度，负责运输、海岸封锁、劫掠和波罗的海远征，是丹麦扩张的关键力量。
description.en: Raised through traditional Nordic fleet-levy institutions, they handled transport, coastal blockade, raiding, and Baltic expeditions, making them central to Danish expansion.

description.zh: 部署于南部边境、港口据点与新近控制区域，负责警戒、守备与维护王室威信。
description.en: Stationed on the southern frontier, in ports, and in recently controlled areas, they provided watch, garrison duty, and visible royal authority.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王克努特六世
commanderInChief.en: King Canute VI

technology.zh: 丹麦军力建立在成熟的中世纪北欧海军传统、长船与较大型运输船、锁子甲、长矛、战斧、剑、弓和早期石木城防之上。与德意志和法兰西世界接触使骑士化和攻城技术逐渐增强。
technology.en: Danish military power rested on mature medieval Nordic naval traditions, longships and larger transports, mail armor, spears, battle-axes, swords, bows, and early timber-and-stone fortification. Contact with the German and French worlds gradually strengthened knightly warfare and siegecraft.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方的德意志诸侯与边境竞争、波罗的海沿岸斯拉夫和异教部族的反抗、瑞典与挪威的潜在竞争，以及海上补给线过长带来的远征风险。
threats.en: Major threats came from German princes and border rivalry in the south, resistance by Slavic and pagan peoples along the Baltic coast, potential competition from Sweden and Norway, and the risks of overextended maritime supply lines.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气尚可，常规水平
description.en: Adequate morale, average cohesion

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

populationDescription.zh: 人口约七十万，分布于日德兰、西兰、菲英、斯科讷及周边岛屿。农业聚落密集于肥沃地区，沿海和海峡附近城镇与贸易据点较为活跃。人口总体稳步增长，受益于相对稳定的王权与贸易扩张。
populationDescription.en: The population of about 700,000 was distributed across Jutland, Zealand, Funen, Scania, and surrounding islands. Agricultural settlement was dense in fertile regions, while towns and trade sites were active along coasts and straits. Population was generally growing steadily, benefiting from relative political stability and commercial expansion.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、伯爵和大地主贵族及高级教士；中层包括骑士、小地主、富裕农民、商人和手工业者；下层包括普通自由农、依附民、佣工和残余奴婢人口。总体而言，自由农传统仍强于许多西欧地区。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, counts and major landed nobles, and senior clergy; the middle included knights, lesser landholders, prosperous peasants, merchants, and artisans; the lower included ordinary free peasants, dependents, laborers, and a residual thrall population. Overall, the tradition of free peasantry remained stronger than in many parts of western Europe.

allies.zh: 与教廷关系总体良好，并在若干时期与德意志北部诸侯、波罗的海传教势力及部分斯拉夫附庸合作；对外政策常借助教会支持以正当化东向扩张。
allies.en: Relations with the papacy were generally good, and in some periods Denmark cooperated with northern German princes, Baltic missionary forces, and certain Slavic dependents; foreign policy often relied on church backing to legitimize eastward expansion.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括南波罗的海的异教部族、反丹麦的斯拉夫势力、部分德意志竞争者，以及在北欧事务中与丹麦争衡的瑞典和挪威。
enemies.en: Principal opponents included pagan tribes of the southern Baltic, anti-Danish Slavic powers, some German rivals, and Sweden and Norway when competing with Denmark in Nordic affairs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以控制波罗的海西部和南岸、巩固海峡霸权、借十字军意识形态推进征服、并通过婚姻与教会联盟稳固王室地位为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the western and southern Baltic, consolidating dominance over the straits, advancing conquest under crusading ideology, and securing the dynasty through marriage ties and ecclesiastical alliances.

era.zh: 十字军时代盛期的中世纪北欧
era.en: High Medieval Nordic world of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 改良海船与运输能力、石砌教堂和要塞建筑、拉丁文书和王室印玺使用扩大、较成熟的铸币、重犁和三圃制在适宜地区推广，以及骑士装备和攻城术的引入。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included improved seagoing and transport vessels, stone church and fortress construction, expanded use of Latin documentation and royal seals, more mature coinage, the spread of the heavy plow and three-field methods where suitable, and the adoption of knightly equipment and siege techniques.

strengths.zh: 丹麦的优势在于稳固的海权、较高的区域整合度、自由农和贵族共同提供的军事动员能力、与教会合作带来的合法性，以及对波罗的海贸易与征服收益的把握。
strengths.en: Denmark's strengths lay in solid seapower, relatively high regional integration, military mobilization supported by both free peasants and nobles, legitimacy gained through cooperation with the church, and its command of Baltic trade and conquest revenues.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于中央官僚化程度有限、地方性强、王位继承仍可能引发贵族争斗、财政基础依赖地产与临时征发、远征控制成本高，且人口与资源规模无法长期支撑大陆级战争。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited central bureaucratization, strong regionalism, succession still prone to aristocratic conflict, a fiscal base dependent on land and extraordinary levies, the high cost of holding overseas conquests, and a population and resource base too small for sustained continental-scale war.

outlook.zh: 1200年的丹麦正处于上升阶段，若王室能维持与教会及贵族的平衡，并继续控制海峡与南波罗的海沿岸，其影响力仍可扩大；但一旦继承危机、地区离心或外部反击加剧，其扩张成果也可能迅速受损。
outlook.en: In 1200 Denmark was in an ascending phase; if the crown could preserve its balance with church and nobility while maintaining control of the straits and southern Baltic shore, its influence could grow further. Yet succession crisis, regional fragmentation, or stronger external resistance could quickly erode those gains.

description.zh: 波罗的海地区强势扩张的北欧王国，对南岸斯拉夫地区影响显著。
description.en: A strong Nordic kingdom expanding in the Baltic, exerting significant influence over the southern shores.

governmentForm.zh: 以封建君主制为核心、辅以王室文书行政与郡县司法体系的王国
governmentForm.en: A feudal monarchy centered on royal authority, supported by chancery administration and a shire-based judicial system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、伯爵与男爵等封建贵族、主教与修道院长等高级教士、骑士与自由佃农、城市商人与手工业者，以及大量农奴和半自由农民构成；诺曼—法兰西统治精英居上层，盎格鲁-英格兰主体人口构成基层社会。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the king, feudal nobles such as earls and barons, senior clergy including bishops and abbots, knights and free tenants, urban merchants and artisans, and a large mass of villeins and semi-free peasants; a Norman-French ruling elite dominated the upper strata over a predominantly English rural population.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王室官僚、诺曼与安茹贵族、主教团及大修道院长
rulingClass.en: The king, royal administrators, Norman and Angevin nobility, the episcopate, and major abbots

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，原则上依男性长子继承，但需贵族承认并常受王朝内部争夺影响
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy, in principle by male primogeniture, though requiring noble recognition and often affected by dynastic contest

structure.zh: 1200年的英格兰王国已形成相对成熟的王室治理结构：国王通过宫廷、财政署、文书署、司法机构与郡长体系行使权力。大贵族仍拥有地方领主权，但王权通过巡回法庭、财政稽核和王令网络不断深入地方。国王议事依赖御前会议，由主教、伯爵、男爵及高级王室官员组成。
structure.en: By 1200 the kingdom of England had developed a comparatively mature royal governing structure: the king ruled through the household, Exchequer, Chancery, judicial institutions, and sheriffs. Great magnates still possessed local lordship, but royal power increasingly penetrated the realm through eyre courts, fiscal audits, and networks of writs. Deliberation centered on the king's council, composed of bishops, earls, barons, and senior royal officers.

function.zh: 征收与核算王室收入，审核郡长账目，管理封建税、王室地产收入、司法罚金与特别征收
function.en: Collected and audited royal revenues, examined sheriffs' accounts, and managed feudal dues, royal demesne income, judicial fines, and extraordinary levies

function.zh: 起草、加盖与颁发王令、特许状和土地文书，维持中央书面行政
function.en: Drafted, sealed, and issued royal writs, charters, and land instruments, sustaining written central administration

function.zh: 处理重要诉讼、王室权益案件及高级上诉，协调巡回法官的司法活动
function.en: Handled major pleas, cases involving royal rights, and high appeals, while coordinating the work of itinerant justices

function.zh: 负责国王日常宫廷运作、差旅、供给、侍从、军需与私人财务
function.en: Managed the king's daily court operations, travel, provisioning, attendants, logistics, and private finances

function.zh: 在各郡执行王令、征税、召集民兵、主持郡法院并维护王室治安
function.en: Enforced royal writs in the shires, collected taxes, mustered local forces, presided over county courts, and maintained the king's peace

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以郡为核心，由郡长主持，辅以百户区、郡法院、庄园法庭和主教区网络。王室通过巡回法官和文书命令制衡地方贵族。城市如伦敦、约克、温彻斯特和诺里奇则拥有一定自治特权，由市民团体和地方官共同管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on the shire under the sheriff, supported by hundreds, county courts, manorial courts, and diocesan networks. The Crown balanced local magnates through itinerant justices and written writ procedure. Towns such as London, York, Winchester, and Norwich possessed varying municipal privileges and were governed jointly by civic bodies and local officers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以普通法的早期形态、国王和平观念、封建习惯法和教会法并存。王室法庭通过巡回审判和令状程序扩大管辖，陪审呈报与证据调查逐渐成形。教会法院处理婚姻、遗嘱和教士案件。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined the early common law, the king's peace, feudal customary law, and canon law. Royal courts expanded jurisdiction through eyres and writ procedure, while jury presentment and evidentiary inquiry continued to develop. Ecclesiastical courts handled marriage, testamentary, and clerical matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以王室地产收入、郡长上缴、封建役税替代金、犹太社群特别课税、关市税、司法罚金和临时徭赋为主。遇到大陆战争或赎买军事义务时，国王会征收盾牌钱等特别税。教会也可被要求提供非常贡赋，但往往需谈判。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on royal demesne income, sheriffs' ferms, commutation of feudal service, special levies on Jewish communities, customs and tolls, judicial amercements, and occasional extraordinary exactions. During continental war or commutation of military service, the king could levy scutage and similar payments. The church might also be pressed for exceptional contributions, though usually through negotiation.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教（罗马公教）占绝对主导，教会深度介入教育、慈善、司法和政治合法性。修道院、主教座堂和圣徒崇拜构成宗教生活中心，朝圣与十字军理念对贵族文化影响显著。
religion.en: Latin Christianity (Roman Catholicism) was overwhelmingly dominant, and the church was deeply involved in education, charity, justice, and political legitimacy. Monasteries, cathedrals, and the cult of saints formed the core of religious life, while pilgrimage and crusading ideals strongly shaped aristocratic culture.

philosophy.zh: 思想以基督教神学、教会法理、修道传统和新兴经院学为核心；政治观念强调君权受神授但须维护正义与教会秩序。贵族社会同时受到骑士伦理、封建忠诚和圣战观念影响。
philosophy.en: Thought was centered on Christian theology, canonistic reasoning, monastic tradition, and emerging scholasticism; political ideas stressed that kingship was divinely sanctioned yet bound to uphold justice and ecclesiastical order. Aristocratic society was also shaped by chivalric ethics, feudal loyalty, and crusading ideology.

writingSystem.zh: 书写系统使用拉丁字母。行政、教会和学术文书主要用拉丁文；宫廷与贵族文化普遍使用盎格鲁-诺曼法语；民间口语以中古英语诸方言为主。
writingSystem.en: The writing system used the Latin alphabet. Administration, ecclesiastical records, and scholarship were chiefly conducted in Latin; courtly and aristocratic culture widely employed Anglo-Norman French; popular speech was dominated by Middle English dialects.

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括罗曼式与早期哥特式宗教建筑、发达的编年史写作、法学文书传统、修道院抄写文化，以及在宫廷中流行的法语诗歌和骑士文学。英格兰的普通法与文书行政本身也是制度文化上的重大成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included Romanesque and early Gothic ecclesiastical architecture, developed chronicle writing, legal and documentary traditions, monastic manuscript culture, and French-language poetry and chivalric literature at court. England's common law and documentary governance were themselves major institutional cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系：行政与教会使用拉丁语，贵族常用罗曼语支的盎格鲁-诺曼法语，民间主体语言为西日耳曼语支的中古英语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European: Latin in administration and church life, Anglo-Norman French of the Romance branch among the nobility, and Middle English of the West Germanic branch among the wider population

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的英格兰经济在拉丁西欧中属较高组织水平，以农业剩余、羊毛生产、王室财政抽取和海峡贸易为支柱。与宋朝等超大型商业经济相比仍较小，但在西北欧已具较强货币化和行政征收能力。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200 the English economy was comparatively well organized within Latin Western Europe, resting on agricultural surplus, wool production, royal fiscal extraction, and cross-Channel trade. It remained much smaller than the great commercial economies of Song China, but in northwestern Europe it showed relatively strong monetization and administrative capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧与羊毛、庄园农业、葡萄酒转口、林业、采矿（铅、锡、铁）、盐业、建筑、船运和城市手工业
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, pastoralism and wool, manorial agriculture, wine redistribution, forestry, mining (lead, tin, iron), salt production, construction, shipping, and urban crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 主要输出羊毛、毛皮、铅、锡、皮革、粮食余量和部分布匹；主要输入葡萄酒、精制纺织品、香料、奢侈金属器、染料、武器与南方商品
tradeGoods.en: Main exports included wool, hides, lead, tin, leather, grain surpluses, and some cloth; chief imports were wine, fine textiles, spices, luxury metalware, dyes, weapons, and southern goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。大贵族与教会机构控制大量土地、磨坊、林地与司法收益；骑士和自由佃农拥有较稳定资产；普通农户财富主要体现为牲畜、农具、口粮储备和土地使用权，抗灾能力有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Great magnates and church institutions controlled extensive lands, mills, woodlands, and judicial income; knights and free tenants held more secure assets; ordinary peasant wealth consisted mainly of livestock, tools, food stores, and access rights to land, with limited resilience to crisis.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为以庄园封建关系为基础、同时不断货币化的混合经济。王室通过税收、司法罚款和市场监管抽取资源，城市与港口贸易快速发展，但农业和领主控制仍占主导。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed one grounded in manorial-feudal relations yet increasingly monetized. The Crown extracted resources through taxation, judicial fines, and market regulation, while towns and ports expanded trade; nevertheless agriculture and lordly control remained dominant.

source.zh: 关税、市场税与港口收入
source.en: Customs, market tolls, and port revenues

source.zh: 犹太社群课税与特殊征敛
source.en: Taxation of Jewish communities and special levies

source.zh: 封地继承金、监护权与其他偶发收入
source.en: Reliefs, wardships, and other incidental income

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库规模在西欧属较强，但并非现代意义上的稳定现金储备，而是由硬币、贵金属器、应收款、封建权利和临时征敛能力共同构成。约翰继位初期财政上仍具动员力，但大陆战争将持续消耗其资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was strong by western European standards, though not a modern cash reserve; it consisted of coin, plate, receivables, feudal rights, and the capacity for extraordinary exaction. Early in John's reign the Crown still possessed notable fiscal mobilization power, but continental warfare would continue to strain these resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向高强度征敛与法理化收费，强调将封建义务货币化并通过司法与文书程序扩大王室收入。这一做法短期增强了王权，但也加剧了贵族、教会与地方精英的不满。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy leaned toward intensive extraction and legally framed charges, emphasizing the monetization of feudal obligations and the expansion of royal income through judicial and documentary procedure. This strengthened the Crown in the short term but deepened resentment among nobles, clergy, and local elites.

description.zh: 由国王直属封臣、大贵族及其骑士组成，是战场冲击核心，也承担城堡守备和快速机动任务。
description.en: Drawn from the king's tenants-in-chief, magnates, and their knights, they formed the striking core of the army and also served in castle defense and rapid operations.

description.zh: 包括宫廷武士、弩手、职业步兵和来自海峡两岸的雇佣部队，是约翰王较依赖的常备军事力量。
description.en: Including household warriors, crossbowmen, professional infantry, and hired troops from both sides of the Channel, these were the more permanent forces on which King John increasingly relied.

description.zh: 由郡长和地方领主征集的步兵与民兵，适合短期防御、围城勤务、道路与城镇守备，但远征能力有限。
description.en: Infantry and militia raised by sheriffs and local lords, suitable for short-term defense, siege duties, and guarding roads and towns, but limited in long-distance campaigning.

description.zh: 并非常设海军，而是由五港同盟、沿海城镇和王室船只构成的临时海上力量，用于运兵、护航和海峡控制。
description.en: Not a permanent navy in the modern sense, but an ad hoc maritime force drawn from the Cinque Ports, coastal towns, and royal ships for transport, convoy, and Channel control.

commanderInChief.zh: 英格兰国王约翰
commanderInChief.en: King John of England

technology.zh: 军事技术属高阶中世纪西欧水准，核心装备包括锁子甲、鸢形盾、长枪、剑、弩、投石器和石木结合城堡。攻城与守城能力较强，海峡运输与补给经验丰富，但尚未出现火药武器。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a high medieval western European level, centered on mail armor, kite shields, lances, swords, crossbows, siege engines, and mixed stone-and-timber castles. Siegecraft and defense were relatively strong, and the kingdom had substantial experience in Channel transport and supply, but gunpowder weapons were absent.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自法兰西卡佩王权在诺曼底、安茹和普瓦图方向的扩张，以及苏格兰边境压力、威尔士诸领地的不稳定与海峡交通风险。内部而言，大贵族对高压财政与军事征召的不满也构成潜在政治危机。
threats.en: The main threats came from Capetian expansion in Normandy, Anjou, and Poitou, along with pressure on the Scottish frontier, instability in the Welsh marches, and risks to Channel communications. Internally, baronial resentment over heavy fiscal and military demands also posed a potential political crisis.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口约350万，绝大多数居住于乡村庄园、村社和教会领地。全国人口在11至12世纪总体增长，农业开垦扩大，但区域差异明显，东南部与中部人口更密集，北部和边疆地带相对稀疏。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 3.5 million, with the overwhelming majority living in rural manors, villages, and ecclesiastical estates. Overall population had grown across the eleventh and twelfth centuries as cultivation expanded, though density varied sharply, being greater in the southeast and midlands than in the north and frontier zones.

description.zh: 大英帝国首都，全球贸易与金融中心
description.en: Capital of the British Empire, global trade and financial center

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括王室与高贵族、高级教士、骑士与小地主、城市商人和行会手工业者、自由农、维兰农与农奴。法律地位和租役义务差异极大，土地控制与人身自由度决定了社会位置。
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included the royal house and high nobility, senior clergy, knights and lesser landholders, urban merchants and guild craftsmen, free peasants, villeins, and serfs. Legal status and tenurial obligations varied sharply, and control of land together with personal freedom largely determined social position.

allies.zh: 正式稳定盟友有限，但英格兰王室与部分佛兰德、神圣罗马帝国诸侯、普瓦图及阿基坦地区忠于安茹家的贵族保持合作；与教廷关系在1200年尚未完全破裂。
allies.en: Stable formal allies were limited, but the English Crown maintained cooperation with parts of Flanders, certain princes of the Holy Roman Empire, and nobles in Poitou and Aquitaine loyal to the Angevin house; relations with the papacy in 1200 had not yet fully broken down.

enemies.zh: 最大敌手是法兰西王国的卡佩王朝，双方在诺曼底、安茹、曼恩、图赖讷和普瓦图存在持续竞争；边缘敌对力量包括不稳定的威尔士诸侯与时有摩擦的苏格兰王国。
enemies.en: The principal enemy was the Capetian monarchy of France, with ongoing competition in Normandy, Anjou, Maine, Touraine, and Poitou; secondary hostile pressures came from unstable Welsh rulers and the occasionally contentious kingdom of Scotland.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护海峡两岸的安茹遗产、保障诺曼底及西法封地、分化法国诸侯并维持与教廷和帝国势力的外交空间为中心。英格兰王权在这一时期仍将自己视为跨海峡政治体，而非单纯岛国内王国。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving the cross-Channel Angevin inheritance, securing Normandy and western French possessions, dividing the French nobility, and maintaining diplomatic room with the papacy and imperial powers. At this date the English monarchy still viewed itself as a cross-Channel polity rather than merely an insular kingdom.

era.zh: 十字军时代的高地中世纪
era.en: High Middle Ages of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度优势包括较成熟的石制城堡建筑、重骑兵作战体系、弩与攻城器械的广泛使用、风车与水磨技术、改良犁耕与三圃制农业、拉丁文书行政、财政署账册制度以及较发达的海峡航运组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and advantages included mature stone-castle construction, heavy cavalry warfare, widespread use of crossbows and siege engines, windmill and watermill technology, improved plough agriculture and three-field practices, Latin documentary administration, Exchequer accounting, and relatively advanced Channel shipping organization.

strengths.zh: 该王国的优势在于财政征收能力强、普通法和文书行政发达、伦敦与港口贸易活跃、农业基础稳固，并且拥有海峡机动与跨海投送经验。与多数同时代西欧王国相比，其中央治理深度和现金化财政更为突出。
strengths.en: The kingdom's strengths lay in strong fiscal extraction, developed common law and documentary administration, active trade through London and the ports, a solid agrarian base, and extensive experience in Channel mobility and overseas deployment. Compared with many contemporary western European kingdoms, its central governing reach and cash-based finance were especially notable.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统治结构高度依赖国王个人能力，跨海峡领地防御成本极高，贵族与教会对王室征敛极为敏感，威尔士和边疆治理不稳。此外，安茹帝国式版图缺乏统一制度整合，容易被法国王权逐块蚕食。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a governing structure highly dependent on the king's personal capacity, the immense cost of defending cross-Channel lands, noble and clerical sensitivity to royal exactions, and unstable rule in Wales and the frontier regions. Moreover, the Angevin imperial configuration lacked unified institutional integration and was vulnerable to piecemeal reduction by the French Crown.

outlook.zh: 1200年的局势表面稳定，但前景暗藏危机。若约翰无法稳固大陆封地、控制征税规模并维持贵族合作，王国将在未来十余年面临领土损失与宪制冲突。短期仍具强大资源，长期则取决于能否把行政强国转化为政治共识。
outlook.en: The situation in 1200 was outwardly stable but fraught with latent danger. If John failed to secure the continental lands, restrain fiscal pressure, and preserve baronial cooperation, the kingdom would face territorial losses and constitutional conflict within the next decade or two. In the short term it still possessed substantial resources; in the longer term, its fate depended on whether administrative strength could be translated into political consensus.

description.zh: 安茹帝国晚期核心王国，仍在欧洲大陆保有大量利益。
description.en: The core kingdom of the late Angevin realm, still holding major continental interests.

governmentForm.zh: 由国王、地方贵族与区域议会共同制衡的早期封建选举君主制
governmentForm.en: An early feudal elective monarchy constrained by the king, regional nobles, and provincial assemblies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以自由农民、地方贵族、教士与奴仆或依附人口构成；在内陆农业区和沿海贸易区之间存在明显差异，地方社群与亲族网络仍极具影响力。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of free peasants, local nobles, clergy, and thralls or dependent people; strong differences existed between inland farming districts and coastal trading zones, while local communities and kinship networks remained highly influential.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、地方大地主贵族、主教与高级教士共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The royal house, landed magnates, bishops, and senior clergy formed the ruling class

succession.zh: 王位继承并非完全固定，通常由王族血统、贵族支持、区域议会承认与教会祝圣共同决定
succession.en: Succession was not fully fixed, and normally depended on royal bloodline, noble backing, recognition by provincial assemblies, and ecclesiastical consecration

structure.zh: 王国政府仍较为松散，核心由国王家臣、王室随从、主教团与各地法律代言人组成。王权在约塔兰和斯韦阿兰不同地区的贯彻程度不一，重要事务常需通过地方集会与贵族协商。
structure.en: The kingdom's government remained relatively loose, centered on the king's household, royal retainers, the episcopate, and local law-speakers. Royal authority was uneven across Götaland and Svealand, and major decisions often required negotiation with regional assemblies and nobles.

function.zh: 管理王室庄园、贡赋、宴飨、仓储和随行财政，是王权最核心的行政机构。
function.en: Managed royal estates, tribute, hospitality, storage, and itinerant finances, forming the core administrative arm of royal power.

function.zh: 依靠主教、神职人员和少量受拉丁教育的书记员起草文书、保存契据并处理与教廷及北欧教省的联系。
function.en: Using bishops, clergy, and a small number of Latin-trained clerks, it drafted documents, kept charters, and handled contacts with the papacy and the Nordic ecclesiastical province.

name.en: Local Levy and Military Service Office

function.zh: 协调海上征召、地方军役义务、船只召集和边地防卫，尤其面向东向远征与沿海防御。
function.en: Coordinated naval levies, local military obligations, ship musters, and frontier defense, especially for eastward expeditions and coastal protection.

function.zh: 协助国王与地方法律代言人主持诉讼、宣读法律、确认罚金与仲裁争端。
function.en: Assisted the king and local law-speakers in hearing cases, proclaiming law, confirming fines, and arbitrating disputes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠各省议会、百户区或村社共同体、地方首领、法律代言人以及主教区网络。王室直接任命官员的覆盖范围有限，许多地区实际由世族和教会分享管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on provincial assemblies, hundred-district or village communities, local chieftains, law-speakers, and episcopal networks. The crown's directly appointed officials had limited reach, and many areas were effectively governed jointly by noble lineages and the Church.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以各地区口传习惯法为主，逐渐由教会书写化并受到基督教婚姻、继承与教产规则影响。赔偿金、宣誓、证人与集会裁断仍是司法核心。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on regional customary law, increasingly written down by ecclesiastical influence and shaped by Christian rules on marriage, inheritance, and church property. Compensation payments, oaths, witnesses, and assembly judgments remained central to justice.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自王室地产产出、贡赋、罚金、贸易征收、渡口与港口收费，以及临时军役负担。常规全国性货币税仍不发达，实物征收和劳役十分重要。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from royal estate output, tribute, fines, trade dues, ferry and harbor charges, and occasional military obligations. A regular kingdom-wide monetary tax system was still underdeveloped, and payment in kind and labor service remained very important.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教已成为官方宗教，但旧诺斯信仰的习俗和节庆记忆在乡村地区仍有残存影响。
religion.en: Latin Christianity had become the official religion, though memories and survivals of older Norse beliefs and customs still influenced rural society.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以天主教神学、教会法观念、王权基督教合法性以及传统北欧荣誉伦理并存为特征。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was characterized by Catholic theology, notions from canon law, Christian legitimacy of kingship, and the continued presence of traditional Nordic honor ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母在教会与文书中逐步取代卢恩文字；卢恩仍用于纪念碑、短铭文和部分地方实践。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was increasingly replacing runes in church and documentary use; runes still survived in memorial stones, short inscriptions, and some local practices.

culturalAchievements.zh: 王国文化成就体现在基督教会组织的巩固、石砌教堂和早期砖石建筑的扩展、卢恩石传统的延续、口述英雄叙事与法律传统的保存，以及对东波罗的海扩张所形成的边疆宗教文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included the consolidation of church organization, the spread of stone churches and early masonry building, the continuation of runestone traditions, the preservation of oral heroic narratives and legal traditions, and a frontier religious culture shaped by expansion into the eastern Baltic.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支古瑞典语
languageFamily.en: Old Swedish of the North Germanic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、渔业、林产和区域贸易为主，人均产出在欧洲北部属中等偏低，但自由农民比例较高，家庭自给能力较强。货币化程度有限，许多交易仍以实物、银重与互惠义务完成。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, animal husbandry, fishing, forest products, and regional trade. Per-capita output was modest by northern European standards, but the proportion of free peasants was high and household self-sufficiency was strong. Monetization was limited, and many transactions still took place through goods, weighed silver, and reciprocal obligations.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、渔业、铁器锻造、焦油与木材生产、毛皮采集、船只建造及区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, livestock raising, fishing, ironworking, tar and timber production, fur gathering, shipbuilding, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 铁、焦油、木材、兽皮、毛皮、蜂蜡、皮革、鱼干，以及输入的盐、细布、葡萄酒、金属器和教会用品
tradeGoods.en: Iron, tar, timber, hides, furs, wax, leather, dried fish, and imported salt, fine cloth, wine, metalware, and ecclesiastical goods

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富集中于土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、船只、工具和亲族网络，而非大量铸币。沿海地主、商人和教会机构拥有更高可动员财富。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was concentrated in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, boats, tools, and kinship networks rather than large coin reserves. Coastal landowners, merchants, and church institutions held greater mobilizable wealth.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自由农民经济为基础、夹杂封建义务和王室贡赋的混合型乡村经济，贸易集中于港市、季节市场和波罗的海航道。
economicSystem.en: A mixed rural economy based on free peasant production, combined with feudal obligations and royal tribute, with trade concentrated in ports, seasonal markets, and Baltic sea routes.

source.en: Regional tribute and payments in kind

category.zh: 地方联盟、馈赠与贵族安抚
category.en: Local alliances, gifts, and noble appeasement

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备并非完全集中于固定金库，而是分散在银币、称量银、贵重器物、武器、牲畜、库存谷物与王室地产收益权之中。财政弹性有限，遇到大规模远征或内战时容易紧张。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves were not concentrated in a single treasury alone, but dispersed among coin, weighed silver, valuable objects, arms, livestock, stored grain, and claims on royal estate income. Fiscal flexibility was limited, and major expeditions or civil war could quickly strain finances.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持王室巡回统治、依靠地产和传统征收而避免过度触怒地方贵族；在需要东征或对外动员时，则通过临时贡赋、馈赠网络和教会合作来扩大资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to sustain the itinerant monarchy through demesne income and traditional dues while avoiding excessive provocation of local nobles; when eastern campaigns or major mobilization were needed, resources were expanded through extraordinary levies, gift networks, and cooperation with the Church.

name.en: Royal Hearth Troops and Noble Retinues

description.zh: 由国王近臣、职业武士、贵族骑乘战士和重装随从构成，是王国最可靠但规模较小的核心武力。
description.en: Composed of the king's close retainers, professional fighters, mounted noble warriors, and heavily armed followers, this was the kingdom's most reliable but relatively small core force.

description.zh: 以自由农民为主体，装备长矛、盾牌、斧与少量弓箭，适合地方防御、集会战和短期征讨。
description.en: Drawn mainly from free peasants, they were equipped with spears, shields, axes, and some bows, suitable for local defense, assembly warfare, and short campaigns.

description.zh: 依靠沿海地区提供船只与桨手，用于波罗的海航行、对芬兰和东波罗的海沿岸的行动，以及海岸巡防。
description.en: Relied on coastal districts to provide ships and rowers for Baltic sailing, operations toward Finland and the eastern Baltic, and coastal patrol.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王斯韦克尔二世
commanderInChief.en: King Sverker II

technology.zh: 军事技术以锁子甲、圆盾、长矛、战斧、剑、弓和浅吃水木船为主；攻城技术有限，作战仍强调近战冲击、地方征召与海上机动。
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail armor, round shields, spears, battle-axes, swords, bows, and shallow-draft wooden ships; siege capabilities were limited, and warfare still emphasized close combat shock, local levies, and maritime mobility.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与丹麦、挪威的王室竞争、国内贵族派系斗争、波罗的海海盗与东向边疆的长期不稳定；对芬兰和异教波罗的海地区的扩张既是机会也是消耗。
threats.en: Main threats came from royal rivalry with Denmark and Norway, internal noble factionalism, Baltic raiding, and chronic instability on the eastern frontier; expansion toward Finland and pagan Baltic lands was both an opportunity and a drain on resources.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在斯韦阿兰、约塔兰和部分沿海与湖区，聚落以农村村社和分散农庄为主。人口密度低，大片森林、湖泊和边缘地区仍开发不足。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in Svealand, Götaland, and some coastal and lake districts, with settlement centered on villages and dispersed farmsteads. Population density was low, and large forested, lacustrine, and frontier areas remained only lightly developed.

name.zh: 乌普萨拉地区宗教中心
name.en: Ecclesiastical center in the Uppsala region

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、显贵家族与主教；中层包括较富自由农民、地方首领、商人和低级教士；下层为普通自由农、佃户、仆役与少量未完全摆脱依附地位的人群。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, magnate families, and bishops; the middle strata included wealthier free peasants, local leaders, merchants, and lower clergy; the lower strata comprised ordinary free farmers, tenants, servants, and a small number of people not fully free from dependency.

allies.zh: 与教廷、北德商贸网络及部分支持其王位的地方贵族保持合作；与某些东向传教和十字军事业参与者存在宗教共同利益。
allies.en: The kingdom cooperated with the papacy, north German trading networks, and local nobles supporting the king's position; it also shared religious interests with participants in missionary and crusading efforts to the east.

enemies.zh: 直接敌对并不固定，但与丹麦、挪威在边境、海上影响力和王位政治上存在竞争；东波罗的海异教社群和袭扰者常被视为军事目标。
enemies.en: Direct enemies were not fixed, but Sweden competed with Denmark and Norway over borders, maritime influence, and dynastic politics; pagan communities and raiders in the eastern Baltic were often treated as military targets.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固王国合法性、争取教会支持、保持与北欧邻国的实力平衡，并逐步将芬兰和东波罗的海纳入宗教与政治影响范围为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on strengthening royal legitimacy, securing church support, maintaining a balance of power with neighboring Nordic kingdoms, and gradually extending religious and political influence into Finland and the eastern Baltic.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键进展包括石砌教堂建造、拉丁文书行政的扩展、改进型海船和沿海航行经验、区域铸币、铁器制造与森林资源开发技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key advances included stone church construction, the expansion of Latin documentary administration, improved seagoing vessels and coastal navigation, regional coinage, iron production, and techniques for exploiting forest resources.

strengths.zh: 王国拥有较高比例的自由农民、强韧的地方共同体、良好的海上机动能力、不断加强的教会组织，以及向东扩张的地缘机会。
strengths.en: The kingdom possessed a high proportion of free peasants, resilient local communities, solid maritime mobility, a strengthening church organization, and geopolitical opportunities for expansion eastward.

weaknesses.zh: 王权仍不稳固，继承规则模糊，地方贵族和区域议会制约明显，行政与税收体系薄弱，城市和货币经济规模有限。
weaknesses.en: Royal authority remained insecure, succession rules were ambiguous, local nobles and provincial assemblies imposed strong constraints, and both administration and taxation were weak while towns and the monetary economy remained limited.

outlook.zh: 至1200年前后，王国总体稳定但并未完全整合。若国王能借教会与东向军事行动增强威望，瑞典王权有望继续集中；若贵族派系再度激化，则仍可能出现内斗与地区分裂。
outlook.en: Around 1200, the kingdom was broadly stable but not fully integrated. If the king could enhance prestige through church support and eastward military activity, Swedish royal authority might continue to centralize; if noble factionalism intensified again, internal conflict and regional fragmentation would remain possible.

description.zh: 正在向芬兰与波罗的海东岸拓展的北欧王国，王权仍受地方贵族制约。
description.en: A Nordic kingdom expanding toward Finland and the eastern Baltic, with royal power still constrained by local nobles.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，结合王室廷臣体系、地区贵族权力与教会机构
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy combining royal household rule, regional aristocratic power, and ecclesiastical institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、世俗贵族、主教与教士、自由农民、佃农与仆役构成。挪威在12世纪末至13世纪初仍保留相当强大的自由农民传统，地方集会与海上义务对社会组织具有重要作用。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the king, secular aristocrats, bishops and clergy, freeholding farmers, tenants, and servants. Norway around 1200 still preserved a relatively strong tradition of free peasants, with local assemblies and maritime obligations playing an important role in social organization.

rulingClass.zh: 王室家臣、地方显贵、主教团与高级教士
rulingClass.en: Royal retainers, regional magnates, bishops, and senior clergy

succession.zh: 原则上为王族世袭，但12世纪挪威长期存在王位竞争与内战传统；到1200年前后，斯韦雷一系正努力将王位继承更稳定地集中于其家族。
succession.en: In principle the throne was hereditary within the royal line, but 12th-century Norway had a long tradition of disputed succession and civil war; by around 1200 Sverre's line was attempting to stabilize succession more firmly within its own family.

structure.zh: 中央政权以国王及其宫廷为核心，依靠王室侍从、法务官、税吏、地方长官与主教网络统治全国。与后世官僚国家相比，机构较为精简，许多行政职能通过个人效忠、地区集会和教会组织来完成。
structure.en: Central authority was centered on the king and his court, ruling through royal retainers, lawmen, tax collectors, local governors, and a network of bishops. Compared with later bureaucratic states, the apparatus was relatively lean, and many administrative functions were carried out through personal allegiance, regional assemblies, and church institutions.

function.zh: 管理国王日常政务、诏令发布、王室财产与外交文书
function.en: Managed the king's daily government, issuance of decrees, royal estates, and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 征收地租、罚金、封地收益、航运相关贡赋，并管理王室农庄与地产
function.en: Collected rents, fines, feudal revenues, and shipping-related dues, and managed royal farms and landed property

function.zh: 协调地区法律、主持或监督地方议会审判、执行王室法令
function.en: Coordinated regional law, presided over or supervised assembly justice, and enforced royal decrees

function.zh: 组织海上征召、船只征集、沿海警备与军事动员
function.en: Organized naval levies, ship requisition, coastal defense, and military mobilization

function.zh: 处理王权与主教区、修道机构、什一税与教会法相关事务
function.en: Handled matters involving royal relations with bishoprics, monastic institutions, tithes, and canon law

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托各地区法区、郡县式沿海辖区与王室委任的地方长官运行，重要事务仍通过地方廷会与法律会议处理。冰岛、法罗群岛等海外属地更多依靠承认挪威王权、纳贡与精英联系维系，而非严密直辖。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through regional law areas, coastal districts roughly comparable to counties, and royal-appointed local governors, while important matters still passed through local assemblies and legal meetings. Overseas dependencies such as Iceland and the Faroes were maintained more through recognition of Norwegian kingship, tribute, and elite ties than through tight direct administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以地区习惯法、王室法令和教会法并行。审判常在地方议会进行，重视赔偿、宣誓与见证。斯韦雷时期王权试图加强对司法的控制，但仍须与地方传统和教会权威妥协。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined regional customary law, royal decrees, and canon law. Judgments were often made in local assemblies, with compensation, oath-taking, and witness testimony central to procedure. Under Sverre, the monarchy sought to strengthen royal influence over justice but still had to compromise with local custom and ecclesiastical authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要来自王室地产、地租、罚金、港口与贸易税、海外贡赋及不定期征发。税制并非全面按人头统一征收，而是以地产收益、法庭罚款、海上义务和属地贡纳为主。
taxationSystem.en: State income came chiefly from royal lands, rents, fines, port and trade dues, overseas tribute, and occasional extraordinary levies. Taxation was not a uniform per-capita system but relied mainly on landed income, court fines, maritime obligations, and tribute from dependent territories.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教，属罗马天主教体系。尽管旧北欧传统记忆尚存，国家宗教与合法性已明确建立在基督教王权与教会组织之上。
religion.en: Latin Christianity within the Roman Catholic system. Although memories of older Norse traditions remained, state religion and legitimacy were clearly founded on Christian kingship and ecclesiastical organization.

philosophy.zh: 思想以基督教神学、王权合法性观念、教会法伦理和北欧法律习惯相结合，尚未形成高度学院化哲学传统，但道德秩序、誓言、荣誉与合法继承观念十分重要。
philosophy.en: Thought combined Christian theology, ideas of royal legitimacy, canon-law ethics, and Norse legal custom. It had not yet developed a highly scholastic philosophical tradition, but moral order, oath, honor, and legitimate succession were highly important.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母书写古诺斯语与拉丁语；符文在日常或纪念性场合仍有残存使用。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom mainly used the Latin alphabet to write Old Norse and Latin; runes still survived in some everyday and commemorative contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 挪威在这一时期的重要文化成就在于王权史叙述、圣徒崇拜、法典整理传统、木构教堂建筑以及海洋航行文化。口述诗歌与王者传奇传统正逐渐被书面化，形成后来萨迦文学的重要基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements of the period included royal historical narrative, saintly cults, traditions of legal codification, stave church architecture, and maritime seafaring culture. Oral poetry and royal saga traditions were increasingly being written down, laying foundations for the later saga literature.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支，主要语言为古诺斯语的西诺斯变体
languageFamily.en: North Germanic language family, with West Norse forms of Old Norse as the principal language

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的挪威经济规模在欧洲属中等偏小，但在人口较少的北方王国中具备相当韧性。经济以渔业、畜牧、沿海农耕、木材、海运和区域贸易为基础，难与宋朝等大型农业帝国相比，但在北海—北大西洋海上网络中占有重要位置。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200, Norway's economy was modest by broader European standards but fairly resilient for a sparsely populated northern kingdom. It rested on fishing, pastoralism, coastal farming, timber, shipping, and regional trade. It could not compare with major agrarian empires such as Song China, but it occupied an important place in the North Sea and North Atlantic maritime network.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、畜牧业、沿海农业、木材采伐、船舶建造、毛皮与海产贸易、铁器与手工业
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, pastoralism, coastal agriculture, timber extraction, shipbuilding, fur and marine-product trade, ironworking, and handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 干鱼、毛皮、木材、焦油、兽皮、鲸与海象制品、羊毛制品、铁器；输入谷物、布匹、盐、酒、金属制成品和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Stockfish, furs, timber, tar, hides, whale and walrus products, woolens, and iron goods; imports included grain, cloth, salt, wine, manufactured metal goods, and luxuries

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富差异较大。西部沿海自由农民若兼营渔业与贸易，物质条件往往优于边远内陆农户；贵族与主教掌握更大地产、船只和贡赋收益。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied considerably. Freeholding coastal farmers in western Norway who combined fishing with trade were often materially better off than remote inland households; nobles and bishops controlled larger estates, ships, and tributary income.

economicSystem.zh: 以农村自给经济为基础、辅以港口贸易和王室—贵族—教会地产体系的混合经济。市场交换重要但不完全主导，大量生产与义务仍嵌入地方共同体、封臣关系和海上征役体系。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on rural subsistence, supplemented by port trade and a royal-noble-ecclesiastical estate system. Market exchange was important but not fully dominant, and much production and obligation remained embedded in local communities, lordship, and maritime levy structures.

source.zh: 特别征发与封臣义务折值
source.en: Extraordinary levies and commuted feudal obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由银币、银锭、实物储备、船只、武器与可动员贡赋构成。由于王权仍具巡行性质，财富并非集中存放于单一固定库房，而是分散在卑尔根、王室庄园与教会安全点之间。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of silver coin, bullion, stores in kind, ships, weapons, and mobilizable tribute. Because kingship remained itinerant, wealth was not kept in a single fixed vault but distributed among Bergen, royal estates, and secure ecclesiastical sites.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏谨慎保守，优先保障宫廷、舰船动员和王权网络，通过控制港口、强化海外联系和维护国内秩序来稳步扩大收入，而非建立高度抽税型国家。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and conservative, prioritizing the court, fleet mobilization, and maintenance of royal networks. Revenue was expanded gradually through control of ports, overseas connections, and domestic order rather than through a highly extractive tax state.

description.zh: 由国王直属战士、近臣和精锐护卫组成，是王权最可靠的机动力量
description.en: Composed of the king's direct warriors, close retainers, and elite guards, forming the most reliable mobile force of the monarchy

description.zh: 由自由农民和地区义务兵构成，战时按法区和地方单位集结，适合防御与短期作战
description.en: Drawn from free peasants and regional obligation troops, assembled by law districts and local units in wartime, suitable for defense and short campaigns

description.zh: 依托长船与其他沿海船只征集的海上武装，承担巡航、运输、袭击与海上控制任务
description.en: A maritime force raised through levies of longships and other coastal vessels, responsible for patrol, transport, raiding, and sea control

description.zh: 数量有限，以地方显贵及其随从为主，主要用于指挥、快速机动和象征性冲击
description.en: Limited in number, consisting mainly of regional magnates and their followers, used chiefly for command, rapid movement, and occasional shock action

commanderInChief.zh: 国王斯韦雷
commanderInChief.en: King Sverre

technology.zh: 军备以锁子甲、圆盾、长矛、剑、斧和弓为主，海军技术成熟，擅长沿海航行与北海远航。攻城技术相对西欧大陆强国较弱，但在内战经验中积累了围城和机动作战能力。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on mail armor, round shields, spears, swords, axes, and bows, with well-developed naval technology and strong capabilities in coastal and North Sea navigation. Siege techniques were weaker than those of major continental western powers, but civil-war experience had given the kingdom practical knowledge of sieges and mobile warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自丹麦和瑞典在斯堪的纳维亚的竞争、国内残余派系冲突、海上交通受阻，以及苏格兰与北海岛屿方向的势力变化。
threats.en: Principal threats came from Danish and Swedish rivalry in Scandinavia, residual domestic factional conflict, disruption of maritime communications, and shifts in power involving Scotland and the North Sea islands.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏而分散，主要集中于西南沿海峡湾地带、特隆赫姆周边与若干内陆谷地。相较欧洲农业核心区，挪威人口密度较低，但沿海聚落高度依赖海洋资源与船运连接。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and dispersed, concentrated mainly in the southwestern fjord regions, around Trondheim, and in certain inland valleys. Compared with Europe's agrarian core regions, Norway had low population density, but coastal settlements were strongly integrated by marine resources and shipping.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与贵族、主教和教士、富裕自由农民、普通自由民、佃农、仆役和少量奴婢残余。与许多欧洲地区相比，自由农民在挪威社会中仍具较强地位与军事义务。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included the royal family and aristocracy, bishops and clergy, wealthy freeholders, ordinary freemen, tenants, servants, and some residual unfree dependents. Compared with many parts of Europe, free peasants still held a relatively strong position and military obligation in Norwegian society.

allies.zh: 无稳定正式大同盟；现实中与部分北海商人网络、教廷内可争取派系及若干岛屿首领维持合作关系。
allies.en: No stable grand alliance; in practice the kingdom maintained cooperative ties with segments of North Sea merchant networks, factions within the papal sphere that could be courted, and certain island chieftains.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是丹麦王国及其在斯堪的纳维亚的影响力；与瑞典亦存在竞争。教会冲突时期，挪威王权与罗马教廷及本国主教团关系紧张。
enemies.en: The main rival was the Kingdom of Denmark and its influence in Scandinavia; there was also competition with Sweden. During periods of church conflict, the Norwegian monarchy had tense relations with the papacy and the domestic episcopate.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制北海与北大西洋航道、巩固对冰岛和法罗群岛等地的宗主影响、限制丹麦干预并维护王权国际合法性为核心。挪威并非欧陆霸权，但在海上通道与岛屿网络方面拥有独特战略价值。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling North Sea and North Atlantic routes, consolidating overlordship over places such as Iceland and the Faroes, limiting Danish interference, and defending the international legitimacy of the crown. Norway was not a continental hegemon, but it possessed distinctive strategic value in maritime lanes and island networks.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的北方远洋与沿海造船术、木构教堂工程、寒冷海域航海经验、区域法制书写化、港口与海产保存技术。挪威技术强项主要体现在海洋运输、造船和环境适应，而非大型城市工程。
keyInnovations.en: Mature northern shipbuilding for oceanic and coastal travel, stave church construction, navigation experience in cold seas, increasing written recording of regional law, and port and fish-preservation techniques. Norway's chief technological strengths lay in maritime transport, shipbuilding, and environmental adaptation rather than in large-scale urban engineering.

strengths.zh: 挪威的优势在于海上机动能力、北大西洋网络、较强的自由农民基础、王权在内战后逐步整合，以及卑尔根等港口的区域贸易潜力。
strengths.en: Norway's strengths lay in maritime mobility, its North Atlantic network, a relatively strong base of free peasants, the gradual consolidation of kingship after civil conflict, and the regional trade potential of ports such as Bergen.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口稀少、农业余量有限、中央官僚薄弱、王位继承传统不稳、对海路和外来谷物依赖较高，并且容易受到教会冲突和地区豪强离心的影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were sparse population, limited agricultural surplus, a thin central bureaucracy, unstable traditions of succession, significant dependence on sea routes and imported grain, and vulnerability to church conflict and regional aristocratic centrifugalism.

outlook.zh: 若王室能够继续压制内战派系、改善与教会关系并强化对岛屿与港口的控制，挪威将在13世纪前期保持稳定并扩大海上影响；但若继承争端再起或海上贸易受阻，王国的财政与政治平衡将迅速承压。
outlook.en: If the crown could continue suppressing civil-war factions, improve relations with the Church, and strengthen control over islands and ports, Norway was likely to remain stable in the early 13th century and expand its maritime influence; but renewed succession disputes or disruption of sea trade would quickly strain the kingdom's fiscal and political balance.

description.zh: 北大西洋导向的王国，控制挪威沿海并影响冰岛、法罗与北海航线。
description.en: A North Atlantic-oriented kingdom controlling the Norwegian coast and influencing Iceland, the Faroes, and North Sea routes.

governmentForm.zh: 王朝分封制下的诸公国联盟，名义上承认克拉科夫高级公爵的优先地位，但实际上高度分裂
governmentForm.en: A dynastic confederation of duchies under the system of partition, nominally acknowledging the precedence of the senior duke at Krakow but in practice highly fragmented

socialStructure.zh: 社会由世袭王朝诸支、教会高级神职、地方贵族骑士、自由农民、依附农民、城镇居民与少量外国商人组成
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of hereditary Piast princely lines, high clergy, local nobles and knights, free peasants, dependent peasants, townspeople, and a small number of foreign merchants

rulingClass.zh: 皮雅斯特诸公、主教与修道院院长、地方显贵和武装骑士构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Piast dukes, bishops and abbots, regional magnates, and armed knights

succession.zh: 遵循皮雅斯特家族内部的分封继承与兄弟、父子分产传统，导致领地不断再分割并引发高级权争夺
succession.en: Succession followed Piast dynastic partition and patrimonial inheritance among brothers and sons, leading to repeated territorial subdivision and struggles over the seniorate

structure.zh: 1200年的波兰并非统一王国，而是由小波兰、弗罗茨瓦夫所在的西里西亚、大波兰、马佐夫舍、库亚维等皮雅斯特公国组成。克拉科夫的高级公爵在礼仪上具有优先地位，但其他公爵多独立施政。治理依托宫廷、总管、铸币与财政官、城堡长官以及教会网络。
structure.en: In 1200, Poland was not a unified kingdom but a set of Piast duchies including Lesser Poland, Silesia centered on Wroclaw, Greater Poland, Masovia, Kuyavia, and others. The senior duke in Krakow held ceremonial precedence, but the other dukes governed largely independently. Administration relied on ducal courts, palatines, chamberlains and treasury officers, castellans, and the ecclesiastical network.

function.zh: 辅佐公爵处理政务、军务协调、司法裁断与贵族关系
function.en: Assisted the duke in governance, military coordination, judicial rulings, and relations with the nobility

function.zh: 管理公爵庄园收入、关税、市场税、贡赋及地方铸币
function.en: Managed ducal domain income, customs, market dues, tribute, and local minting

function.zh: 监督要塞、征收属地赋役、维持地方治安并传达公爵命令
function.en: Oversaw strongholds, collected local dues and labor obligations, maintained order, and transmitted ducal commands

function.zh: 起草拉丁文文书、处理教会特权、地产确认与对外通信
function.en: Drafted Latin charters, handled church privileges, confirmed land rights, and managed external correspondence

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城堡区和公爵领地为核心，由城堡长官、总管及教会领主共同运作。各公国内部差异明显，西里西亚和小波兰的文书化程度较高，边缘地区更依赖习惯统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on castellanies and ducal estates, operated by castellans, stewards, and ecclesiastical lords. There were major internal differences among duchies; Silesia and Lesser Poland were more document-based, while peripheral regions relied more on customary rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以斯拉夫习惯法、王朝敕令、教会法和领主司法并存。重大争端常由公爵法庭或教会审理，土地、贡赋与封地义务是核心法律议题。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Slavic customary law, dynastic decrees, canon law, and seigneurial jurisdiction. Major disputes were often judged by ducal courts or ecclesiastical authorities, with landholding, tribute, and feudal obligations as central issues.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自公爵庄园产出、市场税、桥梁与关卡通行费、铸币收益、狩猎与林地权利、非常时期的军役与贡赋。统一全国税制尚不存在，各公国税负标准不一。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came mainly from ducal estates, market taxes, bridge and toll duties, mint profits, hunting and forest rights, and extraordinary military dues. No uniform kingdom-wide tax system existed, and burdens varied by duchy.

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教为主，罗马教会体系深入贵族与王朝合法性。修道院、主教座堂与圣徒崇拜在文化生活中居核心地位，乡村仍保留少量前基督教习俗残余。
religion.en: Latin Christianity predominated, with the Roman Church deeply embedded in noble society and dynastic legitimacy. Monasteries, cathedrals, and saint cults stood at the center of cultural life, while some pre-Christian customs still survived in rural practice.

philosophy.zh: 思想上受拉丁基督教神学、教会法观念与早期经院方法影响，政治观强调基督教君主职责、慈善施舍与维护教会秩序。
philosophy.en: Thought was shaped by Latin Christian theology, canon-law concepts, and early scholastic methods, emphasizing the duties of a Christian ruler, charity, and the maintenance of ecclesiastical order.

writingSystem.zh: 官方与教会书写主要使用拉丁字母和拉丁文，口语为早期波兰语及相关西斯拉夫方言；地名、人名与习惯法内容开始在拉丁文文书中得到更稳定记录。
writingSystem.en: Official and ecclesiastical writing used the Latin alphabet and Latin language, while spoken language consisted of early Polish and related West Slavic dialects; place names, personal names, and customary legal material began to be recorded more steadily in Latin charters.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括罗马式教堂与修道院建筑、主教座堂学校、编年史写作传统的延续、圣徒崇拜网络以及德意志法城市化的早期引入。波兰政治虽分裂，但教会组织与王朝记忆维持了共同文化空间。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included Romanesque churches and monasteries, cathedral schools, the continuation of chronicle writing, networks of saintly devotion, and the early introduction of urban institutions under German law. Though politically divided, Poland retained a shared cultural space through church organization and dynastic memory.

languageFamily.zh: 西斯拉夫语族
languageFamily.en: West Slavic

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和领主庄园为基础，整体属于中等偏低货币化水平。小波兰与西里西亚的贸易和采矿较活跃，大部分人口仍自给性耕作。分裂局势限制了统一市场的形成，但区域交换网络在增长。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture and seigneurial estates, with a modest degree of monetization. Trade and mining were more active in Lesser Poland and Silesia, while most people remained engaged in subsistence farming. Political fragmentation limited the formation of a unified market, but regional exchange networks were growing.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、盐业、铅银采掘、林木与蜂蜡采集、铁器锻造、皮革加工、地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, animal husbandry, salt production, lead and silver extraction, timber and wax collection, ironworking, leatherworking, and local crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、铅、白银、毛皮、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、木材、牲畜、谷物、布料、金属器具、琥珀和输入的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Salt, lead, silver, furs, wax, honey, timber, livestock, grain, cloth, metal goods, amber, and imported luxury wares

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、农具和储粮；贵族与教会财富集中于庄园、磨坊、林地、盐井、什一税和市场权。城镇居民财富开始增加，但规模仍有限。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in land-use rights, livestock, tools, and stored grain; noble and ecclesiastical wealth was concentrated in estates, mills, forests, salt works, tithes, and market rights. Urban wealth was growing but remained limited in scale.

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园经济、领主控制和地方市场交换为基础，辅以逐步增长的城市商业与德意志移民殖垦。经济制度兼具自然经济和初步货币经济特征。
economicSystem.en: The economic system rested on manorial production, lordly control, and local market exchange, supplemented by growing urban commerce and colonization by German settlers. It combined features of a natural economy with an emerging monetary economy.

source.zh: 公爵庄园与直属地产收入
source.en: Ducal demesne and direct estate income

treasuryDescription.zh: 由于各公国财政分散，并不存在单一国家金库。克拉科夫、西里西亚和大波兰等地分别保有公爵银储、贡品、器物和教会借贷能力。1200年前后因内战与动员需要，财政缓冲有限。
treasuryDescription.en: Because finances were dispersed among the duchies, there was no single state treasury. Krakow, Silesia, and Greater Poland each maintained ducal silver reserves, tribute goods, valuables, and access to church credit. Around 1200, fiscal buffers were limited due to internal conflict and mobilization needs.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持公爵家产、战争动员和教会赞助，更多依赖庄园与特权收入而非统一普遍税。为争夺支持，公爵常授予教会和移民聚落减免，长期削弱集中财力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized preservation of ducal patrimony, war mobilization, and church patronage, relying more on estate and privilege income than on universal taxation. To win support, dukes often granted exemptions to churches and settler communities, weakening long-term fiscal centralization.

description.zh: 由公爵随从、贵族骑士及披甲骑兵构成，是战场冲击核心，也是内战中最关键的机动力量。
description.en: Composed of ducal retainers, noble knights, and armored cavalry, this was the main striking arm on the battlefield and the crucial mobile force in internal wars.

description.zh: 负责守备主要城堡、桥渡和集市，多由步战侍从、属民和地方征发兵构成。
description.en: Responsible for defending major strongholds, crossings, and market centers, they were made up of foot retainers, dependents, and locally levied troops.

description.zh: 由各公国在战时召集的自由民、附庸和村社兵员组成，训练不均，适合短期会战、防御和围城勤务。
description.en: Raised by the duchies in wartime from freemen, vassals, and village communities, this force was unevenly trained and suited for short campaigns, defense, and siege labor.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令；名义上由克拉科夫高级公爵优先指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander in chief; nominal precedence belonged to the senior duke of Krakow

technology.zh: 军事技术属中世纪拉丁欧洲常规水平，重视锁子甲、长枪、剑、盾、弩和攻城器械。城堡防御体系逐渐强化，但尚未形成高度统一的军制和后勤体系。
technology.en: Military technology matched the standard of medieval Latin Europe, emphasizing mail armor, lances, swords, shields, crossbows, and siege engines. Castle defenses were strengthening, but there was still no highly unified military system or logistics structure.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自皮雅斯特诸公之间的内战与高级权斗争，其次是西方德意志诸侯与边境扩张压力、北方普鲁士和波美拉尼亚边地冲突，以及与波希米亚和匈牙利势力相关的外交军事竞争。
threats.en: The main threats came from civil wars among the Piast dukes and struggles over the seniorate, followed by pressure from western German princes and frontier expansion, conflicts with Prussian and Pomeranian borderlands in the north, and diplomatic-military rivalry involving Bohemia and Hungary.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于维斯瓦河与奥得河流域农业地带，小波兰、大波兰和西里西亚最为稠密，马佐夫舍与边境林地较为稀疏。总体仍以乡村人口为主，但若干公国正在经历殖垦与城镇增长。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the agricultural zones of the Vistula and Oder basins, with Lesser Poland, Greater Poland, and Silesia the most densely settled, while Masovia and forest frontiers were more thinly populated. The population remained overwhelmingly rural, though some duchies were experiencing colonization and urban growth.

description.zh: 波兰古都
description.en: Ancient capital of Poland

socialClasses.zh: 上层为皮雅斯特诸公与高级教士，其次为地方贵族和骑士；中层包括教士、城镇工匠、商人和部分自由农；下层则为依附农民、庄园劳作者和边地居民。各地德意志移民社群与犹太商人群体规模尚小但日益重要。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the Piast dukes and high clergy, followed by regional nobles and knights; the middle strata included clerics, urban artisans, merchants, and some free peasants; the lower strata consisted of dependent peasants, estate laborers, and frontier populations. German settler communities and Jewish merchant groups were still small but increasingly important.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友体系；各公国分别与匈牙利王国、神圣罗马帝国内个别诸侯、教廷或邻近波美拉尼亚统治者结盟
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; individual duchies separately allied with the Kingdom of Hungary, particular princes within the Holy Roman Empire, the Papacy, or neighboring Pomeranian rulers

enemies.zh: 主要敌对关系源于诸公国内部争斗；外部则与部分德意志边侯、波希米亚势力、异教普鲁士人和不稳定的波美拉尼亚边地存在冲突
enemies.en: The principal hostile relationships arose from internal struggles among the duchies; externally there were conflicts with some German border lords, Bohemian power, pagan Prussians, and the unstable Pomeranian frontier

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策缺乏统一方向，以王朝婚姻、教会支持、边境争夺和临时军事协定为主。各公爵既寻求对外承认，也利用外援介入内部继承与高级权竞争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy lacked a unified direction and centered on dynastic marriages, church backing, frontier contests, and temporary military agreements. Individual dukes sought external recognition while also using foreign support in internal succession and senioral disputes.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键发展包括更成熟的罗马式石建筑、城堡体系强化、拉丁文文书行政扩展、矿冶与盐业改进、德意志法城市制度传播，以及农业拓殖与重犁、轮作等中世纪中欧技术的持续应用。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included more mature Romanesque stone construction, stronger castle systems, expanded Latin charter administration, improvements in mining and salt production, the spread of urban institutions under German law, and the continuing use of Central European medieval techniques such as heavy plough agriculture and crop rotation.

strengths.zh: 皮雅斯特诸公国拥有共同王朝传统、拉丁教会网络、较坚实的农业基础，以及位于中欧贸易通道上的战略位置。西里西亚和小波兰的资源、矿业和城镇化潜力尤其突出。
strengths.en: The Piast duchies possessed a shared dynastic tradition, the Latin Church network, a solid agricultural base, and a strategic position on Central European trade routes. The resources, mining, and urban potential of Silesia and Lesser Poland were especially significant.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治分裂、继承制度导致的重复内战、财政和军力分散，以及高级权缺乏稳定合法执行机制。各公国难以形成统一的边防、税制和外交战略。
weaknesses.en: The greatest weaknesses were political fragmentation, recurrent civil war caused by the succession system, dispersed finances and military power, and the lack of a stable mechanism for enforcing senioral authority. The duchies struggled to create unified frontier defense, taxation, or diplomatic strategy.

outlook.zh: 短期内局势仍将以王朝竞争和局部整合反复交替。若某一强势公支获得教会与贵族支持，并掌握克拉科夫、经济核心区和婚姻联盟，未来存在重新统一波兰的可能；否则分裂状态将持续并加深外部干预。
outlook.en: In the short term, the situation would likely continue to alternate between dynastic rivalry and limited regional consolidation. If one strong Piast branch secured church and noble backing while controlling Krakow, the economic core regions, and marriage alliances, reunification of Poland would be possible; otherwise fragmentation would persist and invite deeper external intervention.

description.zh: 波兰处于地方分封时期，各公国共存且围绕高级公位竞争。
description.en: Poland was in a period of territorial fragmentation, with duchies coexisting and contesting senior authority.

governmentForm.zh: 基督教封建君主制，兼具王家县制与教会特权
governmentForm.en: Christian feudal monarchy combining royal county administration with ecclesiastical privileges

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、王族与大贵族、高级教士、王家侍从与城堡武士、自由民、特权移民社群、农民与少量奴婢后裔构成；地方社会以县、主教区、修道院庄园与贵族领地为核心
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the king, royal kin and magnates, high clergy, royal servientes and castle warriors, freemen, privileged immigrant communities, peasants, and a small remnant of servile dependents; local life centered on counties, bishoprics, monastic estates, and noble lordships

rulingClass.zh: 阿尔帕德王室、世俗大贵族、主教与修道院院长共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Árpád royal house, secular magnates, bishops, and major abbots

succession.zh: 阿尔帕德王朝世袭君主制，以王室男性成员继承为原则，但常受教宗承认、贵族支持与王室内部竞争影响
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy within the Árpád dynasty, generally favoring male dynastic succession but often shaped by papal recognition, noble support, and intra-dynastic rivalry

structure.zh: 王国由国王统治，中央依托宫廷与王室官职，地方则通过王家县、城堡辖区、采邑和教会领地管理。1200年前后，王权仍较强，但大贵族和教会享有日益扩大的地产与司法权
