structure.en: The kingdom was ruled by the king, with central authority operating through the royal court and household offices, while the local realm was administered through royal counties, castle districts, benefices, and ecclesiastical estates. Around 1200, royal power remained strong, though magnates and the Church possessed growing landed and judicial authority

function.zh: 辅佐国王处理朝廷事务、仲裁贵族纠纷、在国王缺席时代表王权
function.en: Assisted the king in court affairs, arbitrated noble disputes, and represented royal authority in the king's absence

function.zh: 管理王室庄园、关卡、铸币收益、盐业与王室收入征收
function.en: Managed royal estates, tolls, mint revenues, salt operations, and collection of royal income

function.zh: 起草敕令、颁发特许状、保存王国文书并处理外交文牍
function.en: Drafted charters, issued privileges, preserved royal records, and handled diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 通过郡长、法官与王家官员执行王室司法、治安与征发
function.en: Enforced royal justice, order, and levies through ispáns, judges, and royal officers

function.zh: 协调主教区、修道院、什一税争议与王权对教会任命的影响
function.en: Coordinated with bishoprics and monasteries, addressed tithe disputes, and managed royal influence over church appointments

function.zh: 组织边境防务、召集城堡军、贵族军役及辅助部队
function.en: Organized frontier defense and mobilized castle troops, noble service, and auxiliary forces

localAdmin.zh: 地方以王家县为基础，由郡长管理城堡、司法、税役与军事义务；此外存在主教辖区、修道院领地、王后领地与贵族庄园。边境地带设置较灵活的防御区，并吸纳佩切涅格人、塞克伊人及其他军役社群
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on royal counties headed by ispáns, who managed castles, justice, dues, and military obligations; there were also bishoprics, monastic lands, queenly estates, and noble lordships. Frontier zones were organized more flexibly and included Pechenegs, Székelys, and other military service communities

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合基督教王法、王室敕令、习惯法与教会法。重大案件由国王、总管或高级法官审理，地方诉讼多在县、教会法庭和领主法庭进行
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Christian royal law, royal decrees, customary law, and canon law. Major cases were judged by the king, palatine, or senior judges, while local suits were commonly heard in county assemblies, ecclesiastical courts, and seigneurial courts

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要依赖王室地产、关税、渡口税、市场税、铸币收益、盐税及非常征发，而非统一的人头税体系。教会征收什一税，贵族与教会特权使税负分布不均
taxationSystem.en: State income relied chiefly on royal domains, customs, ferry tolls, market dues, mint profits, salt revenues, and extraordinary levies rather than a uniform poll-tax system. The Church collected tithes, and noble and ecclesiastical privileges made the tax burden unevenly distributed

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，王权与主教团紧密合作；同时王国内仍有东仪基督徒、犹太人和穆斯林商人社群，受特许或限制并存
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the state religion, with close cooperation between monarchy and episcopate; the kingdom also contained Eastern Christians, Jews, and Muslim merchant communities, who existed under a mix of privileges and restrictions

philosophy.zh: 思想基础以拉丁基督教世界观为主，重视王权神授、修道传统、教会法与早期经院学影响；政治伦理强调国王护教与维持和平正义的责任
philosophy.en: Its intellectual foundations were rooted in the Latin Christian worldview, stressing divine kingship, monastic tradition, canon law, and early scholastic influence; political ethics emphasized the king's duty to defend the faith and uphold peace and justice

writingSystem.zh: 以拉丁字母书写拉丁文为官方文书语言；匈牙利语广泛用于口语社会，但尚未形成成熟的国家文书标准
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet and Latin language were used for official documentation; Hungarian was widely spoken in society but had not yet developed into a mature state documentary standard

culturalAchievements.zh: 11至12世纪匈牙利完成基督教国家建构，建立主教区与修道院网络，兴建罗马式教堂与王室建筑，发展拉丁文编年史与圣徒崇拜传统，并在中欧、巴尔干与拉丁基督教世界之间形成文化中介地位
culturalAchievements.en: By the 11th and 12th centuries Hungary had completed its construction as a Christian kingdom, establishing bishoprics and monastic networks, building Romanesque churches and royal architecture, fostering Latin chronicles and saint cults, and serving as a cultural intermediary between Central Europe, the Balkans, and the wider Latin Christian world

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属乌拉尔语系芬兰-乌戈尔分支的匈牙利语；行政与教会使用印欧语系意大利分支的拉丁语，境内亦有斯拉夫语、德语和罗曼语社群
languageFamily.en: The principal vernacular belonged to the Uralic family, Finno-Ugric branch, namely Hungarian; administration and the Church used Indo-European Italic Latin, while Slavic, German, and Romance-speaking communities were also present

gdpDescription.zh: 匈牙利王国经济以农业、畜牧、盐业和过境贸易为基础，在中欧属于较强王国经济，但整体货币化程度仍有限。喀尔巴阡盆地的粮食、牲畜、葡萄酒、蜂蜡、皮革和金属资源支持王权与贵族财富，尤其盐矿和交通税对王室财政意义重大
gdpDescription.en: The Hungarian economy rested on agriculture, stockbreeding, salt extraction, and transit trade, making it one of the stronger royal economies in Central Europe, though monetization remained limited overall. The grain, livestock, wine, wax, leather, and metal resources of the Carpathian Basin underpinned royal and aristocratic wealth, with salt mines and transport tolls especially important for the crown

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧业、葡萄种植与酿酒、盐矿开采、铁与贵金属采掘、林业、手工业、河运与商贸
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, animal husbandry, viticulture and wine production, salt mining, iron and precious-metal extraction, forestry, crafts, river transport, and commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口牲畜、马匹、皮革、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、盐、葡萄酒、木材和部分金属；进口高档纺织品、武器、奢侈品、香料和教会用品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included cattle, horses, hides, wax, honey, salt, wine, timber, and some metals; imports included luxury textiles, weapons, luxury wares, spices, and ecclesiastical goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地使用权、牲畜、农具、谷仓存粮与家庭劳力；贵族和教会则以庄园、磨坊、葡萄园、税权与依附人口积累财富
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, livestock, tools, grain stores, and family labor; noble and ecclesiastical wealth rested on estates, mills, vineyards, fiscal rights, and dependent populations

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园农业和王室地产经济为核心的封建混合经济，辅以市场交换、铸币流通、过境贸易和受特许的城市商业
economicSystem.en: A feudal mixed economy centered on manorial agriculture and royal domain management, supplemented by market exchange, coined money circulation, transit trade, and privileged urban commerce

source.en: Customs, ferry tolls, and market dues

source.zh: 司法罚金与封建附加贡赋

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室库藏以现钱、金银器、教会赞助可调动资源、盐货与庄园实物储备构成。由于收入很大部分来自地产与实物征收，财政流动性受年份与军务影响明显
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury consisted of coin, precious metal vessels, mobilizable resources tied to ecclesiastical patronage, salt stocks, and in-kind reserves from estates. Because much income came from land and in-kind exactions, fiscal liquidity varied significantly with harvests and military needs

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王室地产控制、保护盐业与交通税源、适度发行和更新货币、通过赏赐换取忠诚为核心；在稳定年份尽量保持结余，以应对边疆战争与王朝继承风险
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on preserving control of royal domains, protecting salt revenues and transport tolls, managing coin issue and renewal, and exchanging patronage for loyalty; in stable years the crown sought modest surpluses to meet frontier wars and dynastic succession risks

description.zh: 由城堡武士、驻防人员和县内征发者组成，是王权最直接控制的军事骨干
description.en: Composed of castle warriors, garrison personnel, and county levies, forming the military core most directly controlled by the crown

description.zh: 由大贵族、小贵族和王家侍从提供，以重装与中装骑兵为主，是野战主力
description.en: Provided by magnates, lesser nobles, and royal servientes, consisting chiefly of heavy and medium cavalry and serving as the main field force

description.zh: 用于守城、运输、筑垒与短期作战，战斗力低于骑士与专业驻军
description.en: Used for garrison duty, transport, fortification work, and short-term campaigning, with combat value below that of knights and professional garrisons

description.zh: 包括塞克伊人、佩切涅格人、库曼前驱群体及其他边民骑射与侦察力量，擅长巡逻、追击和警戒
description.en: Included Székelys, Pechenegs, early Cuman elements, and other frontier horse-archer and scouting forces skilled in patrol, pursuit, and screening

description.zh: 由主教、修道院领地和附庸领主按义务提供，规模有限但在会战时可补充兵力
description.en: Supplied by bishops, monastic estates, and vassal lords according to obligation; limited in scale but useful in major campaigns

commanderInChief.zh: 国王伊姆雷克
commanderInChief.en: King Emeric

technology.zh: 军事技术属12世纪末至13世纪初中欧水平，以骑枪冲击、锁子甲、圆盾与风筝盾、长矛、剑、弩和复合弓并用；防御依赖土木与石制城堡、边境堡垒和河线据点
technology.en: Military technology matched the Central European level of the late 12th and early 13th centuries, combining lance cavalry shock with mail armor, round and kite shields, spears, swords, crossbows, and composite bows; defense relied on earth-and-timber and stone castles, frontier forts, and river strongpoints

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与保加利亚的巴尔干竞争、拜占庭事务牵连、边疆草原民族流动以及王室内部继承纷争引发的贵族军事动员；1200年时蒙古尚未直接威胁王国，但欧亚草原形势正在变化
threats.en: The principal threats came from Balkan rivalry with Bulgaria, entanglement in Byzantine affairs, frontier steppe population movements, and noble mobilization linked to dynastic disputes; in 1200 the Mongols did not yet directly threaten the kingdom, though the Eurasian steppe situation was changing

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口约二百万，主体分布于多瑙河与蒂萨河流域、外多瑙地区、上匈牙利山地和特兰西瓦尼亚盆地。族群构成复杂，包括匈牙利人、斯拉夫人、德意志移民、瓦拉几亚前身群体、犹太人及穆斯林商人社群
populationDescription.en: The population was about two million, concentrated in the Danube and Tisza basins, Transdanubia, Upper Hungary, and the Transylvanian basin. The kingdom was ethnically mixed, including Hungarians, Slavs, German settlers, proto-Vlach groups, Jews, and Muslim merchant communities

socialClasses.zh: 王室与大贵族位于顶层，其次为主教和修道院领袖；其下是王家侍从、小贵族、城堡武士、自由移民与市民、边境军役社群，底层为广大农民与佃户。奴役关系已显著弱化，但人身依附和地租劳役仍普遍存在
socialClasses.en: The royal house and magnates stood at the top, followed by bishops and monastic leaders; beneath them were royal servientes, lesser nobles, castle warriors, free settlers and townsmen, and frontier service communities, while the broad base consisted of peasants and tenants. Classical slavery had greatly weakened, but personal dependence and rent-labor obligations remained common

allies.zh: 与教宗、部分德意志诸侯、婚姻联系下的中欧王室及若干十字军势力保持合作；在亚得里亚海和巴尔干事务中也与地方盟友临时结盟
allies.en: Hungary maintained cooperation with the papacy, some German princes, Central European dynasties linked by marriage, and certain crusading forces; in Adriatic and Balkan affairs it also entered temporary alliances with local partners

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括第二保加利亚帝国，以及在达尔马提亚、克罗地亚和巴尔干影响力问题上发生冲突的拜占庭与地方势力；与威尼斯也常因沿海利益产生摩擦
enemies.en: Its main opponents included the Second Bulgarian Empire, along with Byzantine and regional forces competing over influence in Dalmatia, Croatia, and the Balkans; Venice was also a recurrent rival over coastal interests

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护喀尔巴阡盆地统治、控制克罗地亚并影响达尔马提亚、干预巴尔干继承与边境事务、维持与罗马教廷的良好关系为主。王国在东欧草原、巴尔干与拉丁西欧之间采取务实平衡外交
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving control over the Carpathian Basin, retaining Croatia and influence in Dalmatia, intervening in Balkan succession and frontier affairs, and maintaining good relations with the papacy. The kingdom pursued pragmatic balancing diplomacy between the East European steppe, the Balkans, and Latin Western Europe

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 采用拉丁文书行政、罗马式石造建筑、改良犁耕与三圃制传播、河运与盐运组织化、铸币与王室财政技术逐步成熟；军事上发展骑士装备、边境骑射辅助战术与城堡网络
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included Latin documentary administration, Romanesque stone construction, wider use of improved plough agriculture and three-field practices, organized river and salt transport, and gradually more sophisticated minting and royal fiscal techniques; militarily, the kingdom developed knightly equipment, frontier horse-archer auxiliary tactics, and castle networks

strengths.zh: 地处多瑙与喀尔巴阡战略枢纽，土地肥沃，盐业与交通税提供稳固财政基础；王权在1200年前后仍具整合能力，能够动员可观骑兵与边境辅助力量，并在中欧与巴尔干事务中发挥重要影响
strengths.en: Hungary occupied a strategic Danubian-Carpathian crossroads, enjoyed fertile lands, and drew solid revenue from salt and transit tolls; around 1200 the crown still retained substantial integrative authority, could mobilize significant cavalry and frontier auxiliaries, and exercised major influence in Central European and Balkan affairs

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合依赖王室地产与个人效忠，行政文书化程度有限；大贵族和教会地产扩张削弱长期王权基础，边疆广阔且族群复杂，继承危机容易迅速转化为军事与财政压力
weaknesses.en: The kingdom’s cohesion depended heavily on royal domains and personal loyalty, while bureaucratic documentation remained limited; the expansion of magnate and ecclesiastical landholding weakened the long-term basis of royal power, and the broad, ethnically mixed frontiers meant that succession crises could quickly become military and fiscal strains

outlook.zh: 1200年的匈牙利王国总体稳定且仍属中欧强国，但未来数十年将面临王权与贵族关系重组、巴尔干竞争升级及草原世界剧变的挑战。若王室无法维持财政与军役控制，稳定性可能在下一代明显下降
outlook.en: In 1200 the Kingdom of Hungary was broadly stable and remained one of the stronger powers of Central Europe, but the coming decades would bring challenges from the restructuring of royal-magnate relations, intensified Balkan rivalry, and major upheavals in the steppe world. If the monarchy failed to preserve fiscal and military control, stability could deteriorate markedly in the next generation

description.zh: 中欧强国，控制喀尔巴阡盆地，并对巴尔干与多瑙流域保持影响。
description.en: A major Central European kingdom controlling the Carpathian Basin and influencing the Balkans and Danube region.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主集权为核心、依托地方贵族与军事封臣的东正教帝国
governmentForm.en: An Orthodox imperial monarchy centered on the tsar, supported by regional nobles and military vassals

socialStructure.zh: 社会由沙皇与王室、博雅尔贵族、高级教士、地方军政官、城市商人和手工业者、自由农民、依附农民及边地牧民构成；瓦拉几亚人与库曼人等边疆人群也在军事与贸易体系中占有一席之地。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the tsar and royal house, boyar nobility, senior clergy, provincial military-administrative elites, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependent peasants, and frontier pastoral groups; Vlachs and Cumans also held important places in the military and trade systems.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、博雅尔贵族和东正教高级教士共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The royal house, the boyar aristocracy, and the senior Orthodox clergy formed the ruling class

succession.zh: 名义上为王朝世袭，实际上常受王室内部争位、贵族拥立和军事功绩影响
succession.en: Nominally dynastic hereditary succession, but in practice often shaped by internal royal rivalry, aristocratic backing, and military success

structure.zh: 1200年的第二保加利亚帝国仍处于复兴与整合阶段。中央政权围绕沙皇宫廷、宗主教区前身的高级教会机构、王室书记官体系和地方总督网络运作。行政制度延续第一保加利亚帝国传统，同时吸收拜占庭宫廷与财政做法。
structure.en: In 1200 the Second Bulgarian Empire was still in a phase of revival and consolidation. The central government operated around the tsar's court, senior ecclesiastical institutions preceding the patriarchate, a royal chancery, and a network of provincial governors. Its administration continued First Bulgarian imperial traditions while also borrowing courtly and fiscal practices from Byzantium.

function.zh: 起草诏令、管理外交文书、确认土地授予与封赏、保管国家印玺
function.en: Drafted decrees, managed diplomatic correspondence, confirmed land grants and rewards, and kept the state seal

function.zh: 征收王室收入、管理贡赋、关税、盐税和战利品分配
function.en: Collected royal income and managed tribute, customs dues, salt taxes, and distribution of war booty

function.zh: 监督地方总督、防务、征兵、要塞补给与秩序维持
function.en: Supervised provincial governors, defense, mobilization, fortress supply, and maintenance of order

function.zh: 协调主教区、修道院、教产、司法庇护和宗教外交
function.en: Coordinated bishoprics, monasteries, church property, judicial sanctuary, and religious diplomacy

function.zh: 管理多瑙河渡口、山口、市场税和商旅通行费
function.en: Managed Danubian crossings, mountain passes, market taxes, and merchant transit tolls

localAdmin.zh: 地方由城堡长官、地区总督和贵族领主共同管理，重要城镇设驻军与税吏；偏远山区和边疆地带则更多依赖地方首领、军事盟约和贡赋关系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by fortress commanders, regional governors, and noble lords, with garrisons and tax officers in major towns; remote mountain and frontier areas relied more heavily on local chiefs, military compacts, and tributary relations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以斯拉夫—保加利亚习惯法、王室敕令、教会法及拜占庭法传统混合而成。贵族与教会享有较大司法特权，土地、军役、赋税和叛乱案件由王权重点干预。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Slavic-Bulgarian customary law, royal edicts, canon law, and Byzantine legal traditions. Nobility and church institutions enjoyed major judicial privileges, while the crown intervened strongly in matters of land, military service, taxation, and rebellion.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠农业地租、实物征收、关税、市场税、牲畜税、盐业收入、附庸贡赋和战时掠获。许多地区税收以粮食、酒、蜂蜜、羊毛和牲畜形式缴纳，而非纯粹货币化。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income relied mainly on agrarian rents, payments in kind, customs dues, market taxes, livestock taxes, salt income, tributary payments, and wartime plunder. In many areas taxes were paid in grain, wine, honey, wool, and animals rather than in fully monetized form.

religion.zh: 东正教基督教为主导信仰，同时边地存在保加尔旧习俗残余、库曼草原信仰和少量天主教接触影响。
religion.en: Eastern Orthodox Christianity was the dominant faith, though frontier regions still retained remnants of older Bulgar customs, Cuman steppe beliefs, and limited Catholic influence.

philosophy.zh: 政治与伦理思想以东正教王权观、修道主义、斯拉夫基督教书写传统和拜占庭神学为核心，强调正统信仰、君主护教职责与末世救赎观念。
philosophy.en: Political and moral thought centered on Orthodox kingship, monasticism, Slavic Christian literary traditions, and Byzantine theology, emphasizing right belief, the ruler's duty to defend the faith, and an eschatological vision of salvation.

writingSystem.zh: 以西里尔字母和教会斯拉夫语书写传统为主，行政和外交场合也受希腊文影响。
writingSystem.en: The civilization chiefly used the Cyrillic script and Church Slavonic literary tradition, while Greek also influenced administration and diplomacy.

culturalAchievements.zh: 特尔诺沃逐渐发展为宗教与文化中心；修道院抄写、圣像与壁画艺术、石砌教堂建筑、宫廷铭文传统和斯拉夫礼仪文学持续繁荣。国家复兴本身也被塑造成恢复保加利亚帝国荣耀的文化叙事。
culturalAchievements.en: Tarnovo was developing into a religious and cultural center; monastic manuscript copying, icon and mural art, stone church construction, court inscription traditions, and Slavic liturgical literature all continued to flourish. The state's revival itself was framed as a cultural restoration of Bulgarian imperial glory.

languageFamily.zh: 南斯拉夫语支，书面礼仪语言为教会斯拉夫语
languageFamily.en: South Slavic, with Church Slavonic as the principal written and liturgical language

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的保加利亚经济以农业为基础，但因其控制巴尔干山脉通道、多瑙河贸易节点与黑海内陆联系线，商业活力明显高于许多单纯内陆封建王国。国家复兴和战争扩张刺激了军需、筑城、牲畜贸易与战利物流通。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200 the Bulgarian economy was agrarian at its base, but its control of Balkan passes, Danubian trade points, and inland links to the Black Sea gave it commercial vitality above that of many purely inland feudal kingdoms. State revival and military expansion stimulated supply industries, fortification building, livestock trade, and the circulation of booty.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒酿造、畜牧、养蜂、木材采伐、盐业、铁器与武器锻造、皮革加工、纺织、石材建筑和河道贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine production, pastoralism, beekeeping, timber extraction, salt, ironworking and weapons forging, leatherworking, textiles, stone construction, and river trade

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、葡萄酒、蜂蜜、蜡、皮毛、牲畜、羊毛、木材、盐、金属制品、奴隶、马匹以及从拜占庭与地中海转运的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, honey, wax, furs, livestock, wool, timber, salt, metal goods, slaves, horses, and luxury goods transshipped from Byzantium and the Mediterranean

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以土地使用权、粮仓、牲畜、葡萄园、蜂箱和工具体现；城市商人和博雅尔则以金银器、进口织物、武器和奴仆显示财富。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth took the form of land-use rights, granaries, livestock, vineyards, beehives, and tools; urban merchants and boyars displayed wealth through precious metal objects, imported textiles, weapons, and servants.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业庄园、贡赋关系和王室再分配为基础，并由城镇市场、跨山口贸易和边疆军事经济补充的混合型中世纪经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed medieval economy based on agrarian estates, tributary relations, and royal redistribution, supplemented by town markets, trans-mountain trade, and a frontier military economy

source.en: Agrarian rents and taxes in kind
Customs, market taxes, and transit tolls
Salt, mineral income, and royal demesne

category.zh: 教会、修道院与宗教建筑

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以贵金属、贡品、织物、武器、粮食储备和牲畜等多种形式存在，不完全是铸币现金。战争中缴获的拜占庭器物和赎金对王室流动财力尤其重要。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed in mixed forms including precious metal, tribute goods, textiles, weapons, grain stores, and livestock rather than as coin alone. Captured Byzantine valuables and ransom payments were especially important to royal liquidity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向军事优先与机会主义增收：在战争胜利时扩大贡赋和掠获收入，在和平阶段巩固关税网络、控制要道并扶植王室直辖地产。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored military priority and opportunistic revenue expansion: increasing tribute and plunder after victories, while in peacetime consolidating customs networks, controlling strategic routes, and strengthening royal demesne holdings.

description.zh: 由王室近卫、博雅尔随从和具装备能力的骑士构成，是战场突击与指挥核心。
description.en: Composed of royal guards, boyar retainers, and well-equipped mounted elites, serving as the core striking and command arm on the battlefield.

description.zh: 包括城镇步兵、农兵、矛盾手、弓手和要塞驻军，擅长守城、山地作战与道路封锁。
description.en: Included town infantry, peasant levies, spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and fortress troops, effective in sieges, mountain warfare, and road interdiction.

description.zh: 由库曼盟军及边地骑兵组成，机动性强，适合侦察、袭扰、诱敌和追击。
description.en: Made up of Cuman allies and frontier horsemen, highly mobile and suitable for scouting, raiding, feigned retreat, and pursuit.

name.en: River Crossing and Logistical Support Units

description.zh: 负责多瑙河渡运、攻城器具运输、粮草转运和工事修筑。
description.en: Handled Danube crossings, transport of siege materials, supply movement, and field fortification works.

commanderInChief.zh: 沙皇卡洛扬
commanderInChief.en: Tsar Kaloyan

technology.zh: 军队技术结合拜占庭攻城传统、巴尔干山地防御经验和草原骑兵战法。装备包括锁子甲、长矛、复合弓、短剑、盾牌和攻城机械；并善于利用峡谷、关隘与诱伏战术。
technology.en: Military technology combined Byzantine siege traditions, Balkan mountain defense experience, and steppe cavalry methods. Equipment included mail armor, lances, composite bows, swords, shields, and siege engines; the army also excelled in using passes, defiles, and ambush tactics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自拜占庭帝国及其地方将领、匈牙利王国在多瑙河与贝尔格莱德方向的压力、拉丁十字军势力可能介入巴尔干，以及内部贵族离心与边疆联盟不稳。
threats.en: Major threats came from the Byzantine Empire and its regional commanders, pressure from the Kingdom of Hungary around the Danube and Belgrade, possible intervention by Latin crusading powers in the Balkans, and internal aristocratic centrifugalism together with unstable frontier alliances.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口约一百五十万，主要集中在多瑙河南岸、巴尔干山地间盆地、色雷斯北部和平原农业区。族群上以保加利亚—斯拉夫主体为核心，并包含瓦拉几亚人、希腊人、库曼人、阿尔巴尼亚山民、亚美尼亚与犹太商人等群体。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 1.5 million, concentrated mainly south of the Danube, in intermontane Balkan basins, northern Thrace, and arable plains. Ethnically it was centered on a Bulgar-Slavic majority while also including Vlachs, Greeks, Cumans, Albanian highlanders, and Armenian and Jewish merchant communities.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、博雅尔和高级教士；中层为地方军官、修道院管理者、商人和手工业者；下层为自由农民、依附农民、佃户、牧民与少量奴仆。边疆骑兵群体和异族盟友拥有比普通农民更高的军事流动性。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family, boyars, and senior clergy; the middle stratum included local officers, monastic administrators, merchants, and artisans; below them were free peasants, dependent peasants, tenants, pastoralists, and a limited servant/slave population. Frontier cavalry groups and ethnic allies enjoyed greater military mobility than ordinary peasants.

allies.zh: 主要依靠库曼部族联盟，并在需要时寻求与教廷、反拜占庭势力或局部巴尔干领主进行策略性合作。
allies.en: The empire relied chiefly on alliances with Cuman tribes and, when useful, sought tactical cooperation with the Papacy, anti-Byzantine forces, or regional Balkan lords.

enemies.zh: 首要对手是东罗马帝国；同时与匈牙利王国存在边境竞争，并警惕未来可能出现的拉丁十字军政权干涉。
enemies.en: Its primary rival was the Eastern Roman Empire; it also faced border competition with the Kingdom of Hungary and watched for possible future intervention by Latin crusader polities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交目标是巩固新复兴帝国的合法性，争取独立教会地位，扩大在马其顿、色雷斯与多瑙边境的影响，并通过灵活结盟削弱拜占庭主导地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to secure the legitimacy of the revived empire, obtain an independent church status, expand influence in Macedonia, Thrace, and the Danubian frontier, and weaken Byzantine predominance through flexible alliance-making.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 并无世界级原创科技突破，但在山地筑城、石砌教堂建筑、攻城技术、渡河组织、骑射战术整合和多语言文书治理方面表现成熟，体现出巴尔干与拜占庭世界的技术融合。
keyInnovations.en: There were no world-changing original inventions, but the empire showed mature capability in mountain fortification, stone church construction, siegecraft, river-crossing organization, integration of mounted archery tactics, and multilingual documentary administration, reflecting a technological fusion of Balkan and Byzantine practices.

strengths.zh: 国家复兴势头强劲，统治者富有军事才能，地理位置控制巴尔干通道，能够结合重骑兵、边疆轻骑兵与山地防御；政治宣传善于塑造恢复保加利亚帝国传统的合法性。
strengths.en: The state possessed strong revival momentum, a militarily capable ruler, and a strategic position commanding Balkan routes. It could combine heavy cavalry, frontier light horse, and mountain defense, while its political messaging effectively framed the restoration of Bulgarian imperial legitimacy.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权尚未完全稳固，贵族离心、地区差异和边疆盟友不稳定始终构成隐患；经济仍以农业和战时掠获为主，对持续内战或大规模外敌入侵较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was not yet fully secure, and aristocratic centrifugal tendencies, regional diversity, and unstable frontier allies remained persistent risks. The economy still depended heavily on agriculture and wartime booty, making it vulnerable to prolonged civil conflict or major external invasion.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后，保加利亚帝国前景总体乐观。若卡洛扬继续成功利用库曼骑兵、对外胜利和教会合法化手段，帝国有望迅速扩张并成为巴尔干最强权之一；但若王室继承危机或贵族叛离加剧，其扩张成果也可能迅速被削弱。
outlook.en: Around 1200 the empire's outlook was broadly favorable. If Kaloyan continued to exploit Cuman cavalry, foreign victories, and ecclesiastical legitimation effectively, Bulgaria could expand rapidly and become one of the strongest powers in the Balkans; however, dynastic crises or intensified aristocratic rebellion could quickly erode these gains.

description.zh: 新近复兴的巴尔干强国，正积极挑战拜占庭在地区的影响。
description.en: A recently revived Balkan power actively challenging Byzantine influence in the region.

governmentForm.zh: 由贵族家族主导的寡头共和制，名义上由总督统领，实权由各议事机构和商人贵族共享
governmentForm.en: An oligarchic republic dominated by patrician families, nominally headed by the Doge, with power shared among councils and merchant nobles

socialStructure.zh: 社会以商人贵族为上层，其下为城市自由民、工匠、船员、书记员、海外商站人员与教士，另有数量有限的家内仆役与奴隶；政治参与高度集中于显贵家族
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by merchant patricians, followed by urban freemen, artisans, sailors, clerks, overseas commercial agents, and clergy, with a limited number of household servants and slaves; political participation was highly concentrated in prominent families

rulingClass.zh: 威尼斯商人贵族与显贵家族，包括参与大议会和高级官职的门第
rulingClass.en: Venetian merchant patricians and notable families, including lineages participating in the Great Council and high office

succession.zh: 总督由选举产生而非世袭，选举受贵族家族协商、议事机构与政治妥协影响
succession.en: The Doge was elected rather than hereditary, with selection shaped by patrician negotiation, councils, and political compromise

structure.zh: 1200年前后的威尼斯政体由总督、总督顾问、人民大会遗存传统以及日益重要的大小议事机构共同构成。尽管制度尚未完全达到后世定型状态，但核心特征已十分明显：总督权力受誓约、顾问监督与贵族集体政治限制，外交、财政、司法和海外事务由专门官员与委员会分掌。
structure.en: Around 1200, the Venetian polity consisted of the Doge, ducal councillors, residual traditions of the popular assembly, and increasingly important major and minor councils. Although institutions had not yet assumed their later fully stabilized form, the core pattern was already clear: ducal authority was constrained by oaths, councillors, and collective patrician politics, while diplomacy, finance, justice, and overseas affairs were handled by specialized magistrates and committees.

function.zh: 协助总督处理政令、外交文书、誓约执行及日常统治事务，防止个人专断
function.en: Assisted the Doge in decrees, diplomatic correspondence, oath enforcement, and daily governance while restraining personal rule

function.zh: 征收关税、管理盐业专卖、监督公债和国家收入，是共和国财政基础之一
function.en: Collected customs, managed the salt monopoly, and supervised public debt and state revenues, forming a core basis of republican finance

function.zh: 负责舰船征调、船坞生产、武备储备、船员组织以及护航和作战准备
function.en: Oversaw ship requisition, dockyard production, armament reserves, crew organization, and convoy and war readiness

function.zh: 处理商业纠纷、海事案件、契约认证、遗产与民事诉讼，保障贸易秩序
function.en: Handled commercial disputes, maritime cases, contract authentication, inheritance, and civil suits, safeguarding trade order

name.zh: 海外商站与殖产事务署
name.en: Overseas Commercial Stations and Colonial Affairs Office

function.zh: 管理驻君士坦丁堡、达尔马提亚、亚得里亚海及东地中海各据点的官员、仓储和贸易权益
function.en: Managed officials, warehouses, and commercial privileges in Constantinople, Dalmatia, the Adriatic, and eastern Mediterranean outposts

localAdmin.zh: 威尼斯本岛与潟湖诸社区由教区、城市区划与专门官员共同管理；海外则依靠伯国总督、港务官、商站长和临时使节进行统治与协调。达尔马提亚沿岸、伊斯特拉以及亚得里亚海航线上的关键港口受到威尼斯直接或间接控制。
localAdmin.en: Venice proper and the lagoon communities were governed through parishes, urban districts, and specialized magistrates; overseas administration relied on rectors, port officials, commercial station chiefs, and temporary envoys. Key ports along Dalmatia, Istria, and the Adriatic routes were under direct or indirect Venetian control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合威尼斯习惯法、罗马法传统、商法实践与公证文书文化。海事契约、借贷、合伙和保险性质安排受到严格书面记录，法院重视证词、契据与商业惯例。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Venetian customary law, Roman legal tradition, mercantile practice, and a strong notarial culture. Maritime contracts, loans, partnerships, and insurance-like arrangements were carefully recorded in writing, and courts gave great weight to testimony, charters, and commercial custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖关税、港口费用、盐业收益、罚金、领地贡赋与临时摊派，而非广泛的土地税。国家可在战争或大型远征前向富裕市民和商人征收特别贷款或摊款。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on customs, port dues, salt income, fines, tributary payments from subject territories, and extraordinary levies rather than a broad land tax. In wartime or before major expeditions, the state could demand special loans or assessments from wealthy citizens and merchants.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教为主，正式隶属罗马教会，但威尼斯在宗教事务上保持强烈自治倾向。圣马可崇拜构成共和国身份核心之一，同时因长期对拜占庭和伊斯兰世界贸易往来而呈现出开放而务实的宗教环境。
religion.en: Latin Christianity predominated, formally under the Roman Church, yet Venice maintained a strong tendency toward ecclesiastical autonomy. The cult of Saint Mark formed a central part of republican identity, while long commercial contact with Byzantium and the Islamic world fostered an open and pragmatic religious atmosphere.

philosophy.zh: 主导思想并非抽象哲学体系，而是以基督教道德、罗马法理、商人实用主义与共和国共同体意识相结合。公共利益、契约信誉、海上秩序与政治平衡被视为核心价值。
philosophy.en: The dominant outlook was not a single abstract philosophy but a blend of Christian morality, Roman legal reasoning, mercantile pragmatism, and republican civic consciousness. Public utility, contractual trust, maritime order, and political balance were treated as core values.

writingSystem.zh: 采用拉丁字母，官方与公证文书主要使用拉丁文，日常城市生活和商业交流则广泛使用早期威尼斯意大利方言
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet; official and notarial documents were mainly written in Latin, while early Venetian Italian vernacular was widely used in daily urban and commercial life

culturalAchievements.zh: 威尼斯在1200年前后已形成独特的海洋城市文化，建筑上融合拉丁、西方、拜占庭与部分伊斯兰装饰风格；圣马可大教堂体现了这种跨地中海审美。公证制度、商业契约文化、航海知识积累及海外社群网络，也可视为其重要制度性文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: By around 1200, Venice had developed a distinctive maritime urban culture. Architecturally it blended Latin, western, Byzantine, and some Islamic decorative influences; Saint Mark's Basilica embodied this trans-Mediterranean aesthetic. The notarial system, commercial contract culture, accumulation of nautical knowledge, and overseas community networks were also major institutional cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系—意大利语族—罗曼语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European - Italic - Romance

gdpDescription.zh: 以人口规模而论，威尼斯并非大型农业帝国，但其人均产出与商业密度在同时代欧洲名列前茅。财富高度集中于航运、转口贸易、金融借贷、造船和高附加值手工业。其经济影响力远大于领土与人口规模。
gdpDescription.en: By population, Venice was not a large agrarian empire, but its per capita output and commercial density ranked among the highest in contemporary Europe. Wealth was concentrated in shipping, entrepôt trade, finance, shipbuilding, and high-value craft production. Its economic influence far exceeded its territorial and demographic scale.

mainIndustries.zh: 远洋贸易、航运服务、造船、盐业、玻璃制造、纺织品转运与加工、香料贸易、金属制品、公证与金融借贷
mainIndustries.en: Long-distance trade, shipping services, shipbuilding, salt production, glassmaking, textile transit and finishing, spice trade, metal goods, and notarial and financial lending

tradeGoods.zh: 香料、丝绸、棉布、胡椒、糖、盐、木材、奴隶、金属、毛织物、蜡、谷物、酒、橄榄油及奢侈工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Spices, silk, cotton cloth, pepper, sugar, salt, timber, slaves, metals, woolens, wax, grain, wine, olive oil, and luxury crafted goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大。显贵商人家族拥有船队、仓库、债权和乡间地产；中层家庭依赖工匠行会、港口劳作、零售和小规模投资；下层船员与劳工收入受贸易季节和战争波动影响显著。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Great merchant families possessed fleets, warehouses, credit claims, and rural estates; middling households relied on craft guilds, port labor, retail, and small-scale investment; lower sailors and laborers saw their incomes fluctuate sharply with trade seasons and war.

economicSystem.zh: 市场导向的商业经济，由共和国通过特许、关税、专卖和海军保护加以引导。私人与国家利益深度交织，战争、外交和贸易常通过契约与合资形式结合。
economicSystem.en: A market-oriented commercial economy guided by the republic through charters, customs, monopolies, and naval protection. Private and state interests were deeply intertwined, and war, diplomacy, and trade were often combined through contracts and joint ventures.

source.zh: 附属港口贡赋与司法罚金
source.en: Tribute from dependent ports and judicial fines

source.zh: 商业执照、文书与公证收费
source.en: Commercial licenses, documents, and notarial fees
Extraordinary levies and public loan subscriptions

category.zh: 公共工程、港湾与船坞维护
category.en: Public works, harbor, and dockyard maintenance

category.zh: 宗教仪式、施济与礼仪开支
category.en: Religious ceremonies, charity, and ceremonial expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非单纯以现银储存，而是由现金、金银器、征税权、待收债权、盐务收益与可迅速动员的商人信贷共同构成。威尼斯的真正财政力量在于其信用、税关控制和调动私人资本的能力。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not merely a pile of specie, but a combination of cash, precious plate, tax claims, receivables, salt income, and merchant credit that could be mobilized quickly. Venice's true fiscal strength lay in its credit, customs control, and ability to harness private capital.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调维持贸易畅通、避免压垮商人阶层、在战争时通过贷款和临时征收迅速筹资，并将国家财政与商业扩张绑定。相较封建王国，威尼斯更依赖流通税与信用融资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized keeping trade flowing, avoiding excessive burdens on the merchant class, rapidly raising funds through loans and extraordinary levies in wartime, and tying state finance to commercial expansion. Compared with feudal kingdoms, Venice relied more on transaction taxes and credit finance.

description.zh: 共和国军力核心，由战船船员、弩手、海军步兵和船上军官组成，适合护航、封锁、夺港和两栖行动
description.en: The core of republican power, composed of warship crews, crossbowmen, naval infantry, and shipboard officers, suited for convoy duty, blockade, port seizure, and amphibious operations

description.zh: 由本地公民、港口人员和武装船只组成，负责威尼斯本岛、防火、防突袭与紧急动员
description.en: Made up of local citizens, port personnel, and armed craft, responsible for Venice proper, fire protection, anti-raid defense, and emergency mobilization

description.zh: 主要用于达尔马提亚、陆上据点和海外远征，在需要时从意大利本土、伊斯特拉或巴尔干招募
description.en: Used mainly in Dalmatia, mainland positions, and overseas expeditions, recruited as needed from the Italian mainland, Istria, or the Balkans

commanderInChief.zh: 恩里科·丹多洛总督为名义最高统帅，海上行动由受任命的舰队司令与高级贵族指挥
commanderInChief.en: Doge Enrico Dandolo served as nominal commander-in-chief, while naval operations were directed by appointed fleet commanders and senior patricians

technology.zh: 威尼斯军事技术优势主要在造船、海图经验、港口后勤、弩和海上运输组织，而非重骑兵数量。其桨帆战船、海运补给和舰队调度能力在拉丁欧洲极为突出。
technology.en: Venetian military technology was strongest in shipbuilding, navigational experience, harbor logistics, crossbows, and sea transport organization rather than in heavy cavalry numbers. Its oared war galleys, maritime supply, and fleet coordination were exceptional in Latin Europe.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自热那亚和比萨等海上竞争者、亚得里亚海沿岸反抗、拜占庭关系变化、匈牙利对达尔马提亚的影响，以及十字军政治带来的不确定性。对穆斯林东地中海强权的竞争更多体现为贸易与航线控制，而非持续全面战争。
threats.en: Major threats came from maritime rivals such as Genoa and Pisa, resistance along the Adriatic littoral, shifts in relations with Byzantium, Hungarian influence over Dalmatia, and the uncertainties created by crusading politics. Competition with Muslim eastern Mediterranean powers was more often expressed in trade and route control than in constant total war.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 拜占庭海战秘密武器，水上不灭之火
description.en: Byzantine naval secret weapon, inextinguishable fire on water

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 约11万人主要分布于威尼斯本岛、潟湖岛屿及其紧密联系的港务社区。就中世纪欧洲城市而言，这一规模极为可观，使威尼斯跻身最重要的大城市与商业中心之列。
populationDescription.en: About 110,000 people were concentrated in Venice proper, the lagoon islands, and closely linked port communities. For medieval Europe, this was a very large urban concentration, placing Venice among the foremost great cities and commercial centers.

description.zh: 中世纪海洋贸易帝国，水上之城
description.en: Medieval maritime trading empire, city on water

socialClasses.zh: 上层为商人贵族与高级官员；中层包括船主、工匠师傅、公证人、医生、零售商和教士；下层由船员、码头工、手工业工人、仆役与外来劳工组成。海外侨民和暂居商人也是人口结构的重要组成部分。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of merchant patricians and high officials; the middle included shipowners, master artisans, notaries, physicians, retailers, and clergy; the lower stratum comprised sailors, dockworkers, craft laborers, servants, and migrant workers. Overseas expatriates and transient merchants were also an important part of the social structure.

allies.zh: 与教廷、十字军诸侯、部分亚得里亚海城市及拉丁基督教商业伙伴保持务实合作；在需要时也与拜占庭、穆斯林港口政权达成条约
allies.en: Maintained pragmatic cooperation with the Papacy, crusader lords, certain Adriatic cities, and Latin Christian commercial partners; when useful, it also concluded treaties with Byzantium and Muslim port powers

enemies.zh: 主要对手为热那亚、比萨等意大利海上共和国；在特定时段也与拜占庭地方势力、达尔马提亚叛乱城市及挑战其航线控制者发生冲突
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were the Italian maritime republics of Genoa and Pisa; at particular moments it also clashed with Byzantine local powers, rebellious Dalmatian cities, and others contesting its control of sea routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策高度务实，以保障通商特权、港口准入、航线安全和商业侨民利益为优先。共和国乐于利用十字军运动、帝国纷争和地区战争扩大合同、赔偿、关税减免与殖民据点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was intensely pragmatic, prioritizing trading privileges, port access, route security, and the interests of merchant communities abroad. The republic readily exploited crusades, imperial disputes, and regional wars to enlarge its contracts, indemnities, customs exemptions, and colonial footholds.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 标准化船舶建造、复杂海事契约、公证信用文书、远程商业网络管理、武装商船组织、弩兵海战运用以及高效港口仓储和转运体系
keyInnovations.en: Standardized ship construction, sophisticated maritime contracts, notarized credit instruments, long-distance commercial network management, organization of armed merchant shipping, naval use of crossbowmen, and efficient port warehousing and transshipment systems

strengths.zh: 威尼斯的最大优势在于海上机动、商业资本、制度化信用、熟练造船业和将国家利益与商人利益结合的能力。其人口虽不庞大，却能凭借舰队、外交和金融在地中海施加远超体量的影响。
strengths.en: Venice's greatest strengths lay in maritime mobility, commercial capital, institutionalized credit, skilled shipbuilding, and its ability to align state interests with merchant interests. Though not demographically large, it could project influence across the Mediterranean far beyond its size through fleets, diplomacy, and finance.

weaknesses.zh: 其脆弱性在于粮食与原材料依赖外部输入、陆军薄弱、政治权力集中于少数家族、贸易高度依赖国际局势，以及任何大规模海上失败都可能严重损害财政与信誉。
weaknesses.en: Its vulnerabilities lay in dependence on imported grain and raw materials, weak land forces, political concentration in a small number of families, heavy reliance on international conditions, and the fact that any major maritime failure could badly damage both finance and credit.

outlook.zh: 1200年的威尼斯前景极为强劲。凭借总督恩里科·丹多洛的强势领导和共和国成熟的商业—海军体制，威尼斯即将深度介入第四次十字军并从东地中海权力重组中获取巨大收益。但这种扩张也将加深其与拜占庭及其他海上强国的长期敌意。
outlook.en: In 1200, Venice's outlook was exceptionally strong. Under the forceful leadership of Doge Enrico Dandolo and with a mature commercial-naval system, Venice was about to become deeply involved in the Fourth Crusade and extract enormous gains from the restructuring of power in the eastern Mediterranean. Yet this expansion would also deepen its long-term enmity with Byzantium and rival maritime powers.

description.zh: 地中海最具活力的商贸海上共和国之一，即将深度介入第四次十字军。
description.en: One of the most dynamic commercial maritime republics of the Mediterranean, soon to play a decisive role in the Fourth Crusade.

governmentForm.zh: 由教宗直接统治的拉丁基督教神权君主制，兼具宗教与世俗主权
governmentForm.en: A Latin Christian theocratic monarchy ruled directly by the pope, combining spiritual and temporal sovereignty

socialStructure.zh: 社会由高级教士、罗马与地方贵族、城市公社精英、骑士、商人、工匠、农民与佃户构成；教会机构在社会组织、教育、慈善与司法中具有核心地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of high clergy, Roman and provincial nobles, urban communal elites, knights, merchants, artisans, peasants, and tenants; ecclesiastical institutions played a central role in social organization, education, charity, and justice.

rulingClass.zh: 教宗、枢机主教团、高级教士、教廷法官与财政官员，以及与教廷合作的罗马和意大利中部贵族家族
rulingClass.en: The pope, the College of Cardinals, senior clergy, papal judges and financial officers, and Roman and central Italian noble families cooperating with the curia

succession.zh: 教宗由枢机主教选举产生，非世袭；世俗领地随教宗职位而转移，由新任教宗继承其统治权
succession.en: The pope was chosen by election of the cardinals rather than hereditary succession; temporal rule passed with the papal office to the newly elected pope

structure.zh: 1200年的教皇国以教廷为核心，中央政务由教宗、枢机主教、教廷法院与财政机关主持；地方则通过总督、侯任使节、主教与城市公社的协作来治理拉齐奥、翁布里亚、罗马涅与马尔凯等地区。
structure.en: In 1200 the Papal States were centered on the Roman curia, with central administration conducted by the pope, cardinals, papal courts, and fiscal offices; local rule in Lazio, Umbria, Romagna, and the Marches depended on governors, papal legates, bishops, and cooperation with urban communes.

function.zh: 起草、认证并发布教宗敕书、特许状与官方文书，维持教宗对基督教世界与本土领地的法理权威
function.en: Drafted, authenticated, and issued papal bulls, privileges, and official documents, sustaining papal legal authority over Christendom and the pope's own lands

function.zh: 管理税收、贡赋、圣座地产、通行费、司法罚金与封建收入，并监督库藏与财政记录
function.en: Managed taxation, dues, papal estates, tolls, judicial fines, and feudal revenues, while overseeing the treasury and fiscal records

function.zh: 审理教会法与部分世俗争端，处理上诉、封建权利、财产纠纷与教会纪律案件
function.en: Heard canon-law and some secular disputes, handling appeals, feudal rights, property conflicts, and ecclesiastical discipline

function.zh: 向各地派遣使节、总督与地方官，执行教宗命令，维持治安、征税并约束地方贵族与城市公社
function.en: Dispatched legates, rectors, and local officers to enforce papal orders, maintain order, collect revenues, and restrain local nobles and communes

name.zh: 圣伯多禄产业管理机构

function.zh: 经营圣伯多禄产业、农业地产、林地、磨坊与租佃收益，是教皇国领地财政的重要基础
function.en: Administered the Patrimony of St. Peter, including estates, farmland, woodland, mills, and tenancy revenues, forming a key base of papal territorial finance

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政并不完全统一。罗马由教宗与城市贵族、行政官及教会机构共同影响；外省通常由教宗任命的总督、使节或地方主教监督，同时必须与佩鲁贾、奥尔维耶托、维泰博等自治城市的公社机构谈判。某些地区名义上臣服教宗，但实际控制力因贵族叛乱与帝国干预而时强时弱。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was not fully uniform. Rome was shaped by the pope alongside urban nobles, civic officers, and ecclesiastical institutions; the provinces were usually supervised by papal rectors, legates, or bishops, who had to negotiate with the communal governments of cities such as Perugia, Orvieto, and Viterbo. Some territories acknowledged papal sovereignty in principle, but practical control fluctuated with noble resistance and imperial intervention.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以教会法、罗马法传统、封建习惯法与城市法令并存为特征。教廷法院在婚姻、遗嘱、圣职、教产及上诉案件上具有权威；世俗案件则常由地方领主法庭、城市法官或教宗代表裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was characterized by coexistence of canon law, Roman legal tradition, feudal custom, and urban statutes. Papal courts held authority in marriage, testament, clerical, ecclesiastical property, and appellate matters; secular disputes were often judged by local lordly courts, communal magistrates, or papal representatives.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自领地地租、通行税、市场税、港口与桥梁税、司法罚金、封臣贡赋、空缺圣职收益及临时征收。教宗还能通过更广泛的教会网络调动捐献与十字军相关税项，但这些并非全部直接纳入教皇国地方财政。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived from rents on papal lands, tolls, market taxes, port and bridge dues, judicial fines, feudal payments, income from vacant benefices, and occasional extraordinary levies. The pope could also mobilize donations and crusading taxes through the wider Church, though not all of these flowed directly into the territorial finances of the Papal States.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教为绝对主导宗教，罗马既是圣伯多禄与圣保禄殉道地，也是西方教会的中心。圣人崇拜、朝圣、修道生活、礼拜仪式与教会节期深刻塑造社会生活。
religion.en: Latin Christianity was overwhelmingly dominant, and Rome was both the site of Saint Peter and Saint Paul and the center of the Western Church. Saint cults, pilgrimage, monastic life, liturgy, and the church calendar deeply shaped social life.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以拉丁教父传统、教会法学、早期经院哲学与奥古斯丁式政治神学为主。英诺森三世时代特别强调教宗在基督教世界中的普世仲裁权与精神优位。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by the Latin patristic tradition, canon law scholarship, early scholasticism, and Augustinian political theology. The age of Innocent III especially stressed the pope's universal arbitral role in Christendom and spiritual supremacy.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写语言为拉丁文，采用拉丁字母；地方口语为早期意大利罗曼斯方言，但正式行政、礼仪、法学与外交几乎均以拉丁文书写。
writingSystem.en: The principal written language was Latin in the Latin alphabet; local speech consisted of early Italian Romance vernaculars, but formal administration, liturgy, jurisprudence, and diplomacy were conducted overwhelmingly in Latin.

culturalAchievements.zh: 罗马在1200年前后是西欧宗教艺术、建筑与典礼文化中心之一。重要成就包括罗马教堂修缮、马赛克与壁画传统延续、修道院抄写文化、教会法汇编实践，以及以圣年朝圣传统前身为基础的国际宗教交通。教廷礼仪、法学文书与教宗敕令形式对整个拉丁欧洲影响深远。
culturalAchievements.en: Around 1200 Rome was one of western Europe's chief centers of religious art, architecture, and ceremonial culture. Major achievements included the restoration of Roman churches, continuation of mosaic and fresco traditions, monastic manuscript culture, the practice of canon-law compilation, and international religious traffic rooted in pilgrimage traditions that prefigured later jubilees. Papal liturgy, legal documentation, and the formal style of papal bulls exerted lasting influence across Latin Europe.

languageFamily.zh: 行政与学术语言属印欧语系意大利语族的拉丁语；民间语言属印欧语系罗曼语族的早期意大利语方言
languageFamily.en: The administrative and learned language was Latin of the Indo-European Italic branch; vernacular speech belonged to the Romance branch of the Indo-European family in early Italian dialect forms

gdpDescription.zh: 教皇国经济在1200年处于繁荣状态，但其性质并非单纯农业王国。除拉齐奥、翁布里亚和马尔凯的农业生产外，罗马作为朝圣、司法、外交与教会财政中心，吸引大量人员、捐献与服务消费。其经济总量相较法国或神圣罗马帝国核心区较小，但在意大利中部属于高流通、高货币化的政治体。
gdpDescription.en: The Papal States in 1200 were prosperous, but not merely an agrarian realm. Beyond the agricultural production of Lazio, Umbria, and the Marches, Rome functioned as a center of pilgrimage, justice, diplomacy, and ecclesiastical finance, attracting people, donations, and service consumption. Its total output was smaller than that of France or the imperial heartlands, but in central Italy it was a comparatively high-circulation, highly monetized polity.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与葡萄种植、橄榄油、畜牧、地产租佃、朝圣服务、手工业、建筑、文书抄写、教会器物制造、市场贸易与税关收入
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, viticulture, olive oil, pastoralism, estate tenancy, pilgrimage services, handicrafts, construction, manuscript production, manufacture of ecclesiastical objects, market exchange, and customs revenues

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、葡萄酒、橄榄油、羊毛、皮革、盐、木材、铁器、蜡烛、圣物容器、纺织品，以及输入的香料、丝绸、奢侈布料与金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, wool, leather, salt, timber, iron goods, candles, reliquaries, textiles, and imported spices, silks, luxury cloth, and metalware

name.zh: 德纳里与银币混合流通体系
name.en: Denaro and mixed silver coin circulation

householdWealth.zh: 城乡差距显著。罗马及部分主教城市的商人、公证人、高级教士与贵族拥有明显财富积累，农民家庭则多以土地使用权、牲畜和有限现金为主要资产。教会地产广泛，使许多家庭处于租佃关系之中。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply between town and countryside. Merchants, notaries, senior clergy, and nobles in Rome and some episcopal cities accumulated noticeable wealth, while peasant households chiefly held land-use rights, livestock, and limited cash. The extensive reach of ecclesiastical estates meant that many families lived under tenancy arrangements.

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园与租佃农业、城市市场交换、教会地产经营和国际朝圣经济结合的中世纪混合经济。货币流通程度高于许多内陆封建地区，但财政结构深受教会机构与政治权威影响。
economicSystem.en: A medieval mixed economy combining manorial and tenancy agriculture, urban market exchange, ecclesiastical estate management, and an international pilgrimage economy. Monetization was higher than in many inland feudal regions, but the fiscal structure was strongly shaped by church institutions and political authority.

source.zh: 封建贡赋与附庸义务金
圣职空缺收益与教会地产杂项
source.en: Vacant benefices and miscellaneous ecclesiastical property income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模可观，反映罗马作为教会首都的现金流优势，但可自由支配的部分并非完全等同于整个拉丁教会财富。大量资金带有用途限制，或与教廷外交、十字军、教产管理及特定圣职收益相绑定。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial, reflecting Rome's advantages as the capital of the Church, but the freely disposable portion did not equal the wealth of the entire Latin Church. Much of the money was earmarked or tied to diplomacy, crusading, management of ecclesiastical property, and specific benefice revenues.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体审慎而务实：维持教宗领地的经常性收入，依靠文书化管理提高征收效率，在必要时通过临时税、司法收益与附庸缴纳弥补军政开支。英诺森三世亦倾向将财政资源服务于更广泛的教宗外交与十字军事业。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly cautious and pragmatic: maintaining regular revenue from papal lands, improving collection through documentary administration, and using extraordinary levies, judicial income, and vassal payments to cover military and governmental costs when necessary. Innocent III also tended to direct fiscal resources toward wider papal diplomacy and crusading efforts.

name.zh: 教宗家臣骑兵与封建骑士
name.en: Papal household cavalry and feudal knights

description.zh: 由教宗直属骑士、贵族附庸与受雇重骑兵组成，是野战中的核心突击力量，但数量有限且成本高昂。
description.en: Composed of papal household knights, noble vassals, and hired heavy cavalry, they formed the principal striking arm in field warfare, though in limited numbers and at high cost.

description.zh: 来自罗马及各公社城市的步兵，装备长矛、盾牌、弩与短兵器，适合守城、巡逻与地区性冲突。
description.en: Drawn from Rome and the communal towns, these infantrymen were armed with spears, shields, crossbows, and sidearms, suited for garrison duty, patrols, and regional conflict.

description.zh: 用于镇压叛乱、守卫要塞和补充战时兵力，包括意大利各地受雇士兵与地方城堡守军。
description.en: Used to suppress revolts, guard fortresses, and reinforce wartime manpower, including hired soldiers from various parts of Italy and local castle garrisons.

description.zh: 规模较小，负责台伯河口、沿海据点与交通线警戒，主要承担治安与运输保护，而非远洋海战。
description.en: Small in scale, responsible for guarding the Tiber mouth, coastal posts, and transport lines, serving chiefly in security and convoy protection rather than blue-water naval warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 英诺森三世教宗（名义统帅），由教宗使节与受任统帅实际指挥
commanderInChief.en: Pope Innocent III as nominal commander-in-chief, with practical command exercised by papal legates and appointed captains

technology.zh: 军事技术属12世纪末至13世纪初拉丁欧洲常见水平：重骑兵冲锋、石制城堡、攻城塔、投石机、弩兵与城防工事是主要特色。教皇国自身并非技术创新中心，但能吸收意大利城市与更广泛欧洲的军备实践。
technology.en: Military technology matched the common Latin European standard of the late twelfth and early thirteenth centuries: heavy cavalry charges, stone castles, siege towers, trebuchets, crossbowmen, and fortified defenses were central features. The Papal States were not themselves a major center of military innovation, but they could absorb the military practices of Italian cities and wider Europe.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自神圣罗马帝国在意大利的干预、罗马贵族内斗、地方城市与伯爵叛服无常，以及来自南意大利或托斯卡纳方向的地区权力竞争。对外虽不常面对大规模入侵，但政治军事安全高度依赖外交平衡。
threats.en: The main threats came from imperial intervention in Italy, factional struggles among Roman nobles, the wavering loyalty of local communes and counts, and regional power competition from southern Italy or Tuscany. Although the state did not often face major foreign invasion, its political and military security depended heavily on diplomatic balance.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口约150万，分布于罗马、拉齐奥乡村、翁布里亚山谷、马尔凯城镇和罗马涅部分地区。整体人口密度在意大利属中等偏高，但因山地、沼泽与政治控制不均而分布差异明显。罗马作为首都与宗教中心，拥有远超周边地区的流动人口。
populationDescription.en: The population stood at about 1.5 million, spread across Rome, the countryside of Lazio, the valleys of Umbria, the towns of the Marches, and parts of Romagna. Overall density was moderate to fairly high by Italian standards, though uneven because of mountains, marshes, and variable political control. Rome as capital and religious center had a transient population far above that of surrounding areas.

description.zh: 永恒之城，罗马帝国首都
description.en: Eternal City, capital of the Roman Empire

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括教宗廷臣、枢机、主教、修道院长与世俗贵族；中层包括公社官员、法学家、公证人、商人与熟练工匠；下层为城市贫民、佃农、农民、牧民与雇工。教会慈善在缓和社会贫困方面具有重要作用，但城乡不平等依旧明显。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included papal courtiers, cardinals, bishops, abbots, and secular nobles; the middle strata included communal officials, jurists, notaries, merchants, and skilled artisans; the lower strata consisted of the urban poor, tenants, peasants, herdsmen, and laborers. Ecclesiastical charity played an important role in easing poverty, though inequality between town and countryside remained pronounced.

allies.zh: 在1200年前后，教皇国没有固定意义上的永久盟国，但通常与法国王国、部分十字军国家、圭尔夫派意大利城市以及在特定议题上服从教宗裁决的基督教君主合作。
allies.en: Around 1200 the Papal States had no permanent allies in the modern sense, but they often cooperated with the Kingdom of France, some Crusader states, Guelf-leaning Italian cities, and Christian rulers willing to accept papal arbitration on specific issues.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是试图控制意大利与教会任命权的皇帝势力，尤其是德意志—意大利帝国政治集团；此外，罗马本地敌对贵族、部分拒绝服从的意大利城市以及在十字军议题上对立的穆斯林政权也可构成敌对对象。
enemies.en: The principal adversary was imperial power seeking control over Italy and influence over ecclesiastical appointments, especially the German-Italian imperial political bloc; hostile Roman noble families, resistant Italian communes, and Muslim powers opposed in crusading contexts could also become enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 英诺森三世的外交政策极具主动性，目标在于维护教宗对基督教世界的仲裁地位、限制皇帝在意大利的扩张、支持十字军事业、确认西西里等关键地区的宗主权，并通过绝罚、赦免、婚姻裁判与使节谈判来塑造欧洲政治秩序。
foreignPolicy.en: Innocent III pursued an exceptionally active foreign policy aimed at upholding the pope's arbitral position in Christendom, limiting imperial expansion in Italy, supporting crusading, asserting overlordship in key regions such as Sicily, and shaping European political order through excommunication, absolution, marriage judgments, and legatine negotiation.

era.zh: 十字军时代的盛期中世纪拉丁欧洲
era.en: High medieval Latin Europe in the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 关键优势不在机械发明本身，而在制度与文书技术：成熟的拉丁文档案体系、教宗敕令格式、教会法汇编与跨区域通信网络。建筑上表现为石砌教堂、钟楼、防御工事和道路桥梁维护；军事上广泛采用弩、投石机与改良城堡体系。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in mechanical invention than in institutional and documentary techniques: mature Latin archival practice, papal bull formularies, canon-law compilation, and transregional communication networks. In construction this appeared in stone churches, bell towers, fortifications, and maintenance of roads and bridges; militarily in the widespread use of crossbows, trebuchets, and improved castle systems.

strengths.zh: 最大优势是无可替代的宗教合法性、罗马作为朝圣与外交中心的吸引力、较高的货币流通、成熟的书面行政能力，以及教宗能够动员超越本土边界的广泛基督教网络。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were unmatched religious legitimacy, Rome's attraction as a center of pilgrimage and diplomacy, relatively high monetization, mature written administration, and the pope's ability to mobilize a broad Christian network extending beyond his own territory.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于领地控制分散、地方贵族与城市自治强势、军事实力有限、财政常受更广泛教会事务牵制，且政权稳定性高度依赖教宗个人能力与意大利政治格局。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in fragmented territorial control, strong local nobles and urban autonomies, limited military power, finances often constrained by broader ecclesiastical obligations, and a political stability heavily dependent on the personal capacity of the reigning pope and the wider Italian balance of power.

outlook.zh: 就1200年而言，教皇国前景强劲。英诺森三世的威望使其在欧洲政治与宗教事务中达到高峰，领地也较为繁荣。然而这种强势更多建立在教宗个人权威、外交斡旋和对意大利复杂势力的平衡之上，长期仍将面临帝国挑战、城市自治扩张与内部派系斗争。
outlook.en: As of 1200, the Papal States had a strong outlook. The prestige of Innocent III placed them at a high point in European politics and religion, and the territory itself was relatively prosperous. Yet this strength rested more on papal personal authority, diplomatic maneuver, and balancing Italy's complex powers than on uniformly centralized control, so in the long run imperial challenges, expanding urban autonomy, and internal factional conflict would remain persistent dangers.

description.zh: 由教皇直接统治的中意大利政权，在欧洲宗教与外交中居核心地位。
description.en: A central Italian polity ruled directly by the pope, central to European religion and diplomacy.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主集权为核心、结合拉丁封建关系与王室官僚行政的王国
governmentForm.en: A centralized monarchy combining Latin feudal relationships with a strong royal bureaucratic administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、诺曼与伦巴第贵族、希腊与阿拉伯裔行政人员、拉丁与希腊教士、城市商人、自由农民、佃农及穆斯林与犹太社群构成，呈现多族群多法传统并存的结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the king, Norman and Lombard nobles, Greek and Arab administrators, Latin and Greek clergy, urban merchants, free peasants, tenants, and Muslim and Jewish communities, forming a multi-ethnic order with parallel legal traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、诺曼—伦巴第封建贵族、高级拉丁与希腊教会人士，以及受王室任用的宫廷官僚
rulingClass.en: The royal house, Norman-Lombard feudal nobility, senior Latin and Greek churchmen, and court officials appointed by the crown

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，原则上按合法血统继承，但在贵族、教廷与帝国势力干预下常伴随摄政、婚姻继承与政治协商。
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy, in principle following legitimate dynastic succession, though regencies, marital inheritance, and political negotiation often shaped succession under noble, papal, and imperial pressure.

structure.zh: 1200年的西西里王国由王室宫廷主导，继承诺曼时期形成的高度组织化行政体系。中央政府围绕巴勒莫王廷运作，下设财政、司法、文书与军务机构，并依靠王室直辖地、伯国与城市共同维持统治。王国兼具拉丁封建法、拜占庭行政习惯与阿拉伯财政技术的混合特征。
structure.en: In 1200 the Kingdom of Sicily was directed by the royal court, inheriting the highly organized administration formed under the Normans. The central government operated around the palace at Palermo, with fiscal, judicial, chancery, and military offices, and ruled through a combination of royal demesne, counties, and cities. The kingdom blended Latin feudal law, Byzantine administrative habits, and Arab fiscal techniques.

function.zh: 处理君主诏令、重大政策、贵族关系与摄政事务，协调全国行政。
function.en: Handled royal orders, major policy, noble relations, and regency affairs, coordinating administration across the realm.

function.zh: 征收地租、关税、王室专卖收益与王室领地收入，管理金库与账册。
function.en: Collected rents, customs, royal monopoly revenues, and demesne income, and managed the treasury and accounts.

function.zh: 以拉丁文、希腊文与阿拉伯文起草和保存敕令、契据、任命文书及外交文件。
function.en: Drafted and preserved decrees, charters, appointments, and diplomatic documents in Latin, Greek, and Arabic.

function.zh: 监督王室法庭、处理封建诉讼、城市案件、土地纠纷与不同社群间的法律事务。
function.en: Oversaw royal courts, feudal litigation, urban cases, land disputes, and legal affairs among different communities.

function.zh: 管理封臣服役、城堡守备、舰船征调、雇佣兵支付与沿海防务。
function.en: Managed feudal service, castle garrisons, ship requisition, mercenary payments, and coastal defense.

function.zh: 监管巴勒莫、墨西拿、萨莱诺等港口的贸易、税收、船只登记与商人事务。
function.en: Supervised trade, taxation, ship registration, and merchant affairs at ports such as Palermo, Messina, and Salerno.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由伯爵、司法官、王室总管、城镇官员与主教共同构成。西西里本岛和南意大陆部分地区保留较强的王室直接控制；封臣领地则负有军事与司法义务。部分希腊语和阿拉伯语地区继续沿用旧有税籍与地方习惯。
localAdmin.en: Local administration consisted of counts, justiciars, royal officials, town officers, and bishops. Sicily proper and parts of the southern mainland remained under relatively strong direct royal control, while feudal lords held military and judicial obligations. Some Greek- and Arabic-speaking districts continued to use older tax registers and local customs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系多元并行，包括王室法令、封建习惯法、罗马法传统、教会法，以及希腊与穆斯林社群在特定事务中的惯行法。王权倾向通过王室法庭加强统一裁判权。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was plural, including royal legislation, feudal customary law, Roman legal tradition, canon law, and the customary laws of Greek and Muslim communities in certain matters. The crown increasingly sought to strengthen unified jurisdiction through royal courts.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括土地税、王室领地租赋、港口关税、市场税、盐税、渔业与林地收益，以及向部分宗教与族群社群征收的人头或特别税。海关与王室庄园收入是财政的重要支柱。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included land taxes, rents and dues from royal demesne, port customs, market taxes, salt dues, revenue from fisheries and woodlands, and poll or special taxes on some religious and communal groups. Customs and royal estate income were key pillars of finance.

religion.zh: 主导宗教为拉丁基督教，同时存在希腊正教传统、穆斯林社群与犹太社群。王国在制度上承认多宗教并存，但王权与拉丁教会的地位最为显著。
religion.en: The dominant religion was Latin Christianity, alongside Greek Orthodox traditions, Muslim communities, and Jewish communities. The kingdom institutionally tolerated a multi-religious society, though the crown and the Latin Church held primacy.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化融合拉丁经院传统、希腊教父学与阿拉伯科学知识，实用行政理性强于纯粹神学争辩。宫廷环境重视翻译、医学、天文学与法律知识。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life blended Latin scholastic tendencies, Greek patristic traditions, and Arabic scientific knowledge, with practical administrative rationality often more important than purely theological disputation. Court culture valued translation, medicine, astronomy, and legal learning.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系包括拉丁字母、希腊字母与阿拉伯字母，官方文书常按对象与地区使用多语并行。
writingSystem.en: The main writing systems were the Latin, Greek, and Arabic scripts, and official documentation often used multiple languages depending on audience and region.

culturalAchievements.zh: 西西里宫廷与教堂建筑融合诺曼、拜占庭与伊斯兰艺术风格，如巴勒莫宫廷建筑和金色马赛克装饰传统。王国在地中海世界以多语文书、精细行政、医药知识传播和跨文化建筑闻名。
culturalAchievements.en: Sicilian court and church architecture fused Norman, Byzantine, and Islamic artistic forms, seen in Palermo’s palatial architecture and mosaic traditions. The kingdom was renowned in the Mediterranean for multilingual chancery practice, refined administration, circulation of medical knowledge, and cross-cultural architecture.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于罗曼语族、希腊语支和阿拉伯语支并存，另有诺曼法语与犹太社群语言使用。
languageFamily.en: Its major languages spanned Romance, Greek, and Arabic traditions, with Norman French and Jewish communal languages also in use.

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济在12世纪末至13世纪初的地中海属于较富庶水平，农业高产、港口贸易繁荣、王室财政组织严密。与埃及、拜占庭、北意大利和十字军诸国的商业联系使其财富高于多数单纯农业型欧洲王国。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom’s economy was prosperous by late twelfth- and early thirteenth-century Mediterranean standards, with productive agriculture, active port commerce, and tightly organized royal finances. Trade with Egypt, Byzantium, northern Italy, and the Crusader states made it wealthier than most purely agrarian European kingdoms.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、橄榄油、葡萄酒、柑橘与果园经营、畜牧业、渔业、制糖初步生产、丝织与纺织、陶器、金属工艺、造船与港口服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive oil, wine, citrus and orchard farming, pastoralism, fishing, early sugar production, silk and textiles, ceramics, metalworking, shipbuilding, and port services

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、奶酪、盐、橄榄油、葡萄酒、丝绸、糖、皮革、珊瑚、染料、木材、金属器、奴隶及转口东方香料与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Wheat, cheese, salt, olive oil, wine, silk, sugar, leather, coral, dyes, timber, metal goods, slaves, and re-exported eastern spices and luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富差异显著：城市商人与地主家庭拥有房产、仓储、牲畜和贵金属储蓄；乡村农户多以土地使用权、牲畜、工具和谷物存量为主要财富。西西里与沿海城市家庭总体生活水平高于许多内陆欧洲地区。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly: urban merchant and landholding families possessed real estate, storage facilities, livestock, and precious-metal savings, while rural households relied chiefly on land-use rights, animals, tools, and grain stocks. Sicily and the coastal cities generally enjoyed living standards above many inland European regions.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为王室主导下的混合封建—商业经济。农业是基础，王室通过庄园、税关与专卖获取收入，同时鼓励地中海贸易、港口城市发展和跨宗教商人网络运作。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a royal-led mixed feudal-commercial economy. Agriculture formed the base, while the crown drew income from estates, customs, and monopolies, and also encouraged Mediterranean trade, port-city growth, and cross-confessional merchant networks.

source.zh: 司法罚金与王室权利收益
铸币、盐业与其他杂项收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库在欧洲王国中相对充实，得益于长期积累的海关收入、王室庄园收益与较成熟的财政文书制度。储备需同时应对舰队、雇佣兵、摄政政治和对外外交开支。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was comparatively well stocked by European royal standards, thanks to accumulated customs income, demesne revenues, and a relatively mature fiscal record-keeping system. These reserves had to support fleets, mercenaries, regency politics, and foreign diplomacy.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向谨慎集权，优先维持王室直辖收入、海关征收和军事支付能力。政权避免将关键税源完全让渡给封臣，以保持对城市、港口和战略要地的直接控制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautiously centralizing, prioritizing direct royal income, customs collection, and military payment capacity. The regime avoided surrendering key tax sources entirely to feudal lords, preserving direct control over cities, ports, and strategic locations.

description.zh: 由诺曼、伦巴第及其他封臣提供的重骑兵，是王国野战中的核心冲击力量。
description.en: Heavy cavalry provided by Norman, Lombard, and other vassals, forming the core shock arm of the kingdom in field warfare.

description.zh: 由王室直接维持的步兵、弩手和城堡守军，负责岛屿要塞、城市与大陆据点防务。
description.en: Infantry, crossbowmen, and castle troops maintained directly by the crown, defending island fortresses, cities, and mainland strongholds.

description.zh: 由桨帆船船员、海军士兵与港口征召人员组成，用于护航、巡逻和两西西里之间运输。
description.en: Composed of galley crews, naval soldiers, and port levies, used for escorting, patrol, and transport between Sicily and the southern mainland.

description.zh: 包括意大利南部地方兵、穆斯林弓箭手传统残余及按需雇佣的外来士兵，用于补充战时兵力。
description.en: Included southern Italian local troops, remnants of Muslim archer traditions, and hired foreign soldiers employed as needed to supplement wartime manpower.

commanderInChief.zh: 腓特烈二世国王（由摄政与王室军政官代行指挥）
commanderInChief.en: King Frederick II, with command exercised in practice by regents and royal military officers

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在诺曼重骑兵、石砌城堡、防御工事、弩、攻城器械与地中海桨帆舰技术之上。其优势不在数量，而在机动海运、坚固堡垒体系与跨海补给能力。
technology.en: Military capability rested on Norman heavy cavalry, stone castles, fortifications, crossbows, siege equipment, and Mediterranean galley technology. Its strengths lay less in numbers than in seaborne mobility, strong fortresses, and cross-sea logistical capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自教廷与德意志帝国政治纠纷、安茹和北意大利海上势力的竞争、拜占庭与东地中海局势波动，以及南意大陆封臣叛乱和穆斯林社群局部不稳。
threats.en: Main threats came from political conflict involving the papacy and the German imperial sphere, rivalry with Angevin and northern Italian maritime forces, volatility in the Byzantine and eastern Mediterranean world, and occasional baronial revolt or unrest among Muslim communities in the south.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

populationDescription.zh: 人口约250万，包含西西里岛与意大利南部大陆领地。人口分布以农村为主，但沿海港口和行政中心较为繁荣。族群与语言多样，拉丁、希腊、阿拉伯、犹太和伦巴第等社群长期共居。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 2.5 million, including both Sicily and the southern Italian mainland possessions. Settlement was predominantly rural, though coastal ports and administrative centers were prosperous. The population was ethnically and linguistically diverse, with Latin, Greek, Arabic, Jewish, and Lombard communities living together over the long term.

description.zh: 诺曼-阿拉伯文化交融之城
description.en: City of Norman-Arab cultural fusion

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室、贵族、骑士、拉丁与希腊教士、城市商人与手工业者、自由农、佃农、庄园劳作者，以及穆斯林和犹太社群内部的学者、商人和纳税居民。法律地位因宗教、地域与封建身份而异。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal house, nobles, knights, Latin and Greek clergy, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, estate laborers, and within Muslim and Jewish communities their own scholars, merchants, and taxable inhabitants. Legal status varied by religion, region, and feudal standing.

allies.zh: 在1200年前后，王国并无绝对稳定的正式盟友体系，但常与部分意大利城市、十字军国家及德意志霍亨斯陶芬关系网络保持合作；与埃及和穆斯林商人也存在务实贸易联系。
allies.en: Around 1200 the kingdom had no permanently fixed alliance bloc, but it often cooperated with some Italian cities, Crusader states, and the wider Hohenstaufen political network, while also maintaining pragmatic commercial relations with Egypt and Muslim merchants.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括试图限制王国独立性的教廷政治力量、在亚得里亚海和东地中海竞争的威尼斯等海上共和国，以及可能挑战其南意控制权的安茹和地方叛乱贵族。
enemies.en: Its chief adversaries included papal political forces seeking to limit the kingdom’s independence, maritime republics such as Venice competing in the Adriatic and eastern Mediterranean, and baronial or Angevin forces that could challenge control of southern Italy.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护王国自主、控制西西里海峡与南意通道、平衡教廷与帝国压力、并通过贸易联系扩大地中海影响为核心。其外交风格通常务实，重商且善于跨宗教交往。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving royal autonomy, controlling the Strait of Sicily and southern Italian routes, balancing papal and imperial pressures, and extending Mediterranean influence through trade links. Its diplomacy was typically pragmatic, commercial, and adept at cross-confessional engagement.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度优势包括多语王室文书体系、成熟海关与财政记账、石砌城堡建筑、弩与攻城技术、地中海桨帆舰航运，以及吸收阿拉伯医学、灌溉与天文知识的跨文化传播机制。
keyInnovations.en: Key advantages included a multilingual royal chancery, mature customs and fiscal bookkeeping, stone castle construction, crossbow and siege techniques, Mediterranean galley seafaring, and cross-cultural transmission of Arabic medicine, irrigation practices, and astronomy.

strengths.zh: 王国的优势在于地理位置优越、港口贸易发达、财政制度较欧洲多数王国更集中、宫廷具多语与跨文化能力，并拥有较强海军运输和坚固要塞网络。
strengths.en: The kingdom’s strengths were its strategic location, thriving port trade, a more centralized fiscal system than that of most European kingdoms, a multilingual and cross-cultural court, and strong naval transport with a solid fortress network.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于王位继承与摄政时期易受外部干预，南意贵族离心、教廷长期敌意、岛屿与大陆领地统合成本高，以及多族群秩序在危机时可能转为政治脆弱性。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in vulnerability to outside intervention during succession and regency, centrifugal southern Italian nobility, long-term papal hostility, the high cost of integrating island and mainland territories, and the possibility that a multi-ethnic order could become politically fragile in times of crisis.

outlook.zh: 到1200年，西西里王国总体稳定且富庶，具备继续作为地中海强国的潜力。但其未来将取决于腓特烈二世成年后的统治能力，以及能否在教廷、帝国和意大利诸势力之间维持平衡。
outlook.en: By 1200 the Kingdom of Sicily was broadly stable and prosperous, with the potential to remain a major Mediterranean power. Its future would depend on Frederick II’s eventual personal rule and on whether the monarchy could maintain balance among papal, imperial, and Italian political forces.

description.zh: 南意大利与西西里组成的富庶王国，融合拉丁、希腊与阿拉伯传统。
description.en: A prosperous kingdom of southern Italy and Sicily blending Latin, Greek, and Arab traditions.

ruler.zh: 朱罗皇帝拘罗通迦三世

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、依托寺庙网络与地方自治村社运作的南印度王朝帝国
governmentForm.en: A South Indian dynastic empire centered on monarchy, supported by temple networks and locally self-administering village institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以种姓、职业团体、土地控制和寺庙赞助关系为核心，婆罗门、军事贵族、地主农民、商人公会成员、工匠与农民共同构成秩序，另有从属劳动群体与边缘社群。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around caste, occupational groups, landholding, and temple patronage, with Brahmins, military nobles, landed peasants, merchant guild members, artisans, and cultivators forming the main order, alongside dependent laboring groups and marginal communities.

rulingClass.zh: 朱罗王室、王族支系、婆罗门精英、军事贵族、纳杜与瓦兰纳杜层级的地方地主首领，以及与大寺院相联系的高层施主集团
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Chola royal house, collateral royal kin, Brahmin elites, military nobility, local landed chiefs at the nadu and valanadu levels, and major donor groups tied to large temples.

succession.zh: 王位继承以父子世袭为原则，但常通过共治、册立太子和王族内部协调来确保平稳过渡
succession.en: Succession was principally hereditary from father to son, but was often stabilized through co-regency, formal designation of an heir, and negotiation within the royal family.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕皇帝、王室书记机构、财政征收体系、军政官员与寺庙财政网络展开；地方则由曼达拉姆、瓦兰纳杜、纳杜、库兰与村社层级组成，并保留较强自治传统。
structure.en: The central state revolved around the emperor, royal secretariat, fiscal extraction system, military-administrative officers, and temple financial networks; locally it was organized into mandalam, valanadu, nadu, kurram, and village tiers, preserving strong traditions of self-government.

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存石刻与铜板文书、协调中央与地方命令传达
function.en: Drafted royal edicts, preserved inscriptional and copper-plate records, and coordinated the transmission of orders between the center and provinces

function.zh: 负责土地丈量、税额评定、灌溉地分类与农业贡赋征收
function.en: Managed land surveys, tax assessment, irrigation-based land classification, and the collection of agrarian dues

function.zh: 统筹常备军、驻屯部队、地方附庸兵力、战象与海上作战准备
function.en: Oversaw the standing army, garrison troops, feudatory contingents, war elephants, and maritime war preparations

function.zh: 监督皇家赞助寺庙、水库堤坝、运河、道路和礼仪性工程
function.en: Supervised royally endowed temples, tanks, embankments, canals, roads, and ceremonial works

function.zh: 管理海港、商税、对外贸易监管以及与商人公会的协作
function.en: Administered ports, customs duties, foreign trade regulation, and cooperation with merchant guilds

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托曼达拉姆、瓦兰纳杜、纳杜与村社体系。婆罗门定居村的议会、非婆罗门村社集会以及商镇组织均在税收、灌溉、司法与公共工程上发挥作用，中央更多通过监督、确认和征收来维持统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on the mandalam, valanadu, nadu, and village structure. Assemblies in Brahmin settlements, non-Brahmin village councils, and market-town bodies all played roles in taxation, irrigation, justice, and public works, while the center governed more through supervision, confirmation, and extraction than through direct bureaucratic penetration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以王命、地方习惯法、种姓规范、寺庙章程与婆罗门法学传统并行为特征；土地纠纷、税务、灌溉权和寺庙捐赠记录常由村社与地方集会裁定，并以碑铭形式固定。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal command, local customary law, caste norms, temple regulations, and Brahmanical jurisprudence; land disputes, tax obligations, irrigation rights, and temple endowments were often adjudicated by village and regional assemblies and fixed in inscriptions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以土地税为核心，辅以灌溉费、商税、港口关税、专业税、贡赋和附庸献纳。税收依据土地质量、水源条件和作物种类差异化征收，部分地区以粮食、部分以货币或实物支付。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was centered on land revenue, supplemented by irrigation dues, trade taxes, port customs, professional levies, tribute, and feudatory contributions. Assessments varied by land quality, water access, and crop type, with payment made in grain, cash, or kind depending on locality.

religion.zh: 以印度教为主，特别是湿婆崇拜占核心地位，同时毗湿奴崇拜、地方神灵祭祀、耆那教和佛教遗存仍然存在；王权通过大寺庙礼仪与捐赠强化合法性。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Hindu, with Shaivism at the core, alongside Vaishnavism, local deity cults, and lingering Jain and Buddhist presence; kingship drew legitimacy from major temple ritual and endowment.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以婆罗门教法学、阿伽摩寺庙传统、南印度奉爱运动诗学以及梵语与泰米尔双重学术文化并存为特征。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was marked by Brahmanical jurisprudence, Agamic temple traditions, the poetics of the South Indian bhakti movement, and a dual Sanskrit-Tamil scholarly culture.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用泰米尔文字记录泰米尔语，同时在礼制、铭文和高层文化中广泛使用梵语，辅以格兰塔文字书写梵语内容。
writingSystem.en: Tamil script was used for Tamil, while Sanskrit remained prominent in ritual, inscriptional, and elite culture, often written in Grantha script.

culturalAchievements.zh: 朱罗时期在巨型石构寺庙、青铜铸像、碑铭行政文书、灌溉工程和海洋商业文化方面成就卓著。坦贾武尔与恒河孔达朱罗城等王家寺院群体现了南印度建筑与王权象征的高峰，湿婆纳塔拉迦青铜像尤为著名。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chola age excelled in monumental stone temples, bronze casting, inscriptional administration, irrigation works, and maritime commercial culture. Royal temple complexes at Thanjavur and Gangaikonda Cholapuram represent a peak of South Indian architecture and kingship symbolism, while bronzes of Shiva Nataraja are especially celebrated.

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系，以泰米尔语为主，梵语为礼仪与学术通用语言之一
languageFamily.en: Dravidian language family, with Tamil dominant and Sanskrit serving as a major liturgical and scholarly language

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础极为稳固，依赖高产稻作农业、成熟灌溉体系、寺庙主导的土地经营、区域市场整合以及印度洋贸易。按当时标准，朱罗帝国属于欧亚范围内富庶而高度商业化的区域强权之一。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was highly solid, resting on productive rice agriculture, mature irrigation systems, temple-managed landholding, integrated regional markets, and Indian Ocean trade. By the standards of the time, the Chola Empire was among the wealthier and more commercialized regional powers of Eurasia.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、灌溉农业、棉纺织、金属工艺、青铜铸造、造船、寺庙相关服务业、盐业、香料与转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, irrigated agriculture, cotton textiles, metalworking, bronze casting, shipbuilding, temple-based services, salt production, spices, and entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、细织物、香料、胡椒、珍珠、宝石、象牙、金属器、青铜器、盐、米粮，以及来自东南亚和中国的奢侈品转口货物
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, fine textiles, spices, pepper, pearls, gemstones, ivory, metal goods, bronzes, salt, rice, and re-exported luxury items from Southeast Asia and China

name.en: Chola gold and copper coinage system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显。灌溉核心区地主、寺庙关联家庭和商人公会成员拥有较高财富，而普通农户多依赖小块土地、租佃与季节性市场交换维生。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was unevenly distributed. Landholders in irrigated core zones, temple-linked families, and merchant guild members held substantial wealth, while ordinary cultivators often depended on small plots, tenancy, and seasonal market exchange.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余为基础、寺庙与村社组织为枢纽、并与商人公会和海上贸易深度结合的宫廷—寺庙—市场混合经济
economicSystem.en: A court-temple-market mixed economy based on agrarian surplus, organized through temples and village bodies, and closely linked to merchant guilds and maritime trade

source.en: Tribute from subordinates and incoming war gains

source.zh: 寺庙关联收益、专业税与杂项
source.en: Temple-linked income, professional levies, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 王室、宫廷与地方官吏俸给
category.en: Royal household, court, and official maintenance

category.zh: 外交赠礼、储备与杂项支出
category.en: Diplomatic gifts, reserves, and miscellaneous spending

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由王室金银储备、战争掠获、港税收入和可动用的寺庙—宫廷金融资源共同构成。真正可立即动员的现金储备有限，但通过寺庙、商人和地方精英网络可迅速筹措额外资源。
