philosophy.zh: 政治思想强调王权神圣、功德积累、宇宙秩序与慈悲施政。婆罗门宇宙观和佛教救济理想共同塑造了国家建筑、公共工程与统治合法性。
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized sacred kingship, merit-making, cosmic order, and compassionate rule. Brahmanical cosmology and Buddhist ideals of relief and charity together shaped state architecture, public works, and legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古高棉文与梵文铭刻，文字源自南印度婆罗米系，主要见于石碑、寺庙铭文、地契和宗教捐献记录。
writingSystem.en: Old Khmer and Sanskrit were used in inscriptions, written in scripts derived from the South Indian Brahmi tradition, chiefly on stone stelae, temple inscriptions, land records, and religious donation texts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 阇耶跋摩七世时期的文化成就包括巴戎寺、塔普伦寺、班蒂喀黛等大型寺庙建筑，王道与桥梁系统、医院与休息站网络，以及将王权、宇宙象征和浮雕叙事结合到极高水平的石雕艺术。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements under Jayavarman VII included major temple complexes such as the Bayon, Ta Prohm, and Banteay Kdei, extensive royal roads and bridges, networks of hospitals and rest houses, and stone sculpture that fused kingship, cosmic symbolism, and narrative bas-relief at a very high level.

languageFamily.zh: 南亚语系孟高棉语族高棉语支
languageFamily.en: Austroasiatic, Mon-Khmer branch, Khmer subgroup

unit.en: taels of silver annual output equivalent

unit.zh: 两白银人均年产值当量
unit.en: taels of silver per capita annual output equivalent

gdpDescription.zh: 吴哥在1200年前后处于强盛期，经济基础是高度组织化的稻作农业与水利体系，辅以渔业、森林采集、手工业和区域贸易。其财富主要体现为粮食剩余、劳动力动员能力与宗教工程规模，而非高度货币化的市场经济。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200 CE Angkor was in a flourishing phase, with an economic base rooted in highly organized wet-rice agriculture and hydraulic management, supplemented by fishing, forest extraction, craft production, and regional trade. Its wealth was expressed chiefly in food surplus, capacity to mobilize labor, and the scale of religious construction rather than in a highly monetized market economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、内陆渔业、林产采集、石工与建筑、青铜与铁器制造、陶器、织造、战象与运输畜养、寺院经济
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, inland fisheries, forest extraction, stoneworking and construction, bronze and iron production, ceramics, weaving, elephant and transport animal management, and temple-based economy

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或交换品包括稻米、林木、树脂、蜂蜡、象牙、犀角、豆蔻、漆料、盐鱼、奴仆与手工品；输入品包括中国陶瓷、丝绸、金属器、珠宝、棉织物和印度洋奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports or exchange goods included rice, timber, resins, beeswax, ivory, rhinoceros horn, cardamom, lacquer materials, salted fish, slaves, and handicrafts; imports included Chinese ceramics, silk, metal goods, jewelry, cotton textiles, and Indian Ocean luxury items.

name.zh: 以物纳税与贵金属并用
name.en: In-kind obligations with limited bullion use

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在水田使用权、家畜、渔具、木屋和家庭劳动力；精英财富则集中于土地、役属人口、仓储、金银器与寺庙捐献网络。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in access to paddy land, livestock, fishing gear, wooden houses, and family labor; elite wealth was concentrated in land, dependent labor, storage capacity, precious metal goods, and temple endowment networks.

unit.zh: 两白银人均年收入当量
unit.en: taels of silver per capita annual income equivalent
taels of silver annual foreign trade equivalent

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的农业—赋役经济，核心以寺庙、王室与地方首领控制土地与劳动力，市场交换存在但处于次要地位；远距离贸易主要通过与占城、马来海上政权及中国商路的连接实现。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed agrarian and labor-dues economy in which temples, the crown, and local chiefs controlled land and manpower, while markets existed in a secondary role; long-distance trade depended largely on links with Champa, Malay maritime polities, and routes connected to China.

unit.en: taels of silver annual revenue equivalent
taels of silver annual expenditure equivalent
taels of silver annual surplus equivalent

source.en: Tribute from vassals and frontier regions

source.zh: 王室地产与林产专属收入
source.en: Royal estates and forest monopoly revenues

category.en: Hydraulic works, roads, bridges, and hospitals
Relief, granary reserves, and diplomatic gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 吴哥国库并非单纯以铸币储存，而是由贵金属器物、贡赋库存、仓粮、布匹、牲畜与王室可调动的寺院资源共同构成。其财政韧性强，但高度依赖农业丰收和劳动力持续征发。
treasuryDescription.en: The Angkorian treasury was not a simple hoard of coin, but a composite reserve of precious objects, tribute stockpiles, stored grain, cloth, livestock, and temple resources available to the crown. It was resilient in kind, but heavily dependent on agricultural success and the continued mobilization of labor.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向大规模公共工程与宗教营建，通过实物税和徭役维持国家能力。繁荣时期这一模式极具动员力，但若战争、气候波动或地方离心加重，财政负担会迅速放大。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored major public works and religious construction, sustained through in-kind taxation and labor dues. In times of prosperity this model was highly mobilizational, but war, climatic fluctuation, or stronger provincial centrifugal forces could quickly magnify fiscal strain.

description.zh: 军队主体，由矛兵、刀盾兵、弓箭手和征发农兵组成，承担守城、野战与工程护卫任务。
description.en: The core of the army, composed of spearmen, sword-and-shield troops, archers, and conscripted peasant soldiers for garrison duty, field operations, and protection of engineering works.

description.zh: 用于冲击、指挥平台和威慑，常由贵族与精锐随从配合使用，是高棉军事象征性最强的兵种之一。
description.en: Used for shock action, command platforms, and intimidation, often paired with nobles and elite retainers; it was one of the most symbolically important Khmer arms.

description.zh: 负责宫廷保卫、要地驻守与王命执行，装备和待遇优于普通征发部队。
description.en: Responsible for palace security, key garrisons, and enforcement of royal commands, with better equipment and status than ordinary levy troops.

name.zh: 地方征发军与附庸部队

description.zh: 由地方首领、边地诸侯和附庸地区提供，机动性和忠诚度参差不齐，但对远征和边境控制十分重要。
description.en: Supplied by local chiefs, frontier lords, and tributary areas; their mobility and loyalty varied, but they were vital for campaigns and border control.

description.zh: 依托湖泊与河道承担运输、巡逻和后勤支援，在洞里萨湖与湄公河流域尤其关键。
description.en: Operating along lakes and rivers for transport, patrol, and logistical support, especially important on Tonle Sap and in the Mekong basin.

commanderInChief.zh: 阇耶跋摩七世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Jayavarman VII

technology.zh: 军事技术以冷兵器、复合或竹木弓、长矛、刀剑、皮甲与布甲、战象运用、攻城器和河运后勤为主。吴哥军队不具备中国式火药战争能力，但在热带陆水混合环境中的道路修筑、桥梁建设与大型部队调度方面经验丰富。
technology.en: Military technology relied on edged weapons, bows of bamboo or composite type, spears, swords, leather and cloth armor, the use of war elephants, siege devices, and riverine logistics. Angkor did not possess Chinese-style gunpowder warfare, but it was highly experienced in road building, bridge construction, and moving large forces in tropical land-water environments.

unit.en: taels of silver annual military spending equivalent

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自占城的海岸与陆上冲突、与大越在中南半岛东部势力范围的竞争、边疆附庸离心，以及远距离征服带来的补给压力。1190年代吴哥曾压制占城，但控制持续性仍具挑战。
threats.en: The main threats came from coastal and land conflict with Champa, competition with Dai Viet over influence in eastern mainland Southeast Asia, centrifugal tendencies among frontier tributaries, and the supply burdens of long-distance conquest. Angkor had subdued Champa in the 1190s, but sustaining control remained difficult.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 拜占庭海战秘密武器，水上不灭之火
description.en: Byzantine naval secret weapon, inextinguishable fire on water

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五百万，主体为高棉人，另包括孟族、占族、山地部族及来自周边地区的战俘、商人和宗教人员。人口高度集中于洞里萨湖周边与吴哥核心平原。
populationDescription.en: The population was about five million, predominantly Khmer, with Mon, Cham, upland groups, and captives, merchants, and religious personnel from surrounding regions also present. Population was heavily concentrated around the Tonle Sap basin and the Angkorian core plain.

description.zh: 高棉帝国首都，世界最大前工业城市
description.en: Khmer capital, world's largest pre-industrial city

name.zh: 占城驻防与附属行政中心

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室、贵族官僚、婆罗门与佛教僧侣、地方首领、军人、工匠商人、普通农民渔民及役属人口。社会流动有限，但通过军功、王室任命和寺庙服务可获得一定上升空间。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal family, noble officials, Brahmins and Buddhist clergy, local chiefs, soldiers, artisans and merchants, ordinary peasants and fishers, and dependent laborers. Social mobility was limited, but military service, royal appointment, and temple service could provide some advancement.

allies.zh: 吴哥在1200年前后缺乏持久而平等的正式盟友，更多依靠附庸、朝贡关系与临时性地方合作；与部分马来贸易政权和中南半岛内陆首领维持务实往来。
allies.en: Around 1200 CE Angkor lacked durable and equal formal allies, relying more on vassalage, tributary relations, and temporary regional cooperation; it maintained pragmatic ties with some Malay trading polities and inland mainland Southeast Asian chiefs.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为占城残余势力，以及在区域影响力上存在潜在竞争的大越；边疆叛附的地方政权也构成持续威胁。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were residual Cham forces and, as a potential rival in regional influence, Dai Viet; rebellious frontier polities also posed continuing threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以中南半岛霸权、控制交通走廊、维持附庸体系和获取海上贸易通道为核心。吴哥既通过军事远征扩张，也通过婚姻、朝贡、宗教声望和工程援建施加影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on mainland Southeast Asian hegemony, control of transport corridors, maintenance of tributary networks, and access to maritime trade routes. Angkor projected influence through military expeditions as well as marriage ties, tribute, religious prestige, and infrastructural patronage.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度优势包括大型蓄水池和运河体系、堤道与石桥工程、晚期吴哥寺庙建筑技术、铁器和青铜器工艺、长距离道路补给组织，以及将宗教机构纳入国家资源管理的高效制度。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional strengths included massive reservoirs and canal systems, causeway and stone bridge engineering, mature late-Angkor temple construction techniques, iron and bronze working, long-distance road supply organization, and efficient integration of religious institutions into state resource management.

strengths.zh: 吴哥在1200年前后拥有东南亚最强大的农业基础设施之一、极高的劳动力组织能力、宏伟的宗教与公共建筑传统，以及在中南半岛腹地投射军事力量的能力。阇耶跋摩七世的统治提升了国家威望与整合度。
strengths.en: Around 1200 CE Angkor possessed one of Southeast Asia’s strongest agrarian infrastructures, exceptional labor-organizing capacity, a monumental tradition of religious and public construction, and the ability to project military power across the mainland interior. Jayavarman VII’s rule enhanced both prestige and integration.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于对水利维护、农业稳定和大规模徭役的高度依赖；远征占城带来的过度扩张风险；地方附庸忠诚度不稳；以及宫廷宗教工程可能加重财政与社会负担。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in heavy dependence on hydraulic maintenance, agricultural stability, and mass corvée; the danger of overextension from campaigns in Champa; uneven loyalty among provincial tributaries; and the fiscal and social burden imposed by court-sponsored religious building.

outlook.zh: 短期内吴哥仍将维持繁荣并继续作为大陆东南亚霸主之一，建筑与行政扩张可能持续数十年。但从长期看，若外部战争、地方离心、宗教转型与生态压力叠加，其高度集中而工程化的帝国模式将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: In the short term Angkor was likely to remain prosperous and continue as one of mainland Southeast Asia’s hegemons, with construction and administrative expansion lasting for decades. Over the longer term, however, if external war, provincial fragmentation, religious change, and ecological stress converged, its highly centralized and infrastructural imperial model would face severe strain.

description.zh: 中南半岛最强帝国之一，在阇耶跋摩七世治下大规模建设与扩张。
description.en: One of mainland Southeast Asia's strongest empires, undergoing major building and expansion under Jayavarman VII.

governmentForm.zh: 君主制王国，以佛教王权与儒家文官制度并行为特征。
governmentForm.en: A monarchical kingdom characterized by Buddhist kingship alongside an increasingly Confucian civil administration.

socialStructure.zh: 大越社会以王室、宗室与文武官僚为上层，地方豪族和寺院势力在地方社会中具有重要影响。广大人口为自耕农、佃农、工匠与渔民，山地地区则由各族首领维持较强自治。佛寺不仅是宗教中心，也承担教育、慈善与土地管理职能。
socialStructure.en: Dai Viet society was headed by the royal family, princely lineages, and civil-military officials, while local magnates and Buddhist monasteries held major influence in the countryside. Most people were peasants, tenant farmers, artisans, and fishers, with upland groups retaining stronger autonomy under their own chiefs. Temples served not only as religious centers but also as institutions of education, charity, and land management.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由李朝皇室、宗室、科举出身文官以及世袭武将构成。宫廷高僧和大寺院住持在政治与文化上也拥有相当影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Ly royal house, princely relatives, examination-trained civil officials, and hereditary military commanders. Senior monks and abbots of major monasteries also wielded substantial political and cultural influence.

succession.zh: 王位原则上由李氏皇族世袭，通常由嫡系皇子继承，但宫廷内部也常受宗室、后妃与权臣影响。若皇位交替时中央权威不足，地方豪族与军将便可能扩大影响。
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the Ly lineage, usually passing to a legitimate prince, though succession was often shaped by court factions, consorts, and powerful ministers. When central authority weakened during transitions, regional magnates and commanders could increase their influence.

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕皇帝、朝廷与尚书性机构运作，兼具唐宋式文官制度与本土王权传统。地方上设路、府、州、县等层级，但对偏远山区与边地的控制多依赖羁縻与土官合作。
structure.en: The central government operated around the king, court, and secretariat-style ministries, combining Chinese-inspired bureaucratic forms with indigenous royal traditions. In the provinces, administrative layers of circuits, prefectures, and districts existed, but control over uplands and frontier zones often relied on indirect rule through local chiefs.

function.zh: 负责诏令起草、政务协调与奏章审核。
function.en: Handled drafting edicts, coordinating policy, and reviewing memorials.

function.zh: 管理田赋、户籍、仓储与徭役征发。
function.en: Managed land tax, household registration, granaries, and labor obligations.

function.zh: 掌军籍、驻防调度、边防与兵器供给。
function.en: Oversaw military registers, garrisons, frontier defense, and arms supply.

function.zh: 负责祭祀、外交礼仪、学校与科举事务。
function.en: Supervised state rituals, diplomatic protocol, schools, and examinations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由朝廷任命官员与地方豪族共同维系，平原地区控制较稳固。山地与边境地区则更多采用羁縻政策，让土著首领保留一定自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was maintained jointly by court-appointed officials and local magnates, with firmer control in the lowland core. In upland and frontier areas, the state often used indirect rule, allowing indigenous chiefs a degree of autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系受汉法传统影响，兼具王朝敕令、习惯法与寺院调解。刑罚和赋役秩序由国家维护，但地方纠纷常通过官府与乡里长者协商处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was influenced by Sinic legal traditions while also incorporating royal decrees, customary law, and monastic mediation. The state enforced punishment and tax order, but many local disputes were settled through negotiation by officials and village elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税源为田赋、贡赋、徭役与盐铁等专卖或征课。税收多以稻米、丝织品、铜钱和劳役形式征纳，体现农业王国财政特征。
taxationSystem.en: Main revenues came from land tax, tribute, labor service, and levies or monopolies on goods such as salt and metals. Taxes were commonly collected in rice, textiles, copper cash, and labor, reflecting the fiscal character of an agrarian kingdom.

religion.zh: 佛教/儒教
religion.en: Buddhism/Confucianism

culturalAchievements.zh: 李朝时期佛寺建筑、铜钟铸造与宫廷礼制颇为发达，升龙成为北部文化中心。教育与科举逐步制度化，推动儒学官僚成长。
culturalAchievements.en: Under the Ly, temple architecture, bronze bell casting, and court ritual flourished, and Thang Long became the cultural center of the north. Education and examinations became more institutionalized, fostering the growth of a Confucian bureaucracy.

languageFamily.zh: 越芒语族（南亚语系）
languageFamily.en: Vietic branch of the Austroasiatic language family

philosophy.zh: 以佛教为主导，辅以儒家政治伦理与本土祖先崇拜。
philosophy.en: Predominantly Buddhist, supplemented by Confucian political ethics and indigenous ancestor veneration.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用汉字书写政令与典籍，口语为古越语，后世喃字传统尚在形成中。
writingSystem.en: Classical Chinese characters were used for administration and literature, while the spoken language was early Vietnamese; the later chu Nom tradition was only beginning to emerge.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 大越经济以稻作农业为核心，红河三角洲提供了主要剩余产出。手工业、内河运输与对宋贸易为王国带来额外财富，但整体仍属以农业为本的中等规模区域经济。
gdpDescription.en: Dai Viet's economy was centered on wet-rice agriculture, with the Red River delta producing most of its surplus. Craft production, river transport, and trade with Song China added further wealth, though the realm remained a medium-sized agrarian regional economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、盐业、丝织、陶瓷、青铜铸造、木作与造船
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, fishing, salt production, silk weaving, ceramics, bronze casting, woodworking, and boatbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 大米、盐、丝绸、陶器、香料、林产、象牙、铜器
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, silk, ceramics, aromatics, forest products, ivory, and bronze goods

name.en: Copper cash and payment in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在耕地、农具、水牛、粮食储备与家织布匹。城市与市镇商工家庭拥有更高流动财富，但总体社会财富仍以土地和粮仓为主。
householdWealth.en: Typical household wealth was embodied in cultivated land, tools, water buffalo, grain stores, and homespun cloth. Urban and market-town artisan and merchant households held more liquid assets, but wealth overall remained rooted in land and granaries.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租与国家征课为基础的王朝经济，辅以地方市场和区域海陆贸易。
economicSystem.en: A dynastic agrarian economy based on rents and state levies, supplemented by local markets and regional maritime and overland trade.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由铜钱、粮仓储备、丝帛和金属器物共同构成，并非全部以贵金属形式保存。粮仓储备对灾年与军需尤为关键。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of copper cash, grain reserves, textiles, and metal goods rather than bullion alone. Granary stocks were especially important for famine relief and military supply.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持王都、寺院、军镇与水利体系的平衡，倾向以实物税和劳役减轻货币不足问题。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to balance support for the capital, monasteries, garrisons, and irrigation works, relying heavily on in-kind taxation and labor service to offset limited monetization.

description.zh: 驻守升龙与王室要地，装备较优，是国王直接控制的核心兵力。
description.en: Stationed in Thang Long and key royal sites, better equipped, and forming the core force under direct royal control.

description.zh: 由各路州县征发，为主要作战与守备力量。
description.en: Raised from provincial units and serving as the main field and garrison force.

description.zh: 依托河网与海岸活动，用于运输、巡逻与对占城方向作战。
description.en: Operating on rivers and coasts, used for transport, patrol, and warfare toward Champa.

commanderInChief.zh: 李高宗与枢密近臣
commanderInChief.en: Ly Cao Tong and his senior court commanders

technology.zh: 军队以长矛、弓弩、刀盾、战船和象兵辅助为主，善于河道作战与防御性布阵。火器尚未成体系，但工程、栅寨与水网机动能力较强。
technology.en: The army relied on spears, bows and crossbows, blades and shields, war boats, and some use of war elephants. It was strong in riverine warfare and defensive deployments; firearms were not yet systematized, but fortifications and waterway mobility were significant assets.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方占城冲突、边境山地不稳以及中国南方政局变化带来的潜在压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from conflict with Champa to the south, instability in upland frontier zones, and potential pressure arising from political changes in southern China.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于红河三角洲和平原稻作区，山区则分布着多样族群。整体人口仍以农村为主，城市化程度有限但升龙已具区域都会性质。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Red River delta and lowland rice zones, while diverse ethnic groups inhabited the uplands. Society remained overwhelmingly rural, though Thang Long had already assumed the character of a regional capital city.

socialClasses.zh: 皇室宗族、文武官僚、僧侣、地方豪族、自耕农与佃农、工匠商人、边地部族
socialClasses.en: Royal kin, civil and military officials, clergy, local magnates, peasant proprietors and tenants, artisans and merchants, and frontier tribes

allies.zh: 无固定军事同盟，但与宋朝维持朝贡与边贸关系，并与部分边地首领保持合作。
allies.en: No fixed military allies, but it maintained tributary and trade relations with Song China and cooperative ties with some frontier chiefs.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为占城，边境上也时有与山地势力的冲突。
enemies.en: Its principal rival was Champa, alongside recurrent clashes with upland frontier groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上采取对北方大国恭顺而守自主、对南方邻国争衡并重边贸安抚的务实路线。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy was pragmatic: deferential yet autonomous toward the northern great power, competitive toward southern neighbors, and reliant on trade and pacification along the frontiers.

era.zh: 中世纪东南亚农耕王国阶段
era.en: Medieval Southeast Asian agrarian kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 水稻灌溉体系、河海舟船技术、青铜铸造、佛寺建筑与早期科举官僚制度。
keyInnovations.en: Wet-rice irrigation systems, river and coastal boat technology, bronze casting, Buddhist temple construction, and an early examination-based bureaucratic order.

strengths.zh: 拥有稠密农业核心区、稳定王朝传统、较强内河交通和成熟的佛教文化网络。
strengths.en: It possessed a dense agrarian core, a stable dynastic tradition, strong inland water transport, and a mature Buddhist cultural network.

weaknesses.zh: 货币化程度有限，地方豪族与寺院势力较强，边疆控制不均衡。
weaknesses.en: Monetization remained limited, local magnates and monasteries were powerful, and frontier control was uneven.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍能维持稳定，但若宫廷衰弱与财政压力加重，地方离心与外部入侵风险将上升。
outlook.en: It was likely to remain stable in the short term, but weakening court authority and fiscal strain could raise the risks of regional fragmentation and external invasion.

description.zh: 红河流域的成熟王朝国家，在中国与东南亚之间发展独特文化秩序。
description.en: A mature dynastic state of the Red River basin, developing a distinctive order between China and Southeast Asia.

governmentForm.zh: 以上座部佛教君主制为核心、结合宫廷官僚与地方食邑网络的王国
governmentForm.en: A Theravada Buddhist monarchy centered on royal authority, supported by a court bureaucracy and local fief-holding networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王与王族为顶层，其下为宫廷贵族、军事与行政官员、佛教僧团、地方首领、自由农民、工匠与商人，以及依附劳役人口。寺院拥有大量土地与佃户，在社会组织与文化生活中占核心地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the king and royal family, followed by court nobles, military and administrative officials, the Buddhist sangha, local chiefs, free peasantry, artisans and merchants, and dependent laboring populations. Monasteries held extensive lands and attached cultivators, making them central to social organization and cultural life.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、宫廷大臣、军政贵族与大型佛教寺院上层僧侣
rulingClass.en: The royal family, court ministers, military-administrative aristocracy, and the senior clergy of major Buddhist establishments

succession.zh: 原则上由王室内部继承，通常由在位国王指定或在诸王子间竞争后确立，缺乏绝对固定的长子继承规则
succession.en: Succession was dynastic within the royal house, usually determined by royal designation or competition among princes rather than by a fully fixed rule of primogeniture

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕国王、王宫秘书机构、税赋与土地管理官员、军事指挥层及宗教捐献管理网络运作。地方则通过总督、城镇官员和附属酋长治理，王权依靠灌溉控制、土地授予与宗教施舍巩固统治。
structure.en: The central state operated through the king, palace secretariat, officials concerned with revenue and land administration, military commanders, and networks managing religious endowments. Provincial administration relied on governors, town officials, and subordinate chiefs, while royal authority was reinforced through irrigation control, land grants, and religious patronage.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存碑铭与土地文书、传达王命并协调中央政务
function.en: Drafted royal edicts, preserved inscriptions and land documents, transmitted royal commands, and coordinated central administration

function.zh: 登记耕地、核定赋役、征收粮税与实物贡赋，并监督王田与灌溉相关收益
function.en: Registered cultivated land, assessed dues and labor obligations, collected grain taxes and payments in kind, and supervised revenue from royal estates and irrigation works

function.zh: 管理近卫军、地方征召、军备仓储、边境守备与战象配置
function.en: Managed the royal guard, provincial levies, military stores, frontier defense, and the deployment of war elephants

name.en: Religious Endowments and Monastic Estates Office

function.zh: 记录寺院捐献、确认免税地、调解王权与僧团之间的土地与劳役关系
function.en: Recorded donations to monasteries, confirmed tax-exempt lands, and mediated land and labor relations between the crown and the sangha

name.zh: 地方治理与边疆事务署
name.en: Provincial Administration and Frontier Affairs Office

function.zh: 监督属城与边地首领、组织贡纳、维持交通与河运节点的秩序
function.en: Supervised dependent towns and frontier chiefs, organized tribute, and maintained order at transport and riverine nodes

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由王室任命的总督、城镇官员与地方世袭首领共同承担。核心伊洛瓦底江流域受中央控制较强，边缘山区与掸、钦等区域则多依赖朝贡关系和间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by royal governors, town officials, and hereditary local chiefs. The core Irrawaddy basin was under stronger direct control, while upland and frontier regions, including Shan- and Chin-inhabited areas, were more often governed through tribute ties and indirect rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法和佛教道德规范并行为主。土地边界、寺产、赋役和婚姻继承等事务常以碑铭、地契与地方见证裁定，严重案件由王权或高级官员审理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decrees, customary law, and Buddhist moral norms. Land boundaries, monastic property, taxation, labor obligations, marriage, and inheritance were often settled through inscriptions, charters, and local testimony, while serious cases were judged by royal authority or senior officials.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以农业实物税、劳役、地方贡纳、关津税和王室领地收入为基础。大量土地因捐赠寺院而享有减免，这一趋势增强宗教合法性但逐渐侵蚀王室税基。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on agricultural payments in kind, labor service, provincial tribute, transit dues, and income from royal estates. Large amounts of land enjoyed exemptions after donation to monasteries, a pattern that enhanced religious legitimacy but gradually eroded the royal tax base.

religion.zh: 以上座部佛教为主，并吸收大乘、密教残余传统及本地纳特神信仰。王权通过护持佛法、建塔造寺和供养僧团来证明其正统性。
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism predominated, while also incorporating residual Mahayana and esoteric elements and local nat spirit worship. Kingship expressed its legitimacy through support of the dhamma, temple construction, and patronage of the sangha.

philosophy.zh: 思想以佛教功德观、布施伦理、王者护法理念和僧团经典学习为核心，同时保留地方宇宙观与王权神圣性的观念。
philosophy.en: The intellectual world centered on Buddhist merit, the ethics of donation, the ideal of the king as protector of the faith, and scholastic monastic learning, while also retaining local cosmological ideas and notions of sacral kingship.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古缅文文字书写，源自南印度婆罗米系统并受孟文传统影响，主要见于石碑、宗教题记和土地捐献文书，也使用巴利语作为宗教与学术语言。
writingSystem.en: Old Burmese script was used, derived from the South Indian Brahmi tradition and influenced by Mon scribal practice. It appeared mainly in stone inscriptions, religious dedications, and land grants, while Pali served as the principal religious and scholastic language.

culturalAchievements.zh: 蒲甘时期以大规模砖石佛塔与寺院建筑闻名，如阿难陀寺等代表作；碑铭传统发达，壁画、佛像铸造、木构工艺和灌溉景观共同塑造了王国的文化黄金时代。
culturalAchievements.en: The Pagan age was renowned for its vast brick temple and stupa construction, with masterpieces such as the Ananda Temple. Rich inscriptional practices, mural painting, bronze Buddha casting, timber craftsmanship, and irrigated landscapes together defined a cultural golden age.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言属汉藏语系缅彝语支；宗教精英广泛使用巴利语，宫廷与南部地区亦受孟语文化影响
languageFamily.en: The main vernacular belonged to the Tibeto-Burman branch of the Sino-Tibetan family; Pali was widely used by the religious elite, and Mon cultural influence was also significant at court and in the south

gdpDescription.zh: 1200年前后的蒲甘王国是东南亚内陆最强盛的农业国家之一，经济基础建立在伊洛瓦底江中部干区的大规模灌溉、水利整修、稻作与芝麻、豆类等旱地作物上。王国同时控制南北河运要道，并通过下缅甸与印度洋贸易网络相连。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1200, Pagan was one of the strongest agrarian states in mainland Southeast Asia. Its economy rested on large-scale irrigation and water control in the dry zone of central Irrawaddy Burma, producing rice as well as sesame, pulses, and other dry-field crops. The kingdom also controlled north-south river routes and was linked through Lower Burma to the wider Indian Ocean trade network.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、稻米与豆类种植、芝麻与油料、畜牧、砖石建筑、佛塔寺院营造、金属铸造、木工、漆器、内河运输与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, rice and pulse cultivation, sesame and oilseed production, livestock raising, brick construction, temple building, metal casting, woodworking, lacquerware, inland transport, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、芝麻、豆类、盐、木材、漆器、棉布、宝石、象牙、战象，以及输入的纺织品、金属器、奢侈香料和宗教用品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, sesame, pulses, salt, timber, lacquerware, cotton cloth, gems, ivory, and war elephants, alongside imported textiles, metal goods, luxury spices, and religious items

householdWealth.zh: 一般农户财富主要体现在耕地使用权、水利可及性、役畜、粮仓储备和家族劳动力，而非统一铸币现金。靠近灌溉干渠的村社通常较富裕。
householdWealth.en: Typical household wealth was held in access to cultivated land, irrigation, draft animals, grain storage, and family labor rather than in standardized coinage. Villages near major canals were generally more prosperous.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室土地、村社耕作、寺院庄园和贡赋交换并存的农业型再分配经济，辅以内河商业与对外贸易。国家在水利和运输控制方面作用突出，但市场交换并未缺席。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian redistributive economy combining royal lands, village cultivation, monastic estates, and tribute exchange, supplemented by riverine commerce and foreign trade. The state played a major role in irrigation and transport control, though market exchange was also important.

source.en: Transit Dues and River Trade Taxes

category.en: Irrigation, Roads, and River Transport Maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以粮仓、贵金属、珠宝、贡品、役畜和可调用劳力构成，并非全部以现金形式保管。国库尚称稳健，但因大规模寺院捐献与免税地增加，长期财政承压。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves consisted of granaries, precious metals, jewels, tribute goods, draft animals, and mobilizable labor rather than cash alone. The treasury remained sound, but heavy religious endowments and the growth of tax-exempt lands created long-term fiscal pressure.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向在丰年扩大宗教营建和王权展示，同时维持灌溉体系与军队。短期内有助于巩固合法性，长期则因寺产膨胀削弱可征税土地基础。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored temple patronage and royal display in prosperous years while still supporting irrigation and the army. This strengthened legitimacy in the short term but weakened the taxable land base over time as monastic estates expanded.

description.zh: 负责保卫国王、首都与王宫，装备相对精良，是中央最可靠的常备力量。
description.en: Tasked with protecting the king, the capital, and the palace; comparatively well equipped and the most reliable standing force of the center.

description.zh: 由核心地区与附属城镇按赋役征发，战时为主力，平时多从事耕作与地方守备。
description.en: Raised through labor and military obligations from the core regions and dependent towns; the main wartime force, though in peacetime most remained farmers or local guards.

description.zh: 数量不大，多用于侦察、追击与边境行动，部分依赖北方和东部边地的骑战传统。
description.en: Limited in number, used mainly for reconnaissance, pursuit, and frontier operations, drawing in part on martial traditions of northern and eastern borderlands.

description.zh: 用于冲击、统帅平台和威慑，在东南亚战争中具有重要象征与战术作用，但维护成本高昂。
description.en: Used for shock action, command platforms, and intimidation; they had major symbolic and tactical value in Southeast Asian warfare but were expensive to maintain.

description.zh: 依托伊洛瓦底江运送军粮、工匠和士兵，对远征和防御都极为关键。
description.en: Dependent on the Irrawaddy for moving grain, craftsmen, and troops, making them vital to both campaigning and defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 那罗波帝悉都国王
commanderInChief.en: King Narapatisithu

technology.zh: 军备以弓箭、矛、刀剑、盾牌、战象、攻城器具和内河舟船为主。冶铁与铜器铸造水平良好，但尚未形成火药武器体系。军事效能高度依赖河流补给、季风时令和地方动员能力。
technology.en: Military technology centered on bows, spears, swords, shields, war elephants, siege equipment, and river craft. Ironworking and bronze casting were competent, but gunpowder weapon systems were absent. Military effectiveness depended heavily on river supply, monsoon timing, and provincial mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自边疆叛服不定的山地与掸系势力、南方交通与港口控制的竞争，以及远方草原新兴势力未来可能改变亚洲政治格局。1200年时并无立即毁灭性外敌，但边疆整合始终是长期挑战。
threats.en: Primary threats came from unstable upland and Shan-related frontier powers, competition over southern routes and ports, and the possibility that rising steppe powers might eventually transform the Asian political order. In 1200 there was no immediate existential invader, but frontier integration remained a persistent challenge.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于伊洛瓦底江中游干区、灌溉盆地与沿江聚落。核心区人口密度高，边疆山地与森林地带则较为稀疏，民族与语言构成多元。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the dry zone of middle Irrawaddy Burma, irrigated basins, and riverside settlements. The core regions were densely settled, while frontier uplands and forest zones were more thinly populated and ethnolinguistically diverse.

socialClasses.zh: 社会大体可分为王族与高官、地方首领与军功贵族、僧团、自由农民、工匠商人，以及依附于王室、贵族或寺院土地的劳役人口。僧侣虽不属世俗等级，却拥有极高威望和经济影响力。
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into the royal family and high officials, local chiefs and service nobility, the sangha, free peasants, artisans and merchants, and dependent laborers attached to royal, noble, or monastic estates. Though not a secular estate, the monkhood possessed immense prestige and economic influence.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式同盟体系；与下缅甸孟人地区、部分掸地首领及周边小邦维持朝贡、联姻或临时合作关系，并与印度洋商人保持务实往来。
allies.en: There was no stable formal alliance system in the later medieval sense; Pagan maintained tributary, marital, or temporary cooperative ties with Lower Burmese Mon regions, some Shan chiefs, and neighboring minor polities, while engaging pragmatically with Indian Ocean merchants.

enemies.zh: 并无与列举诸大文明的持续全面战争。主要对手是边疆不服从的地方势力、南方贸易节点的竞争者，以及可能挑战王权控制的属邦与山地集团。
enemies.en: Pagan was not engaged in sustained total war with the major listed civilizations. Its principal adversaries were disobedient frontier powers, rivals over southern trade nodes, and tributary or upland groups that might challenge royal control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制伊洛瓦底流域、确保下游通海通商、维持边疆朝贡秩序和扩大佛教声望为核心。王国更偏好区域霸权、附庸化与贸易整合，而非远距离征服。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling the Irrawaddy basin, securing access to the sea and commerce downstream, maintaining tributary order on the frontiers, and enhancing Buddhist prestige. The kingdom preferred regional hegemony, vassalage, and trade integration over distant conquest.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于干区大型蓄水池、堤坝与渠道灌溉体系，成熟的砖石建筑技术，佛塔与拱券寺院营造，青铜铸像、碑铭刻写以及内河运输组织。其技术优势主要体现在国家组织工程与宗教建筑，而非机械或火药革命。
keyInnovations.en: Key capacities included large reservoirs, embankments, and canal irrigation in the dry zone, mature brick construction methods, stupa and vaulted temple architecture, bronze casting, inscriptional carving, and organized river transport. Pagan’s technological strength lay above all in state-supported engineering and religious architecture rather than in mechanical or gunpowder revolutions.

strengths.zh: 王国拥有稳固的伊洛瓦底核心区、强大的佛教合法性、壮观的寺院文化、有效的灌溉农业和对内河交通的控制。那罗波帝悉都时期政治较为稳定，国力处于高峰。
strengths.en: The kingdom possessed a secure Irrawaddy core, strong Buddhist legitimacy, an impressive temple culture, effective irrigated agriculture, and control over inland river transport. Under Narapatisithu, political stability was relatively high and state power was near its peak.

weaknesses.zh: 财政高度依赖农业与劳役，边疆控制不均，骑兵力量有限，对海上贸易的直接掌控弱于海洋国家。最深层问题是寺院免税地产不断扩大，长期削弱中央税收与兵役基础。
weaknesses.en: Finances depended heavily on agriculture and labor obligations, frontier control was uneven, cavalry was limited, and direct command over maritime trade was weaker than that of seaborne states. The deepest structural problem was the continual expansion of tax-exempt monastic estates, which gradually undermined the central revenue and manpower base.

outlook.zh: 1200年时蒲甘王国前景总体乐观，仍是东南亚大陆的强国之一。但若继续大量把可征税土地转为寺产，并在边疆整合和南方港口控制上停滞，未来数十年到一世纪内将面临财政脆弱化与军事动员下降的风险。
outlook.en: In 1200, Pagan’s outlook was broadly favorable, and it remained one of the major powers of mainland Southeast Asia. Yet if taxable lands continued to be transferred to monasteries and if the kingdom stagnated in frontier integration and southern port control, it would face growing fiscal fragility and declining military mobilization over the coming decades and century.

description.zh: 伊洛瓦底江流域的强大佛教王国，以寺塔林立与灌溉农业著称。
description.en: A powerful Buddhist kingdom of the Irrawaddy basin known for temple building and irrigated agriculture.

name.zh: 马来海上贸易政体（室利佛逝）

governmentForm.zh: 以港口—河口中心为核心、依附网络构成的曼荼罗式海上君主制
governmentForm.en: A mandala-style maritime monarchy centered on port-estuary hubs and dependent networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与港口贵族、地方达图与河口首领、佛教僧团、外来商人社群、自由民、船工与农渔民、以及受役属民组成；不同港口与内陆河流区域的从属关系较为弹性。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and port aristocracy, local datus and estuarine chiefs, Buddhist monastic communities, foreign merchant groups, freemen, sailors and farming-fishing populations, and dependent laboring groups; ties of subordination were relatively elastic across ports and inland river zones.

rulingClass.zh: 以巨港王室、港口贵族、海关与航运管理者、以及附属港埠首领为统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Palembang royal family, port aristocrats, customs and shipping administrators, and chiefs of subordinate ports

succession.zh: 王位继承大致为王族世袭，并受宫廷结盟、地方首领拥戴及海上贸易节点控制力影响，继承程序并不固定。
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary within the royal line, but was influenced by court alliances, the support of local chiefs, and control over maritime trade nodes; the process was not strictly fixed.

structure.zh: 政权结构较分散，中央朝廷设于巨港，直接控制核心河口与港市，对苏门答腊、马来半岛及周边海峡据点通过册封、盟约、贡赋与婚姻关系维系统治。
structure.en: The polity had a decentralized structure, with the central court at Palembang directly controlling the core estuary and port, while authority over Sumatra, the Malay Peninsula, and nearby strait settlements was maintained through investiture, alliance, tribute, and marriage ties.

function.zh: 管理宫廷仪礼、王室财产、使节接待与王命传达
function.en: Managed court ritual, royal property, reception of envoys, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 征收关税、监管码头与仓储、登记进出船只、处理贸易争议
function.en: Collected customs, supervised docks and warehouses, registered incoming and outgoing ships, and handled trade disputes

name.en: Sea Lanes and Maritime Defense Office

function.zh: 巡视海峡航道、组织巡逻船队、压制海盗并维护灯火与引航体系
function.en: Patrolled sea lanes, organized naval patrols, suppressed piracy, and maintained beacon and pilot systems

function.zh: 联络附属港埠与内陆首领，征收贡品，处理誓约与属邦朝贡事务
function.en: Liaised with dependent ports and inland chiefs, collected tribute goods, and managed oath and tributary affairs

function.zh: 保管香料、树脂、木材、金属、粮食及军需物资
function.en: Stored spices, resins, timber, metals, foodstuffs, and military supplies

function.zh: 资助佛教寺院、管理宗教捐献、接待来往僧侣与学者
function.en: Supported Buddhist monasteries, oversaw religious endowments, and received traveling monks and scholars

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖港口长官、达图、河口首领与附属地区统治者。中央通常不深入村社日常治理，而通过征收港税、贡物与劳役，以及要求提供船只与人力来维持控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on port governors, datus, estuarine chiefs, and rulers of dependent districts. The center usually did not penetrate village life deeply, instead maintaining control through port dues, tribute, labor obligations, and demands for ships and manpower.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以王命、习惯法、海商惯例与宗教规范并行为主。港口贸易多依契约、口头担保、社群仲裁与关税官裁决处理；对海盗、叛附与逃税则施以严厉处罚。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on royal command, customary law, mercantile practice, and religious norms. Port trade was often governed by contract, oral surety, communal arbitration, and customs officials’ judgments; piracy, rebellion, and tax evasion were punished severely.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以港口关税、转口贸易抽成、附属地区贡赋、航运收费、特许贸易收益和王室控制资源收入为主，农业土地税相对次要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied primarily on port customs, shares from entrepôt trade, tribute from dependent regions, shipping dues, licensed trade revenues, and income from crown-controlled resources, while agricultural land taxation was comparatively secondary.

religion.zh: 以上座部与大乘、密教因素并存的佛教为王室主要赞助宗教，同时融合本地祖灵、神灵与印度教因素；穆斯林商人社群已在部分港口出现，但尚未主导政权文化。
religion.en: Buddhism, with overlapping Mahayana, esoteric, and some Theravada elements, remained the principal religion patronized by the court, while local ancestral and spirit beliefs and Hindu elements persisted; Muslim merchant communities were present in some ports but did not yet dominate state culture.

philosophy.zh: 政治理念强调王权神圣、海上秩序维护、功德布施与世界性贸易中心的合法性；宫廷文化受印度化王权观念与佛教功德思想深刻影响。
philosophy.en: Political thought emphasized the sanctity of kingship, maintenance of maritime order, meritorious donation, and the legitimacy of a cosmopolitan trade center; court culture was strongly shaped by Indianized concepts of sovereignty and Buddhist ideas of merit.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用源自南印度婆罗米传统的古马来文与梵文书写体系，常见于碑铭、宗教文书与贸易相关记录；口头行政与商贸交流同样重要。
writingSystem.en: Writing primarily used Old Malay and Sanskrit scripts derived from South Indian Brahmi traditions, especially in inscriptions, religious texts, and trade-related records; oral administration and mercantile communication were also highly important.

culturalAchievements.zh: 室利佛逝长期作为佛教学术与海上交流中心而著称，曾吸引域外僧侣停留学习；其成就在于整合海峡贸易网络、发展港口礼仪文化、传播马来语作为贸易通语，并维持跨印度洋与南海的宗教和商业联络。
culturalAchievements.en: Srivijaya was long renowned as a center of Buddhist learning and maritime exchange, attracting foreign monks for study; its achievements lay in integrating strait trade networks, developing port ceremonial culture, spreading Malay as a trade lingua franca, and sustaining religious and commercial links across the Indian Ocean and South China Sea.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，以古马来语为主要通语，并夹杂梵语、泰米尔语、汉语与阿拉伯—波斯商语影响
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family, with Old Malay as the principal lingua franca, influenced by Sanskrit, Tamil, Chinese, and Arabic-Persian mercantile usage

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 到1200年前后，室利佛逝已较其鼎盛时期显著衰落，部分贸易被爪哇与其他港口分流，但凭借巨港及海峡网络仍保有可观的转口收入。其经济强项不是高密度农业，而是依托海运中介、仓储、税收和区域再分配形成的港市经济。
gdpDescription.en: By around 1200, Srivijaya had declined markedly from its peak, with part of its trade diverted to Java and other ports, yet it still retained substantial entrepôt revenues through Palembang and the strait network. Its economic strength lay not in dense agrarian output but in maritime intermediation, warehousing, taxation, and regional redistribution.

mainIndustries.zh: 转口贸易、航运、造船与修船、港口仓储、林产采集、黄金与锡等矿产输出、渔业、稻作与河口农业
mainIndustries.en: Entrepôt trade, shipping, shipbuilding and repair, port warehousing, forest product collection, export of gold and tin, fishing, rice cultivation, and estuarine agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 樟脑、安息香、沉香、檀香转运、胡椒、丁香与肉豆蔻转运、金、锡、象牙、犀角、龟甲、蜂蜡、藤、木材、瓷器、丝绸、棉布、珠宝与马匹
tradeGoods.en: Camphor, benzoin, agarwood, re-exported sandalwood, pepper, re-exported cloves and nutmeg, gold, tin, ivory, rhinoceros horn, tortoiseshell, beeswax, rattan, timber, ceramics, silk, cotton textiles, jewelry, and horses

name.zh: 金银锭、外来铜钱与商品计值并用
name.en: Mixed bullion, imported cash, and commodity valuation

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分化明显。港口商人、航运承包者和与王室关联的贵族掌握大量流动财富、仓储和奴役劳力；普通渔农家庭财富有限，多以舟具、稻地、果树与家畜为主要资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Port merchants, shipping contractors, and nobles tied to the court controlled substantial liquid wealth, warehouses, and dependent labor; ordinary fishing and farming households possessed limited assets, mainly boats, rice fields, fruit trees, and livestock.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 王室主导的海上转口经济与地方首领控制资源采集相结合，市场交换活跃，但重要港税、航道控制与部分高价值商品贸易受统治者和港口精英强力干预。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined a crown-led maritime entrepôt system with resource extraction controlled by local chiefs; market exchange was active, but key customs, sea-lane control, and parts of the high-value trade were strongly influenced by the ruler and port elites.

source.zh: 王室特许贸易与垄断收益
source.en: Royal licensed trade and monopoly revenues

category.zh: 港口设施、仓储与航道维护
category.en: Port facilities, warehousing, and sea-lane maintenance
Rewards to dependencies and diplomatic gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由金银锭、珠宝、高价值香料、树脂、织物与可迅速变卖的货物构成，而非单一铸币储备。因政权衰落和属地松动，财政储备虽仍可观，但稳定性弱于鼎盛时期。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of bullion, jewelry, high-value spices, resins, textiles, and other readily saleable goods rather than a purely coined reserve. Because of political decline and loosening dependencies, these reserves remained notable but were less secure than in the polity’s heyday.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持港口吸引力与航道安全，避免对商人征敛过重，同时通过贡赋和特许贸易补足收入；在衰退背景下，朝廷更依赖对核心港口和剩余海峡节点的集中榨取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving port attractiveness and sea-lane security while avoiding excessive exactions on merchants, supplementing income through tribute and licensed trade; amid decline, the court relied more heavily on concentrated extraction from core ports and remaining strait nodes.

description.zh: 以桨帆并用战船、护航船与巡逻船为主，负责海峡巡防、护商与打击海盗，是政权最重要的军事力量。
description.en: Composed of oared-sailed war craft, escort vessels, and patrol boats, it guarded the straits, protected commerce, and fought piracy; it was the polity’s most important military arm.

description.zh: 驻守巨港及主要据点，负责仓库、码头、宫廷与关卡防卫，也可在叛乱时迅速出动。
description.en: Stationed at Palembang and major nodes, these troops defended warehouses, quays, the court, and checkpoints, and could be rapidly deployed against unrest.

description.zh: 由附属港口、河口首领和内陆社群提供，多为临时征召的舟师、矛兵与弓兵，战斗力和忠诚度不一。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate ports, estuarine chiefs, and inland communities, these were largely temporary levies of boat crews, spearmen, and archers with uneven combat quality and loyalty.

commanderInChief.zh: 摩诃罗阇兼最高统帅，由海军与宫廷贵族分掌作战
commanderInChief.en: The Maharaja as commander-in-chief, with naval and court nobles sharing operational command

technology.zh: 军事技术以热带海域航海、桨帆战船、弓矢、矛、短刀、藤甲与木制防具为主；擅长伏击、登船战、河海联动与海峡巡逻，但在重型攻城和大规模陆战方面较弱。
technology.en: Military technology centered on tropical seafaring, oared-sailed war craft, bows, spears, blades, rattan armor, and wooden protection; it excelled in ambush, boarding actions, river-sea coordination, and strait patrols, but was weaker in heavy siegecraft and large-scale land warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爪哇势力扩张、附属港口离心、海盗活动、香料航线被竞争港分流，以及对马六甲—巽他航道控制力下降。
threats.en: Major threats came from Javanese expansion, centrifugal tendencies among dependent ports, piracy, diversion of spice routes to rival harbors, and declining control over the Malacca-Sunda sea lanes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口约一百万，分布极不均衡。多数居民散居于苏门答腊东南部河流、沼泽与沿海村社，城市与港口人口比重有限，但流动商旅和季节性航海人口很多。
populationDescription.en: The population was about one million and very unevenly distributed. Most people lived in dispersed riverine, marsh, and coastal communities in southeastern Sumatra, while urban and port populations were limited in share, though mobile merchants and seasonal seafaring populations were numerous.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、港口官员与达图、宗教人士、外来商人、工匠与船工、自由农渔民、依附劳工与奴役人口并存。外来社群如泰米尔、华人、阿拉伯和波斯商人虽人数不占多数，却在港市中影响显著。
socialClasses.en: Royal and noble elites, port officials and datus, religious personnel, foreign merchants, artisans and sailors, free farming and fishing populations, and dependent laboring and enslaved people coexisted. Foreign communities such as Tamil, Chinese, Arab, and Persian merchants were not numerically dominant but were highly influential in the ports.

allies.zh: 与部分马来半岛港口、苏门答腊地方首领及若干依赖海峡贸易的商人社群维持务实联盟；对宋朝继续保持朝贡与通商关系。
allies.en: It maintained pragmatic alliances with some Malay Peninsula ports, Sumatran local chiefs, and merchant communities dependent on strait commerce; it also continued tributary and commercial relations with the Song.

enemies.zh: 主要竞争与敌对压力来自爪哇诸政权，尤其是更具进取性的东爪哇势力；此外海盗集团与脱离控制的附属港埠也是现实威胁。
enemies.en: Its main competitive and hostile pressure came from Javanese polities, especially the more assertive East Javanese powers; pirate groups and subordinate ports slipping from control were also practical threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护海峡中介地位、吸引远洋商人、向中国展示合法性、并在周边港埠间进行均衡与册封为核心。面对衰退，政策更趋防御性与商贸导向，而非积极扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving its intermediary role in the straits, attracting long-distance merchants, demonstrating legitimacy to China, and balancing and investing surrounding ports. In decline, policy became more defensive and commerce-oriented rather than expansionist.

era.zh: 十字军时代的海上贸易与区域港市文明阶段
era.en: Age of Crusades maritime trade and regional port-polity stage

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于季风航海知识、海峡引航、热带造船、港口仓储组织、跨文化商贸协作和以古马来语为核心的贸易通语体系；虽非发明密集型强国，但在网络整合与海上后勤方面高度成熟。
keyInnovations.en: Its key capabilities lay in monsoon navigation knowledge, strait pilotage, tropical shipbuilding, port warehousing organization, cross-cultural commercial coordination, and a trade lingua franca centered on Old Malay; though not a high-invention state, it was highly mature in network integration and maritime logistics.

strengths.zh: 最大优势是地处马六甲与巽他之间的关键中转位置、长期积累的海上贸易声望、多语商人网络、以及对香料与奢侈品流通的中介能力。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were its strategic position between the Malacca and Sunda routes, long-established prestige in maritime trade, multilingual merchant networks, and strong intermediary capacity in the circulation of spices and luxury goods.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治结构松散、对附属港口控制力下降、财政对贸易过度依赖、陆上农业腹地有限，以及难以阻止贸易中心向爪哇和其他新兴港市转移。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a loose political structure, weakening control over dependent ports, excessive fiscal dependence on trade, a limited agrarian hinterland, and difficulty preventing trade centers from shifting toward Java and other rising ports.

outlook.zh: 到1200年，室利佛逝仍是重要的海上节点，但已不再是无可争议的海峡霸主。若不能重建对关键港埠和航道的控制，其地位将继续被爪哇与新兴马来港口侵蚀，逐步转化为较区域化的贸易政权。
outlook.en: By 1200, Srivijaya remained an important maritime node but was no longer the uncontested master of the straits. Unless it could rebuild control over key ports and sea lanes, its position would continue to be eroded by Java and emerging Malay harbors, gradually reducing it to a more regional trading polity.

description.zh: 曾主导马六甲与巽他海峡贸易的海洋强权，1200年前后已趋衰落但仍重要。
description.en: A maritime power that had long dominated the Malacca and Sunda trade routes, declining by 1200 yet still important.

governmentForm.zh: 印度化海上君主制，由诸区域王公与宫廷贵族共同支撑的分层王权
governmentForm.en: Indianized maritime monarchy supported by regional princes and court aristocracy in a layered royal system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、婆罗门祭司、地方领主、商人、工匠、稻农、渔民与山地部族组成，沿海港市与内陆谷地之间差异明显，并保留母系亲属传统的若干影响
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, Brahmin priests, local chiefs, merchants, artisans, rice cultivators, fishermen, and upland tribes, with marked differences between coastal ports and inland valleys and some continuing influence of matrilineal kinship traditions

rulingClass.zh: 王族、占城贵族、婆罗门宗教精英与控制港口和灌溉土地的地方领主
rulingClass.en: The royal family, Cham nobility, Brahmanical religious elites, and local lords controlling ports and irrigated lands

succession.zh: 以王族内部继承为主，通常由最有实力的王子或近支宗亲在贵族与宗教精英支持下即位，继承并非绝对长子制
succession.en: Succession was primarily within the royal lineage, usually by the strongest prince or close dynastic kinsman with support from nobles and religious elites, rather than strict primogeniture

structure.zh: 中央王廷设于毗阇耶，国王通过宫廷、宗教机构与地方首领统治若干沿海区域中心；政权整合度有限，各地在纳贡、征兵与司法上保有较强自治
structure.en: The central court at Vijaya ruled through the palace, religious institutions, and local chiefs over several coastal regional centers; political integration was limited, and localities retained significant autonomy in tribute, levies, and justice

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、礼仪、使节接待与御用仓储
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremony, reception of envoys, and palace stores

function.zh: 征收港税、市场税、朝贡物资与航运关费，监督主要港口贸易
function.en: Collected port duties, market taxes, tribute goods, and shipping tolls, and supervised major port trade

function.zh: 登记稻田、调配水利劳役、核定农业赋役与维护堤坝渠道
function.en: Recorded rice lands, organized irrigation labor, assessed agrarian obligations, and maintained dikes and canals

function.zh: 管理印度教神庙捐赠地产、祭祀供养与婆罗门特权
function.en: Administered Hindu temple endowments, ritual provisions, and Brahmin privileges

function.zh: 协调王家卫队、地方征兵、海上武装与战时补给
function.en: Coordinated the royal guard, regional levies, naval forces, and wartime supply

function.zh: 起草敕令、确认土地争议裁决、处理贵族与寺院诉讼
function.en: Drafted edicts, confirmed judgments in land disputes, and handled litigation involving nobles and temples

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托区域性王公、城邑长官、港口税吏与村社首领。各地以河谷与沿海据点为单位组织，王权通过册封、婚姻、宗教赞助与军事威慑维持控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on regional princes, town governors, port tax officers, and village headmen. Areas were organized around river valleys and coastal nodes, with royal control maintained through investiture, marriage ties, religious patronage, and military pressure.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合王室敕令、习惯法与印度法传统，土地、寺产、婚姻和贡赋纠纷常由地方首领、婆罗门与王廷共同裁决，刑罚包括罚金、劳役、没收与体罚。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal edicts, customary law, and Indian legal traditions; disputes over land, temple property, marriage, and tribute were often judged jointly by local chiefs, Brahmins, and the court, with penalties including fines, labor, confiscation, and corporal punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以稻谷实物征收、港口关税、市场税、附庸贡赋、战利品与劳役为主；寺院与贵族领地享有部分免税权，导致税源分散且中央掌握不完全。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based mainly on rice in kind, port customs, market taxes, tributary payments, war booty, and labor services; temple and noble estates enjoyed partial exemptions, leaving revenues fragmented and only partly under central control.

religion.zh: 以湿婆派印度教为主，并兼有大乘佛教与地方祖灵、海神崇拜；王权通过神庙建筑和祭祀与神圣秩序相联结。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Shaivite Hinduism, with Mahayana Buddhist elements and local ancestor and sea-spirit cults; kingship was linked to sacred order through temple building and ritual.

philosophy.zh: 思想基础主要来自印度婆罗门教宇宙观、王权神授观念与地方祖先传统，重视祭祀功德、王者护法与海陆秩序。
philosophy.en: Its philosophical foundations came chiefly from Indian Brahmanical cosmology, divine kingship, and local ancestral traditions, emphasizing ritual merit, royal protection of sacred order, and maritime as well as agrarian balance.

writingSystem.zh: 使用占语碑铭文字与梵文书写系统，铭文多见于神庙、王室诏令和捐赠记录。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom used Cham script alongside Sanskrit writing traditions, especially in temple inscriptions, royal edicts, and donation records.

culturalAchievements.zh: 占婆以红砖神庙建筑、石雕林迦与神像、海上贸易文化、碑铭传统及受印度影响而本地化的艺术风格著称；至1200年前后，毗阇耶地区仍是重要政治与艺术中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Champa was known for its red-brick temple architecture, stone lingas and divine statuary, maritime trade culture, inscriptional traditions, and a localized artistic style shaped by Indian influences; around 1200, the Vijaya region remained an important political and artistic center.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，占语支
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family, Chamic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 占婆经济为中等规模的海陆混合型经济，依赖沿海贸易、稻作河谷、渔业、林产与转口商业。与大型东亚帝国相比体量较小，但在南海航线上具有明显区位价值。
gdpDescription.en: The Cham economy was a medium-scale mixed maritime and agrarian economy, dependent on coastal trade, rice-growing valleys, fisheries, forest products, and entrepot commerce. Smaller than the great East Asian empires, it nonetheless held clear strategic value on the South China Sea routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻农业、渔盐业、造船与航海、港口贸易、林木采集、象牙与香木输出、手工纺织与金属工艺
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing and salt production, shipbuilding and seafaring, port commerce, forest extraction, export of ivory and aromatic woods, textile crafts, and metalworking

tradeGoods.zh: 出口沉香、檀香、象牙、犀角、龟甲、奴隶、盐、海产与部分纺织品；进口中国陶瓷、金属器、丝绸、印度布匹、珠宝、宗教用品与奢侈消费品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included agarwood, sandalwood, ivory, rhinoceros horn, tortoiseshell, slaves, salt, marine products, and some textiles; imports included Chinese ceramics, metal goods, silk, Indian cloth, jewels, religious items, and luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为稻谷储备、牲畜、渔具、小舟、织物与亲属网络；沿海商人和贵族家庭则持有金银、进口陶瓷、奴仆、船只与土地。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners was mainly held in rice stores, livestock, fishing gear, small boats, textiles, and kin networks; coastal merchant and noble households held bullion, imported ceramics, servants, vessels, and land.

economicSystem.zh: 以王权—地方领主—港市商人共同运作的贡赋型商业经济，农业提供生计基础，海贸提供现金性财富与奢侈品流通。
economicSystem.en: A tributary-commercial economy jointly operated by the monarchy, local lords, and port merchants, in which agriculture provided subsistence while maritime trade generated monetizable wealth and the circulation of luxuries.

source.en: Tribute from dependents and local offerings

source.zh: 王室领地与寺产关联收益
罚金、战利品与杂项征收

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室库藏规模有限，包含金银器、进口奢侈品、珠宝、战时储粮与可动员的寺院财富。因长期对外战争和地方离心，财政缓冲能力偏弱。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was limited in scale, consisting of gold and silver objects, imported luxuries, jewels, wartime grain stores, and temple wealth that could be mobilized. Because of prolonged external warfare and centrifugal local forces, fiscal reserves were relatively weak.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重战时汲取与港口税收，王廷在和平时期依赖贸易收入和地方进贡，一遇战争则加强征发、索贡与宗教名义动员。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized wartime extraction and port taxation; in peacetime the court relied on trade income and local tribute, while in war it intensified levies, demands for offerings, and mobilization under religious legitimacy.

description.zh: 负责保卫毗阇耶宫廷与核心城防，装备长矛、剑、盾与部分弓弩，是王权最可靠的常备力量。
description.en: Tasked with protecting the court at Vijaya and the core fortifications, equipped with spears, swords, shields, and some bows and crossbows; this was the most reliable standing force of the crown.

description.zh: 由地方领主和村社临时征发，数量最多但训练不均，主要用于守城、突袭和补充战线。
description.en: Raised temporarily by local lords and village communities, these were the most numerous troops but unevenly trained, mainly used for garrisoning, raids, and reinforcing fronts.

description.zh: 象队象征王权并用于冲击敌阵、震慑步兵与支援指挥中枢，但维护成本高且数量有限。
description.en: War elephants symbolized royal authority and were used to shock enemy lines, intimidate infantry, and support command positions, though they were costly to maintain and limited in number.

description.zh: 占婆骑兵规模不大，多为贵族护从与快速联络、追击单位，受地形和马匹来源限制。
description.en: Cham cavalry was modest in scale, consisting mostly of noble retainers and fast communication or pursuit units, constrained by terrain and horse supply.

description.zh: 由桨帆战船、水手、弓箭手与海上突击队组成，负责护港、巡海、运兵和沿岸袭击，是占婆最具特色的武装力量。
description.en: Composed of oared and sailed warcraft, sailors, archers, and maritime assault troops, these forces protected ports, patrolled coastal waters, transported troops, and launched coastal raids, making them the most distinctive military arm of Champa.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王阇耶因陀罗跋摩
commanderInChief.en: King Jaya Indravarman

technology.zh: 军备以冷兵器时代技术为主，使用长矛、刀剑、弓箭、盾牌、战象、木制与砖石城防以及沿海战船；军事优势更多来自机动海袭和熟悉海岸地形，而非重型攻城技术。
technology.en: Military technology remained primarily that of the pre-gunpowder age, using spears, blades, bows, shields, war elephants, timber and brick fortifications, and coastal warcraft; its advantages came more from mobile maritime raiding and familiarity with the coastline than from heavy siege capabilities.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方和南方的高棉吴哥势力，以及北方大越的持续压力；内部则存在地方领主离心、战时补给不足和港口争夺。
threats.en: The principal threats came from Khmer Angkor to the west and south and sustained pressure from Dai Viet to the north; internal dangers included centrifugal local lords, wartime supply shortages, and rivalry over ports.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 1200年前后，占婆总人口约七十万，主要分布于中南部沿海河谷、港口聚落与若干内陆农业区，人口密度远低于红河和湄公河大平原国家。
populationDescription.en: Around 1200, Champa had an estimated population of about 700,000, concentrated in the central and southern coastal river valleys, port settlements, and several inland agricultural zones, with population density far below that of the Red River and lower Mekong states.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族、婆罗门与大商人；中层包括地方自由民、熟练工匠、船主与中小地主；下层为佃农、劳役民、债役者、奴仆及被征服或依附的山地族群。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata comprised the royal house, nobles, Brahmins, and major merchants; the middle strata included free local commoners, skilled artisans, shipowners, and lesser landholders; the lower strata consisted of tenant cultivators, laborers, debt dependents, servants, and conquered or subordinate upland peoples.

allies.zh: 占婆在1200年前后缺乏稳定的长期强盟，通常依靠临时性的海贸伙伴、地方附庸与对吴哥或大越的机会主义平衡。
allies.en: Around 1200, Champa lacked stable long-term great-power allies, relying instead on temporary maritime partners, local dependents, and opportunistic balancing against Angkor or Dai Viet.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者为吴哥高棉帝国与大越王国，两者都曾在不同时期对占婆领土、港口和政治自主形成强压。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were the Khmer Empire of Angkor and the kingdom of Dai Viet, both of which at different times exerted strong pressure on Cham territory, ports, and political autonomy.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存性均衡、恢复港口贸易与维持沿海独立为核心，通过朝贡、通商、联姻、袭扰与短期结盟等多种手段周旋于区域强权之间。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival, restoration of port commerce, and maintenance of coastal independence, using tribute, trade, marriage diplomacy, raiding, and short-term alliances to maneuver among stronger regional powers.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与能力包括季风航海经验、沿海造船、砖砌宗教建筑、灌溉稻作、石雕与碑铭制作，以及跨海贸易组织能力；整体技术水平在东南亚沿海国家中具有竞争力，但不及宋朝等大型文明。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and capabilities included monsoon navigation, coastal shipbuilding, brick religious architecture, irrigated rice cultivation, stone carving and inscription making, and the organization of overseas trade; overall technological level was competitive among Southeast Asian coastal states but below that of major civilizations such as Song China.

strengths.zh: 地处南海航线要冲，港口网络灵活，海上袭击和转口贸易能力强，文化凝聚力依托王权与神庙体系，且拥有适应沿海地形的军事传统。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in its position on major South China Sea routes, flexible port networks, strong raiding and entrepot capabilities, cultural cohesion rooted in kingship and temple institutions, and military traditions adapted to the coast.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与资源基础有限，领土狭长分散，中央集权较弱，地方领主自主性强，易受吴哥和大越两面夹击，长期战争损耗财政与人口。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a limited demographic and resource base, elongated and fragmented territory, weak centralization, strong local autonomy among lords, vulnerability to pressure from both Angkor and Dai Viet, and the fiscal and demographic strain of prolonged war.

outlook.zh: 短期内占婆仍能凭借海贸与地方动员维持独立，但若无法有效整合各区域中心并减轻两线战争压力，其国力将继续相对衰退，在十三世纪面临更大外部干预风险。
outlook.en: In the short term, Champa could still preserve its independence through maritime trade and regional mobilization, but unless it integrated its regional centers more effectively and reduced pressure from warfare on two fronts, its relative power would continue to decline and it would face greater external intervention in the thirteenth century.

description.zh: 越南中南部海岸的航海王国，长期与高棉和大越冲突。
description.en: A maritime kingdom on the central and southern Vietnamese coast, long in conflict with Khmer and Dai Viet.

name.zh: 后托尔特克中墨西哥文明

ruler.zh: 多个地方统治者（特拉托阿尼及其他贵族领袖）
ruler.en: Multiple local rulers (tlatoani and other noble leaders)

rulerTitle.zh: 特拉托阿尼、领主、祭司统治者

capital.zh: 前图拉地区及周边城邦
capital.en: Former Tula zone and surrounding city-states

governmentForm.zh: 由多个纳瓦及相关族群城邦组成的松散邦联，权力分散于地方宫廷、武士贵族与祭司集团
governmentForm.en: A loose confederational order of Nahua and related city-states, with power divided among local courts, warrior nobility, and priestly groups

socialStructure.zh: 社会以贵族、祭司、武士、自由平民、佃耕者、工匠、商旅与奴隶组成；血缘、城邦归属与军功决定地位
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of nobles, priests, warriors, free commoners, tenant cultivators, artisans, merchants, and slaves; status depended on lineage, city-state affiliation, and military prestige

rulingClass.zh: 地方世袭贵族、军功显贵、大神庙祭司与控制贡赋网络的城邦领袖
rulingClass.en: Local hereditary nobles, military elites, major temple priests, and city-state rulers who controlled tribute networks

succession.zh: 多为地方贵族家族内部继承，通常由宗族长老、武士集团和祭司共同认可；部分城邦存在竞争性择立
succession.en: Succession was usually within local noble houses, with recognition by lineage elders, warrior groups, and priests; some city-states also saw competitive selection among eligible nobles

structure.zh: 1200年前后的中墨西哥并无统一帝国，而是由若干城邦、部族领地和区域联盟构成。图拉崩溃后，传统托尔特克威望仍被各地统治者借用以合法化自身统治，但实际行政以地方宫廷为核心，依托宗庙、贡赋与军事追随关系运作。
structure.en: Around 1200, central Mexico was not a unified empire but a mosaic of city-states, tribal territories, and regional alliances. After the fall of Tula, Toltec prestige continued to be invoked by many rulers for legitimacy, but real administration was local in character, centered on courts and sustained by temples, tribute, and military followings.

function.zh: 辅佐地方统治者处理继承、联盟、贡赋分配与对外战争决策
function.en: Assisted local rulers in succession, alliance management, tribute distribution, and decisions on external warfare

function.zh: 征收玉米、棉布、可可、盐、黑曜石制品与劳役，并管理公共仓储
function.en: Collected maize, cotton cloth, cacao, salt, obsidian goods, and labor dues, while managing public storehouses

function.zh: 主持历法、祭祀、神谕、教育与王权合法性仪式
function.en: Oversaw calendrics, rituals, divination, education, and ceremonies legitimizing rulership

function.zh: 组织城邦武装、守卫贸易通道、发动劫掠与保卫农田和聚落
function.en: Organized city-state forces, guarded trade routes, launched raids, and defended farmlands and settlements

function.zh: 监督集市秩序、度量习惯、专业工匠分工以及跨地区交换
function.en: Supervised market order, customary measures, specialized craft production, and interregional exchange

name.zh: 地方司法与纠纷调停机构

function.zh: 处理土地、婚姻、债务、贡役与人身伤害纠纷，以习惯法和统治者裁断为主
function.en: Handled disputes over land, marriage, debt, tribute labor, and bodily injury, relying mainly on customary law and rulership decisions

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城邦、附属村社和农耕聚落为单位。每个主要城邦通常由统治家族、祭司和战士首领共同管理，周边村落通过贡赋、劳役和军事从属关系纳入体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through city-states, dependent villages, and farming communities. Each major city-state was typically governed by a ruling lineage working with priests and war leaders, while surrounding settlements were integrated through tribute, labor obligations, and military subordination.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为口传习惯法、宗教规范和领主裁决的结合。重视财产、婚姻、贡赋义务、市场秩序与对神庙的冒犯；惩罚包括赔偿、劳役、没收、奴役与仪式性处置。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined oral customary law, religious norms, and rulership judgments. It emphasized property, marriage, tribute obligations, market order, and offenses against temples; punishments included compensation, labor service, confiscation, enslavement, and ritual penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础不是统一货币税，而是实物贡赋与劳役征发。主要包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、羽毛、陶器、黑曜石器、盐和建筑劳役；贸易关津和战利品也是重要补充。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal extraction was based not on a uniform money tax but on tribute in kind and labor drafts. Main obligations included maize, beans, chili, cotton cloth, feathers, pottery, obsidian tools, salt, and construction labor; trade tolls and war booty were also important supplements.

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以雨神、羽蛇神、战神、玉米与大地神灵崇拜为核心，祭祀、历法、祖先记忆与神庙礼仪深度结合政治权威
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on rain gods, the Feathered Serpent, war deities, maize, and earth divinities, with ritual, calendrics, ancestral memory, and temple ceremony closely tied to political authority

philosophy.zh: 世界观强调周期性时间、天地平衡、祭献义务、武士荣誉与统治合法性的神圣根源；知识主要由祭司、贵族与口头传统保存
philosophy.en: Its worldview emphasized cyclical time, cosmic balance, obligations of offering, warrior honor, and the sacred roots of political legitimacy; knowledge was preserved mainly by priests, nobles, and oral tradition

writingSystem.zh: 图像—表意兼表音的抄本和纪事传统，辅以历法记数与地名符号；尚未形成像欧亚文字那样完全音素化的书写体系
writingSystem.en: A pictorial-logographic and partly phonetic manuscript and annal tradition, supported by calendrical notation and place glyphs; it had not developed into a fully phonetic script like those of Eurasia

culturalAchievements.zh: 继承托尔特克时期的建筑、浮雕、羽饰、石工、黑曜石工艺与神话传统；中墨西哥集市网络、宗教图像和城邦仪礼继续繁荣，尽管大一统政治已瓦解。
culturalAchievements.en: It inherited Toltec-era architecture, relief carving, featherwork, stoneworking, obsidian craftsmanship, and mythic traditions; central Mexican market networks, religious imagery, and city-state ceremonial life remained vibrant even though unified political order had collapsed.

languageFamily.zh: 以乌托-阿兹特克语系中的纳瓦诸语为主，并有奥托-曼格诸语及其他区域语言并存
languageFamily.en: Primarily Nahuan languages of the Uto-Aztecan family, with Oto-Manguean and other regional languages also present

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业为基础，辅以区域贸易和工艺生产。图拉衰落后，财富分散于多个城邦和市集中心，整体生产能力仍可观，但政治破碎削弱了大规模动员和跨地区整合。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was fundamentally agrarian, supported by regional trade and craft production. After Tula's decline, wealth became dispersed among multiple city-states and market centers; overall productive capacity remained significant, but political fragmentation weakened large-scale mobilization and interregional integration.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与辣椒种植、棉纺织、陶器制造、黑曜石开采与加工、石工、建筑、羽饰工艺、盐业与地区贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and chili cultivation, cotton weaving, pottery manufacture, obsidian extraction and finishing, stoneworking, construction, featherwork, salt production, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、棉布、黑曜石刀具、陶器、盐、可可、羽毛、玉石、石灰、猎物皮革和祭仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, cotton cloth, obsidian blades, pottery, salt, cacao, feathers, jade and greenstone, lime, game hides, and ritual goods

name.zh: 以货物交换为主的准货币体系

unitName.en: cacao beans and cotton cloth lengths

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、玉米储粮、陶器、纺织品、工具和亲族互助网络；贵族家庭则控制土地、附属村社、贡赋与奢侈工艺品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly embodied in access to farmland, maize stores, pottery, textiles, tools, and kin support networks; noble households controlled land, dependent communities, tribute, and luxury craft goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、城邦贡赋、市场交换和礼物再分配并存的混合经济；并非市场资本主义，而是家户生产与政治再分配占主导
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining village agriculture, city-state tribute, market exchange, and gift redistribution; it was not market capitalism, but a system dominated by household production and political redistribution

source.en: Labor dues and public works service

treasuryDescription.zh: 并不存在统一的中央国库，而是由若干主要城邦神庙、宫廷仓库和贵族储藏构成分散财政储备。由于政治衰退与争战，财政安全性不高。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single central treasury; instead, reserves were dispersed among major city-state temples, palace storehouses, and noble holdings. Owing to political decline and conflict, fiscal security was limited.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持祭祀、守备和对附属村社的控制，在歉收或战乱时更多依赖加重劳役和临时征敛，长期上加剧地方离心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed mainly to sustain ritual life, garrisons, and control over dependent communities; in poor harvests or wartime it relied on heavier labor drafts and emergency exactions, which in the long run increased local centrifugal tendencies.

unit.en: cacao beans and cotton cloth lengths

description.zh: 由贵族武士及其追随者组成，装备木甲、棉甲、矛、投矛器、棍剑和盾牌，是主要作战力量
description.en: Made up of noble warriors and their retainers, equipped with quilted armor, spears, atlatls, clubs, swords, and shields; this was the principal combat arm

description.zh: 由农民和附属村社成年男性临时征集，用于守城、运输、围攻和战时补充
description.en: Temporarily raised from peasants and adult males of dependent communities for town defense, transport, sieges, and wartime reinforcement

description.zh: 直属地方统治者与大贵族的精锐战士，承担突击、护卫和仪式性军事任务
description.en: Elite fighters directly attached to local rulers and great nobles, serving in assault, guard, and ceremonial military roles

description.zh: 负责山道、集市通路和边缘地带警戒，兼任信使与小规模袭扰
description.en: Responsible for watch over mountain routes, market roads, and frontier zones, also serving as messengers and conducting small-scale raids

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各城邦统治者及其军首领分别统军
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each city-state ruler and his war leaders commanded separately

technology.zh: 以黑曜石刃器、投矛器、长矛、棉甲、木盾和石木攻城器具为主，善于短程冲击、俘虏战和地区性围攻，但缺乏骑兵、铁器与大型攻城机械
technology.en: Military technology relied on obsidian-edged weapons, atlatls, spears, quilted armor, wooden shields, and simple siege tools; it was effective in short-range shock combat, captive-taking warfare, and regional sieges, but lacked cavalry, iron weapons, and large siege engines

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边竞争城邦、北方半游动族群南下袭扰、对贸易通道的争夺，以及图拉旧核心区权威衰落后不断爆发的地方战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival neighboring city-states, raids by semi-nomadic groups from the north, struggles over trade routes, and recurring local wars after the decline of authority in the old Tula core zone.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于中墨西哥高原谷地、湖盆周边、灌溉和旱作混合农业区，以及旧托尔特克影响圈内的城镇与村社。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the valleys and basins of the central Mexican highlands, around lake zones, in mixed irrigated and dry-farming areas, and in towns and villages within the former Toltec sphere of influence.

name.zh: 索奇卡尔科遗留聚落区
特斯科科湖区早期纳瓦聚落
name.en: Early Nahua settlements of the Texcoco lake zone

socialClasses.zh: 上层为世袭贵族、祭司和武士领袖；中层为自由平民农户、地方工匠和商旅；下层包括附属劳役者、债务奴与战俘奴隶。不同族群与城邦之间地位差异明显。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of hereditary nobles, priests, and war leaders; the middle strata included free peasant households, local artisans, and traders; the lower strata included dependent laborers, debt bondsmen, and war captives. Status differences between ethnic groups and city-states were pronounced.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友体系，各城邦常按婚姻、贸易和共同敌人结成短期联盟；与部分中墨西哥与普埃布拉-特拉斯卡拉地区政体保持往来
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; city-states formed short-lived alliances based on marriage, trade, and common enemies, with connections to polities in central Mexico and the Puebla-Tlaxcala region

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是争夺旧托尔特克遗产和贸易要道的邻近城邦，以及北方奇奇梅卡诸群体的袭扰势力
enemies.en: Its enemies were mainly neighboring city-states competing over the Toltec legacy and key trade corridors, as well as raiding Chichimeca groups from the north

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以地方生存和声望竞争为主，通过联姻、朝贡、贸易互惠和战争相结合来维持势力范围；缺乏跨区域统一外交战略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was driven chiefly by local survival and prestige competition, using a mixture of marriage alliances, tribute relations, reciprocal trade, and warfare to maintain spheres of influence; there was no unified long-range diplomatic strategy.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 延续成熟的黑曜石工艺、阶梯金字塔与石建筑技术、灌溉与梯田农业、羽饰与纺织工艺、历法祭仪知识以及区域市场组织能力
keyInnovations.en: Key capabilities included mature obsidian craftsmanship, stepped pyramid and stone building techniques, irrigation and terraced agriculture, featherwork and textile production, calendrical ritual knowledge, and strong regional market organization

strengths.zh: 农业基础稳固，市场交换活跃，手工业尤其黑曜石与纺织业发达；托尔特克文化威望仍具整合作用，宗教与仪式体系能动员人力与资源。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in a solid agricultural base, active market exchange, and strong craft production, especially in obsidian and textiles; Toltec cultural prestige still had integrative value, and religious-ritual systems could mobilize labor and resources.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治碎片化、缺乏统一统帅、财政分散和城邦间持续冲突；旧中心衰落削弱了贸易安全与权威，北方迁徙和袭扰进一步加剧不稳定。
weaknesses.en: Its greatest weaknesses were political fragmentation, absence of unified command, dispersed finance, and continuing conflict among city-states; the decline of the old center weakened trade security and authority, while northern migration and raiding further deepened instability.

outlook.zh: 短期内将继续处于分裂与重组并存的局面，旧托尔特克秩序难以恢复；但中墨西哥的人口、农业和宗教中心仍具强大再生能力，未来可能由新的纳瓦城邦联盟重新整合地区政治。
outlook.en: In the short term it would likely remain caught between fragmentation and recomposition, with the old Toltec order unlikely to be restored; yet the population, agriculture, and religious centers of central Mexico retained strong regenerative capacity, making future regional re-integration by new Nahua city-state alliances plausible.

description.zh: 托尔特克秩序瓦解后，中部墨西哥由多个继承城邦与区域联盟分立。
description.en: After the collapse of Toltec order, central Mexico was divided among successor city-states and regional alliances.

ruler.zh: 多位库胡尔·阿豪统治者（神圣领主）

capital.zh: 奇琴伊察、马亚潘及其他城邦中心
capital.en: Chichen Itza, Mayapan, and other city-state centers

governmentForm.zh: 分散的城邦体制，兼有区域联盟与霸权网络
governmentForm.en: Decentralized city-state system with regional leagues and hegemonic networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会由神圣君主、世袭贵族、祭司、武士、商人、工匠、农民与奴隶构成；各城邦结构相似，但因地区而异。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of sacred rulers, hereditary nobles, priests, warriors, merchants, artisans, commoner farmers, and slaves; structures were broadly similar across city-states but varied by region.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、贵族宗族、祭司集团与军事领袖共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: Royal families, noble lineages, priestly groups, and military leaders formed the ruling class

succession.zh: 多以父系世袭为主，但常受贵族协商、军事力量与联盟政治影响，继承并不总是稳定
succession.en: Succession was generally patrilineal and hereditary, but often shaped by noble negotiation, military force, and alliance politics, making inheritance not always stable

structure.zh: 1200年前后的玛雅低地并无统一帝国，而是由多个城邦、港市与区域联盟构成。每个主要政体由阿豪或库胡尔·阿豪统治，下设贵族议事集团、宫廷祭司、税贡征收者与军事头领。奇琴伊察影响已衰落，北部尤卡坦的马亚潘联盟开始兴起，但地区政治仍高度多中心化。
structure.en: Around 1200 CE the Maya lowlands did not form a unified empire, but consisted of multiple city-states, port towns, and regional leagues. Each major polity was ruled by an ajaw or k'uhul ajaw, supported by noble councils, court priests, tribute collectors, and military captains. Chichen Itza's dominance had waned, while the Mayapan league in northern Yucatan was rising, yet regional politics remained highly polycentric.

function.zh: 处理统治、宫廷礼仪、使节接待、联盟婚姻与重大政治决策
function.en: Handled rulership, court ceremony, reception of envoys, alliance marriages, and major political decisions

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维持历法、占卜吉凶、记录王权仪式与神圣合法性
function.en: Conducted rituals, maintained calendars, divined auspicious dates, and recorded royal ceremonies and sacred legitimacy

function.zh: 征收实物贡赋，管理玉米、棉布、盐、可可、蜂蜜与羽毛等储备
function.en: Collected tribute in kind and managed stores of maize, cotton cloth, salt, cacao, honey, and feathers

function.zh: 组织武士集团、召集附庸兵力、守卫道路、港口、神庙与城邑
function.en: Organized warrior groups, summoned subordinate forces, and guarded roads, ports, temples, and settlements

function.zh: 监管市场、港口往来、长途贸易商队及沿海独木舟贸易
function.en: Oversaw markets, port traffic, long-distance merchant caravans, and coastal canoe trade

function.zh: 协调村庄劳役、灌溉与水池维护、土地使用与地方纠纷调解
