function.en: Coordinated village labor, maintenance of reservoirs and waterworks, land use, and mediation of local disputes

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠村社首领、贵族代理人和附属小城首长。各地通过家族网络与贡赋关系联系到中心城邦，控制力强弱不一；在北尤卡坦，井泉、盐场和贸易节点附近的管理更为紧密。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on village headmen, noble agents, and subordinate town leaders. Different areas were tied to central city-states through kinship networks and tribute relations, with uneven levels of control; in northern Yucatan, administration was tighter around cenotes, salt flats, and trade nodes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁决和宗教规范为基础。土地、婚姻、贡赋、盗窃、叛乱与亵渎祭祀等事务由地方首领或宫廷裁断，刑罚可包括赔偿、劳役、没收、流放与处死。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal judgment, and religious norms. Matters involving land, marriage, tribute, theft, rebellion, and ritual sacrilege were judged by local leaders or the court, with penalties including compensation, labor service, confiscation, exile, and execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 税贡主要以实物和劳役形式征收，包括玉米、豆类、棉布、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、可可、羽毛、陶器和建筑劳役。港口与市场可能征收通行费或贸易抽成，但制度因城邦而异。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation and tribute were collected mainly in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, honey, wax, cacao, feathers, pottery, and construction labor. Ports and markets could also levy tolls or trade shares, though practices varied by city-state.

religion.zh: 宗教为多神信仰，崇拜库库尔坎、恰克、伊察姆纳、伊什切尔等神祇，并强调祖先崇拜、祭祀献供、圣井与金字塔神庙仪式。人祭在部分中心存在，尤其与战争、王权与祈雨相关。
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, venerating deities such as Kukulkan, Chaac, Itzamna, and Ix Chel, while emphasizing ancestor worship, offerings, sacred cenotes, and rituals at pyramid temples. Human sacrifice existed at some centers, especially in connection with warfare, rulership, and rainmaking.

philosophy.zh: 玛雅思想重视宇宙循环、历法时间、神人与王权的联系，以及维持天地秩序的祭祀责任。政治合法性与天象、祖先和神圣空间密切相连。
philosophy.en: Maya thought emphasized cosmic cycles, calendrical time, the link between gods and rulership, and the ritual responsibility to maintain cosmic order. Political legitimacy was closely tied to celestial signs, ancestors, and sacred space.

writingSystem.zh: 使用玛雅象形文字传统，但到后古典期，低地碑铭书写较古典期减少；文字仍用于抄本、宗教知识、历法与贵族记录，并与图像叙事结合。
writingSystem.en: The Maya hieroglyphic writing tradition remained in use, though monumental inscription in the lowlands had declined from Classic levels by the Postclassic period; writing still served codices, religious knowledge, calendrics, and noble records, often combined with pictorial narration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 后古典期玛雅在天文历法、宗教建筑、壁画、羽饰工艺、陶器、石雕和长途海陆贸易组织方面延续高水平传统。奇琴伊察、乌斯马尔遗产及北尤卡坦的新政治中心体现了托尔特克影响与本土玛雅传统的融合。
culturalAchievements.en: The Postclassic Maya sustained high traditions in astronomy and calendrics, religious architecture, murals, featherwork, ceramics, stone carving, and the organization of long-distance maritime and overland trade. The legacy of Chichen Itza, Uxmal, and emerging northern Yucatan centers reflected a fusion of Toltec influences with native Maya traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系，各地区通行尤卡坦玛雅语、乔尔语支及其他相关语言
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family, with Yucatec Maya, Cholan-related varieties, and other associated languages spoken in different regions

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米农业、园圃种植、盐业、棉纺织、蜂蜜蜡、手工业与区域贸易为基础。由于缺乏大型役畜、铁器和轮式运输，整体生产率有限，但在中美洲海陆交换网络中具有重要商业活力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on maize agriculture, household gardening, salt production, cotton textiles, honey and wax, crafts, and regional trade. Without large draft animals, iron tools, or wheeled transport, overall productivity remained limited, yet the civilization was commercially vibrant within Mesoamerican land and sea exchange networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、棉布织造、盐业、可可流通、陶器、石器、羽饰、蜂蜜与蜡生产、建筑与贸易运输
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cotton weaving, salt production, cacao circulation, pottery, stone tools, featherwork, honey and wax production, construction, and trade transport

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、棉布、可可、黑曜石、玉石、羽毛、蜂蜜、蜡、陶器、铜铃、海贝、鱼干与染料
tradeGoods.en: Salt, cotton cloth, cacao, obsidian, jade, feathers, honey, wax, pottery, copper bells, marine shell, dried fish, and dyes

name.zh: 可可豆与棉布交换体系
name.en: Cacao bean and cotton cloth exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为住房、农地使用权、储粮、衣物、陶器、工具和家禽。贵族家庭则掌握玉石、金属饰物、精美织物、奴仆、贸易关系与宗教政治特权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners consisted mainly of dwellings, use-rights to farmland, grain stores, clothing, pottery, tools, and poultry. Noble households controlled jade, metal ornaments, fine textiles, servants, trade connections, and politico-religious privileges.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为村社农业基础上的贡赋—市场混合体系。日常生活依靠家庭生产与地方交换，统治层通过贡赋、劳役和港市贸易控制剩余，长途商人与沿海航运在后古典期尤为重要。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a tribute-market hybrid built upon village agriculture. Daily life depended on household production and local exchange, while elites controlled surpluses through tribute, labor obligations, and port commerce; long-distance merchants and coastal shipping were especially important in the Postclassic era.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备主要并非标准化硬币，而是以储粮、棉布、可可、盐、玉器、羽毛、金属饰件和祭祀珍品的形式保存，并分散于宫殿、仓库、神庙与贵族家族之中。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were not chiefly held as standardized coin, but as grain stores, cotton cloth, cacao, salt, jade, feathers, metal ornaments, and ritual valuables, distributed among palaces, storehouses, temples, and noble households.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持宗教合法性、精英再分配与军事威望。各城邦通常避免过度集中货币税，而依赖实物贡赋、劳役和对贸易节点的控制；在稳定时期，盈余更多用于大型仪式与公共工程。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized religious legitimacy, elite redistribution, and military prestige. City-states generally avoided highly centralized money taxation, relying instead on tribute in kind, labor dues, and control of trade nodes; in stable times, surpluses were often directed to grand ceremonies and public works.

description.zh: 由贵族武士、宫廷近卫和职业化程度较高的战士构成，是主要进攻力量
description.en: Composed of noble warriors, court guards, and more professionalized fighters, forming the main offensive force

description.zh: 战时由附属城镇和村社提供，多承担守备、搬运、围攻支援与人数补充
description.en: Raised in wartime from subordinate towns and villages, often serving in defense, transport, siege support, and manpower reinforcement

description.zh: 负责海岸监视、港口保护、贸易航线巡查和沿海独木舟作战
description.en: Responsible for coastal watch, port protection, trade route patrol, and canoe-based fighting along the shore

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦统治者及其战首共同统率
commanderInChief.en: City-state rulers and their war captains jointly commanded forces

technology.zh: 武器主要包括投矛器、长矛、弓箭、木剑、石刃与棉甲；后古典期在中美洲交流中也可见少量铜制器物，但金属并未主导战争。防御依赖城墙、台地、神庙区与天然地形。
technology.en: Weapons mainly included atlatls, spears, bows, wooden swords, stone blades, and quilted cotton armor; some copper objects also appeared through wider Mesoamerican exchange in the Postclassic, but metal did not dominate warfare. Defense relied on walls, elevated precincts, temple compounds, and natural terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自玛雅内部城邦竞争、贸易线路争夺、北尤卡坦霸权更替以及与中部美洲势力相关的军事压力。外部跨洋威胁在当时并不存在。
threats.en: The main threats came from rivalry among Maya city-states, struggles over trade routes, shifts in northern Yucatan hegemony, and military pressures linked to wider Mesoamerican powers. There was no transoceanic external threat at this time.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约三百万，主要分布于尤卡坦北部、低地聚落带、沿海港口和部分南部遗续中心。古典期若干核心区人口已较鼎盛时期下降，但北部与贸易节点仍保持较高密度。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 3 million, concentrated in northern Yucatan, lowland settlement belts, coastal ports, and some surviving southern centers. Several Classic-period core zones had declined from their peak, but the north and key trade nodes still sustained relatively high densities.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与贵族、祭司、武士、商人、专业工匠、村社农民、劳役者与奴隶。贵族控制礼仪、土地分配和对外贸易网络；平民承担农业与公共劳役，是社会基础。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included royalty and nobles, priests, warriors, merchants, specialist artisans, village farmers, laborers, and slaves. Nobles controlled ritual, land allocation, and long-distance trade networks, while commoners carried the burden of agriculture and public labor as the social base.

allies.zh: 盟友关系主要是玛雅内部的婚姻联盟、贸易伙伴和临时军事同盟，如北尤卡坦诸城之间围绕马亚潘或其他强权形成的联合
allies.en: Alliances were chiefly internal Maya marriage alliances, trade partnerships, and temporary military coalitions, such as those formed among northern Yucatan cities around Mayapan or other strong powers

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是竞争性玛雅城邦、争夺港口和贡赋的邻近政体，以及中美洲贸易体系中的对手
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly rival Maya city-states, neighboring polities competing for ports and tribute, and commercial competitors within the wider Mesoamerican trade system

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持贸易通道、控制圣地与港口、通过婚姻和宗教威望巩固联盟为核心。由于缺乏统一中央政权，对外关系是多个城邦并行操作的结果，而非单一国家战略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining trade corridors, controlling sacred sites and ports, and reinforcing alliances through marriage and religious prestige. In the absence of a unified central state, external relations reflected parallel actions by multiple city-states rather than a single national strategy.

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识包括精细历法计算、天文观测、灰泥与石砌建筑、蓄水池与井泉利用、沿海独木舟航运、棉甲制造、象形文字抄本传统及复杂市场交换网络。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and knowledge included refined calendrical calculation, astronomical observation, plaster and stone construction, use of reservoirs and cenotes, coastal canoe navigation, quilted cotton armor manufacture, the hieroglyphic codex tradition, and complex market exchange networks.

strengths.zh: 优势在于成熟的宗教—政治传统、灵活的城邦网络、强韧的地方农业、发达的盐业与沿海贸易，以及高度适应热带环境的社会组织。多中心结构使其在局部崩解后仍能持续再生。
strengths.en: Strengths included a mature religio-political tradition, flexible city-state networks, resilient local agriculture, developed salt production and coastal trade, and social organization well adapted to tropical environments. Its polycentric structure allowed regeneration even after local collapse.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、城邦间长期竞争、资源基础受降雨与土壤条件限制、运输效率较低，以及对仪式中心和精英网络的依赖。权力难以稳定统一，易出现联盟破裂与地区冲突。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, chronic rivalry among city-states, resource constraints shaped by rainfall and soils, low transport efficiency, and dependence on ritual centers and elite networks. Power was difficult to consolidate durably, making alliance breakdown and regional conflict common.

outlook.zh: 1200年前后的后古典期玛雅总体处于稳定但竞争激烈的状态。未来数世纪内，北尤卡坦的联盟政治与贸易中心化可能继续加强，但统一帝国出现的可能性较低，地区格局仍将以多城邦并存与霸权轮替为主。
outlook.en: Around 1200 CE the Postclassic Maya were broadly stable but highly competitive. In the coming centuries, alliance politics and trade centralization in northern Yucatan were likely to strengthen further, but the emergence of a unified empire remained unlikely; the regional order would continue to be marked by multiple city-states and rotating hegemonies.

description.zh: 尤卡坦与中美洲低地由多个玛雅城邦和联盟组成，商贸与仪式中心并存。
description.en: Yucatan and the lowlands were divided among multiple Maya city-states and leagues, combining trade and ritual centers.

capital.zh: 卡霍基亚及其他区域中心

governmentForm.zh: 以礼仪中心为核心的分散式酋邦联盟，若干大型首领领地由亲族、宗教与贡赋关系连接
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of chiefdoms centered on ceremonial hubs, with major chiefly territories linked by kinship, religion, and tributary ties

socialStructure.zh: 社会等级分明，上层为神圣化的首领家族与祭司精英，中层为武士、工匠与地方头人，下层为普通农民、渔猎者与劳役承担者，部分地区可能存在战俘或依附人口
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with sacral chiefly families and ritual elites at the top, warriors, artisans, and local headmen in the middle, and common farmers, fishers, hunters, and laboring households below; some regions likely held war captives or dependent populations

rulingClass.zh: 世袭酋长家族、祭司集团与掌控土丘礼仪中心的地方贵族
rulingClass.en: Hereditary chiefly families, ritual specialists, and local nobles controlling mound-centered ceremonial centers

succession.zh: 以世袭为主，通常在特定家族或氏族内部传承，具体可依母系或父系传统及地方习俗而异，并需获得精英与附属社群承认
succession.en: Primarily hereditary, usually within designated families or clans, varying by local custom between matrilineal or patrilineal tendencies, and requiring recognition by elites and subordinate communities

structure.zh: 不存在统一帝国官僚体系；卡霍基亚等中心由大酋长统领，下辖家族首领、礼仪主持者、贡赋管理者、军事随从首领与村落头人，各地区保持高度自治但共享若干宗教象征与交换网络
structure.en: There was no single imperial bureaucracy; centers such as Cahokia were led by great chiefs overseeing lineage leaders, ritual authorities, tribute managers, military retainers, and village headmen. Regions remained highly autonomous while sharing religious symbols and exchange networks

function.zh: 组织祭祀、历法仪式、土丘修筑、广场维护与公共庆典
function.en: Organized ceremonies, calendrical rites, mound construction, plaza maintenance, and public festivals

function.zh: 征集玉米、鱼干、兽皮与手工业品，管理公共仓储并在宴飨或赈济时再分配
function.en: Collected maize, dried fish, hides, and craft goods, managed public storage, and redistributed goods at feasts or in relief

name.zh: 地方聚落与附属社群联络

function.zh: 协调村落头人、附属聚落义务、劳役征发与区域纠纷调停
function.en: Coordinated village headmen, dependent community obligations, labor levies, and regional dispute mediation

function.zh: 统筹酋长卫队、战时动员、木栅防御与对敌对社群的突袭或威慑
function.en: Managed chiefly guards, wartime mobilization, palisade defenses, and raids or deterrence against hostile groups

function.zh: 监督贝珠、铜器、石器、陶器与异地珍贵品的生产和流通
function.en: Oversaw the production and circulation of shell beads, copper goods, stone tools, pottery, and exotic prestige items

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托村落头人与次级酋长运行，核心中心周边控制较强，远距地区多通过亲族纽带、定期进贡、宗教集会与交换关系维系统治
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through village headmen and subordinate chiefs. Control was strongest near core centers, while distant areas were governed through kinship ties, periodic tribute, religious gatherings, and exchange relationships

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、酋长裁决、氏族调解与宗教禁忌为主，重视赔偿、和解、威望惩戒与仪式性处罚，而非成文法典
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary norms, chiefly judgment, clan mediation, and religious taboo, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, status penalties, and ritual punishment rather than written codes

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要以实物贡赋和劳役形式征收，包括玉米、豆类、南瓜、鱼类、鹿皮、工艺品与修筑土丘和木栅的集体劳动
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, squash, fish, deer hides, crafted goods, and collective work for mound and palisade construction

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、太阳与天象崇拜、神圣首领观念及与东南礼仪复合体相关的仪式传统为核心，祭祀、宴飨、葬俗与土丘空间布局具有重要宗教意义
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, solar and celestial worship, sacral rulership, and ritual traditions associated with the Southeastern Ceremonial Complex; sacrifice, feasting, burial, and mound-centered spatial planning carried major religious meaning

philosophy.zh: 并无成体系书面哲学，观念世界强调宇宙秩序、血缘责任、首领神圣性、人与自然循环以及礼仪维系社会平衡
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy, but the worldview stressed cosmic order, kin obligation, sacred chiefly authority, human participation in natural cycles, and ritual as the means of maintaining social balance

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，主要通过口述传统、象征图像、器物纹饰、土丘布局与仪式表演传递记忆和权威
writingSystem.en: No fully developed writing system existed; memory and authority were transmitted through oral tradition, symbolic imagery, artifact decoration, mound layouts, and ritual performance

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括大型平台土丘与广场规划、精细陶器、铜板与贝壳雕刻、复杂木栅与城市布局、长距离交换网络，以及在卡霍基亚表现突出的高密度聚居与礼仪建筑
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included large platform mounds and planned plazas, fine pottery, repoussé copper plates and shell engraving, complex palisades and urban layouts, long-distance exchange networks, and unusually dense settlement with monumental ceremonial architecture at Cahokia

languageFamily.zh: 语言多样，可能包括穆斯科吉语系、卡多语系、苏语系及其他地方语言；卡霍基亚核心区具体语言归属仍存争议
languageFamily.en: Languages were diverse and may have included Muskogean, Caddoan, Siouan, and other regional families; the precise linguistic identity of the Cahokia core remains debated

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米农业为基础，辅以豆类、南瓜、渔猎和采集。总体生产力高于一般流动部落社会，但低于同时代拥有货币、城市市场和成文财政制度的欧亚大型国家。估值仅为跨文明比较所用的粗略白银当量换算。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on maize agriculture, supplemented by beans, squash, fishing, hunting, and gathering. Overall productivity exceeded that of more mobile tribal societies, but remained below that of contemporary Eurasian states with coinage, urban markets, and written fiscal systems. The estimate is only a rough silver-equivalent conversion for cross-civilizational comparison

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、渔猎、鹿皮加工、陶器制作、石器与铜器工艺、贝壳饰品、木工、土丘与公共工程建设
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, fishing and hunting, hide processing, pottery production, stone and copper craftwork, shell ornament making, woodworking, and mound-based public construction

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米余粮、陶器、贝珠、铜片、燧石工具、云母、盐、鹿皮、羽毛、石材与礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Maize surplus, pottery, shell beads, copper sheets, chert tools, mica, salt, deer hides, feathers, stone materials, and ritual items

name.zh: 无统一货币，以实物交换与贡赋为主
name.en: No unified currency; barter and tribute predominated

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为粮食储备、住房质量、亲族劳力、工具、陶器、渔猎装备与少量贵重装饰品；精英则拥有更多异地珍品、祭仪器物和可支配劳役
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in food stores, housing quality, kin labor, tools, pottery, fishing and hunting gear, and a small number of prestige ornaments; elites possessed larger quantities of exotic valuables, ritual objects, and command over labor

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、酋长再分配、礼仪宴飨和区域交换网络为核心的农业—贡赋经济，市场交换存在但不是主导机制
economicSystem.en: An agrarian-tributary economy centered on village production, chiefly redistribution, ceremonial feasting, and regional exchange networks, with market exchange present but not dominant

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 手工业与礼仪用品进献

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非金属货币库藏，而是分散在各中心仓储中的粮食、皮货、铜器、贝饰、工具和礼仪珍品总和，可用于宴飨、赏赐、赈灾和动员劳役
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a metallic coin reserve but the combined stock of grain, hides, copper goods, shell ornaments, tools, and ritual valuables stored across centers, usable for feasting, gifts, relief, and labor mobilization

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持酋长威望与社会整合，通过再分配、公共工程和仪式性消费巩固统治；在歉收时则依赖仓储调剂与附属社群支持
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining chiefly prestige and social integration through redistribution, public works, and ceremonial consumption; in poor harvest years it relied on stored goods and support from dependent communities

description.zh: 由酋长直属随从、精英武士与驻守核心中心的守卫构成，负责护卫首领、维持秩序与应对突发冲突
description.en: Composed of chiefly retainers, elite warriors, and guards stationed at major centers, responsible for protecting leaders, maintaining order, and responding to sudden conflict

description.zh: 战时由村落和附属社群动员，装备以弓箭、长矛、战棍和木盾为主，适合短期区域作战与防御
description.en: Raised from villages and dependent communities in wartime, equipped mainly with bows, spears, war clubs, and wooden shields, suitable for short regional campaigns and defense

description.zh: 机动性较强，熟悉河流、森林与湿地环境，用于侦察、伏击、报复性袭击与边界威慑
description.en: More mobile units familiar with riverine, forest, and wetland environments, used for scouting, ambush, retaliatory raids, and border intimidation

commanderInChief.zh: 各区域由大酋长及其首席战首分别统军
commanderInChief.en: Each region was commanded by its great chief and principal war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以木栅寨、瞭望、弓箭、燧石矛头、战棍、厚皮护具和独木舟机动为主，无骑兵、铁器或攻城器械传统
technology.en: Military technology centered on palisaded settlements, watch posts, bows, chert-tipped spears, war clubs, hide armor, and canoe mobility, without cavalry, iron weaponry, or true siege machinery

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近或竞争性酋邦之间的资源争夺、报复性袭击、边界紧张和内部附属社群离心；不存在与欧亚列强直接军事接触
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring or rival chiefdoms competing over resources, retaliatory raiding, border tensions, and centrifugal tendencies among subordinate communities; there was no direct military contact with Eurasian great powers

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛，集中于密西西比河流域及美国东南部多个农业聚落网络。卡霍基亚是最突出的都会与礼仪中心，但整体人口并非集中于单一国家，而是分散于众多相互联系的酋邦
populationDescription.en: Population was widely distributed across the Mississippi Valley and the southeastern North American agricultural settlement networks. Cahokia was the most prominent urban and ceremonial center, but the total population was not concentrated in a single state and was instead spread across many interconnected chiefdoms

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为神圣首领与贵族、祭司与礼仪专家、武士与地方头人、工匠与专门生产者、普通农民渔猎者，以及附庸人口和少量战俘
socialClasses.en: Society may be broadly divided into sacred chiefs and nobles, priests and ritual specialists, warriors and local headmen, artisans and specialized producers, common farmers and fishers-hunters, and dependent populations with some war captives

allies.zh: 无统一对外同盟体系；各酋邦主要通过婚姻、交换网络、共同仪式和阶段性军事合作建立友好关系
allies.en: There was no unified external alliance system; chiefdoms built friendly relations mainly through marriage, exchange networks, shared rituals, and occasional military cooperation

enemies.zh: 敌对对象因地区而异，通常是竞争性邻近酋邦、拒绝进贡的社群或争夺贸易通道与狩猎地的势力
enemies.en: Enemies varied by region and were usually rival neighboring chiefdoms, communities refusing tribute, or groups competing over trade routes and hunting grounds

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域整合、维持贡赋联系、展示礼仪威望和控制交换节点为主，远程关系多属间接文化扩散而非正式邦交
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on regional integration, maintaining tributary ties, displaying ritual prestige, and controlling exchange nodes; long-distance relations were mostly indirect cultural diffusion rather than formal diplomacy

era.zh: 北美后古典时代土丘建造高峰期

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与组织能力包括高产玉米农业、复杂土丘与广场工程、木栅防御、独木舟河运、精制陶器、铜板冷锻装饰工艺及广域礼仪象征传播
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and capacities included productive maize agriculture, complex mound and plaza engineering, palisaded defenses, canoe-based river transport, refined pottery, cold-worked copper sheet ornamentation, and the wide diffusion of ceremonial symbols

strengths.zh: 优势在于农业基础稳固、河流交通便利、礼仪权威强、公共工程动员能力高、交换网络广泛，且卡霍基亚等中心能整合大量人口与资源
strengths.en: Strengths included a solid agricultural base, excellent river transport, strong ritual authority, high capacity for public works mobilization, broad exchange networks, and the ability of centers like Cahokia to concentrate people and resources

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一中央国家和书面行政体系，政治整合依赖首领威望与宗教合法性，易受气候波动、歉收、内部派系竞争和区域离心影响
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the absence of a unified centralized state and written administration; political integration depended heavily on chiefly prestige and religious legitimacy, making the system vulnerable to climate fluctuation, crop failure, internal factionalism, and regional centrifugal pressures

outlook.zh: 到1200年前后整体仍属稳定并具相当活力，卡霍基亚尤为显赫；但长期看，若核心中心难以持续维持人口、仓储和附属关系，部分大型酋邦可能在未来数代出现分裂、迁移或衰落
outlook.en: Around 1200 the civilization was still stable and vigorous overall, with Cahokia especially prominent; over the longer term, however, if major centers failed to sustain population, storage, and subordinate ties, some large chiefdoms could fragment, relocate, or decline within future generations

description.zh: 北美东南与密西西比流域的大型土丘社会网络，卡霍基亚尤为突出。
description.en: A mound-building network of large societies across the Mississippi valley and southeastern North America, with Cahokia especially prominent.

name.zh: 前印加安第斯文明联盟

capital.en: Chan Chan and Other Regional Centers

governmentForm.zh: 由海岸与高地诸政体组成的松散联盟，兼具城邦、区域王国与部族领地特征
governmentForm.en: A loose confederated order of coastal and highland polities, combining city-states, regional kingdoms, and tribal domains

socialStructure.zh: 社会按贵族、祭司、军事首领、工匠、农民、牧民、渔民与劳役群体分层，各地制度不一，但普遍以亲族、地方共同体与对统治者的贡赋义务为核心
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified into nobles, priests, military leaders, artisans, farmers, pastoralists, fishers, and laboring groups; local systems varied, but kinship, community organization, and tribute obligations to rulers were central

rulingClass.zh: 沿海宫廷贵族、高地族长贵族、祭司集团与军事领袖
rulingClass.en: Coastal court nobility, highland clan aristocracies, priestly elites, and military leaders

succession.zh: 多数政体实行家族世袭，并常受地方贵族承认、宗族协商或武力竞争影响
succession.en: Most polities followed hereditary succession, often shaped by recognition from local nobles, clan negotiation, or armed rivalry

structure.zh: 1200年前后的安第斯地区并无统一帝国，而是由北海岸原始奇穆诸国、的的喀喀湖周边艾马拉诸王国以及山地和谷地首领政体组成。沿海大城拥有宫殿、仓储与手工业行政体系，高地则依托族群共同体、要塞与区域首领网络治理
structure.en: Around 1200 CE the Andes did not form a single empire, but a mosaic of proto-Chimu coastal states, Aymara kingdoms around Lake Titicaca, and mountain and valley chiefdoms. Coastal urban centers maintained palace, storage, and craft administrations, while highland rule relied more on ethnic communities, fortresses, and networks of regional lords

function.zh: 征收粮食、纺织品、劳役与地方贡品，并管理仓库、再分配与救荒储备
function.en: Collected food, textiles, labor service, and regional tribute goods, and managed storehouses, redistribution, and famine reserves

function.zh: 维护运河、梯田、堤坝与水利设施，协调播种、修渠与公共劳役
function.en: Maintained canals, terraces, embankments, and hydraulic works, coordinating sowing, canal repair, and communal labor

function.zh: 组织祭祀、祖先崇拜、王室典礼、宴飨与精英物资分配
function.en: Organized ritual observances, ancestor veneration, royal ceremonies, feasts, and elite distribution of prestige goods

function.zh: 监督金属加工、陶器、木工、纺织与贝壳镶嵌等专业生产，并向统治集团供给奢侈品与礼器
function.en: Oversaw specialized production in metallurgy, ceramics, woodworking, textiles, and shell inlay, supplying luxury and ceremonial goods to ruling elites

function.zh: 动员战士、守卫要塞、保障驿道和边界，并处理邻近政体冲突
function.en: Mobilized warriors, guarded fortresses, protected routes and frontiers, and managed conflict with neighboring polities

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以谷地、灌溉区、族群共同体与城邑为单位。沿海统治者依赖宫殿官员和工匠监督者，高地则更多依靠族长、地方祭司和军事首领。行政重心不在成文官僚，而在面对面统治、仓储再分配和劳役动员
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around valleys, irrigation zones, ethnic communities, and urban centers. Coastal rulers depended on palace officials and workshop supervisors, while highland governance relied more on clan leaders, local priests, and military chiefs. Administration centered less on written bureaucracy than on face-to-face authority, storage redistribution, and labor mobilization

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，由统治者、贵族会议、祭司与地方长者执行。常见事务包括土地与水权、婚姻、贡赋、盗窃、叛乱与群体纠纷。处罚通常为劳役、没收、羞辱性惩罚、驱逐或处死，视各政体传统而定
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, enforced by rulers, noble councils, priests, and local elders. Common matters included land and water rights, marriage, tribute, theft, rebellion, and intercommunity disputes. Punishments commonly involved labor penalties, confiscation, public humiliation, exile, or execution, depending on local tradition

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物贡纳与劳役为主，而非统一货币税。主要征收玉米、马铃薯、藜麦、辣椒、鱼干、骆马制品、纺织品、陶器及建筑、灌溉、军事劳役
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based chiefly on in-kind tribute and labor service rather than uniform monetary taxation. Main exactions included maize, potatoes, quinoa, chili peppers, dried fish, camelid products, textiles, ceramics, and labor for construction, irrigation, and war

religion.zh: 宗教以多神信仰、祖先崇拜、山岳神灵、太阳、月亮、雷电与水神崇拜为核心。沿海与高地各有地方神祇与圣地，祭祀常与农业周期、降雨、生育、战争和王权合法性相连
religion.en: Religion centered on polytheistic worship, ancestor veneration, mountain spirits, and deities associated with the sun, moon, lightning, and water. Coastal and highland peoples maintained local gods and sacred places, and ritual was closely tied to agriculture, rainfall, fertility, warfare, and political legitimacy

philosophy.zh: 不存在类似欧亚的系统文字哲学传统，但安第斯世界具有鲜明的宇宙秩序观，强调互惠、群体义务、祖先连续性、生态层级互补以及人与山川神圣力量之间的平衡
philosophy.en: There was no systematic written philosophical tradition comparable to Eurasian literate schools, but the Andes possessed a strong cosmological worldview emphasizing reciprocity, communal obligation, ancestral continuity, ecological complementarity across zones, and balance between people and sacred landscape forces

writingSystem.zh: 尚无成熟文字系统。信息主要通过口传、图像符号、结绳式或记忆辅助方式、建筑空间秩序与礼仪实践保存；1200年尚未见后世印加式结绳官僚体系的成熟国家化运用
writingSystem.en: There was no fully developed writing system. Information was preserved chiefly through oral transmission, visual symbols, mnemonic cords or related devices, architectural ordering, and ritual practice; the fully state-developed Inca-style quipu bureaucracy had not yet emerged in 1200

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括北海岸夯土城市与宫殿建筑、复杂灌溉工程、精细金银铜工艺、高水平纺织、彩绘与模制陶器、芦苇与木制航海技术，以及高地防御聚落与石构工程
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included monumental adobe cities and palace compounds on the north coast, complex irrigation engineering, refined gold, silver, and copper work, high-level textile production, painted and molded ceramics, reed and wooden maritime technology, and defensive hilltop settlements and stone construction in the highlands

languageFamily.zh: 语言高度多样，主要包括盖丘亚语前身方言群、艾马拉语群、莫奇卡语及其他沿海与山地方言
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, including early Quechuan dialect groups, Aymaran languages, Muchik/Moche-related speech on the coast, and many other regional coastal and highland tongues

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础稳固但区域差异巨大。北海岸依赖高产灌溉农业、渔业和专业手工业，南方高地则依赖混合农业、骆马牧业与区域交换。整体经济并非货币化市场体系，而是以地方自给、精英控制再分配和跨生态带交换构成
gdpDescription.en: The economy was solid but highly regionally varied. The north coast relied on productive irrigated farming, fishing, and specialized craft industry, while the southern highlands depended on mixed agriculture, camelid pastoralism, and regional exchange. The overall economy was not a monetized market system, but one built on local subsistence, elite-controlled redistribution, and exchange across ecological zones

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、牧业、渔业、纺织、陶器、金属加工、建筑与长距离驮运交换
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, fishing, textile production, ceramics, metallurgy, construction, and long-distance caravan exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、马铃薯、藜麦、辣椒、鱼干、盐、羊驼与美洲驼毛、纺织品、铜器、金银饰品、黑曜石、贝壳、羽毛与可卡叶
tradeGoods.en: Maize, potatoes, quinoa, chili peppers, dried fish, salt, alpaca and llama wool, textiles, copper goods, gold and silver ornaments, obsidian, shell, feathers, and coca leaves

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、灌溉水权、亲族关系、羊驼群、储粮、衣物和工具上，而非金属货币。沿海精英则控制宫殿、仓库、工匠和奢侈物资
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly through access to fields, irrigation water, kin ties, llama or alpaca herds, stored food, clothing, and tools rather than coined wealth. Coastal elites controlled palaces, storehouses, craft labor, and prestige goods

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、地方首领征调、宫廷再分配和跨生态带互补交换相结合的经济体系，市场存在但非主导
economicSystem.en: An economic system combining community production, chiefly extraction, palace redistribution, and complementary exchange across ecological zones, with markets present but not dominant

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非统一中央金库，而是分散于各大宫殿、仓库、神庙和地方统治者储备中的粮食、纺织品、金属器、贝壳、牲畜与劳动力控制权。其财政韧性主要取决于灌溉系统和仓储规模
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a single central vault but wealth dispersed among major palaces, storehouses, temples, and local rulers' reserves in the form of food, textiles, metal goods, shell valuables, livestock, and command over labor. Fiscal resilience depended mainly on irrigation systems and storage capacity

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守稳健，重视储粮、再分配与工程维护，以确保旱灾、洪水或战争时期的稳定；在丰年扩大礼仪与建筑开支，在紧张时期则加强征调与地方贡赋
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, emphasizing grain reserves, redistribution, and maintenance of infrastructure to withstand drought, flood, or war; in prosperous years rulers expanded ritual and construction spending, while in strained times they increased levies and local tribute demands

description.zh: 由长矛、投石索、木棍、石斧和盾牌装备的高地战士组成，擅长山地防御、要塞争夺与近距离冲突
description.en: Composed of highland warriors armed with spears, slings, clubs, stone axes, and shields, skilled in mountain defense, fortress fighting, and close combat

description.zh: 由沿海谷地与城市政体征发，负责守卫灌溉区、宫殿与道路，也参与地区间战争
description.en: Raised from coastal valleys and urban polities, these forces guarded irrigation zones, palaces, and routes and also fought in regional wars

description.zh: 安第斯传统远程兵种，在山地和开阔地都十分有效，常用于骚扰、伏击与削弱敌阵
description.en: A classic Andean missile arm, effective in both mountains and open ground, often used for harassment, ambush, and weakening enemy formations

description.zh: 虽非骑兵，但负责军需运输、补给和高地机动，对长距离作战极为重要
description.en: Not cavalry, but responsible for transport, supply, and highland mobility, making them crucial for long-distance campaigning

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各地区领主与军事首领分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; armies were led separately by regional lords and military chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以石、木、铜与青铜工具武器为主，护具有限。防御依赖山地要塞、城墙、壕沟和地形优势。没有马匹、铁器或攻城机械传统，但善于利用地势、投射武器和集体冲锋
technology.en: Military technology relied mainly on stone, wood, copper, and some bronze tools and weapons, with limited armor. Defense depended on hill forts, walls, ditches, and terrain advantage. There were no horses, iron weapons, or developed siege engines, but Andean forces made effective use of landscape, missile fire, and mass assaults

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自区域间争战、灌溉谷地控制权之争、高地王国竞争以及气候异常引发的社会动荡，而非跨大洋外敌
threats.en: The chief threats came from inter-polity warfare, competition for irrigated valleys, rivalry among highland kingdoms, and unrest caused by climatic stress rather than overseas invaders

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约四百万，分布在北部沿海灌溉谷地、安第斯山间盆地、的的喀喀湖区域及若干边缘生态带。人口密度高度依赖水利条件、可耕地和区域安全
populationDescription.en: The population totaled about four million, distributed across northern coastal irrigation valleys, Andean intermontane basins, the Lake Titicaca region, and several marginal ecological zones. Population density depended heavily on water control, arable land, and regional security

name.zh: 蒂亚瓦纳科遗址周边聚落

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括世袭贵族、祭司、军事领袖、地方族长、专业工匠、商旅与驮运者、农民、牧民、渔民及被征调劳役群体。不同地区之间阶层边界差异显著
socialClasses.en: Major social groups included hereditary nobles, priests, military leaders, local clan heads, specialized artisans, traders and caravaners, farmers, pastoralists, fishers, and laborers subject to mobilization. Boundaries among classes varied considerably by region

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系，常见的是相邻谷地、亲族网络和临时军事联盟；部分高地王国会为共同对抗竞争者而结盟
allies.en: There was no permanent unified alliance system; more common were neighboring valley partnerships, kin-based ties, and temporary military alliances. Some highland kingdoms allied to counter local rivals

enemies.zh: 敌对关系主要发生在其他安第斯地区政体之间，包括争夺灌溉土地、牧场、贸易路线和宗教中心控制权的邻近王国与首领集团
enemies.en: Hostility was directed mainly toward other Andean polities, including neighboring kingdoms and chiefdoms competing for irrigated land, pasture, trade routes, and control of religious centers

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以地区均势、婚姻联结、贡赋关系、宗教朝圣网络和有限征服为主，目标在于维持灌溉区与交换路线的稳定控制
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on regional balance, marriage ties, tributary relations, pilgrimage networks, and limited conquest, with the aim of maintaining stable control over irrigation zones and exchange routes

era.zh: 十字军时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度包括大规模夯土建筑、运河灌溉、梯田农业、干燥储藏技术、铜与早期青铜加工、精密纺织、骆马驮运网络以及适应垂直生态带的生产组织
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and institutions included large-scale adobe architecture, canal irrigation, terraced agriculture, drying and storage techniques, copper and early bronze working, fine textile production, llama transport networks, and productive organization adapted to vertical ecological zones

strengths.zh: 优势在于对多样生态带的适应能力、成熟的灌溉与仓储传统、强大的手工业、区域宗教中心的整合作用，以及依托共同体和亲族网络实现的社会韧性
strengths.en: Strengths included adaptation to diverse ecological zones, mature traditions of irrigation and storage, strong craft production, the integrating role of regional religious centers, and social resilience rooted in communal and kin networks

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、缺乏统一中央权威、地区战争频繁、对气候波动与厄尔尼诺型环境冲击高度敏感，以及行政记录手段有限
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, lack of a unified central authority, frequent regional warfare, high vulnerability to climatic fluctuation and El Nino-type environmental shocks, and limited administrative record-keeping

outlook.zh: 1200年前后的前印加安第斯世界总体稳定，但竞争激烈。若某一沿海或高地强权成功整合劳役、宗教与军事资源，未来数世纪有可能走向更大规模的区域国家化；若气候恶化或战争升级，则联盟格局可能持续破碎
outlook.en: Around 1200 the pre-Inca Andean world was broadly stable but intensely competitive. If one strong coastal or highland power succeeded in integrating labor, religion, and military resources, larger regional state formation could emerge in the coming centuries; if climate stress or warfare intensified, the confederated pattern might remain fractured

description.zh: 安第斯山地与太平洋沿岸由奇穆前身、艾马拉与其他区域政体分治。
description.en: The Andes and Pacific coast were divided among proto-Chimu, Aymara, and other regional polities.

name.zh: 阿拉伯部落与绿洲诸政体

capital.zh: 萨那、麦加及各绿洲中心

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落联盟、圣城寡头政体与高地王朝并存的区域政治体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized regional political system combining tribal confederations, holy-city oligarchies, and highland dynastic rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部落血缘、宗教威望、城市商人集团与绿洲农民共同构成；游牧与定居群体并存，南阿拉伯高地的梯田农民和城镇文士也具有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured by tribal kinship, religious prestige, urban merchant groups, and oasis cultivators; nomadic and settled communities coexisted, while the terraced farmers and town-based scribal elites of southern Arabia held important roles.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由地方埃米尔、谢赫、麦加谢里夫家族、也门高地王朝贵族、宗教学者及大商人组成。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of local emirs, tribal shaykhs, the sharifian families of Mecca, dynastic nobles in the Yemeni highlands, religious scholars, and major merchants.

succession.zh: 继承方式因地而异，多数在家族内部世袭，但须得到部落长老、宗教名望者或城市精英的认可；部分职位通过协商、拥立或武力争夺决定。
succession.en: Succession varied by region: many positions were hereditary within leading houses, but required recognition from tribal elders, religious notables, or urban elites; some offices were settled through negotiation, acclamation, or force.

structure.zh: 阿拉伯半岛在1200年前后并无统一中央政府。汉志地区由麦加等圣城的谢里夫家族与城市名流管理朝觐与治安；也门高地和南阿拉伯沿海则存在较成熟的王朝行政，如阿尤布派任命的总督与地方家族官僚；内陆诸绿洲和部落地区多由酋长会议、盟约与习惯法治理。
structure.en: Around 1200, Arabia had no single central government. In the Hijaz, sharifian families and urban notables in holy cities such as Mecca managed pilgrimage and security; in the Yemeni highlands and southern Arabian coast, more developed dynastic administrations existed, including Ayyubid-appointed governors and local household officials; inland oasis and tribal regions were governed largely through councils of chiefs, alliances, and customary law.

function.zh: 负责麦加及相关圣地的秩序、朝觐接待、供水安排、道路安全协调与宗教性捐输管理。
function.en: Handled order in Mecca and associated sanctuaries, pilgrimage reception, water provisioning, coordination of route security, and management of religiously linked contributions.

function.zh: 在也门城市、港口、山地市场和绿洲据点征收地税、商税、关税、过路费与市场摊税。
function.en: Collected land taxes, trade taxes, customs, transit dues, and market fees in Yemeni towns, ports, mountain markets, and oasis posts.

function.zh: 起草命令、保存契约、处理宗教法庭事务，并在城市中监督法官与见证人网络。
function.en: Drafted orders, preserved contracts, handled court business under Islamic law, and supervised networks of judges and legal witnesses in towns.

function.zh: 同部落酋长协商盟约、赎金、护送协议与道路治安，维持商道和朝觐路线的基本安全。
function.en: Negotiated alliances, ransom arrangements, escort agreements, and road security with tribal chiefs, maintaining basic safety along trade and pilgrimage routes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政高度多样化。也门高地城镇常设有总督、税吏、法官与堡寨守臣；麦加、麦地那等地由谢里夫家族、宗教学者与城市头人共同治理；内陆绿洲则依赖家族长老、部落仲裁人与堡寨首领。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was highly varied. Highland Yemeni towns often had governors, tax officials, judges, and fortress commanders; Mecca and Medina were governed by sharifian families, religious scholars, and urban notables; inland oases relied on lineage elders, tribal arbitrators, and fort chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以伊斯兰教法为核心，在城市中由卡迪审理婚姻、继承、契约与商业纠纷；在部落社会中，习惯法、赔偿金、仲裁与盟约同样重要，常与教法并行不悖。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on Islamic law, with qadis in towns hearing cases of marriage, inheritance, contracts, and trade disputes; in tribal society, customary law, compensation payments, arbitration, and covenant obligations were equally important and often operated alongside the sharia.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制不统一，主要包括地税、什一税性质的农业征收、市场税、港口关税、商队通行费、朝觐相关收费与向部落支付的护路贡赋；征收能力在也门和沿海地区较强，在内陆则较弱且依赖协商。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was not uniform and included land taxes, tithe-like agricultural levies, market taxes, port customs, caravan transit dues, pilgrimage-related charges, and protection payments to tribes; collection was stronger in Yemen and coastal areas, but weaker inland and dependent on negotiation.

religion.zh: 居民绝大多数为伊斯兰教徒，以逊尼派为主；也门北部和高地存在宰德派传统，南部和沿海城市则受逊尼法学、苏菲修行和商旅宗教网络影响。麦加与麦地那继续是整个伊斯兰世界的核心圣地。
religion.en: The overwhelming majority of the population was Muslim, predominantly Sunni; northern and highland Yemen preserved strong Zaydi traditions, while the south and coastal towns were influenced by Sunni jurisprudence, Sufi practice, and mercantile religious networks. Mecca and Medina remained the central holy cities of the wider Islamic world.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以伊斯兰法学、圣训学、阿拉伯语文传统、谱系意识与部落荣誉观念为主；也门学界在法学、诗歌和历史记述方面保持活力。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Islamic jurisprudence, hadith scholarship, Arabic literary culture, genealogical consciousness, and ideals of tribal honor; Yemeni scholars remained active in law, poetry, and historical writing.

writingSystem.zh: 通用文字为阿拉伯字母，用于宗教、行政、商业契约与诗歌创作；碑铭与文书传统在南阿拉伯城市中尤为重要。
writingSystem.en: The Arabic script was the common writing system, used for religion, administration, commercial contracts, and poetry; epigraphic and documentary traditions were especially important in South Arabian towns.

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿拉伯半岛在此时期的重要文化成就在于维系朝觐制度、保存阿拉伯诗歌与族谱传统、发展也门高地与沿海城市的学术圈，并通过红海与印度洋贸易把半岛纳入横跨非洲与亚洲的伊斯兰文化交流网络。
culturalAchievements.en: The major cultural achievements of Arabia in this period lay in sustaining the pilgrimage system, preserving Arabic poetry and genealogical traditions, fostering scholarly circles in the Yemeni highlands and coastal towns, and integrating the peninsula into an Islamic cultural exchange network spanning Africa and Asia through the Red Sea and Indian Ocean.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属亚非语系闪米特语族，以阿拉伯语各地方言和古典书面语为主；也门及南部山区保留若干南阿拉伯语支传统。
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to the Afroasiatic Semitic family, dominated by regional Arabic dialects and Classical Arabic in writing; parts of Yemen and the southern mountains retained South Arabian linguistic traditions.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总体属中等水平，区域差异很大。也门高地和沿海港口最为富庶，拥有农业剩余、手工业和海贸收入；汉志凭借朝觐、转运和市场服务获益；中北部内陆部落区则更多依赖畜牧、护送与间歇性商贸。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was moderate overall but highly uneven regionally. The Yemeni highlands and coastal ports were the wealthiest, with agricultural surplus, craft production, and maritime trade income; the Hijaz benefited from pilgrimage, transshipment, and market services; the inland tribal zones of central and northern Arabia relied more on pastoralism, escorting, and intermittent trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括梯田农业、枣椰与谷物种植、乳畜和骆驼牧养、香料与乳香转运、港口贸易、朝觐服务、皮革纺织、金属小作坊和驮运业。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included terraced agriculture, date and grain cultivation, dairy and camel pastoralism, transit trade in aromatics and spices, port commerce, pilgrimage services, leatherworking, textiles, small-scale metalworking, and pack transport.

tradeGoods.zh: 贸易商品包括咖啡前时代的香料转口货、乳香、没药、马匹、骆驼、皮革、纺织品、食盐、谷物、蜂蜜、药材、印度洋进口布料以及来自东非的奴隶和象牙。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included pre-coffee-era spice transit commodities, frankincense, myrrh, horses, camels, leather, textiles, salt, grain, honey, medicinal substances, imported Indian Ocean cloth, and slaves and ivory from East Africa.

name.en: Dinar and dirham in parallel use

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显。商人、谢里夫家族、港口税农和高地地主可积累相当财富，而多数农民、小牧民和部落成员仅维持有限剩余；财富常以牲畜、土地、水权、武器与贵金属首饰储存。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Merchants, sharifian families, port tax farmers, and highland landholders could accumulate substantial means, while most peasants, small pastoralists, and tribal members maintained only limited surpluses; wealth was often stored in livestock, land, water rights, weapons, and precious-metal jewelry.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为分散的市场经济与部落互惠并存模式：城市与港口中货币交易、税租和长途贸易占主导，部落地区则广泛存在保护协议、赠礼交换、牲畜财富与季节性市场。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined decentralized market exchange with tribal reciprocity: monetary transactions, tax rents, and long-distance trade dominated in towns and ports, while protection agreements, gift exchange, livestock wealth, and seasonal markets were widespread in tribal regions.

source.zh: 贡赋、护路费与盟约性缴纳
source.en: Tribute, protection fees, and covenant payments

category.zh: 圣地、清真寺与宗教施惠
category.en: Holy places, mosques, and religious patronage

category.zh: 部落补贴、馈赠与外交支出
category.en: Tribal subsidies, gifts, and diplomatic spending

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散于也门城堡、港口税库、麦加宗教金库和诸酋长私藏之中，并非单一国家金库。其规模足以应对短期歉收、路线冲突或护送需求，但难支持长期统一战争。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were dispersed among Yemeni fort treasuries, port tax stores, Meccan religious treasuries, and the private hoards of chiefs rather than concentrated in a single state treasury. They were sufficient for short-term crop failures, route conflicts, or escort needs, but inadequate for sustained unified warfare.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向务实保守，重在维持圣地运作、平衡部落关系、保护商路并从港口与朝觐中稳定取利；在旱灾或冲突时期，统治者常通过减税、延期征收或增加贡赋谈判来缓冲危机。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was pragmatic and conservative, focused on maintaining holy places, balancing tribal relations, protecting trade routes, and drawing steady revenue from ports and pilgrimage; in times of drought or conflict, rulers often mitigated crisis through tax relief, delayed collection, or renegotiated tribute obligations.

description.zh: 以骆驼骑乘与轻骑兵为主，擅长袭扰、护送、侦察和沙漠快速机动，是半岛最具代表性的军事力量。
description.en: Composed mainly of camel riders and light cavalry, skilled in raiding, escorting, reconnaissance, and rapid desert movement; they were the peninsula's most characteristic military force.

description.zh: 驻守萨那、塔伊兹、麦加及主要绿洲据点，负责城门、税库、蓄水设施和要塞防御。
description.en: Stationed in Sana'a, Ta'izz, Mecca, and major oasis centers, guarding gates, tax stores, water installations, and fortifications.

description.zh: 由城镇居民、山地农民和附属部落成员组成，装备长矛、盾牌、弓箭和短刀，多用于守城、山地作战和随军运输。
description.en: Drawn from townsmen, highland peasants, and dependent tribesmen, armed with spears, shields, bows, and short blades; used mostly for local defense, mountain fighting, and transport support.

description.zh: 规模有限，分布于红海与亚丁湾沿岸，负责港口守卫、小型船只巡逻与海贸节点安全。
description.en: Limited in scale and distributed along the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden coasts, responsible for port defense, small-vessel patrols, and security of maritime trade nodes.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各埃米尔、谢里夫和部落谢赫分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; forces were led separately by emirs, sharifs, and tribal shaykhs

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻装骑乘、复合弓、长矛、皮甲、锁子甲、城堡防御和沙漠后勤经验为主；也门高地具备较好的堡垒与山地防守传统，但缺乏大规模攻城器和统一兵工体系。
technology.en: Military technology centered on light mounted warfare, composite bows, spears, leather armor, mail, fortification defense, and desert logistical expertise; the Yemeni highlands had stronger fortress and mountain-defense traditions, but lacked large-scale siege capability and a unified armaments system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非全面外敌征服，而是部落争斗、商路劫掠、也门内部王朝冲突、红海沿岸竞争，以及来自阿尤布王朝或周边大国的间接干预。
threats.en: The main threats were not full-scale external conquest so much as tribal feuding, caravan raiding, internal dynastic conflict in Yemen, competition along the Red Sea coast, and indirect intervention by the Ayyubids or neighboring great powers.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布极不均衡。也门高地、提哈麦沿海和汉志城镇人口相对稠密，内志与北阿拉伯沙漠地区则稀疏分散。定居农民与城市居民构成多数，但游牧和半游牧部落在政治与军事上影响甚大。
populationDescription.en: Population was distributed very unevenly. The Yemeni highlands, Tihama coast, and Hijazi towns were comparatively dense, whereas Najd and the northern Arabian deserts were sparse and scattered. Settled farmers and townspeople formed the majority, but nomadic and semi-nomadic tribes wielded major political and military influence.

description.zh: 伊斯兰教最神圣的城市
description.en: Holiest city in Islam

description.zh: 伊斯兰教第二圣城
description.en: Second holiest city in Islam

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括谢里夫与王朝贵族、部落酋长、宗教学者、城市商人、工匠、绿洲与高地农民、牧民、奴隶及依附民。血缘、宗教声望与对水源、土地和商路的控制决定社会地位。
socialClasses.en: The main social classes included sharifs and dynastic nobles, tribal chiefs, religious scholars, urban merchants, artisans, oasis and highland cultivators, pastoralists, slaves, and dependent peoples. Lineage, religious prestige, and control of water, land, and trade routes determined status.

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系；地方政权通常同阿尤布埃及—叙利亚政权、也门地方家族、红海港口商人和邻近部落结成临时同盟。
allies.en: There was no durable unified alliance system; local polities commonly formed temporary alignments with the Ayyubid Egyptian-Syrian power, Yemeni regional houses, Red Sea port merchants, and neighboring tribes.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多为地方性，包括竞争部落、争夺朝觐与商路收益的城市势力、也门内部 rival 家族，以及威胁海岸贸易的海上袭扰者。
enemies.en: Hostilities were mostly local, including rival tribes, urban forces competing over pilgrimage and trade revenues, contending Yemeni families, and maritime raiders threatening coastal commerce.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以务实平衡为主：维护朝觐路线的开放性、保障红海与印度洋贸易、在强邻之间保持自治空间，并通过婚姻、馈赠、宗教名望和护送协议而非长期常备外交机构来达成目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was guided by pragmatic balance: keeping pilgrimage routes open, safeguarding Red Sea and Indian Ocean trade, preserving autonomy between stronger neighbors, and achieving aims through marriage ties, gifts, religious prestige, and escort agreements rather than permanent diplomatic institutions.

era.zh: 十字军时代的中世纪伊斯兰—阿拉伯地区
era.en: Medieval Islamic-Arabian world of the Age of Crusades

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括山地梯田灌溉、蓄水池与水渠工程、沙漠驮运组织、红海—印度洋航海经验、阿拉伯文书契约制度以及适应干旱环境的建筑与堡寨技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and practices included terraced mountain irrigation, cistern and channel engineering, organized desert pack transport, Red Sea–Indian Ocean navigational experience, Arabic documentary contract systems, and architecture and fortification suited to arid environments.

strengths.zh: 优势在于控制伊斯兰圣地、连接红海与印度洋贸易、拥有适应沙漠环境的部落机动能力，以及也门相对发达的农业和城镇网络。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in control of Islam's holy places, its position linking Red Sea and Indian Ocean trade, the tribal mobility adapted to desert conditions, and Yemen's relatively developed agriculture and urban networks.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治高度分裂、财政和军力分散、内陆征税与治安能力有限，并且易受旱灾、部落叛离和外部强权干预影响。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, dispersed fiscal and military capacity, limited inland taxation and policing power, and vulnerability to drought, tribal defection, and intervention by outside powers.

outlook.zh: 就1200年而言，阿拉伯半岛整体局势可称稳定但脆弱：圣地与贸易能维持基本繁荣，然而若也门权力更替、红海商路受阻或强邻加深介入，区域平衡便可能迅速改写。
outlook.en: As of 1200, the Arabian Peninsula was stable but fragile overall: the holy places and trade could sustain basic prosperity, yet a shift in Yemeni power, disruption of Red Sea commerce, or deeper intervention by stronger neighbors could quickly reshape the regional balance.

description.zh: 阿拉伯半岛未形成统一帝国，由圣城、也门高地与部族联盟共同构成政治格局。
description.en: Arabia was not unified under a single empire, but formed a political mosaic of holy cities, Yemeni highlands, and tribal alliances.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落—酋邦体系，由各岛酋长依据血缘、神圣威望与航海能力统治。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-chiefdom system in which island chiefs ruled through lineage, sacred prestige, and maritime capability.

socialStructure.zh: 波利尼西亚社会普遍以酋长、祭司、自由平民与依附者分层，但不同群岛差异明显。血统、神圣权威与土地使用权决定地位，大家族与氏族是社会基本单位。航海者、木匠与祭司常享有特殊声望。
socialStructure.en: Polynesian societies were commonly stratified into chiefs, priests, free commoners, and dependents, though practices varied by archipelago. Status depended on lineage, sacred authority, and rights to land use, while extended families and clans formed the core social units. Navigators, canoe builders, and ritual specialists often enjoyed special prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要为拥有神圣血统的酋长家族及其祭司顾问。强势岛屿上，酋长可通过再分配粮食、主持祭仪与组织远航巩固权威。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up chiefly of sacred chiefly lineages and their priestly advisers. On stronger islands, chiefs reinforced authority by redistributing food, leading rituals, and organizing long-distance voyages.

succession.zh: 酋位多在显贵家族内部继承，通常偏向长系血统，但也受个人威望、婚姻联盟与武力竞争影响。若出现继承争议，部落分裂或迁徙并不罕见。
succession.en: Chiefly office usually passed within elite lineages, often favoring senior descent, but personal prestige, marriage alliances, and force also mattered. When succession was disputed, fission of communities or migration was not uncommon.

structure.zh: 不存在统一跨洋国家，而是由多个岛屿酋邦分别治理。政治结构围绕酋长、祭司与亲族首领展开，公共事务主要依赖口头传统、仪式权威与集体劳作组织。
structure.en: There was no single trans-oceanic state, but rather many separate island chiefdoms. Political organization revolved around chiefs, priests, and kin leaders, with public affairs managed through oral tradition, ritual authority, and organized communal labor.

function.zh: 统筹贡品、土地使用安排、公共工程与粮食再分配。
function.en: Coordinated tribute, land-use arrangements, communal works, and food redistribution.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法、禁忌管理与远航决策。
function.en: Oversaw ritual, calendrical knowledge, taboo regulation, and decisions on long-distance voyaging.

localAdmin.zh: 行政基本在村社和岛屿层面展开，由酋长亲族、村落长者和祭司共同协调。较小聚落多凭习惯与亲缘网络自我治理。
localAdmin.en: Administration operated mainly at the village and island level, coordinated by chiefly relatives, village elders, and priests. Smaller settlements often governed themselves through custom and kin networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法和神圣禁忌体系，违犯土地、婚姻、祭仪与财物秩序会招致惩罚或赔偿。许多争端通过长者调停与仪式和解解决。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of customary rules and sacred taboo systems, with penalties or compensation for violations involving land, marriage, ritual, or property. Many disputes were settled through elder mediation and ceremonial reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无严格国家税制，贡纳多以芋头、面包果、鱼类、羽毛、细布及劳役形式呈献给酋长。贡品本质上兼具政治臣服与再分配功能。
taxationSystem.en: There was no strict state tax system; tribute was rendered to chiefs in taro, breadfruit, fish, feathers, fine cloth, and labor service. Such tribute served both as a sign of political submission and as a mechanism of redistribution.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 波利尼西亚人在远洋航海、双体船制造、星象导航与岛屿定居方面成就卓著。口述史诗、祭仪建筑与纹身文化展现出高度发展而多样的海洋文明传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Polynesians achieved remarkable success in blue-water voyaging, double-hulled canoe construction, stellar navigation, and island settlement. Oral epics, ritual architecture, and tattoo traditions reveal a highly developed and diverse oceanic civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语支
languageFamily.en: Polynesian branch of the Austronesian language family

philosophy.zh: 以波利尼西亚多神信仰、祖灵崇拜、神圣禁忌与航海宇宙观为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Polynesian polytheism, ancestor veneration, sacred taboo systems, and a cosmology shaped by voyaging.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字体系，历史、谱系与航海知识主要靠口述、歌谣和记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; history, genealogy, and navigational knowledge were transmitted orally through chant, recitation, and memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础为园圃农业、渔业与岛际交换，生产以满足本地消费和酋长再分配为主。虽然缺乏大规模市场和货币，但高超航海能力显著扩大了资源调配范围。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on horticulture, fishing, and inter-island exchange, with production aimed mainly at local consumption and chiefly redistribution. Although large markets and currency were absent, advanced voyaging skills greatly widened the range of resource movement.

mainIndustries.zh: 芋头与山药种植、面包果栽培、渔业、独木舟与双体船制造、细布编织、羽饰工艺
mainIndustries.en: Taro and yam cultivation, breadfruit horticulture, fishing, canoe and double-hulled vessel construction, barkcloth weaving, and featherwork

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、贝壳、羽毛、细布、木器、鱼干、石斧、独木舟部件
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, shells, feathers, barkcloth, wooden goods, dried fish, stone adzes, and canoe components

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在独木舟、渔具、园圃、树木资源、贝饰与储存食物。酋长家族则可积累更多贡品、精工羽饰和大型船只。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied in canoes, fishing gear, gardens, tree resources, shell valuables, and stored food. Chiefly families could accumulate larger tribute stores, prestige featherwork, and bigger vessels.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、酋长再分配和岛际礼物交换为核心的海洋部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A maritime tribal economy centered on household production, chiefly redistribution, and inter-island gift exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“财库”多体现为酋长储存的食物、细布、羽饰、工具与可用船只，而非金属货币。储备受风暴、歉收和航海损失影响较大。
treasuryDescription.en: The effective 'treasury' was stored food, barkcloth, feather regalia, tools, and serviceable canoes held by chiefs rather than metallic money. Such reserves were vulnerable to storms, crop failures, and voyaging losses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以贡品集中和再分配维系酋长权威，强调仪式消费、公共劳作和灾时互助。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on tribute concentration and redistribution to sustain chiefly authority, with emphasis on ritual expenditure, communal labor, and mutual aid in times of scarcity.

description.zh: 由酋长亲族和精锐随从组成，承担突袭与保卫首领任务。
description.en: Composed of chiefly kin and elite retainers, used for raids and protection of the chief.

description.zh: 战时由成年男子临时集结，以棍棒、石器和投掷武器作战。
description.en: Temporarily assembled adult men who fought with clubs, stone weapons, and thrown arms.

description.zh: 依靠独木舟和双体船进行短程登陆、报复与夺取资源。
description.en: Used canoes and double-hulled craft for short-range landings, reprisal raids, and resource seizure.

commanderInChief.zh: 各岛酋长
commanderInChief.en: Individual island chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以木制棍棒、长矛、投石器、护具和战舟为主，优势在航海机动与地形熟悉。缺乏金属武器与攻城技术，不利于长期大规模战争。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden clubs, spears, slingstones, protective gear, and war canoes, with strengths in maritime mobility and local terrain knowledge. The lack of metal arms and siege methods limited prolonged large-scale warfare.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁为岛际冲突、资源短缺、风暴灾害与个别群岛之间的迁徙竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-island conflict, resource scarcity, storm damage, and competition linked to migration between some island groups.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散在众多海岛上，多居住于沿海村落和可耕地附近。聚落规模通常较小，但航海网络使相距甚远的岛屿仍能保持文化联系。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across many islands, mostly living in coastal villages and near cultivable land. Settlements were usually small, but voyaging networks preserved cultural links across great distances.

name.zh: 萨摩亚乌波卢主要聚落

name.en: Tahiti Settlement in the Society Islands

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、祭司与航海专家、自由平民、依附者与战俘
socialClasses.en: Chiefly lineages, priests and navigational specialists, free commoners, dependents, and captives

allies.zh: 主要是婚姻结盟、礼物交换与同祖谱系岛屿之间的合作关系，并无固定国家同盟。
allies.en: Alliances mainly took the form of marriage ties, gift exchange, and cooperation between islands sharing ancestral connections; there were no fixed state alliances.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系通常局限于相邻岛屿或竞争航线与资源的酋邦。
enemies.en: Hostilities were typically limited to neighboring islands or chiefdoms competing over routes and resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重视维持海上联系、交换稀缺资源与通过联姻降低冲突风险。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on maintaining maritime links, exchanging scarce resources, and reducing conflict through intermarriage.

era.zh: 中世纪大洋洲远航酋邦阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 双体船和远航独木舟、星象导航、精细口述航海图谱、园圃农业与渔猎复合生计。
keyInnovations.en: Double-hulled canoes and long-range voyaging craft, stellar navigation, refined oral wayfinding systems, and a mixed horticultural-fishing subsistence economy.

strengths.zh: 航海技术高超、社会适应岛屿环境能力强、文化网络广泛且富有韧性。
strengths.en: They possessed exceptional maritime technology, strong adaptation to island environments, and wide, resilient cultural networks.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、资源受自然条件限制、政治分散且缺乏持久集权。
weaknesses.en: Population was small, resources were constrained by ecology, and politics remained fragmented without durable centralization.

outlook.zh: 只要海上联系与生态平衡维持，诸岛社会能够继续繁荣，但局部环境压力可能导致迁徙、冲突或酋权更替。
outlook.en: So long as seaborne links and ecological balance were maintained, island societies could continue to thrive, though local environmental stress might trigger migration, conflict, or shifts in chiefly power.

description.zh: 1200年前后的波利尼西亚世界是由分散海岛酋邦构成、以远洋航海、口述传统和贡纳再分配维系的海洋文明网络。
description.en: Around 1200, the Polynesian world was a maritime civilization network of dispersed island chiefdoms sustained by long-distance voyaging, oral tradition, and tribute redistribution.

governmentForm.zh: 部落酋邦制，由大酋长与地方酋长通过亲族、仪式和贡纳网络统治。
governmentForm.en: A tribal chiefdom system in which paramount and local caciques ruled through kinship, ritual authority, and tribute networks.

socialStructure.zh: 泰诺社会通常由酋长、贵族或显贵家族、祭司与平民构成，另有战俘和依附者。村社是基本生活单位，农业、渔业和公共劳动由亲族群体协作完成。仪式球场与祖灵崇拜在社会整合中地位重要。
socialStructure.en: Taíno society generally comprised caciques, noble or notable families, ritual specialists, and commoners, along with captives and dependents. The village community was the basic social unit, and agriculture, fishing, and communal labor were carried out by kin groups. Ceremonial ball courts and ancestor-focused ritual were central to social cohesion.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层为各级酋长及其家族，配合祭司和地方显贵管理村社与贡纳。大酋长的威望建立在血统、粮食再分配与宗教仪式主持能力之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of caciques at different levels and their families, working with ritual specialists and local notables to manage villages and tribute. A paramount cacique's prestige rested on lineage, food redistribution, and the ability to preside over religious ceremonies.

succession.zh: 酋位多按母系或双系亲属传统在显贵家族中继承，不同岛屿可能略有差异。若继承人威望不足，地方酋长和亲族长者可能影响继承结果。
succession.en: Chiefly office often passed within elite families according to matrilineal or cognatic custom, with local variation among islands. If an heir lacked prestige, local caciques and senior kin could influence the final outcome.

structure.zh: 泰诺人并无统一帝国，而是由多个酋邦组成，较大岛屿上存在层级化的大酋长体制。治理主要依靠村社首领、亲族网络、仪式权威与贡品再分配，而非成文官僚机构。
structure.en: The Taíno had no unified empire but were organized into multiple chiefdoms, with more hierarchical paramount chieftaincies on larger islands. Governance relied on village leaders, kinship networks, ritual authority, and tribute redistribution rather than a written bureaucracy.

function.zh: 负责贡品征集、仓储、公共劳作与赈济分配。
function.en: Oversaw tribute collection, storage, communal labor, and relief distribution.

function.zh: 管理祖灵崇拜、节庆、球赛仪式与神像供奉。
function.en: Managed ancestor worship, festivals, ceremonial ball games, and the care of sacred images.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落和酋区为主，村长、亲族首领与酋长代理人负责日常协调。较大岛屿上的大酋长通过朝贡和婚姻联系整合多个村社。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated mainly at the village and district level, with headmen, kin leaders, and chiefly agents handling daily coordination. On larger islands, paramount chiefs integrated multiple communities through tribute and marriage ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、祖先传统和酋长裁断为基础，涉及婚姻、土地使用、贡纳和伤害赔偿。严重冲突通常通过长者仲裁、赔偿和宗教仪式平息。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, ancestral tradition, and chiefly judgment, covering marriage, land use, tribute, and compensation for injury. Serious disputes were usually settled through elder arbitration, restitution, and ritual reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 贡纳主要以木薯、玉米、棉布、鱼获、工艺品与劳役形式呈交酋长。严格意义上的货币税不存在，但贡纳对维持权威和公共储备至关重要。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute was delivered to caciques mainly in cassava, maize, cotton cloth, fish, crafted goods, and labor service. No formal monetary tax existed, but tribute was essential to chiefly authority and communal reserves.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 泰诺人在木薯农业、棉纺织、独木舟制造、村社规划与仪式球场建造方面颇具特色。其神像雕刻、歌舞和祖灵祭祀传统体现了加勒比海地区高度发展的岛屿文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Taíno were notable for cassava agriculture, cotton weaving, canoe building, village planning, and ceremonial ball-court construction. Their sacred carvings, music and dance, and ancestral ritual reflected a highly developed island culture in the Caribbean.

languageFamily.zh: 阿拉瓦克语系
languageFamily.en: Arawakan language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜、神像崇拜与仪式性球赛文化为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestor veneration, sacred idol worship, and ritualized ball-game culture.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，历史与宗教知识依赖口述、歌舞与神像象征传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no developed writing system; history and religious knowledge were transmitted through oral tradition, performance, and sacred imagery.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 泰诺经济建立在木薯园圃农业、渔业与岛际交换之上，整体以自给和酋长再分配为主。较肥沃的大安的列斯群岛可支持较高人口密度，使部分酋邦具备较强组织能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Taíno economy was based on cassava horticulture, fishing, and inter-island exchange, oriented chiefly toward subsistence and redistribution by caciques. The more fertile Greater Antilles supported relatively high population density, allowing some chiefdoms to develop stronger organization.

mainIndustries.zh: 木薯与玉米种植、渔业、棉织、独木舟制造、陶器、木石雕刻
mainIndustries.en: Cassava and maize cultivation, fishing, cotton weaving, canoe building, pottery, and wood-stone carving

tradeGoods.zh: 木薯饼、棉布、贝饰、羽毛、陶器、石器、鱼干、木制工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Cassava bread, cotton cloth, shell ornaments, feathers, pottery, stone tools, dried fish, and wooden crafts

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在园圃、渔具、棉布、独木舟和食物储备。酋长家庭则控制更多土地使用权、贡品仓储与礼仪性贵重物品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay in gardens, fishing gear, cotton cloth, canoes, and stored food. Chiefly households controlled greater rights to land use, tribute stores, and ritual prestige goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以园圃农业、村社合作劳动和酋长贡纳再分配为基础的加勒比海酋邦经济。
economicSystem.en: A Caribbean chiefdom economy based on horticulture, communal village labor, and chiefly tribute redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财库主要为酋长粮仓、棉布、独木舟、装饰品和礼仪用品等实物储备。其价值高度依赖作物收成、渔获和村社服从程度。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury mainly consisted of storehouses of food, cotton cloth, canoes, ornaments, and ritual goods held by caciques. Its value depended heavily on harvests, fishing yields, and the obedience of village communities.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重心在于集中粮食和劳役，再通过节庆、赈济和赠予维系统治合法性与部落凝聚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on concentrating food and labor, then redistributing them through festivals, relief, and gifts to sustain legitimacy and tribal cohesion.

description.zh: 由大酋长与地方酋长的随从组成，负责护卫、执法与突袭。
description.en: Retainers of paramount and local caciques who handled protection, coercion, and raiding.

description.zh: 平时务农渔猎，战时以木棍、矛和石制武器集合应战。
description.en: Men who farmed and fished in peacetime and assembled in war with clubs, spears, and stone weapons.

description.zh: 用于沿岸巡逻、岛际冲突和快速袭击。
description.en: Used for coastal patrol, inter-island conflict, and rapid raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 大酋长与诸地方酋长
commanderInChief.en: Paramount cacique and subordinate local caciques

technology.zh: 武装主要依靠木棍、矛、石制刀刃、盾与独木舟，擅长局部突袭和熟悉岛屿环境的近战。缺乏金属冶炼、甲胄和复杂攻城能力。
technology.en: Arms consisted mainly of clubs, spears, stone-edged weapons, shields, and war canoes, well suited for raiding and close combat in familiar island environments. They lacked metallurgy, heavy armor, and sophisticated siege capacity.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自岛际竞争、对抗加勒比海其他群体的袭扰，以及飓风等自然灾害引发的资源紧张。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-island rivalry, raids involving other Caribbean groups, and resource stress caused by hurricanes and other natural disasters.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 泰诺人口主要分布在大安的列斯群岛和部分巴哈马群岛，以沿海及内陆可耕地区村社为主。聚落通常由圆形或长形房屋、球场和公共空间组成，人口密度在加勒比地区相对较高。
populationDescription.en: The Taíno population was concentrated in the Greater Antilles and parts of the Bahamas, living mainly in villages near coasts and arable interior zones. Settlements typically consisted of circular or elongated houses, ball courts, and communal spaces, with relatively high population density for the Caribbean.

name.zh: 伊斯帕尼奥拉中部大聚落
波多黎各西部酋邦聚落

socialClasses.zh: 大酋长与地方酋长、显贵家族、祭司、平民农渔民、依附者与战俘
socialClasses.en: Paramount and local caciques, notable families, ritual specialists, commoner farmers and fishers, dependents, and captives

allies.zh: 盟友多为通过婚姻、贸易与共同祖先叙事联系的邻近酋邦。
allies.en: Allies were chiefly neighboring chiefdoms linked by marriage, exchange, and shared ancestral traditions.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者是争夺资源和航道的其他酋邦，以及部分加勒比海战斗性更强的外来群体。
enemies.en: Enemies included rival chiefdoms competing over resources and sea routes, as well as some more warlike outside Caribbean groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持岛际交换、减少冲突并在必要时通过联姻和报复性袭击维持威望为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy aimed to preserve inter-island exchange, limit conflict, and when necessary maintain prestige through marriage alliances and retaliatory raiding.

era.zh: 前哥伦布加勒比海酋邦阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 木薯高床种植、棉织工艺、独木舟制造、球场与村社空间规划、祖灵神像雕刻传统。
keyInnovations.en: Cassava mound cultivation, cotton weaving, canoe construction, ceremonial ball-court and village planning, and sacred ancestral carving traditions.

strengths.zh: 农业适应热带岛屿环境，村社组织稳固，人口相对集中，酋邦礼仪有助于整合社会。
strengths.en: Their agriculture was well adapted to tropical island environments, village organization was stable, population was relatively concentrated, and chiefly ritual helped integrate society.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏金属技术和统一政治中心，军事规模有限，面对长期外部冲击韧性不足。
weaknesses.en: They lacked metallurgy and a unified political center, military scale was limited, and resilience against prolonged external shocks was modest.

outlook.zh: 在外部大规模入侵出现前，泰诺诸酋邦仍可维持繁荣与人口增长，但生态压力和岛际战争会周期性削弱个别地区。
outlook.en: Before the onset of major outside invasions, Taíno chiefdoms could continue to prosper and grow, though ecological pressure and inter-island warfare would periodically weaken some areas.

description.zh: 1200年前后的泰诺人是加勒比海地区以木薯农业、村社生活和酋邦礼仪网络为基础的繁盛岛屿社会。
description.en: Around 1200, the Taíno were a flourishing island society of the Caribbean built on cassava agriculture, village life, and networks of chiefly ritual.

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心的绍纳王国，结合神圣王权、亲族网络与对贸易路线的控制进行统治。
governmentForm.en: A Shona kingdom centered on a sacred kingship, ruling through kinship networks and control of trade routes.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、地方酋长、牧民与农民、工匠和商人为主要层次。宗教职能者在王权合法性中占有重要地位，附庸聚落向中心输送牲畜、粮食与劳役。
socialStructure.en: Society was layered around the royal household, local chiefs, herders and farmers, artisans, and traders. Religious specialists played an important role in legitimizing royal authority, while subordinate settlements sent cattle, grain, and labor to the center.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、王族、受封酋长及与王廷关系密切的高级祭司构成。其权力基础在于 cattle 财富、黄金贸易与对石砌中心的控制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal kin, subordinate chiefs, and senior ritual specialists tied to the court. Their power rested on cattle wealth, gold trade, and control of the stone-built centers.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室家族内部继承，但具体人选常受母系与父系亲族平衡、贵族支持及宗教认可影响。继承过程并非完全固定，偶有内部竞争。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal family, but the actual successor was shaped by paternal and maternal kin balances, elite support, and ritual approval. Succession was not entirely fixed and could involve internal rivalry.

structure.zh: 大津巴布韦政权以王廷为核心，通过附属酋长和地区首领实施间接统治。中央机构规模不大，但凭借贡赋、仪式权威和贸易控制维持广泛影响。
structure.en: The polity of Great Zimbabwe was centered on the royal court and governed indirectly through subordinate chiefs and regional headmen. Its central apparatus was limited in size, but it maintained broad influence through tribute, ritual authority, and control of trade.

function.zh: 管理王室仓储、贡品接收、劳役征调与贵族朝觐。
function.en: Managed royal storage, receipt of tribute, labor obligations, and noble attendance at court.

function.zh: 监督黄金、象牙与牲畜交换，并协调通往印度洋沿岸的商路。
function.en: Oversaw exchange in gold, ivory, and cattle, and coordinated routes leading toward the Indian Ocean coast.

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、祖先礼仪与王权象征活动。
function.en: Conducted royal rituals, ancestral observances, and ceremonies symbolizing kingship.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由酋长和村社首领管理，向王廷缴纳牲畜、谷物或黄金。王廷通过婚姻、封赐与仪式联系维持地方忠诚。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were administered by chiefs and village headmen who delivered cattle, grain, or gold to the court. The court maintained local loyalty through marriage ties, patronage, and ritual bonds.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和酋长裁判为基础。纠纷多围绕土地使用、牲畜、婚姻与贡赋义务展开。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal command, and judgments by chiefs. Disputes often concerned land use, cattle, marriage, and tribute obligations.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要表现为贡赋而非统一货币税，包括牲畜、粮食、黄金、象牙与劳役。沿商路与集散点的交易也可能被王廷抽取份额。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute rather than a uniform cash tax, including cattle, grain, gold, ivory, and labor. The court could also take a share from trade at routes and exchange centers.

religion.zh: 姆瓦里崇拜
religion.en: Mwari cult

culturalAchievements.zh: 大津巴布韦以大型干砌石建筑群著称，展现出高度成熟的建筑组织能力。其陶器、金属工艺和长距离贸易网络也反映出显著文化整合。
culturalAchievements.en: Great Zimbabwe is renowned for its large dry-stone architectural complexes, demonstrating highly developed organizational capacity. Its ceramics, metalworking, and long-distance trade networks also show notable cultural integration.

languageFamily.zh: 班图语系，主要为绍纳语支
languageFamily.en: Bantu language family, chiefly Shona branch

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、王权神圣性与姆瓦里相关信仰为核心的宗教世界观。
philosophy.en: A religious worldview centered on ancestor veneration, sacred kingship, and beliefs associated with Mwari.

writingSystem.zh: 无本地成文文字体系，主要依靠口述传统、记忆和仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No native written script; transmission relied primarily on oral tradition, memory, and ritual practice.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是混合农业、畜牧业、黄金开采与转口贸易。人均产出在撒哈拉以南内陆王国中较高，主要得益于其商路中转地位。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on mixed farming, cattle herding, gold extraction, and transit trade. Per-capita output was comparatively high for an inland sub-Saharan kingdom, largely due to its position on trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、畜牧、黄金开采、冶铁、陶器、长途贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, cattle herding, gold mining, ironworking, pottery, long-distance trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、象牙、牲畜、铁器、铜器、珠饰、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Gold, ivory, cattle, iron goods, copper goods, beads, pottery

name.zh: 无统一铸币，以实物与金属重量计值
name.en: No unified coinage; value reckoned in commodities and metal weight

unitName.en: head of cattle and gold weight

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为牲畜数量、谷物储备、金属器物与亲族劳动力。靠近贸易中心的家庭拥有更多输入珠饰和奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was chiefly expressed in cattle holdings, grain stores, metal goods, and kin-based labor. Households near trade centers possessed more imported beads and prestige items.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室控制的贡赋经济与区域贸易相结合的再分配体系。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive system combining royal tribute extraction with regional trade.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Levies on gold and ivory trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备主要以黄金、牲畜、象牙和谷物形式保存，而非统一货币。其流动性受贸易季节与商路安全影响较大。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves were held mainly in gold, cattle, ivory, and grain rather than in standardized coin. Their liquidity depended heavily on trade seasons and route security.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持王廷威望、保障商路与积累实物储备，以应对旱灾或地方动荡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining court prestige, securing trade routes, and building material reserves to face drought or local unrest.

unit.en: head of cattle and gold weight

description.zh: 负责都城、王室与仪式中心防卫的核心部队。
description.en: Core force responsible for defending the capital, royal household, and ritual centers.

description.zh: 由附庸酋长提供，装备矛、盾与弓，用于地区冲突和护卫商路。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate chiefs, armed with spears, shields, and bows for regional conflict and route protection.

description.zh: 小规模机动单位，负责贸易通道、矿区与牲畜群的安全。
description.en: Small mobile units protecting trade corridors, mining zones, and cattle herds.

commanderInChief.zh: 津巴布韦国王
commanderInChief.en: King of Zimbabwe

technology.zh: 以铁制矛、刀、弓箭、皮盾和石砌防御中心为主，缺乏重型攻城技术。军事组织依赖王室亲兵与地方酋长动员。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, blades, bows, hide shields, and stone-built defensive centers, with little heavy siege capability. Organization depended on royal retainers and mobilization through local chiefs.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自区域竞争政体、附庸离心以及商路中断，而非大规模外来征服。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival regional polities, centrifugal tendencies among tributaries, and disruption of trade routes rather than large-scale foreign conquest.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing
