rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary nobility; leaders were typically veteran hunters, warriors, or elders skilled at managing exchange relations. Their authority depended on follower consent and redistributive ability.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在同一家族或姻亲网络中优先产生，但并非绝对世袭。若继承者缺乏威望，部众可转而追随其他能干的领袖。
succession.en: Leadership often passed within the same family or affinal network, but it was not automatically hereditary. If an heir lacked prestige, followers could shift to another capable leader.

structure.zh: 政治组织高度分散，营地或游群是最基本的治理单位。重大事务如迁徙、复仇、结盟与婚姻交换，通常由首领与长者共同议定。跨部落层面的协调只在季节性集会或共同作战时出现。
structure.en: Political organization was highly decentralized, with the camp or band as the basic unit of governance. Major matters such as migration, feud settlement, alliances, and marriage exchange were usually decided by headmen and elders together. Coordination above the band level appeared mainly during seasonal gatherings or joint warfare.

function.zh: 处理迁徙路线、婚配安排、争端调停与对外关系。
function.en: Handled migration routes, marriage arrangements, dispute mediation, and external relations.

function.zh: 组织集体狩猎、营地防卫与临时战斗队伍。
function.en: Organized collective hunts, camp defense, and temporary war parties.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即各营地自理，由家族长者和受尊敬的首领主持。没有固定官署，治理随着季节迁徙而移动。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply camp self-rule under family elders and respected headmen. There were no permanent offices, and governance moved with seasonal migration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以口头传统维系。杀伤、婚姻纠纷与资源争议多通过赔偿、调解、决斗或分离营地解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary and maintained through oral tradition. Injury, marriage disputes, and resource conflicts were settled through compensation, mediation, duels, or separation of camps.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，首领依靠猎获分配、馈赠与战利品再分配维持威望。成员在公共狩猎或防卫行动中承担义务性贡献。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders maintained status through redistribution of game, gifts, and spoils. Members contributed through customary obligations in communal hunts and defense.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于高度适应潘帕斯草原生态的狩猎采集生活方式，以及复杂的口述传统与亲族交换网络。石器、骨器、皮革加工和营地组织体现了精细的环境知识。
culturalAchievements.en: Its major achievements lay in a hunter-gatherer lifeway finely adapted to the Pampas grasslands, along with complex oral traditions and kin-based exchange networks. Stone tools, bone tools, hide working, and camp organization reflected detailed environmental knowledge.

languageFamily.zh: 多种南锥地区土著语言，彼此并不统一
languageFamily.en: Multiple indigenous Southern Cone language groups, not linguistically unified

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵观念与对草原动物和自然循环的敬畏为核心，重视梦兆、禁忌与口述记忆。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestral presence, and reverence for grassland animals and natural cycles, with strong emphasis on omens, taboos, and oral memory.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识通过神话、歌谣、仪式与记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted through myth, song, ritual, and memory.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎采集为主，银值估算仅用于跨文明比较。物质产出不高，但对本地资源的利用效率较强。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was primarily subsistence hunting and gathering, and silver-equivalent figures are used only for cross-cultural comparison. Material output was modest, but local resource use was relatively efficient.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、皮革加工、骨角器制造、有限的部落间交换
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, hide processing, bone and antler tool production, limited inter-tribal exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、石料、骨器、装饰物、食物余产
tradeGoods.en: Hides, feathers, stone materials, bone tools, ornaments, surplus food

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在工具、皮革、装饰品、亲族关系和获取资源的权利，而非可积累金属财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth lay mainly in tools, hides, ornaments, kin ties, and access rights to resources rather than accumulated metal wealth.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、互惠交换和季节性迁徙采猎为基础的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on kin-sharing, reciprocal exchange, and seasonal mobile foraging and hunting.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无固定国库，储备主要以兽皮、工具、礼物和可再分配物资形式存在。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed treasury; reserves existed mainly as hides, tools, gifts, and redistributable goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持首领威望与群体凝聚，通过共享与再分配而非征税运作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining leadership prestige and group cohesion through sharing and redistribution rather than taxation.

description.zh: 以投矛、棍棒、石器与弓箭作战，兼具狩猎与战斗职能。
description.en: Used spears, clubs, stone weapons, and bows, serving both hunting and combat roles.

description.zh: 由成年成员临时组成，负责守卫营地、妇孺与储藏物资。
description.en: Temporarily assembled from adult members to guard camps, families, and stored goods.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由最具威望的战首领指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; command fell to the most prestigious war leader

technology.zh: 军事技术处于石器—骨器水平，依靠熟悉地形、伏击、机动营地和近距离武器。
technology.en: Military technology remained at a stone-and-bone tool level, relying on terrain knowledge, ambush, mobile camps, and close-range weapons.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近游群间的资源竞争、复仇冲突和气候波动导致的迁徙压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition over resources with neighboring bands, revenge conflicts, and migration pressure caused by climatic fluctuation.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于草原、河谷与季节性营地之间，整体密度很低。多数人口生活在小型流动聚落中，没有真正的城市化中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across grasslands, river valleys, and seasonal camps, with very low overall density. Most people lived in small mobile settlements with no true urban centers.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较弱，可区分为首领与长者、成年猎手与采集者、青少年、俘虏或边缘依附者。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was limited, but one may distinguish leaders and elders, adult hunters and gatherers, youths, and captives or marginal dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定国家盟友，主要依赖邻近亲族群体与婚姻结盟。
allies.en: No fixed state allies; alliances relied mainly on neighboring kin groups and marriage ties.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为争夺猎场、水源或复仇的邻近部落。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring tribes competing for hunting grounds, water, or revenge.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持迁徙空间、婚姻网络与资源平衡为主，避免长期大规模冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed mainly at preserving migration space, marriage networks, and resource balance while avoiding prolonged large-scale conflict.

era.zh: 晚期石器与部落口传时代
era.en: Late Stone Age tribal oral era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括适应草原生态的投掷武器、皮革加工、骨角器制作与季节性迁徙知识体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included grassland-adapted projectile weapons, hide processing, bone and antler craftsmanship, and sophisticated seasonal migration knowledge.

strengths.zh: 强项在于生态适应性高、机动性强、亲族纽带稳固，且无需复杂官僚即可维持基本秩序。
strengths.en: Its strengths were high ecological adaptability, strong mobility, durable kin ties, and the ability to maintain order without complex bureaucracy.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是人口少、缺乏集中组织、技术层级低，难以支持长期战争或大规模工程。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included small population, lack of central organization, and low technological complexity, making sustained warfare or large-scale works difficult.

outlook.zh: 短期内可继续稳定存在，但其发展上限受制于分散结构与低剩余生产。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain stable, but its long-term developmental ceiling was limited by decentralized structure and low surplus production.

description.zh: 潘帕斯诸文化是以流动营地、亲族纽带与草原狩猎采集经济为核心的分散部落社会。
description.en: The Pampas cultures were decentralized tribal societies centered on mobile camps, kinship ties, and grassland hunting-gathering economies.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝（澳大利亚原住民诸族群）

governmentForm.zh: 由众多语言—亲族群体组成的部落社会，地方事务由长老主持，跨群体关系通过仪式、婚姻与边界习惯协调。
governmentForm.en: A tribal mosaic of many language and kin groups, with local affairs guided by elders and intergroup relations coordinated through ritual, marriage, and customary boundaries.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以氏族、图腾、婚姻规则和年龄等级为基础，不同地区制度差异很大。长者掌握圣地知识、仪式权威与法则解释权。日常生产依地理环境而异，沿海、沙漠、林地和河流地区各具特色。
socialStructure.en: Social organization was based on clan, totemic affiliation, marriage rules, and age grades, with considerable regional variation. Elders held sacred-site knowledge, ritual authority, and the right to interpret law. Daily production differed by environment, with coastal, desert, woodland, and riverine regions showing distinct patterns.

rulingClass.zh: 无统一统治阶级，但受尊敬的长老、仪式主持者和资深猎采者具有较高权威。其地位主要来自知识、年龄和对传统法的掌握。
rulingClass.en: There was no unified ruling class, but respected elders, ritual specialists, and experienced foragers held higher authority. Their standing derived mainly from knowledge, age, and command of traditional law.

succession.zh: 领导角色通常在特定亲族或仪式资格持有者之间延续，但须经社群认可。仪式知识与圣地责任常按血缘和入会等级传递。
succession.en: Leadership roles often continued within specific kin lines or among holders of ritual qualification, but required community recognition. Ritual knowledge and sacred-site responsibility were commonly transmitted by descent and initiation rank.

structure.zh: 不存在覆盖全大陆的中央政权，治理单位多为地方氏族群或语言群。长老会议负责裁决婚姻、狩猎边界、祭仪安排与争端。跨地区联系主要借由贸易路线、仪式聚会与歌路系统维持。
structure.en: There was no continent-wide central state; governance generally operated at the level of local clan groups or language communities. Councils of elders judged marriage matters, hunting boundaries, ceremonial schedules, and disputes. Long-distance ties were maintained chiefly through trade routes, ritual gatherings, and songline networks.

function.zh: 维持传统法、裁决争端、安排婚姻与资源使用规则。
function.en: Maintained customary law, settled disputes, and regulated marriage and resource use.

function.zh: 主持仪式、传承神话歌路并维护圣地秩序。
function.en: Led ceremonies, preserved mythic songlines, and protected sacred-site order.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依附于亲族地盘和传统土地权，不设固定官署。每个地区按自身习俗管理水源、猎场与仪式地点。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on kin-territories and customary land rights rather than fixed offices. Each region governed water sources, hunting grounds, and ceremonial places according to its own traditions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系即“传统法”，通过口述神话、仪式义务和社会制裁维系。违法行为可导致赔偿、公开羞辱、仪式惩戒或驱逐。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was traditional law maintained through oral myth, ritual obligation, and social sanction. Violations could lead to compensation, public shame, ritual punishment, or expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收制度，社群以共享食物、履行仪式义务和互惠劳动来支持公共事务。大型聚会时由各群体自带贡品与交换物。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communities supported common affairs through food sharing, ritual obligations, and reciprocal labor. At large gatherings, each group brought its own offerings and exchange goods.

religion.zh: 梦幻时代
religion.en: Dreamtime

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于极其复杂的土地知识、火管理、航海与渔猎技术，以及覆盖广域的仪式与交换网络。岩画、木器、编织与歌曲传统在不同地区发展出高度精细的形式。
culturalAchievements.en: Its achievements lay in extraordinarily complex land knowledge, fire management, maritime and fishing techniques, and wide-ranging ceremonial and exchange networks. Rock art, woodcraft, weaving, and song traditions developed highly refined regional forms.

languageFamily.zh: 以帕马-纽恩加语系为主，另有多种非帕马-纽恩加语言
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Pama-Nyungan, alongside many non-Pama-Nyungan languages

philosophy.zh: 以“梦时”宇宙观为核心，认为祖灵在创世过程中塑造地貌、法则与社会秩序，人与土地、祖先和歌路密不可分。
philosophy.en: Centered on the Dreaming, which held that ancestral beings shaped the land, law, and social order, binding people inseparably to country, ancestors, and songlines.

writingSystem.zh: 无普遍使用的文字系统，知识以歌路、岩画、身体彩绘、仪式和口述谱系传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no generally used writing system; knowledge was transmitted through songlines, rock art, body painting, ceremony, and oral genealogy.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、渔业和地方性资源管理为主，银值仅是比较尺度。尽管缺乏国家型农业税收结构，不少地区拥有稳定而高效的生态生产体系。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, fishing, and local resource management, with silver-equivalent figures used only as a comparative metric. Despite lacking state-style agrarian taxation, many regions possessed stable and efficient ecological production systems.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、渔业、水产采集、火耕式景观管理、木石骨器制作、区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Foraging, fishing, aquatic harvesting, fire-based landscape management, wood-stone-bone tool production, regional exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 石料、赭石、贝壳、树脂、编织品、木器、仪式用品、干鱼和食物余产
tradeGoods.en: Stone materials, ochre, shells, resin, woven goods, wooden implements, ritual items, dried fish, and food surplus

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富表现为对土地与水域的使用权、仪式知识、工具、配偶网络和交换关系，而非金属积蓄。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through access rights to land and waters, ritual knowledge, tools, marriage networks, and exchange relationships rather than metal accumulation.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以土地责任、亲族共享、季节迁徙与广域互惠交换为核心的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on custodianship of country, kin sharing, seasonal mobility, and long-distance reciprocal exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，储备分散为仪式物品、赭石、珍贵石料、贝饰和食物资源权利。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified treasury; reserves were dispersed in ritual items, ochre, valued stone, shell ornaments, and rights to food resources.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政逻辑重在维持仪式秩序、互惠网络与长者权威，而非积累集中税赋。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal logic emphasized sustaining ritual order, reciprocal networks, and elder authority rather than accumulating centralized taxes.

description.zh: 由成年男子为主组成，使用矛、投矛器、棍棒、盾牌与回旋镖等武器。
description.en: Drawn mainly from adult men, using spears, spear-throwers, clubs, shields, and returning or fighting boomerangs.

description.zh: 负责保护营地、圣地和集会地点，多为临时编组。
description.en: Protected camps, sacred sites, and gathering places, usually on a temporary basis.

description.zh: 存在于部分沿海和河口地区，用于袭扰、巡护与渔猎冲突。
description.en: Present in some coastal and estuarine areas, used for raiding, patrol, and fishery-related conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由地区战首领和长老分别统筹
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; regional war leaders and elders directed action separately

technology.zh: 军事技术以木、石、骨材料武器为主，部分地区擅长投矛器、盾牌、独木舟和地形伏击战术。
technology.en: Military technology was based on wooden, stone, and bone weapons; some regions excelled in spear-throwers, shields, canoes, and terrain-based ambush tactics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为邻近群体间的边界争议、报复性冲突和生态压力；全大陆层面的统一征服威胁不存在。
threats.en: Main threats were boundary disputes, retaliatory conflict among neighboring groups, and ecological stress; no continent-wide conquest threat existed.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于全澳大陆，但极度分散，聚落规模通常较小。沿海、河谷和资源丰富地区密度较高，内陆荒漠地带密度很低。
populationDescription.en: Population was spread across the Australian continent but was extremely dispersed, with settlements usually small. Densities were higher along coasts, river valleys, and resource-rich zones, and very low in the arid interior.

socialClasses.zh: 没有严格阶级制，主要区分为长老与仪式持有者、成年生产者、受训青年、儿童以及外来婚姻成员。
socialClasses.en: There was no rigid class system; distinctions mainly lay among elders and ritual custodians, adult producers, initiated youths, children, and affinal outsiders.

allies.zh: 盟友多为共享歌路、婚姻关系或交换路线的邻近群体。
allies.en: Allies were usually neighboring groups linked by songlines, marriage ties, or exchange routes.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常是发生边界、婚姻或复仇争执的近邻群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were typically nearby groups involved in boundary, marriage, or revenge disputes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持土地边界、仪式互访和远距离交换的稳定为重，冲突一般局限于地区层面。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations prioritized stable land boundaries, ceremonial visitation, and long-distance exchange, with conflicts generally limited to the regional level.

era.zh: 晚期石器与梦时传统时代
era.en: Late Stone Age and Dreaming tradition era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括投矛器、回旋镖、精细石器打制、火管理、鱼坝与陷阱系统，以及依歌路组织的导航知识。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included spear-throwers, boomerangs, refined stone knapping, fire management, fish traps and weirs, and navigation knowledge organized through songlines.

strengths.zh: 优势在于对多样生态环境的深刻适应、稳定的传统法、长距离信息网络和高度成熟的口述知识体系。
strengths.en: Strengths included deep adaptation to diverse ecologies, resilient traditional law, long-distance information networks, and a highly developed oral knowledge system.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一政权、低度物质集中和有限的军事整合能力，难以形成大型国家结构。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in the absence of unified state power, low material concentration, and limited military integration, which hindered large state formation.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后仍具长期稳定性，但其政治形态将继续保持区域化和多中心特征。
outlook.en: Around 1200 it remained highly sustainable, but its political form was likely to stay regionalized and polycentric.

description.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸社会是以梦时宇宙观、长老权威和对土地的深厚知识为基础的多中心部落文明体系。
description.en: Australian Aboriginal societies formed a polycentric tribal civilization grounded in the Dreaming, elder authority, and profound knowledge of country.

governmentForm.zh: 以神圣王权为核心的海洋王国，通过贵族网络、朝贡与航海联系统治汤加群岛及周边区域。
governmentForm.en: A sacral maritime kingdom centered on divine kingship, ruling Tonga and influencing neighboring islands through nobles, tribute, and seafaring links.

socialStructure.zh: 社会等级分明，最高层为图伊汤加及其宗族，其下为世袭贵族、地方首领、自由平民与依附劳作者。血缘、神圣地位与对土地和劳役的控制共同决定社会位置。妇女在高阶亲属关系和礼仪交换中地位重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with the Tu'i Tonga and royal kin at the top, followed by hereditary nobles, local chiefs, free commoners, and dependent laborers. Social position was shaped by descent, sacred rank, and control of land and labor. High-ranking women also held important status in kinship and ceremonial exchange.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括图伊汤加王室、主要贵族家族和各岛的首领，他们掌握土地、贡赋与海上交通。其权威既有宗教神圣性，也有对劳役动员的现实控制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the Tu'i Tonga royal house, major noble lineages, and island chiefs who controlled land, tribute, and maritime routes. Their authority combined religious sacrality with practical command over labor mobilization.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部依血统与礼仪合法性继承，但贵族与宗族支持同样关键。地方首领职位多带有世袭性质，并受王权确认。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal lineage according to descent and ritual legitimacy, though noble and kin support remained crucial. Local chiefships were largely hereditary and confirmed by royal authority.

structure.zh: 王国以穆阿为王权中心，通过王族、贵族与岛屿首领分层治理。中央并无复杂文官机构，但在贡赋征集、礼仪统筹与远航组织方面具备较强协调能力。地方治理依赖各岛首领及其家臣网络执行。
structure.en: The kingdom was centered on Mu'a and governed through a hierarchy of royal kin, nobles, and island chiefs. It lacked a complex civil bureaucracy, but possessed strong coordination in tribute collection, ceremonial organization, and long-distance voyaging. Local administration depended on island chiefs and their retainers.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、王室礼仪、贡品接纳与大型公共仪式。
function.en: Managed worship, royal ceremony, tribute reception, and major public rituals.

function.zh: 协调各岛贡赋、劳役征发与土地分配秩序。
function.en: Coordinated island tribute, labor obligations, and land allocation order.

function.zh: 组织远航、维护岛际联系并安排使节往来。
function.en: Organized long-distance voyaging, maintained inter-island links, and arranged diplomatic missions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各岛首领和其属下家族首领管理，负责贡赋征收、劳役调配和纠纷处理。中央通过礼仪关系和定期贡纳维持约束力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by island chiefs and subordinate lineage heads, who managed tribute, labor, and dispute settlement. The center maintained authority through ritual ties and regular tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和神圣王权禁忌为基础，首领与祭仪权威共同裁断。对土地、婚姻、等级冒犯和贡赋违背的处罚较为严厉。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom and the taboos of sacred kingship, judged jointly by chiefs and ritual authorities. Violations involving land, marriage, rank offenses, and tribute obligations could be punished severely.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要表现为贡纳与劳役，而非货币税。椰子制品、席垫、食物、木材和航海服务构成王权资源基础。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form of tribute and labor rather than money taxes. Coconut products, mats, food, timber, and voyaging service formed the material base of royal power.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 图伊汤加时期以巨型墓冢、精细的礼仪制度和高水平的远洋航海组织闻名。其岛际交换和王室建筑体现了高度整合的海洋文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Tu'i Tonga era was renowned for monumental tombs, elaborate ceremonial order, and advanced organization of long-distance voyaging. Inter-island exchange and royal construction reflected a highly integrated maritime culture.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语支（汤加语）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch (Tongan)

philosophy.zh: 核心观念是波利尼西亚神圣王权、祖先崇拜与宇宙秩序的结合，强调等级、礼仪与互惠馈赠。
philosophy.en: Its core worldview combined Polynesian sacred kingship, ancestor veneration, and cosmic order, emphasizing hierarchy, ceremony, and reciprocal gifting.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，历史、谱系与航海知识通过口述传统、仪式和记忆专家保存。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system existed; history, genealogy, and navigational knowledge were preserved through oral tradition, ceremony, and memory specialists.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以园圃农业、渔业、贡赋和岛际交换为主，银值估算仅用于横向比较。相较一般部落社会，汤加拥有更高的剩余生产和组织动员能力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on horticulture, fishing, tribute, and inter-island exchange, with silver-equivalent values used only for comparison. Compared with typical tribal societies, Tonga had higher surplus production and stronger organizational capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 园圃农业、渔业、独木舟建造、席垫与树皮布制作、木石工艺、岛际贸易
mainIndustries.en: Horticulture, fishing, canoe building, mat and barkcloth production, wood and stone craftsmanship, inter-island trade

tradeGoods.zh: 树皮布、细席、贝饰、木材、独木舟、食物、羽饰、黑曜石与礼品贡物
tradeGoods.en: Barkcloth, fine mats, shell ornaments, timber, canoes, foodstuffs, feather goods, obsidian, and prestige tribute items

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、椰林、独木舟、细席、树皮布和与高阶家族的关系上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in land-use rights, coconut groves, canoes, fine mats, barkcloth, and ties to high-ranking families.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以王权贡赋、地方园圃生产和岛际礼物交换相结合的海洋再分配经济。
economicSystem.en: A maritime redistributive economy combining royal tribute, local horticultural production, and inter-island gift exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year (estimated)
taels of silver equivalent per year (estimated)
taels of silver equivalent per year (estimated)
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富主要以贡物、细席、树皮布、独木舟、粮食储备和贵重礼品形式保存，而非铸币金库。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth was stored mainly in tribute goods, fine mats, barkcloth, canoes, food reserves, and prestige gifts rather than a coined treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以贡赋再分配为核心，优先保障王室礼仪、贵族忠诚和海上交通能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on tribute redistribution, prioritizing royal ceremony, noble loyalty, and maritime connectivity.

description.zh: 直属图伊汤加与核心贵族，负责穆阿及王室仪式中心的安全。
description.en: Attached directly to the Tu'i Tonga and core nobles, protecting Mu'a and royal ceremonial centers.

description.zh: 由各岛首领在需要时征发，使用棍棒、长矛、投射武器与防护器具。
description.en: Raised by island chiefs when needed, using clubs, spears, missile weapons, and protective gear.

description.zh: 承担岛际投送、护航、突袭和威慑任务，是王国影响力的重要工具。
description.en: Handled inter-island transport, escort, raiding, and deterrence, forming an important instrument of royal influence.

commanderInChief.zh: 图伊汤加（名义最高统帅）
commanderInChief.en: Tu'i Tonga (nominal supreme commander)

technology.zh: 军事技术以高水平造舟、海上导航、木质武器和有组织的岛际投送为特点，虽无金属兵器，但海洋机动能力突出。
technology.en: Military technology featured advanced canoe construction, oceanic navigation, wooden weapons, and organized inter-island projection; though lacking metal arms, maritime mobility was exceptional.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year (estimated)

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自岛际叛离、贵族竞争以及与斐济、萨摩亚网络中的势力角逐和海上风暴风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from island defection, noble rivalry, competition within Fiji-Samoa regional networks, and the ever-present hazard of maritime storms.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于汤加塔布等主要岛屿及可耕作、靠海的聚落带，聚居程度高于多数大洋洲部落社会。穆阿是王权与礼仪中心，但仍属前城市型大型聚落。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated on major islands such as Tongatapu and in cultivable coastal settlement belts, with denser settlement than in most Oceanic tribal societies. Mu'a was the royal and ceremonial center, though still a proto-urban large settlement rather than a true city.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王族、世袭贵族、地方首领、自由平民、手工与航海专长者及依附劳作者。
socialClasses.en: Main strata included royalty, hereditary nobles, local chiefs, free commoners, craft and navigational specialists, and dependent laborers.

allies.zh: 与部分萨摩亚、斐济群岛首领维持婚姻、礼物和航海联系，关系时盟时竞。
allies.en: Maintained marriage, gift, and voyaging ties with some chiefs in Samoa and Fiji, relationships that could be both allied and competitive.

enemies.zh: 敌对力量多来自不服从的地方首领、竞争性贵族支系及邻近岛群中的海上对手。
enemies.en: Opponents mainly included disobedient local chiefs, rival noble branches, and maritime competitors in neighboring island groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护朝贡网络、巩固王室声望和控制关键航海通道为核心，偏重礼仪影响与有限武力并用。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve the tribute network, reinforce royal prestige, and control key voyaging routes, using ceremonial influence backed by limited force.

era.zh: 波利尼西亚海洋王国时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括双体及大型远洋独木舟、恒星导航、精细树皮布与席垫工艺、巨石与土木礼仪建筑以及高效园圃体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included double-hulled and large ocean-going canoes, stellar navigation, refined barkcloth and mat production, monumental stone-and-earth ceremonial building, and efficient horticultural systems.

strengths.zh: 优势在于王权神圣性强、社会组织层级清晰、海上交通发达且能动员跨岛资源。
strengths.en: Strengths included powerful sacral kingship, clear social hierarchy, advanced maritime communications, and the ability to mobilize resources across islands.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是人口与资源总量有限、对航海天气依赖大，且政治整合容易受贵族竞争与岛屿离心影响。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were limited total population and resources, heavy dependence on seafaring conditions, and vulnerability of political integration to noble rivalry and island centrifugal tendencies.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后处于兴盛阶段，若能维持王室威望与航海网络，仍可继续主导中西波利尼西亚的重要海域。
outlook.en: Around 1200 it was in a thriving phase, and if it could sustain royal prestige and voyaging networks, it could continue to dominate important waters of central-western Polynesia.

description.zh: 图伊汤加王国是一个以神圣王权、贡赋再分配和远洋航海能力为支柱的兴盛波利尼西亚海洋国家。
description.en: The Tu'i Tonga realm was a thriving Polynesian maritime state built on sacred kingship, tributary redistribution, and long-distance seafaring power.

governmentForm.zh: 由契丹皇族统治、兼具草原汗国与汉式皇朝特征的多民族帝国。
governmentForm.en: A multiethnic empire ruled by the Khitan royal house, combining steppe khanate traditions with Chinese-style imperial institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 西辽社会由契丹统治集团、突厥与伊朗系地方贵族、城市商人以及绿洲农民和游牧部众构成。七河与中亚绿洲城市拥有较成熟的定居社会，而边缘地区仍以部族关系和牧业组织为主。不同宗教与族群并存，社会整合主要依赖军事威望与税贡体系。
socialStructure.en: Kara-Khitai society consisted of the Khitan ruling elite, Turkic and Iranian local nobles, urban merchants, oasis farmers, and nomadic tribes. The Seven Rivers region and Central Asian oasis cities maintained settled social structures, while peripheral areas remained organized around tribal and pastoral networks. Social cohesion depended largely on military prestige and a tributary tax order across diverse religions and ethnic groups.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以耶律皇族及其契丹贵族为核心，并吸纳地方穆斯林与突厥显贵协助治理。宫廷在礼制上保留辽朝传统，但实际统治必须依赖附属王公与地方总督。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Yelü royal house and Khitan nobles, while incorporating local Muslim and Turkic notables into administration. The court preserved many Liao traditions in ritual, but practical rule depended on subordinate princes and provincial governors.

succession.zh: 皇位原则上由耶律氏宗室继承，但宫廷婚姻、摄政与贵族支持对继承结果影响很大。到直鲁古时期，宗室权威已受附庸势力与外部压力削弱。
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited within the Yelü clan, but court marriage alliances, regencies, and noble backing heavily influenced succession outcomes. By the reign of Zhilugu, dynastic authority had been weakened by vassal powers and external pressures.

structure.zh: 西辽中央政权规模不大，以皇廷与少数核心官署统辖帝国，并通过附庸政权、地方长官和征贡关系维持统治。其制度兼有契丹旧制、草原军事指挥体系及中亚地方行政惯例。对远方地区多实行间接控制。
structure.en: The Kara-Khitai central government was relatively compact, ruling through the imperial court and a small set of core offices while relying on vassal states, local governors, and tribute relations. Its institutions blended Khitan precedents, steppe military command, and Central Asian local administrative practices. Distant territories were often managed indirectly.

function.zh: 负责诏令、朝贡、军事调度与对附庸的总体监督。
function.en: Handled decrees, tribute relations, military mobilization, and overall supervision of vassals.

function.zh: 征收商税、贡赋、牧税与绿洲农业税，管理银币与实物储备。
function.en: Collected customs, tribute, pastoral taxes, and oasis agricultural levies, and managed silver and in-kind reserves.

function.zh: 管理主要绿洲城市、驿站、治安和地方司法。
function.en: Administered major oasis cities, relay stations, local security, and provincial justice.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依地区而异，七河与巴拉沙衮周边由契丹与当地豪强共同管理，中亚诸城则更多保留原有伊斯兰官僚体系。附庸国通常保留本地君主，只需纳贡并提供兵力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration varied by region: the Seven Rivers area and lands around Balasagun were governed jointly by Khitan officials and local magnates, while Central Asian cities often retained earlier Islamic bureaucratic structures. Vassal states usually kept their own rulers in exchange for tribute and military support.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合契丹旧律、习惯法和地方宗教法。穆斯林城市在民事与商业事务上常依伊斯兰法审理，而政治与军政事务最终由皇廷裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Khitan precedents, customary law, and local religious law. Muslim cities often judged civil and commercial cases under Islamic law, while political and military matters remained subject to the imperial court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以商税、农业田赋、牧税和附庸贡赋为主。丝路节点的关税收入尤为重要，也是西辽维持跨区域统治的财政基础。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on customs duties, land taxes, pastoral levies, and tribute from vassals. Customs revenue from Silk Road nodes was especially important and formed the fiscal backbone of Kara-Khitai rule.

religion.zh: 佛教
religion.en: Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 西辽的重要成就在于将辽朝政治传统延续到中亚，并维系了丝绸之路东段与中段的跨文化交流。其宫廷礼制、货币流通与行政模式对中亚产生持续影响。
culturalAchievements.en: Its major achievement was carrying Liao political traditions into Central Asia and sustaining cross-cultural exchange across the eastern and central Silk Road. Its court ritual, coin circulation, and administrative patterns left a durable impact on the region.

languageFamily.zh: 统治层主要为阿尔泰语系契丹语，民间广泛使用突厥语族与伊朗语族语言。
languageFamily.en: The ruling elite chiefly used the Khitan language of the Altaic sphere, while Turkic and Iranian languages were widely spoken among the population.

philosophy.zh: 以佛教宫廷传统为核心，同时容纳儒家政治遗绪、草原习俗与伊斯兰学术环境。
philosophy.en: Court culture was centered on Buddhist traditions while also accommodating residual Confucian statecraft, steppe customs, and Islamic scholarly environments.

writingSystem.zh: 并用汉文、契丹文、波斯文与阿拉伯字母文书。
writingSystem.en: Used Chinese, Khitan script, Persian, and Arabic-script documents in parallel.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 西辽经济建立在绿洲农业、畜牧业与丝路贸易中介之上。人口规模不及中原王朝，但凭借跨区域商路控制获得较高的人均财政能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Kara-Khitai economy rested on oasis agriculture, pastoralism, and mediation of Silk Road trade. Its population was modest compared with Chinese empires, but control of long-distance commerce gave it relatively strong per-capita fiscal capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 绿洲农业、畜牧业、商贸转运、手工业、铸币
mainIndustries.en: Oasis agriculture, pastoralism, commercial transit, handicrafts, and minting

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、羊毛、皮革、丝绸、香料、金属器、玉石、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Horses, wool, leather, silk, spices, metalware, jade, and slaves

name.en: Mixed silver coinage and payments in kind

unitName.zh: 第纳尔、迪尔汗及银两折算

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人和绿洲地主财富较高，游牧牧户以牲畜为主要资产。普通家庭抗风险能力受战乱和商路波动影响明显。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants and oasis landholders were relatively wealthy, while pastoral households measured wealth mainly in livestock. Ordinary families were vulnerable to warfare and disruptions in trade routes.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家控制交通节点与税关，地方经济则由绿洲城市、部族首领和商人网络共同运作。
economicSystem.en: The state controlled transport nodes and customs points, while the wider economy was operated by oasis towns, tribal leaders, and merchant networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存银与实物储备尚可，但因附庸离心和军费上升而趋于紧张。财政稳定性高度依赖丝路税收和宗主权能否维持。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still held a workable reserve of silver and goods, but it was under growing strain from rising military costs and centrifugal vassals. Fiscal stability depended heavily on Silk Road customs and the maintenance of overlordship.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持军政控制与商路通畅，必要时加重附庸贡赋与边地征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military control and secure trade routes, with tribute demands and frontier levies increased when necessary.

unit.zh: 第纳尔、迪尔汗及银两折算

description.zh: 帝国主力，擅长机动突袭、远程骑射与草原会战。
description.en: The main striking arm of the empire, skilled in mobility, mounted archery, and open steppe warfare.

description.zh: 主要来自绿洲城市与附庸政权，用于守城、护税和后勤。
description.en: Drawn largely from oasis towns and vassal states, used for garrison duty, revenue protection, and logistics.

description.zh: 负责宿卫、核心战场支援与监控重要属臣。
description.en: Responsible for palace security, support in key battles, and supervision of major subordinates.

commanderInChief.zh: 直鲁古皇帝
commanderInChief.en: Emperor Zhilugu

technology.zh: 军备以复合弓骑兵、甲胄骑队和城防器械为主，兼受中原与中亚技术影响。攻城能力存在但不如草原机动作战突出。
technology.en: Military technology centered on composite-bow cavalry, armored horsemen, and siege equipment, influenced by both Chinese and Central Asian traditions. Siege capabilities existed, but mobile steppe warfare remained its stronger field.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自乃蛮遗众、花剌子模扩张、附庸离叛及蒙古草原新兴势力的逼近。
threats.en: Major threats came from Naiman remnants, Khwarazmian expansion, rebellious vassals, and the approach of rising Mongol power from the steppe.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

populationDescription.zh: 人口约百万，主要分布于七河地区与中亚绿洲城镇。帝国内族群复杂，包括契丹、突厥人、粟特后裔、伊朗系居民及其他草原部族。
populationDescription.en: The population was about one million, concentrated in the Seven Rivers region and Central Asian oasis towns. The empire was ethnically mixed, including Khitans, Turkic peoples, Sogdian-descended communities, Iranian populations, and other steppe groups.

socialClasses.zh: 皇族与契丹贵族、地方王公与宗教领袖、城市商人和地主、农民、牧民、奴婢与依附人口。
socialClasses.en: Royal and Khitan aristocrats, local princes and religious elites, urban merchants and landholders, peasants, pastoralists, and dependent or enslaved populations.

allies.zh: 名义上的附庸诸国与部分中亚城邦，关系多基于威服而非稳固同盟。
allies.en: Its allies were chiefly nominal vassal states and some Central Asian city polities, though these ties rested more on coercive overlordship than firm alliance.

enemies.zh: 花剌子模、叛离附庸、草原敌对部族及后起蒙古势力。
enemies.en: Khwarazm, rebellious vassals, hostile steppe tribes, and the emergent Mongol forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持宗主权、控制丝路通道和在中亚各势力间保持均势为主。由于国势衰退，其外交越来越偏向防御与权宜妥协。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve suzerainty, control Silk Road corridors, and maintain a balance among Central Asian powers. As the state declined, diplomacy became increasingly defensive and opportunistic.

era.zh: 12世纪末至13世纪初的中亚草原—绿洲复合文明阶段
era.en: Late 12th- to early 13th-century Central Asian steppe-oasis composite civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑兵体系、跨区域驿传、混合货币流通、兼容多法域的行政实践。
keyInnovations.en: Composite-bow cavalry systems, transregional relay communications, mixed monetary circulation, and administration across multiple legal traditions.

strengths.zh: 地处丝路要冲、骑兵传统强、政权具有跨文化整合能力。
strengths.en: It held key Silk Road positions, possessed strong cavalry traditions, and showed notable capacity for cross-cultural integration.

weaknesses.zh: 中央官僚薄弱、对附庸依赖过深、人口与财政基础有限，且面临多线威胁。
weaknesses.en: Its central bureaucracy was thin, it depended heavily on vassals, its demographic and fiscal base was limited, and it faced threats on multiple fronts.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍具区域影响力，但若无强力整顿，极可能被更具扩张性的邻国取代。
outlook.en: It still retained regional influence in the short term, but without strong reform it was likely to be displaced by more expansionist neighbors.

description.zh: 西辽是一个延续辽朝传统、控制中亚丝路要道但已步入衰落的契丹帝国。
description.en: Kara-Khitai was a Khitan empire that carried on Liao traditions, controlled key Central Asian Silk Road routes, and had entered a phase of decline.

governmentForm.zh: 以海上贸易与港口臣属体系为基础的佛教海洋帝国。
governmentForm.en: A Buddhist maritime empire based on overseas trade and a network of subordinate ports.

socialStructure.zh: 三佛齐社会以王室、港口贵族、僧侣、商人、航海者、农民和沿海部族构成。沿海港口人口流动性强，聚集马来人、华人、印度商人及群岛各地商旅。内陆地区则更依赖稻作、河运和地方酋长权威。
socialStructure.en: Srivijayan society was composed of the royal house, port nobles, monks, merchants, sailors, farmers, and coastal tribal communities. Its ports were highly mobile and cosmopolitan, attracting Malays, Chinese, Indian merchants, and traders from across the archipelago. Inland areas depended more on rice cultivation, river transport, and the authority of local chiefs.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层为室利佛逝王室、港口首领与依附王公，他们通过控制河口、海峡与贸易征收维持地位。佛教僧团亦具有重要象征和文化影响。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Srivijayan royal house, port chiefs, and subordinate princes who maintained power by controlling estuaries, straits, and trade levies. Buddhist monastic communities also held major symbolic and cultural influence.

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族继承，但实际继承常受港口豪强、宗族联盟和外部干预左右。至1200年前后，王权已较早期松散，地方离心倾向明显。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal family, but actual succession was often shaped by port magnates, kinship alliances, and outside intervention. By around 1200, royal authority was looser than in earlier centuries and centrifugal local tendencies were pronounced.

structure.zh: 三佛齐的中央政权依托都城与主要港口，统治方式偏向海上节点控制而非深入内陆的官僚行政。王室通过航道、贡赋、盟约与婚姻关系管理诸港。对远方岛屿多为宗主性支配。
structure.en: Srivijaya’s central authority rested on the capital and major ports, favoring control of maritime nodes rather than deep inland bureaucracy. The crown managed ports through sea-lanes, tribute, alliances, and marriage ties. Distant islands were generally held in a suzerain rather than directly administered framework.

function.zh: 管理港口停泊、关税、外商接待与航道治安。
function.en: Managed harbor berthing, customs, reception of foreign merchants, and security of sea routes.

function.zh: 征收属港贡物，管理香料、树脂、稻米与金属储备。
function.en: Collected tribute from subordinate ports and managed stores of spices, resins, rice, and metals.

name.zh: 河口与地方首领事务署

function.zh: 协调地方酋长、内河运输和附属港口的忠诚关系。
function.en: Coordinated local chiefs, inland river transport, and loyalty among subordinate ports.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理多由港口领主、河口酋长和附属王公承担，王室主要通过授封、贡赋和贸易特权控制。内陆地区中央渗透有限。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was mostly handled by port lords, estuary chiefs, and subordinate princes, with the crown exercising control through titles, tribute, and trade privileges. Central penetration inland was limited.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室命令和港口商贸惯例为主，佛教伦理对王权合法性有重要支持作用。涉外贸易纠纷往往以协商、赔偿和港口规则处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was grounded in custom, royal orders, and port mercantile practice, while Buddhist ethics supported royal legitimacy. Foreign trade disputes were often resolved through negotiation, compensation, and harbor regulations.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、过境费、附属港口贡赋和地方贡物为核心。与农业田赋相比，海贸征收更为关键。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on port customs, transit dues, tribute from subordinate harbors, and local tribute goods. Maritime levies were far more important than agricultural land taxes.

religion.zh: 佛教
religion.en: Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 三佛齐长期是东南亚佛教学习与海上交流中心，连接印度洋与南海世界。其港口文化促进了宗教传播、语言融合与跨海商贸网络发展。
culturalAchievements.en: Srivijaya long served as a center of Buddhist learning and maritime exchange in Southeast Asia, linking the Indian Ocean and the South China Sea. Its port culture fostered religious transmission, linguistic blending, and inter-island commercial networks.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属南岛语系马来支，宫廷和宗教层面受梵语强烈影响。
languageFamily.en: The main language belonged to the Malay branch of the Austronesian family, with strong Sanskrit influence in court and religious contexts.

philosophy.zh: 以大乘佛教与密教传统为主，并与马来王权观念和印度化宫廷文化结合。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual world was shaped chiefly by Mahayana and esoteric Buddhism, blended with Malay kingship concepts and Indianized court culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古马来语碑铭与梵文书写，文字多见于巴利/婆罗米系南岛改写系统。
writingSystem.en: Used Old Malay inscriptions and Sanskrit writing, largely within Brahmi-derived regional scripts.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 三佛齐经济高度依赖转口贸易和海峡控制，其财富集中于少数港口与王室网络。虽然总人口不大，但海贸收益使都城及主要港市具有较强消费和进口能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Srivijayan economy depended heavily on entrepôt trade and control of straits, concentrating wealth in a few ports and royal networks. Though total population was modest, maritime profits gave the capital and major ports notable purchasing power and access to imports.

mainIndustries.zh: 港口贸易、航运、稻作、森林采集、造船、香料与树脂转运
mainIndustries.en: Port commerce, shipping, rice cultivation, forest extraction, shipbuilding, and transit of spices and resins

tradeGoods.zh: 胡椒、樟脑、沉香、金、锡、象牙、瓷器、丝绸、棉布
tradeGoods.en: Pepper, camphor, agarwood, gold, tin, ivory, ceramics, silk, and cotton textiles

name.en: Mixed use of precious metal and commodity payments

unitName.en: Gold ingots, silver tael-equivalents, and shell-money equivalents

householdWealth.zh: 港口商人和船主财富明显高于普通农民与渔民。一般家庭多以稻米、舟船、林产和金属器为主要财富形式。
householdWealth.en: Port merchants and shipowners were markedly wealthier than ordinary farmers and fishers. Typical households held wealth in rice, boats, forest products, and metal goods.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度属于港口王权主导的贸易中介模式，王室通过控制海峡与外商往来抽取利益。
economicSystem.en: Its economy followed a port-kingdom model of trade intermediation, with the crown extracting wealth by controlling straits and foreign merchant traffic.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由港口税收和珍货储备构成，流动性受贸易季节与海上局势影响较大。随着爪哇势力上升，其财政弹性正在下降。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of customs income and stores of high-value goods, with liquidity strongly affected by trading seasons and maritime conditions. As Javanese power rose, fiscal flexibility was declining.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策着重保持港口吸引力与海上巡防，同时依靠贡赋和王室垄断商品补足支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining attractive ports and maritime patrols while supplementing expenditure through tribute and royal control over select goods.

unit.en: Gold ingots, silver tael-equivalents, and shell-money equivalents

description.zh: 负责海峡巡逻、护航、港口防御和突袭敌方船队。
description.en: Responsible for strait patrols, convoy protection, port defense, and raids on enemy shipping.

description.zh: 驻守主要港口与仓库，维持治安并防御登陆。
description.en: Stationed in major ports and storehouses to maintain order and repel landings.

description.zh: 由附属港口和地方酋长提供，战斗力不均。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate ports and local chiefs, with uneven quality.

commanderInChief.zh: 室利佛逝王
commanderInChief.en: King of Srivijaya

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻型舰船、桨帆并用舟船、弓矛和沿岸防御工事为主。适于海峡机动作战和袭扰，但不擅长大规模陆战。
technology.en: Military technology emphasized light vessels, oared-and-sailed ships, bows, spears, and coastal fortifications. It was suited for maneuver warfare in straits and raiding, but not for major land campaigns.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爪哇政权竞争、海盗袭扰、属港脱离以及海上贸易路线转移。
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry with Javanese states, piracy, loss of subordinate ports, and shifts in maritime trade routes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口约三十万，集中于巨港及苏门答腊东南部河口地带，并分散于若干属港。社会高度海洋化，人口流动和外来商人比例较高。
populationDescription.en: Its population of roughly 300,000 was concentrated around Palembang and the estuarine zone of southeastern Sumatra, with smaller communities across subordinate ports. Society was strongly maritime, with high mobility and a notable presence of foreign merchants.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与港口贵族、僧侣、远洋商人、船主与航海者、工匠、农民渔民、依附部众与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal and port nobility, monks, overseas merchants, shipowners and sailors, artisans, farmers and fishers, dependent communities, and slaves.

allies.zh: 部分马来港口与受其影响的海峡沿岸属邦，但联盟松散且易变。
allies.en: Some Malay ports and strait-side dependencies remained aligned with it, though these alliances were loose and unstable.

enemies.zh: 爪哇竞争者、海盗集团及试图摆脱控制的属港。
enemies.en: Its enemies included Javanese rivals, pirate groups, and subordinate ports seeking independence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重心在于维持海峡主导权、保障商路并借朝贡和联姻稳住属港。衰落时期更多依赖贸易让利和象征宗主权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving control of the straits, safeguarding commerce, and stabilizing subordinate ports through tribute and marriage ties. In decline, it relied increasingly on trade concessions and symbolic suzerainty.

era.zh: 12世纪末东南亚海洋港市文明阶段
era.en: Late 12th-century Southeast Asian maritime port-polity civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 海峡航运组织、港口仓储体系、热带海贸网络经营、适应季风的远洋航行技术。
keyInnovations.en: Strait-based shipping organization, port warehousing systems, tropical maritime trade networks, and monsoon-adapted seafaring techniques.

strengths.zh: 地处马六甲与巽他海域交通枢纽，港口贸易经验深厚，宗教与商业影响力广泛。
strengths.en: It sat astride key routes near the Malacca and Sunda zones, possessed deep commercial experience, and wielded broad religious and mercantile influence.

weaknesses.zh: 内陆控制薄弱，军事以海防为主，难以压制强势竞争者，财政过度依赖转口贸易。
weaknesses.en: Its inland control was weak, its military was optimized for maritime defense, it struggled against powerful competitors, and its finances were overly dependent on entrepôt trade.

outlook.zh: 若无法重新整合属港并压制爪哇势力，三佛齐将继续分裂并失去区域霸权。
outlook.en: Unless it could reintegrate subordinate ports and contain Javanese rivals, Srivijaya would continue to fragment and lose regional hegemony.

description.zh: 三佛齐是一个以巨港为中心、凭借海峡贸易兴起但在1200年前后明显衰落的佛教海上帝国。
description.en: Srivijaya was a Buddhist maritime empire centered on Palembang that had risen through control of strait commerce but was clearly in decline around 1200.

dynasty.zh: 地方谷地诸领与佛教法王体系
dynasty.en: Valley Chieftaincies under a Buddhist Dharma Kingship Order

governmentForm.zh: 由山地谷地领主与宗教权威共同维系的分散型王国。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized mountain kingdom sustained jointly by valley lords and religious authority.

socialStructure.zh: 不丹地区社会以谷地农民、地方贵族、寺院僧侣和山地部族组成。各谷地之间交通艰难，政治认同更偏向地方共同体而非高度统一王权。宗教网络在跨谷地整合中起到关键作用。
socialStructure.en: Bhutanese society consisted of valley farmers, local nobles, monastic communities, and mountain tribes. Communication between valleys was difficult, so political identity remained more local than tied to a highly unified monarchy. Religious networks played a key role in linking different valleys.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括各谷地领主、宗教导师及与寺院结盟的世俗家族。法王名义上拥有最高精神权威，但实际统治常需依靠地方协商。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included valley lords, religious teachers, and secular families allied with monasteries. The dharma king held nominal supreme spiritual authority, but practical rule usually depended on local negotiation.

succession.zh: 继承方式并未完全制度化，往往由地方豪族、宗教势力和家族关系共同决定。王位或最高权威的传承易受地区均势影响。
succession.en: Succession was not fully institutionalized and was shaped by local noble houses, religious influence, and kinship ties. Transmission of royal or supreme authority was sensitive to the balance among regions.

structure.zh: 1200年前后的不丹尚未形成后世高度整合的中央政体，政权结构较为松散。核心权力集中于若干谷地中心和寺院网络，行政规模很小。王权更多表现为宗教合法性与调停能力。
structure.en: Around 1200, Bhutan had not yet formed the highly integrated central polity of later centuries and remained politically loose. Core power rested in a few valley centers and monastic networks, with only a small administrative apparatus. Kingship expressed itself more through religious legitimacy and mediation than through dense bureaucracy.

function.zh: 负责礼仪、调停谷地领主关系、发布宗教与世俗命令。
function.en: Handled ritual, mediation among valley lords, and issuance of religious and secular commands.

function.zh: 管理寺院供养、地方贡物和劳役征发。
function.en: Managed monastic support, local tribute goods, and labor obligations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由谷地首领、村社长老与寺院共同承担。中央只能在若干核心谷地直接施加影响，边远地区多依习惯自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by valley chiefs, village elders, and monasteries. The center exercised direct influence only in a few core valleys, while remote areas remained largely self-governing under custom.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、宗教规范和领主裁断为主。土地、婚姻和纠纷调解多在地方层级解决，严重案件才上呈更高权威。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on custom, religious norms, and the judgments of local lords. Land, marriage, and dispute mediation were generally handled locally, with only serious cases brought before higher authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物贡赋、劳役和少量牲畜征收为主，货币税很有限。寺院和领主共同分享地方剩余。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on tribute in kind, labor service, and modest livestock levies, with very limited monetary taxation. Monasteries and lords shared much of the local surplus.

religion.zh: 藏传佛教
religion.en: Tibetan Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 此时期的重要文化发展在于藏传佛教向不丹各谷地扩展，并逐步形成寺院与地方权力结合的社会结构。宗教艺术、壁画和经卷抄写开始奠定后世文化基础。
culturalAchievements.en: A major cultural development of the period was the spread of Tibetan Buddhism into Bhutanese valleys and the gradual formation of a social order linking monasteries with local power. Religious art, murals, and manuscript copying began laying the foundations for later Bhutanese culture.

languageFamily.zh: 语言以汉藏语系藏缅语族诸方言为主，书面文化受藏语影响最深。
languageFamily.en: Languages belonged mainly to Tibeto-Burman branches of the Sino-Tibetan family, with written culture most strongly shaped by Tibetan.

philosophy.zh: 以藏传佛教早期传播形态为核心，并保留浓厚山地地方信仰传统。
philosophy.en: Its worldview was centered on early forms of Tibetan Buddhism while retaining strong mountain-based local religious traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用藏文书写宗教与行政文书。
writingSystem.en: Tibetan script was used for religious and administrative writing.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 不丹经济规模很小，以高山农业、畜牧、林产和有限的跨喜马拉雅贸易为基础。人均产出不高，但在谷地共同体内部具有一定自给能力。
gdpDescription.en: Bhutan’s economy was very small, based on highland agriculture, herding, forest products, and limited trans-Himalayan trade. Per-capita output was modest, but valley communities retained a fair degree of self-sufficiency.

mainIndustries.zh: 高山农业、畜牧业、林产采集、手工织造、山道贸易
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, pastoralism, forest extraction, textile handicrafts, and mountain-route trade

tradeGoods.zh: 牦牛、羊毛、食盐、药材、皮革、谷物、织物
tradeGoods.en: Yaks, wool, salt, medicinal herbs, leather, grain, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地、牲畜、粮食储备和织物。贫富差距存在，但整体社会财富积累有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was held mainly in land, livestock, grain stores, and textiles. Inequality existed, but overall accumulation of wealth remained limited.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度以村社自给、领主贡赋和寺院再分配为主，市场交换仅在少数山道节点活跃。
economicSystem.en: The economy relied on village self-sufficiency, lordly tribute, and monastic redistribution, with market exchange active only at a few mountain-route nodes.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备多为粮食、牲畜、布匹和少量贵金属，真正可流通的金银有限。小规模盈余有助于维持稳定，但难以支持长期大战。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were held mostly in grain, livestock, cloth, and a small quantity of precious metal, with limited liquid silver or gold. Modest surpluses supported stability but could not sustain prolonged warfare.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调低度征收、维持谷地忠诚和保障寺院供养，避免过度盘剥引发山地离散。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized light extraction, maintenance of valley loyalty, and support for monasteries, avoiding heavy burdens that could trigger fragmentation in the highlands.

description.zh: 主要由农民与地方随从组成，适合山地防守和短程集结。
description.en: Composed mainly of farmers and local retainers, suited to mountain defense and short-range mobilization.

description.zh: 擅长险隘伏击、守路和谷口防御。
description.en: Skilled in ambushes at passes, route defense, and guarding valley mouths.

description.zh: 数量不多，但装备和忠诚度较高，是各地首领的核心武装。
description.en: Few in number but better equipped and more loyal, they formed the core armed followers of local chiefs.

commanderInChief.zh: 法王与谷地领主联席
commanderInChief.en: The Dharma King in concert with valley lords

technology.zh: 军事技术较为朴素，以弓箭、长矛、短刀、木盾和简易山地工事为主。虽缺乏重型攻城装备，但善于利用高地地形作战。
technology.en: Military technology was relatively simple, centered on bows, spears, short blades, wooden shields, and basic mountain fortifications. It lacked heavy siege equipment but made effective use of highland terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为邻近山地势力冲突、谷地间争斗、道路封锁及来自西藏方向的宗教与政治影响竞争。
threats.en: Major threats included conflict with neighboring highland groups, inter-valley rivalries, blocked routes, and competing political or religious influence from Tibet.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约五万，分散于若干高山谷地与山坡聚落。人口密度低，农业生产受海拔、气候和交通条件强烈制约。
populationDescription.en: The total population was around 50,000, scattered across a number of high mountain valleys and hillside settlements. Population density was low, and agricultural production was strongly constrained by altitude, climate, and transport conditions.

socialClasses.zh: 宗教领袖与领主家族、寺院僧侣、自由农户、牧民、工匠、依附人口。
socialClasses.en: Religious leaders and noble families, monastic clergy, free peasant households, herders, artisans, and dependent populations.

allies.zh: 与部分藏地宗教网络和相邻谷地领主保持松散合作关系。
allies.en: It maintained loose cooperative ties with some Tibetan religious networks and neighboring valley lords.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者并非固定国家，而是周边山地竞争集团与内部不服从的地方势力。
enemies.en: Its main adversaries were not fixed states so much as rival highland groups and disobedient local powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护山道通行、平衡西藏影响并减少外部征服风险为主。整体倾向守势和地方协商。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to keep mountain routes open, balance Tibetan influence, and reduce the risk of outside conquest. It was broadly defensive and negotiated in character.

era.zh: 13世纪初喜马拉雅山地佛教社会形成期
era.en: Early 13th-century Himalayan Buddhist highland formative phase

keyInnovations.zh: 高山梯田耕作、牦牛畜养、山地道路组织、寺院文书与抄经传统。
keyInnovations.en: Highland terraced farming, yak herding, mountain-route organization, and monastic record-keeping and manuscript traditions.

strengths.zh: 山地地形易守难攻，地方共同体稳固，宗教网络有助于维持社会秩序。
strengths.en: Its mountain terrain was defensible, local communities were resilient, and religious networks helped preserve social order.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、财政薄弱、中央整合有限，难以进行长期远征或大规模建设。
weaknesses.en: It had a sparse population, weak finances, and limited central integration, making sustained campaigns or large public works difficult.

outlook.zh: 若宗教权威与谷地领主能持续合作，不丹可维持稳定；但要形成强国家仍需数代整合。
outlook.en: If religious authority and valley lords continued to cooperate, Bhutan could remain stable; however, the emergence of a strong state would require generations of further consolidation.

description.zh: 1200年前后的不丹是一个人口稀少、以谷地共同体和藏传佛教网络维系的喜马拉雅山地王国。
description.en: Around 1200, Bhutan was a sparsely populated Himalayan mountain kingdom held together by valley communities and Tibetan Buddhist networks.

dynasty.zh: 伏尔加保加尔诸汗统治时期
dynasty.en: Period of the Volga Bulgar Khans

governmentForm.zh: 以伊斯兰化君主为核心的商贸型草原—河运王国，汗廷联合城市精英与地方贵族治理国家。
governmentForm.en: A commercial riverine-steppe kingdom centered on an Islamized monarch, with the khanate governed through cooperation between the court, urban elites, and local nobles.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由汗室、军事贵族、伊斯兰学者与法官、城市商人手工业者，以及农村农民与牧民构成。博尔加尔等城市拥有较强的商人阶层，受伏尔加贸易与手工业推动。边缘地区仍保留部族性组织，但总体上已形成较稳定的定居国家社会。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the khan’s household, military nobles, Islamic scholars and judges, urban merchants and artisans, and rural farmers and pastoralists. Cities such as Bolgar supported a strong merchant class driven by Volga trade and manufacturing. Peripheral districts retained tribal organization, but the realm as a whole had developed into a relatively stable settled state.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由汗族宗室、突厥—保加尔贵族以及伊斯兰教法学者组成。城市中的富商与税务承包者也在政治中具有重要影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up of the khan’s dynasty, Turkic-Bulgar nobles, and Islamic jurists. Wealthy merchants and tax farmers in the cities also exercised significant political influence.

succession.zh: 汗位通常在王族内部继承，但实际常受贵族支持与宫廷协商影响。若继承不明，地方强人和宗室支系可能介入争位。
succession.en: The khanship normally passed within the royal family, though in practice succession depended heavily on noble backing and court negotiation. In unclear cases, regional strongmen and collateral princely lines could intervene.

structure.zh: 中央以汗廷为核心，兼具草原汗国传统与伊斯兰城市行政特色。博尔加尔等大城设有税务、司法和市场管理官员，地方则由贵族、城长与部族首领协同治理。国家对贸易干线与渡口的控制尤为重要。
structure.en: The central government revolved around the khan’s court, combining steppe khanate traditions with Islamic urban administration. Major cities such as Bolgar had officials for taxation, justice, and market regulation, while the countryside was managed jointly by nobles, town heads, and tribal leaders. State control over trade arteries and river crossings was especially important.

function.zh: 处理诏令、朝贡、使节接待与王室财产管理。
function.en: Handled decrees, tribute, reception of envoys, and management of royal estates.

function.zh: 征收关税、市税、贡赋，并监督河运贸易与市场度量衡。
function.en: Collected customs, market taxes, and tribute, and supervised river trade and market standards.

function.zh: 依据伊斯兰法与地方惯例裁决民事、商业和部分刑事案件。
function.en: Adjudicated civil, commercial, and some criminal cases under Islamic law and local custom.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城市长官、税务官和附庸贵族为主。边远地区常通过部族首领间接统治，只要其按期纳贡并维持交通安全。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on town governors, tax officers, and subordinate nobles. Outlying districts were often ruled indirectly through tribal chiefs so long as they paid tribute and kept routes secure.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合伊斯兰教法、汗廷敕令与草原习惯法。商业纠纷多由卡迪审理，而部族与边地事务仍常按惯例裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system blended Islamic law, khanal decrees, and steppe customary law. Commercial disputes were commonly heard by qadis, while tribal and frontier matters often continued to be decided by custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖商税、关税、土地贡赋和附庸贡纳。城市贸易繁荣使过境征税成为国家收入的重要来源。
taxationSystem.en: State finance relied mainly on trade taxes, customs, land tribute, and payments from dependents. The prosperity of urban commerce made transit duties a major source of income.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 伏尔加保加尔以伊斯兰学术、清真寺建筑、金属工艺和陶器著称。其城市是北方欧亚商路上的文化中介，连接伊斯兰世界、罗斯与草原诸地。
culturalAchievements.en: Volga Bulgaria was noted for Islamic scholarship, mosque architecture, metalworking, and ceramics. Its cities served as cultural intermediaries on the northern Eurasian trade routes linking the Islamic world, Rus', and the steppe.

languageFamily.zh: 突厥语系，兼用阿拉伯语作为宗教与学术语言。
languageFamily.en: Turkic language family, with Arabic also used as a religious and scholarly language.

philosophy.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰信仰为主，并吸收突厥草原传统与商旅城市文化。
philosophy.en: Predominantly Sunni Islamic in outlook, while also incorporating Turkic steppe traditions and mercantile urban culture.

writingSystem.zh: 以阿拉伯字母书写的保加尔突厥语和阿拉伯语文书并用。
writingSystem.en: Bulgar Turkic written in Arabic script was used alongside Arabic for learned and official texts.

gdpDescription.zh: 其经济在东欧北部属较发达水平，依赖河运贸易、城市手工业和周边农业。博尔加尔与伏尔加水道的区位使其人均产出高于多数纯农业邻国。
gdpDescription.en: Its economy ranked among the more developed in northern Eastern Europe, relying on river commerce, urban crafts, and surrounding agriculture. The position of Bolgar and the Volga waterway gave it output per head above many purely agrarian neighbors.

mainIndustries.zh: 河运贸易、金属加工、皮革、制陶、养蜂、农业、畜牧
mainIndustries.en: River trade, metalworking, leatherworking, pottery, beekeeping, agriculture, and pastoralism

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、蜂蜡、皮革、金属器、谷物、马匹、奴隶、东方输入奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, beeswax, leather, metal goods, grain, horses, slaves, and imported eastern luxuries

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人和工匠家庭财富明显高于乡村农户，边地牧民财富多以牲畜形式保存。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchant and artisan households were markedly wealthier than rural peasant homes, while frontier pastoralists held much of their wealth in livestock.

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换与贡赋并存的商贸王国经济，国家重点控制交通线、关税与城市市场。
economicSystem.en: A mercantile kingdom economy combining market exchange with tribute, with the state focusing on routes, customs, and urban markets.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库因贸易税稳定而较为充实，但其安全高度依赖伏尔加商路畅通。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was relatively healthy thanks to stable trade taxation, though its security depended heavily on uninterrupted Volga commerce.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持低度直接征敛、强化关税与市场征收，并以盈余储备应对边患和商路波动。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored moderate direct exaction, strong customs and market collection, and maintenance of reserves against frontier threats and trade fluctuations.

description.zh: 以贵族扈从和草原骑射兵为核心，机动性强，适于边境巡防与野战。
description.en: Centered on noble retainers and steppe mounted archers, highly mobile and suited for frontier patrols and field warfare.

description.zh: 由城市民兵、守城部队和持盾矛兵组成，负责城防与渡口据守。
description.en: Composed of town militia, garrison troops, and spear-and-shield infantry for defense of cities and river crossings.

description.zh: 由地方部族和附庸首领提供，战斗力不均，但可迅速补充兵力。
description.en: Provided by local tribes and dependent chiefs; uneven in quality but useful for rapidly augmenting manpower.

commanderInChief.zh: 伏尔加保加尔汗
commanderInChief.en: Khan of Volga Bulgaria

technology.zh: 军备兼具草原复合弓骑兵传统与伊斯兰世界影响下的城防、冶铁和铠甲制造技术。
technology.en: Military technology combined steppe composite-bow cavalry traditions with fortification, ironworking, and armor production influenced by the Islamic world.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自草原游牧势力、罗斯诸公国的竞争，以及商路受阻导致的财政与安全危机。
threats.en: Main threats came from steppe nomadic powers, rivalry with Rus' principalities, and fiscal-security crises caused by disruption of trade routes.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口以伏尔加河中游定居农业与城市居民为主，兼有突厥化部族和商旅移民。伊斯兰化在城市最深，乡间则保留更多地方习俗。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the settled agricultural and urban communities of the middle Volga, alongside Turkic tribal groups and merchant migrants. Islamization was strongest in the cities, while the countryside retained more local custom.

socialClasses.zh: 汗室与贵族、乌里玛与卡迪、商人与工匠、农民与牧民、奴隶与依附民
socialClasses.en: Royal household and nobles, ulama and qadis, merchants and artisans, peasants and pastoralists, slaves and dependents

allies.zh: 通常通过贸易与婚盟维持与部分草原部族及穆斯林商旅网络的友好关系。
allies.en: It generally maintained friendly ties through trade and marriage with certain steppe tribes and wider Muslim merchant networks.

enemies.zh: 与部分罗斯诸公国、敌对草原集团及争夺伏尔加通道的势力存在紧张关系。
enemies.en: It faced tensions with some Rus' principalities, hostile steppe confederations, and powers competing for control of the Volga corridor.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保障商路、平衡罗斯与草原势力、并维持伊斯兰世界商贸联系为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing trade routes, balancing Rus' and steppe powers, and maintaining commercial links with the Islamic world.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期欧亚河贸文明

keyInnovations.zh: 铸铁与金属工艺、窑业、城防建设、河运商贸组织、阿拉伯文书与伊斯兰法制实践
keyInnovations.en: Iron casting and metal crafts, kiln industries, fortification building, organized river commerce, and Arabic documentary practice with Islamic legal institutions

strengths.zh: 商路位置优越、城市经济发达、财政依托关税稳定、兼具草原机动兵力与定居城防体系。
strengths.en: Excellent trade position, developed urban economy, customs-based fiscal stability, and a mix of mobile steppe forces with settled urban defenses.

weaknesses.zh: 对贸易通道依赖过深，边境开阔，地方部族整合有限，面对大规模草原入侵时防御纵深不足。
weaknesses.en: Overdependent on trade corridors, exposed frontiers, only partially integrated tribal regions, and limited defensive depth against major steppe invasions.

outlook.zh: 若伏尔加商路持续畅通，国家仍可维持繁荣；但一旦遭遇东来强敌，其富庶城市反而容易成为打击目标。
outlook.en: If Volga commerce remained open, the state could continue to prosper; yet in the face of a major eastern conqueror, its wealthy cities would become prime targets.

description.zh: 伏尔加保加尔是1200年前后连接伊斯兰世界、罗斯与欧亚草原的重要穆斯林商贸王国。
description.en: Around 1200, Volga Bulgaria was a key Muslim commercial kingdom linking the Islamic world, Rus', and the Eurasian steppe.

name.zh: 弗拉基米尔-苏兹达尔公国
弗拉基米尔-苏兹达尔公国

dynasty.zh: 留里克王朝弗拉基米尔—苏兹达尔支系

governmentForm.zh: 由留里克王公统治的东正教公国，君主权力较强，并通过家臣、教会与地方城镇治理广阔领地。
governmentForm.en: An Eastern Orthodox principality ruled by a Rurikid prince, with relatively strong princely authority exercised through retainers, the Church, and local towns over a broad territory.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由大公及其家族、波雅尔贵族、德鲁日纳武士集团、东正教神职人员、城市居民和广大农民组成。北东方向的新开垦地区吸引了移民，推动聚落扩张。虽然城市自治传统存在，但大公在本地的控制力强于许多其他罗斯公国。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the grand prince and his family, boyar nobles, the druzhina warrior retinue, Orthodox clergy, townspeople, and a broad peasant base. Newly cleared lands in the northeast attracted migrants and encouraged settlement growth. Though urban traditions of self-government existed, princely control here was stronger than in many other Rus' principalities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括大公宗室、波雅尔、军事家臣与高级主教。大公通过授地、军役关系和教会支持巩固权威。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the princely dynasty, boyars, military retainers, and senior bishops. The prince reinforced authority through land grants, service ties, and church backing.

succession.zh: 继承原则名义上遵循留里克王朝的家族秩序，但实际上常由实力、资历与贵族支持共同决定。争位和宗支冲突是罗斯政治的常态。
succession.en: Succession nominally followed the wider Rurikid dynastic order, but in practice was shaped by power, seniority, and noble support. Contested claims and branch rivalries were normal features of Rus' politics.

structure.zh: 大公政权以弗拉基米尔宫廷为中枢，依赖王公家臣、驻城官员与教会网络推行政令。各主要城镇设有公国代表、驻军和税吏，但地方贵族与城市共同体仍保有一定影响。与基辅旧中心相比，其行政更偏向北方新兴王公集权。
structure.en: The princely regime was centered on the court at Vladimir and relied on princely retainers, town officials, and church networks to enforce authority. Major towns held princely representatives, garrisons, and tax collectors, though local nobles and urban communities retained influence. Compared with the old Kievan core, its administration leaned more toward emergent northern princely centralization.

function.zh: 负责诏令、外交、王室领地和家臣调度。
function.en: Managed decrees, diplomacy, princely estates, and the assignment of retainers.

function.zh: 征收地租、商税、过路税，并监督仓储与运输。
function.en: Collected rents, trade duties, and road tolls, and oversaw storage and transport.

function.zh: 组织德鲁日纳、城镇民兵和边境防线调度。
function.en: Organized the druzhina, town militias, and deployment along frontier defenses.

function.zh: 协调主教区、教会财产与依据《罗斯法典》处理司法事务。
function.en: Coordinated dioceses, church property, and judicial affairs under the Rus' legal tradition.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由王公任命的城镇长官、军事驻守者和税吏管理，并与波雅尔和地方共同体协商。森林边缘与新垦区的控制较依赖军事殖民和教会建制。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were managed by princely town governors, military commanders, and tax officials working in negotiation with boyars and local communities. Control over forest frontiers and newly settled districts depended more heavily on military colonization and ecclesiastical institutions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以《罗斯法典》传统、王公敕令和教会法并行。血亲赔偿、财产纠纷和商业案件有较成熟的处理习惯。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on the Russkaya Pravda tradition, princely decrees, and canon law operating side by side. Compensation for injury, property disputes, and commercial cases followed comparatively well-established procedures.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地与村社贡赋、贸易税和道路关卡收入为主，并辅以劳役与军役义务。王公直辖领地是财政的重要基础。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system was based on land and communal tribute, trade duties, and checkpoint revenues, supplemented by labor and military obligations. The prince’s own domains formed a major fiscal base.

religion.zh: 东正教
religion.en: Eastern Orthodoxy

culturalAchievements.zh: 该公国以白石教堂建筑、圣像艺术和编年史传统闻名。弗拉基米尔与苏兹达尔地区在12世纪末已成为罗斯东北部新的宗教与文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The principality was renowned for white-stone church architecture, icon art, and chronicle writing. By the late twelfth century, the Vladimir-Suzdal land had become a new religious and cultural center of northeastern Rus'.

languageFamily.zh: 东斯拉夫语支，宗教与文书高度受教会斯拉夫语影响。
languageFamily.en: East Slavic branch, with strong ecclesiastical and literary influence from Church Slavonic.

philosophy.zh: 以东正教基督教世界观为核心，强调君权、救赎、圣像崇敬与修道传统。
philosophy.en: Centered on an Eastern Orthodox Christian worldview that emphasized princely authority, salvation, icon veneration, and monastic tradition.

writingSystem.zh: 使用教会斯拉夫文和古东斯拉夫文，以西里尔文字书写。
writingSystem.en: Used Church Slavonic and Old East Slavic written in the Cyrillic script.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础较为均衡，由农业拓殖、林产、手工业与区域贸易共同支撑。东北罗斯的新开发土地提高了粮食与人口承载力，使其具备持续增长潜力。
gdpDescription.en: Its economic base was relatively balanced, supported by agricultural colonization, forest products, crafts, and regional trade. Newly developed lands in northeastern Rus' increased food and demographic capacity, giving it significant growth potential.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、林业、狩猎采皮、蜂蜡、铁器制造、木工、建筑、区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, forestry, fur hunting, beeswax production, ironworking, woodworking, construction, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、谷物、木材、铁器、皮革、手工制品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, beeswax, honey, grain, timber, iron goods, leather, and manufactured wares

name.zh: 银格里夫纳与毛皮货币并用

householdWealth.zh: 波雅尔和商人家庭拥有较多土地、仓储和役使资源，而普通农户财富主要体现在土地、牲畜与工具。
householdWealth.en: Boyar and merchant households held more land, storage, and dependent labor, while ordinary peasant wealth lay mainly in land, livestock, and tools.

unit.en: silver grivna equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王公领地、村社贡赋和城市市场并存的封建—商业混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A feudal-commercial mixed economy combining princely domains, communal tribute, and urban markets.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况良好，得益于人口增长、土地扩张和较强的大公权力，但大规模内战会迅速耗散储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was in good condition thanks to population growth, territorial expansion, and relatively strong princely authority, though major dynastic wars could rapidly consume reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在巩固王公直辖收入、确保边防军役供给，并通过教会与建筑工程展示合法性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on strengthening direct princely income, sustaining frontier military service, and displaying legitimacy through church and building projects.

description.zh: 由大公及波雅尔家臣组成的精锐骑兵，是公国野战的核心力量。
description.en: Elite cavalry made up of the prince’s and boyars’ retainers, forming the core of field operations.

description.zh: 由城镇居民和地方征召兵组成，擅长守城、持矛作战与辅助工程。
description.en: Drawn from townsmen and local levies, they were suited to garrison duty, spear combat, and engineering support.

description.zh: 来自新垦区和边防聚落，机动性中等，主要承担警戒和区域防御任务。
description.en: Raised from new settlements and frontier communities, they had moderate mobility and served mainly in surveillance and local defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 弗谢沃洛德三世大公
commanderInChief.en: Grand Prince Vsevolod III

technology.zh: 军备以锁子甲、长矛、剑、斧、弓和木石城防工事为主，兼具罗斯重装骑兵与步兵守城传统。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on mail armor, spears, swords, axes, bows, and timber-earthwork fortifications, combining Rus' heavy cavalry with strong infantry defense traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括罗斯内部王公竞争、伏尔加贸易方向的冲突，以及来自草原游牧势力的袭扰。
threats.en: Main threats included rival Rus' princes, conflict along Volga trade directions, and raids by steppe nomadic forces.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

populationDescription.zh: 人口以东斯拉夫农民为主体，并吸纳来自南方罗斯地区的移民。东北森林带的殖垦使聚落分布更分散，但核心城镇快速成长。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly East Slavic peasant, supplemented by migrants from the southern Rus' lands. Colonization of the northeastern forest zone produced dispersed settlement patterns, but core towns grew quickly.

socialClasses.zh: 大公与宗室、波雅尔、德鲁日纳武士、神职人员、城镇居民、农民、依附人口
socialClasses.en: Grand prince and dynasty, boyars, druzhina warriors, clergy, townspeople, peasants, dependent populations

allies.zh: 与部分罗斯支系王公和东正教教会保持合作，并通过婚姻与条约结成暂时同盟。
allies.en: It cooperated with certain other Rus' princely branches and the Orthodox Church, and formed temporary alliances through marriage and treaty.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是敌对罗斯公国、边境草原部族，以及在商路与宗主地位上竞争的势力。
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were hostile Rus' principalities, frontier steppe tribes, and powers contesting trade routes and senior authority.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策旨在扩大大公在罗斯世界中的威望，控制东北商路，并巩固新开发地区。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to expand the grand prince’s prestige within the Rus' world, control northeastern trade routes, and consolidate newly developed territories.

era.zh: 东欧中世纪盛期王公国家
era.en: High medieval East European princely state

keyInnovations.zh: 白石建筑、木城防体系、铁器生产、河运网络、编年史和教会书写文化
keyInnovations.en: White-stone architecture, timber fortification systems, iron production, river transport networks, and chronicle and ecclesiastical writing culture

strengths.zh: 人口增长快、土地开发空间大、大公权力较强、军事动员能力优于多数罗斯邻邦。
strengths.en: Rapid population growth, large scope for land development, comparatively strong princely authority, and better mobilization capacity than many neighboring Rus' polities.

weaknesses.zh: 王朝内斗风险始终存在，城市与贵族仍可能掣肘中央，边境漫长且分散。
weaknesses.en: Dynastic infighting remained a constant risk, towns and nobles could still constrain the center, and the frontiers were long and diffuse.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后公国处于上升期，但若遭遇超区域征服力量，其分散城镇与家族政治将暴露脆弱性。
outlook.en: Around 1200 the principality was on the rise, but in the face of a transregional conquering power its dispersed towns and dynastic politics would reveal serious vulnerabilities.

description.zh: 弗拉基米尔—苏兹达尔公国是1200年前后东北罗斯最强盛、最具上升势头的东正教王公国家之一。
description.en: Around 1200, the Principality of Vladimir-Suzdal was one of the strongest and most ascendant Orthodox princely states of northeastern Rus'.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，诸部族长老共治
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; governed by various tribal elders

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族、年龄等级和部族长老会议为核心的分散型草原—林缘部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized savanna-forest-edge tribal society centered on kinship, age grades, and councils of elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族和家族群体为基础，长老、猎手首领和祭司拥有较高威望。多数人口从事采集、狩猎、小规模耕作和牧养。政治权威通常局限于聚落与邻近活动范围，跨部落协调多依赖婚盟与仪式。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans and extended family groups, with elders, hunting leaders, and ritual specialists holding high prestige. Most people engaged in gathering, hunting, small-scale cultivation, and herding. Political authority was usually limited to settlements and nearby territories, while intertribal coordination relied on marriage ties and ritual.

rulingClass.zh: 并无严格意义上的世袭贵族，威望最高者通常是长老、战首和掌握祭祀权威的人。财富差距有限，但拥有牲畜、铁器和婚姻网络者更具影响力。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary nobility; the most prestigious figures were usually elders, war leaders, and those with ritual authority. Wealth differences were limited, but those controlling livestock, iron tools, and marriage networks wielded greater influence.

succession.zh: 职位多通过年龄资历、家族声望和群体认可继承或选举产生。战时首领可能因个人能力而临时上升。
succession.en: Offices were commonly inherited or selected through seniority, family standing, and group consent. In wartime, temporary leaders could rise through personal ability.

structure.zh: 政治组织松散，以聚落议事会和部族长老会议为主要决策机制。没有成熟的常设官僚机构，只有少数负责调解纠纷、组织迁徙和安排祭仪的人。较大规模协作通常只在旱灾、战争或集体狩猎时出现。
structure.en: Political organization was loose, with settlement councils and tribal elder assemblies serving as the main decision-making bodies. There was no developed standing bureaucracy, only a few persons responsible for dispute mediation, migration planning, and ritual coordination. Larger-scale cooperation usually emerged only during drought, war, or collective hunts.

function.zh: 处理部族决策、婚盟、资源分配与纠纷调解。
function.en: Handled tribal decisions, marriage alliances, resource allocation, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、雨祭、成年礼，并在外敌来袭时组织战士。
function.en: Conducted rituals, rain ceremonies, and initiation rites, and organized warriors in times of attack.

localAdmin.zh: 每个聚落通常由长者和家族首领共同管理，事务以口头协商解决。不存在固定都城，季节性迁移会改变聚落中心。
localAdmin.en: Each settlement was usually governed collectively by elders and lineage heads, with affairs settled through oral discussion. There was no fixed capital, and seasonal movement could shift settlement centers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，通过赔偿、婚姻交换、长老裁断与神圣誓言维持秩序。严重冲突可能导致分支迁徙而非长期监禁。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of customary practice, maintaining order through compensation, marriage exchange, elder judgment, and sacred oaths. Serious disputes might result in fission and migration rather than long-term imprisonment.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有规范化税制，公共事务依靠份额贡献、猎获分配、牲畜奉献和劳作义务。战时或祭典时会临时集中物资。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formalized tax system; common affairs were supported by share contributions, distribution of game, livestock offerings, and labor obligations. Goods were pooled temporarily for war or major rituals.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就体现在口头史诗、节律音乐、面具舞蹈、木器与编织工艺，以及对当地生态知识的积累。年龄等级仪式和季节祭祀维系了群体认同。
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievements lay in oral epics, rhythmic music, masked dance, woodcraft and weaving, and accumulated ecological knowledge of the local environment. Age-grade rites and seasonal ceremonies sustained communal identity.

languageFamily.zh: 多种当地语言并存，可能属于尼日尔-刚果语系及邻近语群。
