description.en: Stationed in Zeila and nearby posts, they protected the port, warehouses, and customs facilities.

commanderInChief.zh: 瓦拉什马苏丹
commanderInChief.en: The Walashma Sultan

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、刀剑、弓箭、皮盾和轻甲为主，适合沿海与半干旱地带机动作战。由于规模有限，其军力更偏向护商、镇压和边境防御，而非大规模会战。
technology.en: Armament centered on spears, swords, bows, leather shields, and light armor, suitable for mobile warfare in coastal and semi-arid zones. Because of its modest scale, its military was better adapted to trade protection, suppression, and frontier defense than to major pitched battles.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内陆基督教高原政权、地方酋长离心、商路中断以及港口竞争。
threats.en: Major threats came from inland Christian highland powers, centrifugal local chiefs, disruption of trade routes, and commercial rivalry at the ports.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布在泽拉港、沿商路城镇以及内陆牧农混合区。港口虽然人口不算巨大，却在财政和文化上远比其规模更重要。
populationDescription.en: Population was distributed across Zeila, trade-route towns, and inland agro-pastoral zones. The port was not enormous in population, but its fiscal and cultural importance far exceeded its size.

socialClasses.zh: 苏丹家族与贵族、法学者与阿訇、商人、酋长与武装随从、牧民农民、奴隶
socialClasses.en: Sultan’s family and nobles, jurists and clerics, merchants, chiefs and armed retainers, pastoralists and cultivators, slaves

allies.zh: 与阿拉伯商人网络、部分沿海穆斯林社群和若干内陆穆斯林酋长保持合作。
allies.en: It cooperated with Arab merchant networks, some coastal Muslim communities, and a number of inland Muslim chiefs.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是高原基督教势力、商路竞争者及不稳定的内陆反对酋长。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were highland Christian powers, trade-route rivals, and unstable dissenting chiefs in the interior.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保护泽拉港、扩大内陆影响并将自身塑造成红海穆斯林贸易体系的重要门户为目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to protect Zeila, extend influence inland, and present the sultanate as an important gateway within the Muslim Red Sea trading system.

era.zh: 中世纪非洲之角港口伊斯兰国家阶段
era.en: Medieval Horn of Africa Islamic port-state phase

keyInnovations.zh: 红海航运对接、港口税关管理、阿拉伯文贸易记录、商队补给组织和清真寺法学教育。
keyInnovations.en: Red Sea shipping integration, port customs administration, Arabic commercial record-keeping, caravan provisioning, and mosque-based legal education.

strengths.zh: 地处红海贸易门户、财政依赖港口税收、文化和宗教联系广泛。
strengths.en: It occupied a gateway position in Red Sea trade, drew revenue from port customs, and possessed wide cultural and religious connections.

weaknesses.zh: 内陆控制力有限，兵力规模较小，政治稳定性容易受贸易波动与地方首领影响。
weaknesses.en: Its control of the interior was limited, its forces were relatively small, and political stability was vulnerable to trade fluctuations and local powerholders.

outlook.zh: 若港口繁荣与王朝联盟得以维持，伊法特可继续扩展区域影响；若海陆商道受阻，则财政和军力都会迅速削弱。
outlook.en: If port prosperity and dynastic alliances held, Ifat could continue expanding its regional influence; if sea and land trade were disrupted, its finances and military capacity would weaken quickly.

description.zh: 伊法特苏丹国是1200年前后依托泽拉港和内陆商道而兴起的非洲之角穆斯林贸易型国家。
description.en: Around 1200, the Sultanate of Ifat was a Muslim commercial state in the Horn of Africa built around Zeila and the inland caravan routes.

governmentForm.zh: 这是一个由若干亲族群体和季节性猎队首领主导、缺乏常设国家机器的北极部族社会。
governmentForm.en: This was an Arctic tribal society led by kin groups and seasonal hunting leaders, without a permanent state apparatus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以扩大家庭、地方营地和季节性迁徙群体为基础。威望通常来自狩猎能力、慷慨分配、航海技术和对环境知识的掌握，而非固定世袭贵族。不同聚落之间保持婚姻、交换和工具传播联系。
socialStructure.en: Social organization rested on extended families, local camps, and seasonally mobile groups. Prestige usually derived from hunting skill, generosity in distribution, seafaring ability, and environmental knowledge rather than fixed hereditary nobility. Different settlements were connected through marriage, exchange, and the circulation of tools.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在真正意义上的统治阶级，只有在特定季节或行动中拥有号召力的猎队领袖、长者和萨满。其权威更多基于共识与能力，而非强制征服。
rulingClass.en: There was no true ruling class, only hunting leaders, elders, and shamans who held influence in specific seasons or undertakings. Their authority rested more on consensus and skill than on coercive domination.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常不按严格世袭继承，而是由经验、亲缘支持和个人能力决定。某些家系可能长期拥有声望，但无法形成稳定王位制度。
succession.en: Leadership was not transmitted through strict hereditary succession, but depended on experience, kin backing, and personal ability. Certain families might retain prestige over time, but no stable throne-like institution existed.

structure.zh: 图勒社会没有正式中央政府，治理主要通过营地协商、长者意见和猎队行动协调实现。季节性聚集时会形成更大的合作单位，用于捕鲸、建造和交换。
structure.en: Thule society lacked a formal central government, and governance operated through camp deliberation, elder advice, and hunting-party coordination. During seasonal aggregations, larger cooperative units formed for whaling, construction, and exchange.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、资源分配、婚姻安排和内部纠纷。
function.en: Coordinated movement, resource sharing, marriage arrangements, and internal disputes.

function.zh: 组织海兽狩猎、防御和工具劳作合作。
function.en: Organized sea-mammal hunts, defense, and cooperative tool and labor tasks.

localAdmin.zh: 地方并无官僚行政，而是由各聚落和营地自行协商事务。较大的冬季聚落中，长者与成功猎手在资源分配上更有发言权。
localAdmin.en: There was no bureaucratic local administration; each settlement and camp managed its own affairs through discussion. In larger winter settlements, elders and successful hunters had greater influence over distribution.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要体现为习惯法、禁忌、调解和集体压力。严重冲突可通过迁离、赔偿、仪式和偶发的暴力报复解决。
legalSystem.en: Law existed mainly as custom, taboo, mediation, and collective pressure. Serious conflicts could be resolved through separation, compensation, ritual means, and occasional retaliatory violence.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正式税制，资源通过共享、互惠义务和首领主持的再分配流动。大型猎获如鲸和海象尤其需要集体分配。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation system; resources moved through sharing, reciprocal obligation, and redistribution overseen by leaders. Large catches such as whales and walrus especially required collective allocation.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 图勒文化在北极环境适应方面成就卓著，发展出高效的鲸猎、雪屋与半地下住宅、狗拉雪橇和皮艇技术。其物质文化和迁徙能力奠定了后世因纽特社会的基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Thule culture achieved remarkable adaptation to the Arctic environment, developing efficient whaling, snow houses and semi-subterranean dwellings, dog sled transport, and kayak technology. Its material culture and mobility laid the foundation for later Inuit societies.

languageFamily.zh: 爱斯基摩-阿留申语系
languageFamily.en: Eskimo-Aleut language family

philosophy.zh: 世界观以北极萨满信仰、人与动物灵性的关系以及对自然节律的敬畏为核心。
philosophy.en: Its worldview centered on Arctic shamanism, the spiritual relationship between humans and animals, and reverence for natural cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，以口述传统、歌谣、记忆技艺和器物装饰传承知识。
writingSystem.en: There was no developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, memory techniques, and material decoration.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 图勒经济并非市场导向，而是以自给性狩猎、季节储藏和小规模交换为主。若按银等值折算，其总量不高，但在极地环境中具有很高的生存效率。
gdpDescription.en: The Thule economy was not market-oriented, but based on subsistence hunting, seasonal storage, and small-scale exchange. In silver-equivalent terms its total output was low, but in the polar environment it was highly efficient for survival.

mainIndustries.zh: 海兽狩猎、捕鱼、驯养与使用雪橇犬、骨角象牙加工、皮革制作
mainIndustries.en: Sea-mammal hunting, fishing, sled-dog use, bone-antler-ivory working, and skin processing

tradeGoods.zh: 海豹皮、海象牙、鲸脂、骨角器、石料、漂木与少量装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Seal skins, walrus ivory, whale oil, bone and antler tools, stone, driftwood, and small ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为猎具、犬群、皮衣、食物储备、船只和与他群体的互助关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly embodied in hunting gear, dog teams, skin clothing, food stores, boats, and reciprocal ties with other groups.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以亲族共享、季节性合作劳动和有限物物交换为核心的生计经济。
economicSystem.en: This was a subsistence economy centered on kin-based sharing, seasonal cooperative labor, and limited barter exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent
taels of silver per year equivalent

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“储备”主要不是金属财富，而是食物储藏、备用工具、皮革和犬只等可用于渡过灾年的资源。
treasuryDescription.en: What counted as a 'treasury' was not metallic wealth but stores of food, spare tools, skins, and dog teams that could help survive bad years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 不存在国家财政政策，集体资源分配的核心目标是减少饥荒风险并确保狩猎合作持续。
fiscalPolicy.en: There was no state fiscal policy; the central aim of collective resource allocation was to reduce famine risk and sustain hunting cooperation.

description.zh: 平时从事捕猎，冲突时使用猎具和近战武器进行防御或袭击。
description.en: Normally engaged in hunting, they used hunting gear and close-combat weapons for defense or raiding when needed.

description.zh: 在聚落遭遇外来威胁时临时集结，多由成年男性组成。
description.en: Temporarily mustered when settlements faced outside threats, mostly drawn from adult men.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定总司令，由资深猎手临时统领
commanderInChief.en: No fixed commander-in-chief; senior hunters led temporarily

technology.zh: 技术重点不在战争而在生存，武器包括骨角或石刃矛、弓箭、鱼叉和小刀。其真正优势在于雪地与海冰机动、犬拉雪橇、皮艇和对极地环境的精确认识。
technology.en: Technology was oriented more toward survival than warfare, with weapons including bone-, antler-, or stone-tipped spears, bows, harpoons, and knives. Their real advantage lay in mobility over snow and sea ice, dog sleds, skin boats, and precise knowledge of the polar environment.

unit.en: taels of silver per year equivalent

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自严酷气候、猎物波动、局部群体冲突和资源短缺，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from harsh climate, fluctuations in game, localized group conflict, and resource scarcity rather than large-scale state warfare.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口极为分散，主要分布于北极沿海、河口和季节性狩猎点。定居规模很小，但冬季聚落会形成较稳定的手工业与共同生活单位。
populationDescription.en: The population was highly dispersed, mainly across Arctic coasts, estuaries, and seasonal hunting sites. Settlement size was small, though winter communities formed more stable units of craft production and shared life.

socialClasses.zh: 无固定阶级，主要区分为长者、成功猎手、萨满、普通家庭成员与少数依附者
socialClasses.en: No fixed classes; distinctions mainly existed among elders, successful hunters, shamans, ordinary family members, and a few dependents

allies.zh: 盟友主要是通过婚姻、交换和共同猎场形成联系的邻近聚落群体。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring settlement groups linked by marriage, exchange, and shared hunting grounds.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系通常局限于局部资源竞争、报复性冲突或与其他北极群体的边界摩擦。
enemies.en: Hostility was usually limited to local competition for resources, retaliatory conflict, or boundary friction with other Arctic groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 不存在正式对外政策，其对外行为以维持交换网络、避免资源冲突和争取安全迁徙通道为主。
foreignPolicy.en: There was no formal foreign policy; external behavior centered on maintaining exchange networks, avoiding resource conflict, and securing safe migration routes.

era.zh: 中世纪高北极海猎文化阶段
era.en: Medieval High Arctic marine-hunting cultural phase

keyInnovations.zh: 大型海兽捕猎、复合鱼叉、狗拉雪橇、皮艇与乌米亚克、雪屋和半地下保温住宅。
keyInnovations.en: Large sea-mammal hunting, composite harpoons, dog sleds, kayaks and umiaks, snow houses, and semi-subterranean insulated dwellings.

strengths.zh: 环境适应能力极强，海猎和迁徙技术成熟，社会共享机制有助于提高生存率。
strengths.en: It possessed exceptional environmental adaptation, mature sea-hunting and mobility technology, and sharing mechanisms that improved survival.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、缺乏集中组织、对气候和猎物变化高度敏感。
weaknesses.en: It had a small population, lacked centralized organization, and was highly sensitive to climate and game fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 只要北极生态条件保持可利用，图勒文化仍能扩散和延续；若气候恶化或关键猎物减少，聚落将被迫分散或迁徙。
outlook.en: So long as Arctic ecological conditions remained usable, Thule culture could continue to spread and endure; if climate worsened or key prey declined, settlements would be forced to disperse or migrate.

description.zh: 图勒文化是1200年前后活跃于高北极地区、以海猎、雪橇和亲族共享为核心的北极部族文化共同体。
description.en: Around 1200, Thule culture was a High Arctic tribal cultural complex centered on marine hunting, sled mobility, and kin-based sharing.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，属各希达共同体
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; organized through separate siida communities

governmentForm.zh: 以希达为基础的分散性部族议事治理，由长老与有威望的猎鹿家庭共同决策。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized siida-based tribal governance system in which elders and respected reindeer-hunting households made decisions collectively.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、亲属群和季节性聚居的希达共同体为核心，人口分散在芬诺斯堪的纳维亚北部的森林、山地与苔原。财富主要体现在驯鹿、渔猎资源获取能力与社会声望上。宗教专家与经验丰富的长者在调解冲突和组织迁徙中具有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on families, kin groups, and seasonally organized siida communities scattered across the forests, mountains, and tundra of northern Fennoscandia. Wealth was measured largely in reindeer, access to fishing and hunting grounds, and social prestige. Religious specialists and experienced elders played important roles in mediation and seasonal coordination.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族阶层，实际领导者是希达长老、富有经验的猎手与萨满。其权威主要来自声望、知识和资源分配能力，而非正式官职。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary nobility; practical leadership rested with siida elders, skilled hunters, and shamans. Their authority came chiefly from prestige, knowledge, and the ability to manage resources rather than formal office.

succession.zh: 领导权通常通过协商、声望积累和家族影响力自然形成。若某位长者失去支持，希达成员可转而追随其他有能力的领袖。
succession.en: Leadership usually emerged through consensus, accumulated prestige, and family influence. If an elder lost support, siida members could shift their allegiance to another capable leader.

structure.zh: 萨米人的治理不是国家官僚制，而是按地域和季节组成的希达会议。涉及迁徙路线、渔场、狩猎地和纠纷时，由长老集体裁断。对外贸易与同周边挪威、瑞典、芬兰和诺夫哥罗德商人的接触，多由有经验的家庭代表处理。
structure.en: Sámi governance was not a state bureaucracy but a system of regional and seasonal siida assemblies. Elders collectively judged matters concerning migration routes, fisheries, hunting territories, and disputes. External trade and dealings with merchants from Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Novgorod were usually handled by experienced household representatives.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、资源分配、纠纷调解与对外联系。
function.en: Coordinated migration, resource allocation, dispute mediation, and external relations.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、口述传统、禁忌解释与共同体仪式。
function.en: Oversaw ritual observance, oral tradition, taboo interpretation, and communal ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即各希达自行处理日常事务，没有固定都城与常设地方官。不同地区的共同体在资源边界问题上通过协商或习惯法解决。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted by each siida itself, with no fixed capital and no permanent local magistrates. Different communities handled boundary issues through negotiation or customary law.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯、口头传统和长老裁决为基础。赔偿、调解与维护共同体和谐比严厉惩罚更常见。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, oral tradition, and the judgments of elders. Compensation, mediation, and preservation of community harmony were more common than harsh punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 内部没有成熟的固定税制，公共义务主要体现为共享猎获、协助迁徙和共同祭仪。对外则可能向邻近王国或商人缴纳皮毛贡赋或交换性贡礼。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fully developed internal tax system; communal obligations mainly took the form of sharing catches, assisting migration, and supporting collective rituals. Externally, furs or tribute-like gifts might be given to neighboring kingdoms or merchants.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨米人在口述传统、约伊克歌唱、雪地与苔原生存技术方面具有高度发展。其对北方生态的细致认识，使其能够在极端环境中维持稳定的季节性经济生活。
culturalAchievements.en: The Sámi showed strong development in oral tradition, joik singing, and snow- and tundra-survival skills. Their detailed ecological knowledge of the north allowed them to sustain a stable seasonal economy in extreme environments.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系萨米语支
languageFamily.en: Uralic language family, Sámi branch

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、自然循环和人与土地关系的平衡观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, natural cycles, and the balance between people and the land.

writingSystem.zh: 无本族成文文字，知识主要通过口述、歌谣与象征记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No native written script; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, song, and symbolic memory.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，但以渔猎、驯鹿放牧、采集和皮毛交换维持生计。由于人口分散、商品化程度有限，人均产出更多体现为自给资源而非市场货币收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small in scale but sustained by fishing, hunting, reindeer herding, gathering, and fur exchange. Because the population was dispersed and commercialization limited, per capita output was expressed more in subsistence resources than in market income.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔猎、驯鹿放牧、皮毛采集、骨角与皮革手工业
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, reindeer herding, fur gathering, and bone-antler-leather crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、驯鹿制品、鱼干、兽皮、骨角工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, reindeer products, dried fish, hides, and bone-antler crafts

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常体现在驯鹿数量、渔猎工具、皮帐和与优良资源区的联系上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was usually embodied in reindeer numbers, fishing and hunting gear, skin tents, and ties to productive resource zones.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户自给和共同体资源共享为主，辅以季节性对外交换。
economicSystem.en: Primarily a household subsistence economy with communal resource sharing, supplemented by seasonal external exchange.

source.zh: 共同体贡礼与共享产出

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中式国库，储备多分散为皮货、金属器物、食物和礼品网络中的信用。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were dispersed as furs, metal goods, food stores, and credit embedded in gift networks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏向维持共同体韧性和季节性储备，而非积累集中税收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice favored maintaining communal resilience and seasonal reserves rather than accumulating centralized taxation.

description.zh: 由熟悉地形的猎手组成，擅长伏击、追踪和雪地机动。
description.en: Composed of terrain-savvy hunters skilled in ambush, tracking, and snow mobility.

description.zh: 负责保护家族营地、物资和迁徙路线节点。
description.en: Tasked with protecting family camps, supplies, and nodes along migration routes.

commanderInChief.zh: 希达长老集议
commanderInChief.en: Council of siida elders

technology.zh: 武器以弓、矛、刀和狩猎用具改造为主，作战依赖地形知识、滑雪机动与冬季生存能力。
technology.en: Weapons consisted mainly of bows, spears, knives, and adapted hunting tools, while warfare relied on terrain knowledge, ski mobility, and winter survival skills.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边王国税赋渗透、商人剥削、资源地争夺和局部边地冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from encroaching taxation by neighboring kingdoms, merchant exploitation, competition over resource zones, and localized frontier clashes.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少且分散，主要分布在今挪威、瑞典、芬兰北部和科拉半岛西部。定居点规模很小，社会流动高度季节化。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and dispersed across what are now northern Norway, Sweden, Finland, and the western Kola Peninsula. Settlements were very small, and social movement was highly seasonal.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与萨满、富裕牧猎家庭、普通家户、依附性较强的贫弱成员。
socialClasses.en: Elders and shamans, prosperous herding-hunting households, ordinary families, and more dependent poorer members.

allies.zh: 无固定国家盟友，常与北欧边民和部分商旅维持互利关系。
allies.en: No fixed state allies, though mutually beneficial relations were often maintained with northern frontier peoples and some traders.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌国，但与周边征税势力和竞争性猎场群体存在摩擦。
enemies.en: No permanent unified enemy, but there were frictions with neighboring taxing powers and competing hunting groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 倾向保持自治、灵活周旋与多方贸易，以避免被单一王权完全控制。
foreignPolicy.en: The foreign posture favored autonomy, flexible balancing, and multi-sided trade to avoid full control by any single kingdom.

era.zh: 中世纪北极亚寒带部族技术阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 滑雪与雪地行进技术、驯鹿利用体系、寒地渔猎工具、轻便皮帐与季节性储藏方法。
keyInnovations.en: Ski and snow-travel techniques, reindeer utilization systems, cold-region fishing and hunting tools, portable skin tents, and seasonal storage methods.

strengths.zh: 极强的寒地适应能力、灵活机动、共同体凝聚力高，并掌握珍贵北方资源。
strengths.en: Strong adaptation to cold environments, high mobility, cohesive communities, and control of valuable northern resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、政治分散、缺乏统一军事与财政中心，易受外来政权逐步渗透。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, and lack of centralized military and fiscal institutions made them vulnerable to gradual encroachment by outside powers.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍能维持自治，但随着北欧国家北扩和税制延伸，独立空间将逐步缩小。
outlook.en: They could preserve autonomy in the short term, but the expansion of Nordic states and taxation would gradually reduce their independent space.

description.zh: 1200年前后的萨米人是一个以希达共同体组织、依赖渔猎与驯鹿经济、在北方边疆保持高度自治的分散社会。
description.en: Around 1200, the Sámi were a decentralized society organized through siida communities, sustained by fishing, hunting, and reindeer economies, and maintaining substantial autonomy across the northern frontier.

dynasty.zh: 下缅甸孟人诸邦，受蒲甘王朝强烈影响
dynasty.en: Mon polities of Lower Burma under strong influence from the Pagan Kingdom

governmentForm.zh: 以孟人地方王权和港口城市为核心、并受上位宗主国制约的佛教王国体制。
governmentForm.en: A Buddhist kingdom system centered on Mon local kingship and port cities, constrained by a stronger overlord state.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、僧团、地方贵族、城镇商人、农民与劳役人口构成。佛寺在教育、土地管理和文化传播中占据核心地位。沿海与河口地区的商人群体较为活跃，使社会比内陆农业区更具流动性。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, monkhood, local nobles, urban merchants, cultivators, and corvée laborers. Monasteries occupied a central place in education, land management, and cultural transmission. Merchant groups were especially active in coastal and estuarine zones, making society more mobile than inland agrarian regions.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括孟族王室、地方城邦贵族和受王室保护的上座部佛教僧团。高级官员通常来自王族、姻亲集团和大寺院相关精英。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the Mon royal house, urban-polity nobles, and the Theravada Buddhist monkhood protected by the crown. Senior officials generally came from royal kin, affinal networks, and elites associated with major monasteries.

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族血统为基础，但常受宫廷势力、地方贵族和宗主国干预影响。若继承不稳，地方城邦可能出现短期分裂或拥立竞争者。
succession.en: Succession was based on royal bloodline but was often shaped by court factions, local nobles, and intervention from the overlord state. When succession was unstable, local polities could briefly fragment or back rival claimants.

structure.zh: 孟人国家在1200年前后多半不是完全独立的强帝国，而是以勃固等中心城市为核心的区域性王国和港口网络。王室依靠宫廷官员、地方首领和寺院系统征调劳役、管理水利并维持税收。由于受蒲甘影响，行政文化兼具地方传统与缅甸上层政治结构特征。
structure.en: Around 1200, the Mon state was not a fully independent great empire but a regional kingdom and port network centered on cities such as Pegu. The crown relied on court officials, local chiefs, and monastic institutions to mobilize labor, manage waterworks, and collect revenue. Under Pagan influence, its administrative culture combined local traditions with upper Burmese political structures.

function.zh: 负责王室文书、典礼、宫廷管理与诏令传达。
function.en: Handled royal documentation, ceremony, palace management, and transmission of decrees.

function.zh: 征收稻田赋税、港口关税和市场税，并监督仓储。
function.en: Collected paddy taxes, port customs, and market duties, while supervising storehouses.

function.zh: 组织河道、堤坝、道路修缮以及徭役调配。
function.en: Organized maintenance of waterways, embankments, roads, and labor obligations.

function.zh: 协调王室与僧团关系，管理寺产捐赠、经典抄写和教育事务。
function.en: Coordinated relations between crown and sangha, managing religious endowments, manuscript copying, and education.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由城镇长官、乡村首领和寺院网络共同运作。王权对核心港市控制较强，对外围乡村和河口地区则更多依赖间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out jointly by town governors, village headmen, and monastery networks. Royal control was stronger in core ports and towns, while peripheral rural and estuarine zones were governed more indirectly.

legalSystem.zh: 法律建立在王令、习惯法和佛教道德规范之上。土地纠纷、婚姻和债务案件通常由地方官与僧侣共同调解或裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on royal decrees, customary practice, and Buddhist moral norms. Land disputes, marriage issues, and debt cases were often mediated or judged by local officials together with monks.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自稻田实物税、港口关税、市场税和徭役。寺院捐地与宗教免役会部分削弱王室直接财政，但也巩固了统治合法性。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came chiefly from paddy taxes in kind, port customs, market duties, and corvée labor. Monastic land grants and religious exemptions partly reduced direct royal revenue but strengthened political legitimacy.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 孟人是东南亚早期上座部佛教文化的重要传播者，在碑铭、寺院建筑、佛教抄本和港市文化方面影响深远。其语言和书写传统对缅甸地区政治文化的发展具有重要作用。
culturalAchievements.en: The Mon were important transmitters of early Theravada Buddhist culture in Southeast Asia, with lasting influence in inscriptions, monastic architecture, Buddhist manuscripts, and port-city culture. Their language and writing tradition played a significant role in shaping the political culture of Burma.

languageFamily.zh: 南亚语系孟-高棉语族孟语支
languageFamily.en: Austroasiatic language family, Mon-Khmer branch

philosophy.zh: 以上座部佛教伦理、功德观念与王权护法理念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Theravada Buddhist ethics, merit-making, and the idea of kingship as protector of the faith.

writingSystem.zh: 使用孟文，并广泛运用巴利文于宗教与碑铭。
writingSystem.en: Used the Mon script, with Pali widely employed in religion and inscriptions.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业为基础，并依靠勃固等港口参与孟加拉湾与安达曼海贸易。相较北方内陆政权，其商业化程度较高，海贸收入是重要补充。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture and was supported by ports such as Pegu participating in Bay of Bengal and Andaman Sea trade. Compared with inland northern polities, it was more commercialized, and maritime trade formed an important supplement.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、港口贸易、制盐、渔业、木材与手工业、佛教寺院经济
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, port commerce, salt production, fisheries, timber and crafts, and monastic economy

tradeGoods.zh: 大米、盐、鱼干、木材、象牙、宝石、漆器、纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, dried fish, timber, ivory, gemstones, lacquerware, and textiles

unitName.zh: 缅甸银重单位与贝币串
unitName.en: Burmese silver weight units and strings of cowries

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为稻田、役畜、渔盐设施、船只和与寺院或商路的联系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in paddy land, draft animals, fishing and salt facilities, boats, and connections to monasteries or trade routes.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业剩余和港口关税为支柱的王室—寺院—市场混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed royal-monastic-market economy supported by agrarian surplus and port customs.

source.en: Corvée commutation and royal domain income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由实物储粮、金银器、港口税收和寺院外的王室储备共同构成，流动性中等。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of grain stores in kind, precious metal goods, port revenues, and royal reserves outside the monasteries, with moderate liquidity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政上重视保障港口税源、维持水利农业并通过宗教捐施巩固正统性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized securing customs revenue, maintaining irrigated agriculture, and reinforcing legitimacy through religious patronage.

unit.zh: 缅甸银重单位与贝币串
unit.en: Burmese silver weight units and strings of cowries

description.zh: 由征发农民和地方武士构成，是军队主体，使用矛、刀、弓与盾。
description.en: Formed from levied cultivators and local warriors, it was the army's main body, using spears, blades, bows, and shields.

description.zh: 由王室和贵族掌握，用于冲击、指挥平台和威慑。
description.en: Controlled by the crown and nobility, they were used for shock action, command platforms, and intimidation.

description.zh: 负责港口巡防、河口运输与近海贸易护卫。
description.en: Responsible for port patrol, estuarine transport, and protection of near-sea trade.

commanderInChief.zh: 孟王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The Mon king as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军备处于东南亚中世纪区域平均水平，拥有战象、木弓、长矛、刀剑、木盾和河海舟船。攻城能力有限，但在湿地、河道和港口防卫中较有优势。
technology.en: Military equipment stood at an average medieval Southeast Asian regional level, with war elephants, wooden bows, spears, swords, shields, and riverine-coastal craft. Siege capabilities were limited, but the state was relatively effective in wetlands, waterways, and port defense.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自蒲甘王朝的控制、沿海海盗活动、地方叛乱以及与周边泰—缅势力的竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from control by the Pagan Kingdom, coastal piracy, local revolts, and rivalry with surrounding Tai-Burman powers.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于下缅甸河口平原、港口与稻作区，城市化程度高于多数周边农业政体。寺院、港市和交通节点构成社会人口的主要聚集地。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Lower Burmese delta plain, ports, and rice-growing zones, with a higher degree of urbanization than many surrounding agrarian polities. Monasteries, port towns, and transport nodes formed the main centers of settlement.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、僧侣、商人、自由农民、工匠、劳役人口与奴仆。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobles, monks, merchants, free cultivators, artisans, corvée laborers, and servants.

allies.zh: 与部分沿海商人网络、南亚海商和地方佛教势力保持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative relations with some coastal merchant networks, South Asian seafarers, and local Buddhist powers.

enemies.zh: 无绝对固定敌国，但常受蒲甘控制压力，并与沿海掠夺者和周边竞争政权对立。
enemies.en: There was no absolute permanent enemy, but the state faced pressure from Pagan and opposition from coastal raiders and rival neighboring polities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重点在于保持港口贸易畅通、争取地方自治空间，并以佛教与贸易关系稳固外部联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on keeping port trade open, preserving room for local autonomy, and using Buddhism and commerce to secure external ties.

era.zh: 中世纪东南亚港口王国技术阶段
era.en: Medieval Southeast Asian port-kingdom technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 孟文碑铭传统、佛教寺院建筑、稻田水利管理、河海交通组织与抄经文化。
keyInnovations.en: Mon inscriptional traditions, Buddhist monastic architecture, paddy-water management, organized river-sea transport, and manuscript copying culture.

strengths.zh: 港口贸易发达、佛教文化成熟、农业基础稳固，并处于区域海陆交通节点。
strengths.en: It benefited from active port trade, mature Buddhist culture, a solid agrarian base, and a location on major regional land-sea routes.

weaknesses.zh: 政治独立性不足，军事力量中等，易受更强内陆王朝支配或分化。
weaknesses.en: Its political independence was limited, military power moderate, and it was vulnerable to domination or fragmentation by stronger inland kingdoms.

outlook.zh: 若港口贸易持续并能摆脱上位宗主控制，仍有发展空间；但中短期内更可能维持区域性附庸或半自治地位。
outlook.en: If maritime trade continued and overlord control weakened, it retained room for growth; yet in the near to medium term it was more likely to remain a regional vassal or semi-autonomous polity.

description.zh: 1200年前后的孟人国家是以下缅甸港口与稻作平原为基础、信奉上座部佛教并受蒲甘王朝强烈影响的区域性王国。
description.en: Around 1200, the Mon state was a regional Buddhist kingdom based in the ports and rice plains of Lower Burma and strongly influenced by the Pagan Kingdom.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，由各猎队与地方群体组成
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; organized through local bands and hunting groups

governmentForm.zh: 以亲属关系和季节性猎队为基础的部落式分散治理，由猎队首领和长者协商决策。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal governance system based on kinship and seasonal hunting bands, with decisions made by band leaders and elders.

socialStructure.zh: 因纽人社会由若干流动性的家族猎队构成，随季节在内陆森林、河流和海岸之间迁移。社会分层有限，威望主要来自狩猎能力、慷慨分配和精神权威。共同体在冬季狩猎和夏季集会时形成较强合作结构。
socialStructure.en: Innu society consisted of mobile family hunting bands that moved seasonally between interior forests, rivers, and coasts. Social stratification was limited, and prestige came mainly from hunting skill, generosity in distribution, and spiritual authority. Communities developed stronger cooperative structures during winter hunts and summer gatherings.

rulingClass.zh: 没有固定贵族阶层，最有影响力的是成功的猎手、长者和萨满式宗教人物。其领导地位取决于说服力与实际能力，而非正式血统头衔。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed noble class; the most influential figures were successful hunters, elders, and shamanic religious specialists. Their leadership depended on persuasion and practical ability rather than formal hereditary titles.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常不严格世袭，而是在家族传统、个人能力和群体认可之间形成。若领袖失去威望，猎队成员可分散或改随他人。
succession.en: Leadership was not strictly hereditary, but arose from a combination of family tradition, personal ability, and group recognition. If a leader lost prestige, band members could disperse or follow someone else.

structure.zh: 因纽人的政治结构极为松散，不存在常设国家机构。各猎队在资源利用、迁徙时机和对外交换方面自主决策，但在大型集会、婚姻安排和争端处理中会寻求长者协商。整体秩序依赖习惯、亲属网络和共享生存规则。
structure.en: The Innu political structure was highly loose, with no permanent state institutions. Bands made autonomous decisions on resource use, migration timing, and exchange, but sought elder mediation during large gatherings, marriage arrangements, and disputes. Overall order rested on custom, kin networks, and shared survival norms.

function.zh: 处理狩猎安排、迁徙路线、资源分享与纠纷调解。
function.en: Handled hunting arrangements, migration routes, resource sharing, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、占卜、治疗和与灵界相关的事务。
function.en: Oversaw ritual, divination, healing, and matters relating to the spirit world.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理就是各猎队和家户的自我组织，没有固定行政区划。河谷、湖区和海岸地带的使用权多按习惯和亲属关系分配。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply the self-organization of bands and households, with no fixed territorial districts. Use rights in river valleys, lake zones, and coastal areas were generally allocated by custom and kinship.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以口头传统、禁忌、赔偿和共同体调解为核心。严重冲突通常通过分离迁徙、礼物补偿或长者仲裁解决。
legalSystem.en: Law centered on oral tradition, taboo, compensation, and community mediation. Serious conflicts were usually resolved through separation of groups, gift compensation, or elder arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 内部没有正式税制，公共义务主要体现为分享猎获、相互援助和集体仪式供给。对外交换通常是自愿贸易而非制度化赋税。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal internal taxation; public obligations mainly took the form of sharing catches, mutual aid, and provisioning communal rituals. External exchange was usually voluntary trade rather than institutional taxation.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 因纽人发展出适应拉布拉多与加拿大东北内陆环境的精细狩猎知识、独木舟交通和季节性营地组织。其神话、叙事和仪式传统对后世北美原住民文化研究具有重要价值。
culturalAchievements.en: The Innu developed refined hunting knowledge, canoe travel, and seasonal camp organization adapted to Labrador and the northeastern Canadian interior. Their myths, narratives, and ritual traditions are of major importance for the study of later Indigenous cultures in North America.

languageFamily.zh: 阿尔吉克语系阿尔冈昆语族
languageFamily.en: Algic language family, Algonquian branch

philosophy.zh: 强调人与动物灵、祖先和土地之间的互惠关系与精神平衡。
philosophy.en: Emphasized reciprocal relations and spiritual balance between people, animal spirits, ancestors, and the land.

writingSystem.zh: 无本族文字，文化主要通过口述叙事、歌谣和仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; culture was transmitted mainly through oral narrative, song, and ritual.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎、捕鱼、采集和季节性迁徙为主，市场交换极有限。财富主要是实物性的生存资源，而非可持续积累的货币资本。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence hunting, fishing, gathering, and seasonal mobility, with very limited market exchange. Wealth consisted primarily of tangible survival resources rather than accumulable monetary capital.

mainIndustries.zh: 驯鹿类与大型猎物狩猎、捕鱼、采集、皮革加工、独木舟与骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: Caribou and large-game hunting, fishing, gathering, hide processing, and canoe and bone-antler tool making

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、干肉、鱼、骨角器、木制器具
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried meat, fish, bone-antler tools, and wooden goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在皮帐、独木舟、狩猎工具、储肉能力和优良猎场联系上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was reflected in skin tents, canoes, hunting equipment, the capacity to store meat, and access to favorable hunting grounds.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户和猎队合作的自给经济为主，辅以小规模礼物交换与邻近群体互市。
economicSystem.en: Primarily a subsistence economy organized by households and hunting bands, supplemented by small-scale gift exchange and trade with neighboring groups.

treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中式国库，储备主要分散在各家户的食物、皮货、工具与社会互助关系中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were dispersed among households in the form of food, hides, tools, and mutual-aid relationships.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政行为以保障生存、降低饥荒风险和维持互助网络为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal behavior focused on survival, reducing famine risk, and sustaining mutual-support networks.

description.zh: 由熟练猎手组成，擅长林地伏击、追踪和小规模突袭。
description.en: Made up of skilled hunters adept at forest ambush, tracking, and small-scale raiding.

description.zh: 负责家户营地、食物储备和迁徙停驻点的防护。
description.en: Protected household camps, food stores, and temporary stopping points during migration.

commanderInChief.zh: 各猎队首领联合
commanderInChief.en: Combined band leaders

technology.zh: 武备以弓箭、矛、木棒、石骨器和皮革护具为主，交通依赖独木舟、雪鞋和步行。战斗方式强调对地形、气候和猎踪知识的利用。
technology.en: Equipment consisted mainly of bows, arrows, spears, clubs, stone-bone implements, and hide protection, while movement relied on canoes, snowshoes, and foot travel. Combat emphasized the use of terrain, climate, and tracking knowledge.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族竞争、食物短缺、严酷气候和局部资源压力，而非大型国家入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring tribal competition, food shortages, severe climate, and local resource stress rather than invasion by large states.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少，主要分布于拉布拉多与加拿大东北内陆的河流、森林和湖区。聚落规模很小，多为季节性营地而非永久城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse, spread across the rivers, forests, and lakes of Labrador and the northeastern Canadian interior. Settlements were very small and mostly seasonal camps rather than permanent towns.

name.en: Headwaters camp of the St. John region

socialClasses.zh: 猎队首领与长者、萨满或疗愈者、普通猎手家庭、贫弱依附者。
socialClasses.en: Band leaders and elders, shamans or healers, ordinary hunting families, and poorer dependents.

allies.zh: 与部分邻近阿尔冈昆语族群体保持通婚、贸易或季节性合作。
allies.en: Maintained marriage ties, trade, or seasonal cooperation with some neighboring Algonquian-speaking groups.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌人，但与邻近猎场竞争群体可能发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state enemies, but conflicts could arise with neighboring groups competing for hunting grounds.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在维持资源边界平衡、交换稀缺物资与避免长期报复性冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on balancing resource boundaries, exchanging scarce goods, and avoiding long cycles of retaliatory conflict.

era.zh: 北美东北林地晚期部落技术阶段
era.en: Late woodland tribal technological stage of northeastern North America

keyInnovations.zh: 轻型独木舟、雪鞋与季节性营地体系、大型猎物协作狩猎、皮革防寒工艺与储肉技术。
keyInnovations.en: Light canoe use, snowshoes and seasonal camp systems, cooperative large-game hunting, hide-based cold-weather clothing, and meat preservation techniques.

strengths.zh: 高度适应森林与寒冷环境，机动性强，社会弹性高，能以低物质条件维持稳定生存。
strengths.en: They were highly adapted to forest and cold environments, strongly mobile, socially resilient, and able to sustain stable life with low material requirements.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、政治分散、缺乏集中防御和大规模储备，易受环境波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, and limited centralized defense or large-scale reserves made them vulnerable to environmental fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 在1200年前后仍可维持长期区域自主，但未来若外来贸易与殖民势力进入，将显著改变其经济与权力结构。
outlook.en: Around 1200 they could maintain long-term regional autonomy, but future outside trade and colonial penetration would significantly alter their economy and power structures.

description.zh: 1200年前后的因纽人是分布于拉布拉多与加拿大东北内陆、以季节性猎队和口述传统维系的高流动性部落社会。
description.en: Around 1200, the Innu were a highly mobile tribal society of Labrador and the northeastern Canadian interior, sustained by seasonal hunting bands and oral tradition.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，属多支阿萨巴斯坎语族部落联盟
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; a mosaic of Athabaskan-speaking tribal groups

governmentForm.zh: 以部族、氏族和地方营地为核心的松散部落治理结构，由威望首领与长者协商决策。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal political order centered on bands, clans, and local camps, with decisions made by respected chiefs and elders through consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族关系、氏族联系和季节性迁徙营地为基础，组织规模较小。长者、猎手、萨满与家族首领在各自社群中拥有较高威望。财富主要体现为食物储备、皮毛、工具与互惠网络，而非固定土地占有。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on kinship, clan ties, and seasonal camps, usually organized on a small scale. Elders, skilled hunters, shamans, and household heads held prestige within their communities. Wealth was expressed mainly through food stores, furs, tools, and reciprocal ties rather than fixed landed property.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族阶层，实际领导者多为有威望的首领、战士和宗教专家。其权威依赖个人能力、馈赠和调解能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal hereditary nobility in the strict sense; leadership rested with respected chiefs, warriors, and ritual specialists. Their authority depended on personal ability, generosity, and skill in mediation.

succession.zh: 首领继承通常并非固定长子继承，而是在家族或氏族内部由长者推举最有能力者。若首领失去威望，部众可转而追随他人。
succession.en: Succession to leadership was usually not rigid primogeniture, but selection of a capable candidate within a family or clan by elders. If a chief lost prestige, followers could shift their allegiance elsewhere.

structure.zh: 政治组织高度分散，地方营地拥有很强自治权。跨部落事务如狩猎地协调、防御和婚姻联盟，多通过议事会和临时协商处理。没有常设官僚机构，治理依靠习俗与人际网络。
structure.en: Political organization was highly decentralized, with strong autonomy at the camp level. Inter-group matters such as hunting-ground coordination, defense, and marriage alliances were handled through councils and ad hoc negotiation. There was no permanent bureaucracy; governance relied on custom and personal networks.

function.zh: 协调营地决策、调解纠纷、安排迁徙与资源分配。
function.en: Coordinated camp decisions, mediated disputes, and arranged migration and resource allocation.

function.zh: 由萨满与资深首领维系祭仪、部落间交流及有限交换关系。
function.en: Maintained ritual life, inter-tribal contact, and limited exchange networks through shamans and senior leaders.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以营地和氏族为单位，由家长、长者和临时首领共同处理事务。没有固定领土边界，实际控制力随季节迁徙而变化。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the level of camps and clans, managed by household heads, elders, and temporary leaders. There were no fixed territorial borders, and effective control shifted with seasonal movement.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要表现为习惯法，强调赔偿、调解和维持群体和解。严重冲突可导致放逐、复仇或通过馈赠达成和解。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing compensation, mediation, and the restoration of social harmony. Serious conflicts could lead to exile, feud, or settlement through gifts.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正式税制，首领的资源主要来自自愿馈赠、战利品分配和共同狩猎成果。公共需求依靠互助与再分配满足。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; chiefs drew resources mainly from voluntary gifts, shares of spoils, and communal hunting returns. Public needs were met through reciprocity and redistribution.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿萨巴斯坎诸群体在口头史诗、地景记忆、狩猎知识与皮革工艺方面发展出高度适应寒带与亚寒带环境的传统。其社会记忆依靠叙事、仪式和家族谱系延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Athabaskan groups developed sophisticated traditions in oral epic, landscape memory, hunting knowledge, and hide-working suited to subarctic and northern environments. Social memory was preserved through storytelling, ritual, and family genealogy.

languageFamily.zh: 纳-德内语系阿萨巴斯坎语支
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan branch of the Na-Dene language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵观念和人与自然平衡为核心的萨满传统
philosophy.en: A shamanic worldview centered on animism, ancestral spirits, and balance between people and nature

writingSystem.zh: 无本土成体系文字，知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承
writingSystem.en: No indigenous formal writing system; knowledge was transmitted chiefly through oral tradition, song, and ritual

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (est.)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎、渔猎、采集和有限部落间交换为主，货币化程度极低。以银值折算仅为便于比较的估计，并不代表本地存在统一银本位市场。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based primarily on subsistence hunting, fishing, gathering, and limited inter-tribal exchange, with very low monetization. Silver equivalents are only a comparative estimate and do not imply a local silver-based market.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、采集、皮毛加工、骨角器与木器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, gathering, fur processing, and the making of bone, antler, and wooden tools

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、燧石与石料、兽皮衣物、干鱼、骨角制品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, flint and stone materials, hide clothing, dried fish, and bone-antler goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常表现为狩猎工具、住具、皮毛储备、食物和亲族互助关系。贫富差异有限，但资源波动受季节和猎获影响很大。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth usually consisted of hunting gear, shelter equipment, fur stores, food, and kin-based support ties. Inequality was limited, but resources fluctuated sharply with seasons and hunting success.

unit.en: taels of silver per adult per year (est.)

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、共有资源利用和馈赠交换为特征的部落互惠经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal reciprocal economy based on household production, shared resource use, and gift exchange

source.zh: 部众馈赠与首领再分配
source.en: Gifts from followers and chiefly redistribution

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”主要是首领与若干营地掌握的食物、皮毛和工具储备，银值仅作折算。储备规模较小，易受严冬和猎物减少影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of food, furs, and tools held by chiefs and several camps, with silver values used only as an estimate. Reserves were modest and vulnerable to hard winters and declines in game.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以储备管理、互助分配和危机时的再分配为主，缺乏稳定征收机制。首领通过慷慨馈赠维持政治支持。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on reserve management, mutual aid, and redistribution in times of need, without any stable tax mechanism. Chiefs maintained support through generosity and gift-giving.

description.zh: 以弓、矛、木盾和短兵器作战，适于伏击、追踪和小规模袭扰。
description.en: Armed with bows, spears, wooden shields, and short weapons, suited for ambush, tracking, and small-scale raiding.

description.zh: 平时为猎手，战时负责营地防御、侦察和物资护送。
description.en: Hunters in peacetime, they served in war as camp defenders, scouts, and escorts for supplies.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各部首领临时统领
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; local chiefs led forces as needed

technology.zh: 军事技术以木弓、骨角与石制武器、皮甲和寒地行军经验为主，缺乏攻城器械与重装体系。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden bows, bone-antler and stone weaponry, hide armor, and cold-environment mobility, lacking siege engines or heavy equipment.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族冲突、狩猎地竞争、气候波动和食物短缺，而非大型国家入侵。
threats.en: Major threats came from neighboring tribal conflict, competition over hunting grounds, climatic variation, and food shortages rather than invasion by large states.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于北方森林、河谷和湖区，以小型聚落和季节性营地为主。寿命和人口增长受气候、疫病与猎物资源限制。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across northern forests, river valleys, and lake regions, living mostly in small settlements and seasonal camps. Life expectancy and population growth were constrained by climate, disease, and game availability.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较弱，大致可分为首领与长者、萨满、熟练猎手、普通家户及少量战俘或依附者。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was weak, with a rough distinction among chiefs and elders, shamans, skilled hunters, ordinary households, and a small number of captives or dependents.

allies.zh: 以邻近亲缘部落和季节性贸易伙伴为主，联盟通常短期且因地而异。
allies.en: Allies were mainly nearby kin-related groups and seasonal trading partners, with alliances usually local and temporary.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为争夺猎场和通道的邻近部落，而非固定国家敌人。
enemies.en: Enemies were generally neighboring groups competing over hunting grounds and routes rather than fixed state-level foes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持狩猎地平衡、婚姻联姻和交换网络为核心，倾向避免长期大规模战争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining balance over hunting lands, marriage ties, and exchange networks, with a preference for avoiding sustained large-scale warfare.

era.zh: 北美北方铁器接触前后并存的晚期石骨器时代
era.en: Late Stone-and-bone tool era in northern North America, before or with minimal iron contact

keyInnovations.zh: 高适应性的寒地狩猎技术、桦皮与木器制作、皮革加工、口述导航与季节迁徙体系。
keyInnovations.en: Highly adaptive cold-region hunting techniques, bark and woodcraft, hide processing, oral navigation, and seasonal mobility systems.

strengths.zh: 环境适应力强、机动性高、社群韧性大，能在严酷生态中维持稳定生存。
strengths.en: Strong environmental adaptation, high mobility, and resilient communities enabled stable survival in harsh ecological conditions.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、政治分散、物资剩余有限，难以形成长期统一军事力量。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, and limited surplus made sustained unified military action difficult.

outlook.zh: 若气候与资源状况平稳，可维持区域性延续；但面对外来疾病、贸易重组或武器技术差距时将较为脆弱。
outlook.en: If climate and resources remained stable, regional continuity was likely; however, the society would be vulnerable to external disease, shifts in trade, or widening gaps in weapon technology.

description.zh: 阿萨巴斯坎人在1200年前后仍是以亲族、营地和萨满传统维系的北方部落社会。
description.en: Around 1200, the Athabaskans were a northern tribal society held together by kinship, camp organization, and shamanic tradition.

dynasty.zh: 拉杰普特诸王朝并立，以朱罗、乔汉、古希罗特、帕拉马拉等家族为主
dynasty.en: Multiple Rajput dynasties, notably the Chahamanas, Guhilots, Paramaras, and related lineages

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭王权、封臣网络和堡垒城市为支柱的区域性印度教王国体系。
governmentForm.en: A regional Hindu kingdom system built on hereditary kingship, feudatory networks, and fortified urban centers.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以种姓秩序和封建化地方关系为基础，刹帝利武士阶层地位突出。婆罗门掌握宗教权威并参与王权合法化，农民、工匠和商人构成税赋与生产主体。部分边地部落和附庸首领被逐步纳入王国政治结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was grounded in caste order and semi-feudal local relations, with the Kshatriya warrior elite occupying a prominent place. Brahmins held religious authority and helped legitimize kingship, while peasants, artisans, and merchants formed the productive and taxable base. Some frontier tribes and subordinate chiefs were gradually incorporated into the political framework of the kingdoms.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由拉杰普特王族、地方封臣、堡主和婆罗门顾问构成。王室通过土地赐封、婚姻联盟与军事效忠维系统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Rajput royal houses, local feudatories, fort commanders, and Brahmin advisers. Royal authority was maintained through land grants, marriage alliances, and military loyalty.

succession.zh: 王位原则上实行父系世袭，但兄弟、堂亲与支系争位极常见。继承纠纷常引发内战或促使附庸倒向外敌。
succession.en: Succession was in principle patrilineal and hereditary, but contests among brothers, cousins, and collateral branches were common. Such disputes often triggered civil war or drove feudatories into the arms of outside powers.

structure.zh: 拉杰普特诸国通常以国王为中心，辅以王廷大臣、军政封臣和地方堡垒体系。中央对核心领地控制较强，但边远地区多依赖附庸王、公侯和土邦首领。行政与军事紧密结合，王国稳定性高度依赖个人君主威望。
structure.en: Rajput polities were typically centered on the king, assisted by court ministers, military-administrative feudatories, and a network of forts. The center exercised firmer control in core territories, while peripheral regions depended heavily on vassal rulers and local chiefs. Administration and warfare were closely intertwined, and stability depended greatly on the prestige of individual monarchs.

function.zh: 处理诏令、文书、外交、土地赐封及王室事务。
function.en: Handled royal edicts, records, diplomacy, land grants, and palace affairs.

function.zh: 征收地税、关税、市场税并管理王室领地收入。
function.en: Collected land revenue, customs, and market taxes, and managed crown-land income.

function.zh: 负责征召封臣兵、维护城堡、军械和边境防务。
function.en: Oversaw feudal levies, fort maintenance, armaments, and frontier defense.

function.zh: 审理案件、确认习惯法，并协调寺庙捐献与宗教地产。
function.en: Adjudicated cases, affirmed customary law, and coordinated temple endowments and religious estates.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由省级总督、封臣、堡主、村社首领与税吏共同承担。村社在灌溉、治安和税粮分配方面保有一定自治传统。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by provincial governors, feudatories, fort lords, village headmen, and revenue officials. Village communities retained a degree of autonomy in irrigation, order, and tax distribution.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合《达摩法》传统、王室敕令、地方习惯与种姓规范。司法常由王廷、婆罗门法学者和地方首领共同参与。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Dharmashastra tradition, royal decrees, local custom, and caste norms. Justice was often administered jointly by the court, Brahmin jurists, and local chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入以地税为主，并辅以商税、关卡税、矿税和附庸贡赋。部分寺庙地产和军功封地享有减免税待遇。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue rested mainly on land tax, supplemented by commercial tolls, customs, mining dues, and tribute from subordinates. Some temple estates and military fiefs enjoyed tax privileges or exemptions.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 拉杰普特时期在堡垒建筑、庙宇雕刻、宫廷诗歌和英雄叙事方面成就突出。阿杰梅尔、奇陶尔等地的城堡与神庙体现了武士文化与宗教赞助的结合。
culturalAchievements.en: The Rajput age was notable for achievements in fort architecture, temple sculpture, court poetry, and heroic narrative. Forts and temples at places such as Ajmer and Chittor reflected the union of warrior culture and religious patronage.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-Aryan branch of the Indo-European language family

philosophy.zh: 印度教王权观、刹帝利荣誉伦理、婆罗门教法传统与地方英雄崇拜并存
philosophy.en: A blend of Hindu kingship ideology, Kshatriya honor ethics, Brahmanical legal tradition, and regional hero cults

writingSystem.zh: 以天城体、沙拉达体及地方婆罗米系文字书写梵语、普拉克里特语和早期拉贾斯坦语
writingSystem.en: Devanagari, Sharada, and related Brahmi-derived scripts used for Sanskrit, Prakrit, and early Rajasthani forms

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year (est.)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础在于农业剩余、灌溉、手工业和穿越西北印度的陆上贸易。王国间战争破坏部分生产，但核心平原和商路城镇仍保持可观财富积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agrarian surplus, irrigation, artisanal production, and overland trade across northwestern India. Warfare among kingdoms damaged some production, but core plains and trade towns still generated considerable wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、棉纺织、金属加工、石工建筑、马匹贸易、盐业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, cotton textiles, metalworking, stone construction, horse trade, and salt production

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、靛蓝、香料、宝石、盐、铜铁器、战马
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, indigo, spices, gemstones, salt, copper and iron goods, and warhorses

name.en: Dramma and dinar in mixed circulation

householdWealth.zh: 乡村家庭财富主要取决于土地、牲畜和灌溉条件；城市商人与手工业者积累银钱、货物和信贷关系。贵族财富高度集中于土地收益、战利品和贡赋。
householdWealth.en: Rural household wealth depended mainly on land, livestock, and irrigation access; urban merchants and artisans accumulated bullion, goods, and credit ties. Aristocratic wealth was heavily concentrated in land revenue, spoils, and tribute.

unit.zh: 第纳尔银值/成年人/年（估）
unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per adult per year (est.)

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租为核心、结合商税和封臣贡赋的王国经济
economicSystem.en: A kingdom economy centered on agrarian rent, supplemented by trade taxes and feudatory tribute

source.en: Tribute from feudatories and war booty

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银钱、金饰、粮储和寺庙借贷网络中的流动资源构成。持续战争和封臣分裂使国库常处于紧张状态。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver specie, gold ornaments, grain reserves, and liquid resources tied to temple-credit networks. Persistent warfare and feudatory fragmentation kept the treasury under strain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持军备、酬赏封臣和保障王权威望，必要时提高附庸贡赋与商路税收。长期来看，战争支出常压缩公共建设和储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military readiness, rewards to feudatories, and the maintenance of royal prestige, with tribute and trade-route taxation raised when necessary. Over time, war expenditure often squeezed public works and reserves.

description.zh: 由拉杰普特武士和附庸贵族提供，是机动作战和冲击的核心力量，重视个人勇武与荣誉。
description.en: Provided by Rajput warriors and subordinate nobles, this was the key mobile striking arm, emphasizing personal valor and honor.

description.zh: 由乡兵、附庸部队和城镇兵组成，负责守城、列阵和支援骑兵。
description.en: Composed of rural levies, feudatory contingents, and town troops, they handled defense, line fighting, and support for cavalry.

description.zh: 用于震慑、冲阵和王家威仪展示，但维护成本高且在灵活机动方面有限。
description.en: Used for shock, intimidation, and royal display, but expensive to maintain and limited in flexibility.

commanderInChief.zh: 拉杰普特诸王及其大将军
commanderInChief.en: The Rajput kings and their mahasenapatis

technology.zh: 军事技术包括铁制刀剑、长矛、复合或强化弓、锁子甲与坚固山地城堡。相较中亚突骑与高度协调的骑射战术，在战役机动和统一指挥上略处下风。
technology.en: Military technology included iron swords, lances, strengthened bows, mail armor, and strong hill forts. Compared with Central Asian mounted archery and highly coordinated cavalry tactics, they were somewhat weaker in operational mobility and unified command.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自突厥-古尔王朝势力东扩、邻近拉杰普特诸国间内耗以及封臣离心。西北边境的骑兵入侵对传统堡垒-封臣体系形成持续压力。
threats.en: Major threats came from the eastward expansion of Turkic-Ghurid power, internecine conflict among Rajput states, and centrifugal feudatories. Cavalry incursions from the northwest placed sustained pressure on the traditional fort-and-feudatory system.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 拜占庭海战秘密武器，水上不灭之火
description.en: Byzantine naval secret weapon, inextinguishable fire on water

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于拉贾斯坦、马尔瓦及邻近平原、绿洲与商路节点，农村人口占绝大多数。城市依托王廷、神庙和商贸活动发展，但整体城市化程度仍有限。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in Rajasthan, Malwa, and nearby plains, oases, and trade nodes, with the vast majority living in rural communities. Cities developed around courts, temples, and commerce, but overall urbanization remained limited.

name.zh: 焦特布尔地区旧城聚落

socialClasses.zh: 大致分为王族与拉杰普特贵族、婆罗门、商人、工匠、农民、部落附庸及贱役群体，各层级受种姓与地方身份共同塑造。
socialClasses.en: Society may be broadly divided into royalty and Rajput nobles, Brahmins, merchants, artisans, peasants, tribal dependents, and servile groups, with caste and local status shaping each rank.

allies.zh: 通过婚姻和共敌关系形成的其他拉杰普特家族、部分印度教土邦及商路城邦。
allies.en: Other Rajput lineages, some Hindu principalities, and trade-route towns linked by marriage ties and common enemies.

enemies.zh: 古尔王朝及其突厥将领、竞争中的拉杰普特诸侯、边境掠袭势力。
enemies.en: The Ghurids and their Turkic commanders, rival Rajput princes, and raiding frontier powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重在维持家族声望、巩固婚盟、控制要塞和商路，并在必要时联合抗击外来穆斯林政权。然内部竞争常削弱一致对外能力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving dynastic prestige, strengthening marriage alliances, controlling forts and trade routes, and at times uniting against incoming Muslim powers. Yet internal rivalry often undermined coordinated action.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期北印度王国技术阶段
era.en: High medieval technological stage of northern Indian kingdoms

keyInnovations.zh: 大型山地与台地堡垒、石造庙宇建筑、区域灌溉体系、成熟棉纺与金属兵器制造。
keyInnovations.en: Large hill and plateau forts, stone temple architecture, regional irrigation systems, and mature cotton textile and metal-weapon production.

strengths.zh: 拥有强烈武士传统、坚固堡垒网络、可观农业税基和活跃商路财富。
strengths.en: They possessed a strong warrior tradition, robust fort networks, a substantial agrarian tax base, and wealth from active trade routes.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂、封臣独立性强、继承争斗频繁，难以形成持久统一战线。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, powerful feudatories, and frequent succession disputes made sustained unified defense difficult.

outlook.zh: 若不能克服内部分裂并适应突厥骑兵战争方式，未来数十年将面临显著战略压力和领土损失风险。
outlook.en: Unless internal divisions were overcome and warfare adapted to Turkic cavalry methods, the coming decades would bring serious strategic pressure and risk of territorial loss.

description.zh: 1200年前后的拉杰普特诸王国是以武士贵族、堡垒城邦和印度教王权为核心、但深受内斗困扰的区域强权群。
description.en: Around 1200, the Rajput Kingdoms were a cluster of regional powers centered on warrior aristocracy, fortified cities, and Hindu kingship, yet deeply troubled by internal conflict.

dynasty.zh: 后期迦摩缕波诸王系，地方王权已趋分散
dynasty.en: Late Kamarupa royal lineages, with increasingly fragmented local kingship

governmentForm.zh: 以婆罗门化王权和地方首领附属关系构成的边疆型印度教王国。
governmentForm.en: A frontier Hindu kingdom built on Brahmanized kingship and the subordination of local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、婆罗门、地方酋长、农民与河谷手工业者组成，边地山地部族保持相当自治。婆罗门移民和土地捐赠推动了河谷地区的印度化与阶层分化。农村聚落沿布拉马普特拉河及其支流展开，水患与地理阻隔使统治较为松散。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, Brahmins, local chiefs, peasants, and river-valley artisans, while hill tribes on the margins retained substantial autonomy. Brahmin settlement and land grants advanced the Indianization and stratification of the valley. Rural communities stretched along the Brahmaputra and its tributaries, and floods plus difficult geography kept rule relatively loose.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括王室、受封婆罗门地主及地方军事首领。王权对远地山林地区的控制常依赖联姻、赐地和象征性臣属。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the royal family, landholding Brahmins supported by grants, and local military chiefs. Royal control over distant forest and hill regions often depended on marriage ties, land gifts, and symbolic submission.

succession.zh: 王位通常为世袭，但在王权衰落时期，地方豪强和支系王族常介入继承。继位合法性需要宗教仪式与地方贵族承认双重支持。
succession.en: The throne was normally hereditary, but in times of royal decline local magnates and collateral dynasts often intervened. Legitimacy required both religious consecration and recognition by local elites.

structure.zh: 迦摩缕波的行政以王廷、河谷税区和附属地方酋领为骨架。核心河谷地区设有较明确的征税与寺产管理，但边远地区多采间接统治。到1200年前后，中央整合力明显下降，地方化倾向增强。
structure.en: Kamarupa's administration was structured around the royal court, river-valley revenue districts, and subordinate local chieftains. Core valley zones had clearer taxation and temple-estate management, while remote areas were governed indirectly. By around 1200, central integrative power had declined markedly and localism was strengthening.

function.zh: 颁布敕令、记录土地赐封、管理外交往来与王室档案。
function.en: Issued edicts, recorded land grants, and managed diplomacy and royal archives.

name.en: Land Revenue and River Transport Office

function.zh: 征收谷物地税、组织渡口与河道运输，并监管仓储。
function.en: Collected grain-based land revenue, organized ferries and river transport, and supervised storage.

name.zh: 军务与边地酋领事务署

function.zh: 协调王家亲军、象军及附属酋长兵力，处理边地臣服关系。
function.en: Coordinated royal retainers, elephant forces, and the troops of subordinate chiefs, and managed frontier allegiance.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠河谷税区官员、村社长与附属酋长共同完成。山地与森林地区多以朝贡和名义臣属方式维系，中央日常干预有限。
localAdmin.en: Local government relied on valley revenue officers, village heads, and subordinate chiefs working together. Hill and forest regions were held mostly through tribute and nominal allegiance, with limited routine intervention from the center.

legalSystem.zh: 法律结合印度教法、王命、村社习惯和部族惯例。土地、婚姻与宗教捐赠常由婆罗门和地方头人共同见证裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Hindu legal norms, royal command, village custom, and tribal practice. Matters of land, marriage, and religious donation were often judged with the participation of Brahmins and local headmen.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以谷物地税、劳役、渡口税和林产贡纳为主。部分婆罗门与寺庙受赐地享有税赋减免。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on grain land tax, labor obligations, ferry dues, and tribute in forest products. Some Brahmin and temple grant lands enjoyed exemptions.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 迦摩缕波在铜版赐地文书、寺庙赞助和河谷印度化方面具有区域影响。其文化特色在于把梵语政治传统与布拉马普特拉流域的地方宗教和酋邦结构结合起来。
culturalAchievements.en: Kamarupa had regional significance in copper-plate land grants, temple patronage, and the Indianization of the valley. Its cultural distinctiveness lay in combining Sanskritic political tradition with local religion and chieftain-based structures of the Brahmaputra basin.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语支，并与藏缅语族语言长期接触
languageFamily.en: Indo-Aryan within the Indo-European family, in long contact with Tibeto-Burman languages

philosophy.zh: 婆罗门化印度教、湿婆与毗湿奴崇拜，并与地方神灵和部族传统融合
philosophy.en: Brahmanized Hinduism, including Shaiva and Vaishnava worship, fused with local deities and tribal traditions

writingSystem.zh: 使用东印度婆罗米后裔文字，向早期阿萨姆文字形态过渡，行政和宗教文书多用梵语
writingSystem.en: Used an eastern Brahmi-derived script transitioning toward early Assamese forms, with Sanskrit common in administration and religion

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year (est.)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业、河运、林产、象与手工业为基础，但受洪水、地理阻隔和政治衰退影响，整体增长有限。银值估算用于跨区域比较，不代表当地高度货币化。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture, river transport, forest products, elephants, and handicrafts, but overall growth was limited by floods, difficult terrain, and political decline. Silver-value estimates are for comparison and do not imply a highly monetized local economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、林产采集、象驯养、纺织、金属与木器手工业、河运
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, forest-product extraction, elephant capture and training, textiles, metal and wood crafts, and river transport

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、丝织与棉织品、象、蜂蜡、漆、木材、香料与林产
tradeGoods.en: Rice, silk and cotton textiles, elephants, beeswax, lacquer, timber, spices, and forest goods

name.en: Cowries and metal coin in mixed use

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在稻田、家畜、渔具和竹木房屋；河运商人与受赐地婆罗门拥有更高积累。受洪水和政治动荡影响，财富区域差异较大。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in rice fields, livestock, fishing gear, and bamboo-wood housing; river traders and grant-holding Brahmins possessed higher levels of accumulation. Flooding and political instability produced substantial regional disparities.

unit.zh: 银第纳尔折值/成年人/年（估）
unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per adult per year (est.)

economicSystem.zh: 以河谷农业和地方贡赋为主、货币化有限的边疆王国经济
economicSystem.en: A frontier kingdom economy based on valley agriculture and local tribute, with limited monetization

category.en: Court expenses and rewards to local chiefs

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备有限，多由谷仓、象贡、少量银币和贵重物资组成。洪灾、地方叛离和王权衰弱使财政缓冲能力不足。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were limited, consisting mainly of granary stores, elephant tribute, small amounts of silver coin, and valuables. Floods, local defection, and weakening kingship reduced fiscal resilience.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在保障河谷核心区收入、维持地方酋长效忠与支撑有限军备。由于中央衰退，更多依赖实物征收和临时贡纳而非稳定现金税源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to secure revenue from the core valley, preserve the loyalty of local chiefs, and sustain a modest military. As the center weakened, it relied increasingly on collection in kind and occasional tribute rather than stable cash income.

description.zh: 由河谷常备亲军和地方征召兵组成，承担守城、护仓与沿河作战任务。
description.en: Made up of royal retainers and locally levied troops from the valley, responsible for garrison duty, storehouse protection, and riverine warfare.

description.zh: 用于冲击、威慑和王权象征，兼有贵族近卫性质。
description.en: Used for shock, intimidation, and as a symbol of kingship, while also serving as noble guard formations.

description.zh: 来自附属酋邦和边地部族，熟悉山林与河网地形，但忠诚度不稳。
description.en: Drawn from subordinate chiefdoms and frontier tribes, familiar with hills, forests, and river networks but uneven in loyalty.

commanderInChief.zh: 迦摩缕波王与军务大臣
commanderInChief.en: The King of Kamarupa and his chief military officer

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制刀矛、弓箭、象军和河运机动为特色，擅长湿地与河谷环境作战。缺乏大型攻城体系和高度专业化骑兵。
technology.en: Military technology featured iron blades and spears, bows, elephants, and river mobility, well suited to wetland and valley warfare. It lacked large siege systems and highly specialized cavalry.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自地方酋长离心、邻近山地部族冲突、东部与北部边境压力，以及王权衰退导致的内部瓦解。
threats.en: Major threats came from centrifugal local chiefs, conflict with neighboring hill peoples, pressure on eastern and northern frontiers, and internal disintegration caused by dynastic weakness.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于布拉马普特拉河谷的农耕带，山地和森林地区则较为稀疏。由于地形复杂和洪水频仍，大型城市不多，聚落多沿河分布。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the agricultural belt of the Brahmaputra valley, while hill and forest zones were sparse. Because of difficult terrain and frequent flooding, large cities were few and settlements clustered along waterways.

socialClasses.zh: 大致分为王室与贵族、婆罗门受赐地主、地方酋长、自由农民、手工业者与河运商人，以及边地部族社群。
socialClasses.en: Society can be broadly divided into royalty and nobles, Brahmin grantees, local chiefs, free peasants, artisans and river traders, and frontier tribal communities.

allies.zh: 核心同盟多为河谷婆罗门地主、附属酋长及若干愿意朝贡的边地部族。
allies.en: Core allies were chiefly valley Brahmin landholders, subordinate chiefs, and certain frontier tribes willing to pay tribute.

enemies.zh: 敌对力量包括叛离的地方首领、边境山地部族以及觊觎河谷通道的邻近势力。
enemies.en: Hostile forces included rebellious local rulers, border hill tribes, and neighboring powers seeking access to the valley routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持河谷核心地带、安抚边地酋领和控制河运节点为主，整体偏防御性。随着王权衰退，更依赖妥协和象征性宗主权而非直接扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving the core valley, placating frontier chiefs, and controlling river transport nodes, and was broadly defensive. As royal power declined, it relied more on compromise and symbolic suzerainty than direct expansion.

era.zh: 中世纪东北印度河谷王国技术阶段
era.en: Medieval northeastern Indian river-valley kingdom technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 河运与渡口管理、湿润环境稻作、铜版赐地文书传统、象军运用和竹木工艺。
keyInnovations.en: River transport and ferry management, wet-rice cultivation, copper-plate grant documentation, elephant warfare, and bamboo-wood craftsmanship.

strengths.zh: 依托大河谷地、稻作基础和地方宗教整合，具备一定区域韧性与防御纵深。
strengths.en: Backed by a major river valley, rice agriculture, and local religious integration, it retained some regional resilience and defensive depth.

weaknesses.zh: 中央权力衰退、边地控制薄弱、货币化不足且受洪水地理严重制约。
weaknesses.en: Central authority was declining, frontier control was weak, monetization was limited, and the state was heavily constrained by flood-prone geography.

outlook.zh: 若不能重建河谷政治整合，迦摩缕波将继续分裂为更地方化的政权与酋邦结构。
outlook.en: Unless political integration in the valley could be rebuilt, Kamarupa was likely to continue fragmenting into more localized polities and chieftaincies.

description.zh: 1200年前后的迦摩缕波是一个以布拉马普特拉河谷为核心、正处于地方化与王权衰退中的东北印度边疆王国。
description.en: Around 1200, Kamarupa was a northeastern Indian frontier kingdom centered on the Brahmaputra valley and undergoing localization and royal decline.

dynasty.zh: 塔克鲁尔王朝（受萨赫勒伊斯兰王权传统影响）
dynasty.en: Kingdom of Takrur ruling house under Sahelian Islamic monarchy

governmentForm.zh: 由穆斯林国王统治的萨赫勒王国，结合王权、地方酋长与伊斯兰法学者的权威。
governmentForm.en: A Sahelian kingdom ruled by a Muslim king, combining royal authority with local chiefs and Islamic scholars.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与贵族、穆斯林学者、自由农牧民、商人和依附性劳动者构成。塞内加尔河流域的定居农业人口与半流动牧民并存，伊斯兰化主要集中于宫廷、商路节点与城镇。地方社群仍保留较强的宗族与村社结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal family and nobles, Muslim scholars, free farmers and pastoralists, merchants, and dependent laborers. Settled agricultural populations along the Senegal River coexisted with semi-mobile pastoral groups, while Islamization was strongest at court, along trade routes, and in towns. Local communities retained strong lineage and village structures.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、王族、地方首领和接受伊斯兰教育的宫廷顾问组成。穆斯林法学者与商人虽不总是出身贵族，但对政策和合法性具有重要影响。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal kin, provincial chiefs, and court advisers trained in Islam. Muslim jurists and merchants were not always aristocratic by birth, but they held important influence over policy and legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，但实践中常需得到主要贵族与地方首领承认。继承既受血统约束，也受军事支持与宗教声望影响。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the royal lineage, but in practice required recognition by major nobles and provincial chiefs. Succession depended on bloodline as well as military backing and religious prestige.

structure.zh: 国王通过宫廷直接统辖核心河谷地区，对边缘地区则依赖臣属首领和地方酋长间接治理。伊斯兰法官与书记官在税收、司法和外交中发挥越来越大的作用，但行政仍较为简约。
structure.en: The king governed the core river valley directly through the court, while peripheral areas were administered indirectly through subordinate chiefs and local headmen. Islamic judges and scribes played a growing role in taxation, justice, and diplomacy, though administration remained relatively compact.

function.zh: 管理王室土地、贡赋、仓储和对附属首领的征收。
function.en: Managed royal lands, tribute, granaries, and collections from subordinate chiefs.

function.zh: 监督河道与陆路商队节点，征收市场税与过境税。
function.en: Oversaw river and caravan-route nodes and collected market dues and transit customs.

function.zh: 处理伊斯兰法裁判、文书记录和与学者社群的联系。
function.en: Handled Islamic legal judgments, record keeping, and liaison with scholarly communities.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠村社长老、酋长与王室任命的使者。核心区控制较强，边缘地带多以纳贡和盟约维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on village elders, chiefs, and royal envoys. Control was stronger in the core territories, while peripheral zones were governed through tribute and alliance.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具习惯法与伊斯兰法。商贸、婚姻和部分刑事案件常由卡迪依教法裁判，土地与宗族事务则多循地方惯例。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined customary law and Islamic law. Trade, marriage, and some criminal matters were often judged by qadis under sharia, while land and lineage affairs usually followed local custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础包括农产贡赋、牲畜征收、市场税和跨撒哈拉贸易关税。附属社群多以实物、劳役或黄金与盐的转运收益纳贡。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base included agricultural tribute, livestock levies, market dues, and customs on trans-Saharan trade. Dependent communities often paid in kind, labor, or through profits from gold and salt transit.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 塔克鲁尔在西非较早实现宫廷伊斯兰化，促进了学者、商人和法官网络的发展。其文化意义在于将塞内加尔河流域纳入更广阔的马格里布—撒哈拉世界。
culturalAchievements.en: Takrur was one of the earlier West African courts to embrace Islam, encouraging the growth of networks of scholars, merchants, and judges. Its cultural significance lay in integrating the Senegal River valley into the wider Maghrebi-Saharan world.

languageFamily.zh: 西大西洋语支诸语言为主，宗教与书面交流使用阿拉伯语
languageFamily.en: Primarily West Atlantic languages, with Arabic for religion and written exchange

philosophy.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰信仰为上层意识形态，同时保留萨赫勒地方习俗与祖先传统。
philosophy.en: Sunni Islam served as the dominant elite worldview, while Sahelian local customs and ancestral traditions remained influential.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯文于宗教与贸易记录，地方语言多依口传传统。
writingSystem.en: Arabic was used mainly for religious and commercial records, while local languages remained largely oral.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以河谷农业、畜牧和转口贸易为核心。人均产出在萨赫勒地区属中等，跨撒哈拉贸易使王室获得高于单纯农业社会的收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on riverine agriculture, pastoralism, and transit trade. Per capita output was moderate by Sahelian standards, while trans-Saharan exchange gave the crown revenues above those of a purely agrarian society.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟米与高粱农业、牧牛牧羊、河运贸易、转口关税、皮革与手工业
mainIndustries.en: Millet and sorghum farming, cattle and sheep herding, river trade, transit customs, leatherwork, and small crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、盐、牲畜、皮革、谷物、奴隶、布匹
tradeGoods.en: Gold, salt, livestock, leather, grain, slaves, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为粮食储备、牲畜和宗族网络。商人与地方首领则更可能持有金粉、进口布匹和奴隶劳力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in grain stores, livestock, and kinship ties. Merchants and local chiefs were more likely to hold gold dust, imported cloth, and slave labor.

unit.en: kg silver per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业与牧业为基础、由王室贡赋和商路税收支撑的区域交换经济。
economicSystem.en: A regional exchange economy based on agriculture and pastoralism, supported by royal tribute and trade-route taxation.
